a.m./a.m./am/am.label: CEFR A1
a.m./a.m./am/am.def: The time from midnight until twelve noon.
a.m./a.m./am/am.ex: School starts at 9 a.m. every morning.

abandoned.label: CEFR B2
abandoned.def: Left empty or unused, often in a deteriorated state, with no one taking care of it.
abandoned.ex: The abandoned warehouse near the railway station has become a haven for urban explorers and street artists.

ability.label: CEFR A2
ability.def: The power or skill to do something.
ability.ex: She has the ability to speak three languages fluently.

able.label: CEFR B1
able.def: Having the power, skill, or knowledge to do something successfully.
able.ex: After months of practice, she was finally able to speak Spanish fluently in meetings.

abnormal.label: CEFR B1
abnormal.def: Not normal or usual; different from what you expect in a strange or worrying way
abnormal.ex: The doctor said his blood pressure was abnormal and wanted to do more tests.

abnormally.label: CEFR B2
abnormally.def: In a way that is different from what is usual or expected; not normal.
abnormally.ex: The patient's blood pressure was abnormally high, prompting the doctor to run additional tests.

aboard.label: CEFR B1
aboard.def: On or into a ship, aircraft, or train.
aboard.ex: All passengers must be aboard the plane before it takes off.

abolish.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
abolish.def: To officially end or remove something, especially a law, system, or practice, completely.
abolish.ex: The government decided to abolish the outdated tax policy after years of public pressure.

aboriginal.label: CEFR B2
aboriginal.def: Relating to the original people who lived in a place before others arrived.
aboriginal.ex: The museum exhibits showcase aboriginal art and culture from Australia's earliest inhabitants.

aborigine.label: CEFR B1
aborigine.def: A person who was among the earliest inhabitants of a country, especially Australia.
aborigine.ex: The aborigines of Australia have a rich cultural heritage that spans thousands of years.

about.label: CEFR A1
about.def: Approximately; a little more or less than an exact amount or time.
about.ex: The film is about two hours long.

above.label: CEFR A1
above.def: At a higher level or position than something else.
above.ex: The picture is above the sofa on the wall.

abroad.label: CEFR A2
abroad.def: In or to a country that is not your own.
abroad.ex: My sister is studying abroad in France for one year.

abruptly.label: CEFR B2
abruptly.def: In a sudden and unexpected way, without warning or gradual change.
abruptly.ex: The meeting ended abruptly when the manager received an urgent phone call.

absence.label: CEFR B1
absence.def: The state of not being present in a place where you are expected to be.
absence.ex: His absence from the meeting was noted by the manager, who asked him to explain why he didn't attend.

absent.label: CEFR B1
absent.def: Not present in a place where you are expected or usually are
absent.ex: She was absent from school yesterday because she had the flu.

absentee.label: CEFR B2
absentee.def: A person who is not present at a place where they are expected or required to be.
absentee.ex: The teacher marked several students as absentees when they failed to appear for the final examination.

absolute.label: CEFR B1
absolute.def: Complete and total, with no doubt or conditions; certain
absolute.ex: She has absolute confidence in her team's ability to win the match.

absolutely.label: CEFR B1
absolutely.def: Completely or totally; used to emphasise that something is certain or true
absolutely.ex: She absolutely refused to change her mind about moving to London.

absorb.label: CEFR B1
absorb.def: To take in liquid, gas, or information; to soak up completely.
absorb.ex: The sponge absorbed all the water from the table after I spilled it.

absurd.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
absurd.def: Completely unreasonable or ridiculous; lacking sense or logic.
absurd.ex: The claim that the Earth is flat in the modern era is absurd given centuries of scientific evidence.

abundance.label: CEFR B1
abundance.def: A large amount of something; more than enough of something.
abundance.ex: The tropical island has an abundance of fresh fruit and vegetables throughout the year.

abundant.label: CEFR B1
abundant.def: Present or available in large quantities; plentiful
abundant.ex: The region has abundant natural resources including oil, gas, and minerals.

abuse.label: CEFR B2
abuse.def: Cruel or violent treatment of a person, often involving physical harm or power over them.
abuse.ex: The investigation revealed widespread abuse of workers in the factory, prompting new labour regulations.

academy.label: CEFR B1
academy.def: A school or institution where students study a particular subject or skill at advanced level.
academy.ex: She attended a music academy to improve her piano skills before applying to university.

accent.label: CEFR B1
accent.def: A way of pronouncing words that shows which country or region someone comes from.
accent.ex: My teacher has a strong French accent, so sometimes the students ask her to repeat difficult words.

accept.label: CEFR A2
accept.def: To say yes to something that is offered or given to you
accept.ex: She accepted the job offer and started working at the company last month.

acceptable.label: CEFR A2
acceptable.def: Good enough or satisfactory; able to be approved or allowed
acceptable.ex: Your homework is acceptable, but you can improve your spelling.

acceptance.label: CEFR B1
acceptance.def: The act of allowing someone or something, or agreeing that something is true or necessary
acceptance.ex: The acceptance of new students at the university depends on their exam scores.

accessibility.label: CEFR B2
accessibility.def: The quality of being easy to reach, enter, or use for all people, including those with disabilities.
accessibility.ex: The new building's accessibility features include ramps, lifts, and accessible toilets for wheelchair users.

accessible.label: CEFR B1
accessible.def: Easy to reach, enter, or use; available to everyone regardless of ability
accessible.ex: The museum is accessible by public transport, and the entrance has a ramp for wheelchair users.

accessory.label: CEFR B1
accessory.def: A small item of clothing or jewellery worn to complete an outfit, such as a scarf or bag.
accessory.ex: She bought a leather belt as an accessory to match her new dress.

accident.label: CEFR A2
accident.def: An unexpected event that causes harm, damage, or injury to people or things.
accident.ex: There was a serious car accident on the motorway this morning.

accidental.label: CEFR B1
accidental.def: Happening by chance, not planned or intended.
accidental.ex: She made an accidental discovery of an old photograph while cleaning the attic.

accidentally.label: CEFR B1
accidentally.def: Without planning to; by chance rather than on purpose.
accidentally.ex: I accidentally deleted the email when I was clearing my inbox.

accommodation.label: CEFR B2
accommodation.def: A place where someone can live or stay temporarily, such as a hotel, flat, or hostel.
accommodation.ex: We booked accommodation near the city centre for our week-long holiday in Barcelona.

accompany.label: CEFR B1
accompany.def: To go somewhere with another person, usually staying with them during an activity.
accompany.ex: My mother accompanied me to the doctor's appointment yesterday.

accomplish.label: CEFR B1
accomplish.def: To complete something successfully or achieve a goal you have been working towards.
accomplish.ex: She worked hard for five years to accomplish her dream of becoming a doctor.

accordingly.label: CEFR B2
accordingly.def: In a way that is suitable or proper in response to something that happened or was said.
accordingly.ex: The report showed poor safety standards, and the company adjusted its procedures accordingly.

account.label: CEFR B1
account.def: To explain why something happened or give a reason for something
account.ex: The heavy rain accounts for why the match was cancelled yesterday.

accountant.label: CEFR B1
accountant.def: A person who manages money records and financial statements for people or businesses.
accountant.ex: My sister works as an accountant at a large company and helps them manage their yearly budget.

accuracy.label: CEFR B1
accuracy.def: The quality of being correct and precise, without errors or mistakes.
accuracy.ex: The accuracy of the weather forecast improved when they used better technology.

accurately.label: CEFR B1
accurately.def: In a way that is exact and correct, without mistakes or errors.
accurately.ex: The weather forecast accurately predicted that it would rain yesterday afternoon.

accusation.label: CEFR B2
accusation.def: A claim that someone has done something wrong or illegal, often made without definite proof.
accusation.ex: The accusation that the company had misused customer data led to a major investigation by regulators.

accuse.label: CEFR B1
accuse.def: To say that someone did something wrong or illegal.
accuse.ex: The teacher accused him of cheating on the exam.

accustom.label: CEFR B1
accustom.def: To make someone familiar with something so they become used to it over time.
accustom.ex: She worked hard to accustom herself to the new school environment before the term began.

ache.label: CEFR B2
ache.def: To have a continuous, dull pain in part of your body.
ache.ex: After the long hike, her legs ached for days.

achievable.label: CEFR B2
achievable.def: Possible to succeed at or complete with reasonable effort
achievable.ex: With better planning and more resources, the company's ambitious sales targets are achievable within the next fiscal year.

achievement.label: CEFR B1
achievement.def: Something important that you succeed in doing through effort and hard work.
achievement.ex: Passing her final exams was a great achievement for Maria after studying so hard.

acid.label: CEFR B2
acid.def: A chemical substance that tastes sour and can dissolve or damage other materials.
acid.ex: The scientist added acid to the solution to test its chemical reaction.

acknowledgment/acknowledgement.label: CEFR B1
acknowledgment/acknowledgement.def: Recognition that something is true or that someone has done something worthy of notice.
acknowledgment/acknowledgement.ex: The athlete received an acknowledgement from the coach for her improved performance this season.

acquaintance.label: CEFR B1
acquaintance.def: A person you know, but who is not a close friend.
acquaintance.ex: I bumped into an old acquaintance from university at the supermarket yesterday.

acquisition.label: CEFR B2
acquisition.def: The process of gaining knowledge, skills, or something valuable through learning or obtaining it.
acquisition.ex: The acquisition of a second language requires consistent practice and exposure to native speakers.

across.label: CEFR A2
across.def: From one side of something to the other side
across.ex: She walked across the street to meet her friend.

act.label: CEFR B1
act.def: To do something or behave in a particular way.
act.ex: She acted quickly when she saw the accident and called the emergency services.

action.label: CEFR A1
action.def: Something that you do; a movement or activity.
action.ex: The firefighters took quick action to stop the fire.

active.label: CEFR B1
active.def: Engaging in physical activity or showing effort; not passive or lazy
active.ex: She maintains an active lifestyle by exercising three times a week and spending time outdoors.

actively.label: CEFR B2
actively.def: In a way that involves doing something energetically and with direct personal involvement.
actively.ex: The government is actively working to reduce carbon emissions through new legislation.

activity.label: CEFR A1
activity.def: Something you do for enjoyment or work
activity.ex: Playing football is my favourite activity after school.

actor.label: CEFR A1
actor.def: A person who performs in films, plays, or television shows.
actor.ex: The actor played the main character in the film.

actress.label: CEFR B1
actress.def: A woman who performs in films, plays, or television productions.
actress.ex: The actress gave an excellent performance in the Shakespeare play at the theatre.

actual.label: CEFR B2
actual.def: Real and existing in fact, not just imagined or theoretical
actual.ex: The actual cost of the project was higher than the initial estimate provided by the contractors.

actually.label: CEFR A2
actually.ex: I thought the film was boring, but my brother actually enjoyed it very much.

acute.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
acute.def: Serious and requiring immediate attention; or very sharp or severe in degree.
acute.ex: The hospital admitted several patients with acute respiratory infections during the winter months.

ad.label: CEFR B1
ad.def: A short film or message designed to persuade people to buy a product or service.
ad.ex: I saw an interesting ad on television for a new smartphone yesterday.

adaptable.label: CEFR B2
adaptable.def: Able to change and adjust to new conditions or circumstances successfully
adaptable.ex: The company's adaptable business model allowed it to thrive even when market conditions shifted dramatically.

add.label: CEFR A1
add.def: To put two or more numbers or things together to get a total.
add.ex: If you add 5 and 3, you get 8.

addict.label: CEFR B2
addict.def: A person who is unable to stop using a substance or engaging in an activity despite harmful consequences.
addict.ex: The documentary follows a former drug addict who rebuilt his life through rehabilitation and community support.

addicted.label: CEFR B2
addicted.def: Unable to stop doing or using something despite wanting to or knowing it causes harm.
addicted.ex: She became addicted to social media and found it difficult to focus on her studies.

addiction.label: CEFR B2
addiction.def: A strong, compulsive need to use something repeatedly, despite harmful consequences.
addiction.ex: The article examines how social media addiction affects teenagers' mental health and sleep patterns.

addition.label: CEFR A2
addition.def: The act of putting numbers or things together to find the total.
addition.ex: In maths class, we learned addition and learned how to add 5 + 3.

additional.label: CEFR A2
additional.def: Extra; more than what you already have or need.
additional.ex: I need additional information before I can help you with your problem.

additionally.label: CEFR B2
additionally.def: Used to add another point or piece of information to what was already said.
additionally.ex: The report highlights cost savings and, additionally, improved customer satisfaction across all regions.

address.label: CEFR B1
address.def: To speak to someone, or to discuss a problem or issue directly
address.ex: The manager addressed the team's concerns about the new working hours at yesterday's meeting.

addressee.label: CEFR B1
addressee.def: The person who receives a letter, email, or message sent by someone else.
addressee.ex: The addressee of the parcel was away on holiday when it arrived at the house.

adequately.label: CEFR B2
adequately.def: In a way that is enough or satisfactory for what is needed.
adequately.ex: The training programme adequately prepared employees for the new software system.

adjective.label: CEFR A2
adjective.def: A word that describes a person, thing, or quality in more detail
adjective.ex: The teacher explained that 'beautiful' is an adjective because it describes how something looks.

adjustment.label: CEFR B2
adjustment.def: A small change made to something to correct or improve it
adjustment.ex: The mechanic made several adjustments to the car's engine to improve its performance.

administration.label: CEFR B1
administration.def: The process of managing and running an organisation, government, or system
administration.ex: The hotel administration was very helpful when I needed to change my booking.

administrative.label: CEFR B2
administrative.def: Relating to the management and organisation of a business, government, or institution.
administrative.ex: The administrative staff handle all the paperwork and scheduling for the hospital.

admirable.label: CEFR B2
admirable.def: Worthy of respect or approval because of good qualities or achievements.
admirable.ex: Her admirable dedication to helping homeless people inspired many others to volunteer.

admiration.label: CEFR B1
admiration.def: A feeling of respect and approval for someone or something impressive
admiration.ex: She expressed admiration for the artist's creative talent and unique style.

admire.label: CEFR A2
admire.def: To think someone or something is very good and feel respect for them.
admire.ex: I really admire my teacher because she is kind and helpful.

admiringly.label: CEFR B2
admiringly.def: In a way that shows respect and approval for someone's qualities or achievements.
admiringly.ex: She looked at the finished painting admiringly, impressed by her colleague's artistic skill.

admission.label: CEFR B1
admission.def: Permission to enter or join a school, university, or organisation.
admission.ex: She received her admission letter from the university last week and decided to accept the offer.

admit.label: CEFR A2
admit.def: To accept that something is true, often something bad or difficult.
admit.ex: He finally admitted that he broke the vase.

admittedly.label: CEFR B2
admittedly.def: It is true that; I accept that something is the case, even if there are other important points.
admittedly.ex: Admittedly, the project was delayed, but the final results exceeded our expectations.

adopt.label: CEFR B1
adopt.def: To legally take a child as your own and care for them as a parent.
adopt.ex: Sarah and Tom decided to adopt a baby from the orphanage last year.

adopted.label: CEFR B2
adopted.def: Legally taken into a family and raised as one's own child; or chosen and used as one's own.
adopted.ex: The adopted son's birth parents visited the family after many years of separation.

adoption.label: CEFR B2
adoption.def: The legal process of becoming a parent to a child who is not biologically yours.
adoption.ex: After years of infertility treatments, the couple decided that adoption was the best way to build their family.

adorable.label: CEFR B1
adorable.def: Extremely cute and lovable; makes you want to show affection and care.
adorable.ex: The puppies in the pet shop window were absolutely adorable, and everyone stopped to look at them.

adore.label: CEFR B2
adore.def: To love someone or something very deeply and strongly.
adore.ex: She absolutely adores her grandmother and visits her every weekend.

adorn.label: CEFR B2
adorn.def: To decorate or make something more attractive by adding beautiful objects or details.
adorn.ex: Colourful paintings adorn the walls of the museum's entrance hall.

advance.label: CEFR B1
advance.def: To move forward or make progress towards something; to promote or help something develop.
advance.ex: The project advanced quickly after the team received more funding.

advanced.label: CEFR A2
advanced.def: Very developed or at a high level; ahead of others in progress or skill
advanced.ex: She is an advanced student who learns faster than her classmates.

advancement.label: CEFR B2
advancement.def: Progress or improvement in position, rank, knowledge, or technology over time.
advancement.ex: The advancement of artificial intelligence has transformed many industries in the past decade.

advantage.label: CEFR A2
advantage.def: Something that helps you succeed or makes you better than others
advantage.ex: Speaking two languages gives her an advantage when applying for jobs.

advent.label: CEFR B2
advent.def: The arrival or beginning of an important event, period, or thing.
advent.ex: The advent of digital publishing has transformed how readers access books and authors reach their audiences.

adventure.label: CEFR A2
adventure.def: An exciting or unusual experience where something unexpected happens
adventure.ex: The children had an adventure in the forest and found a hidden lake.

adventurous.label: CEFR B2
adventurous.def: Willing to try new or risky things; eager to explore or experience something unusual.
adventurous.ex: She had an adventurous spirit and decided to travel alone through Southeast Asia for three months.

adverb.label: CEFR B1
adverb.def: A word that describes or changes a verb, adjective, or another adverb to show how, when, or where something happens
adverb.ex: In the sentence 'She speaks English fluently', the word 'fluently' is an adverb that modifies the verb 'speaks'.

adverbial.label: CEFR B1
adverbial.def: Relating to a word or phrase that describes how, when, where, or why something happens.
adverbial.ex: In the sentence 'She walked slowly to the shop', the phrase 'to the shop' is an adverbial phrase that shows where she went.

adverse.label: CEFR B2
adverse.def: Harmful or unfavourable; having a negative effect on someone or something.
adverse.ex: The adverse weather conditions forced the football match to be postponed until the following week.

adversely.label: CEFR B2
adversely.def: In a way that causes harm, damage, or negative effects on something or someone.
adversely.ex: The strike adversely affected the company's ability to deliver products on time.

adversity.label: CEFR B2
adversity.def: Difficult circumstances or misfortune that causes hardship or suffering.
adversity.ex: She overcame significant adversity in her childhood before becoming a successful engineer.

advert.label: CEFR B1
advert.def: A paid announcement promoting a product, service, or event in media or public spaces
advert.ex: She saw an advert for the new smartphone on television and decided to buy it.

advertise.label: CEFR B1
advertise.def: To tell the public about a product or service through paid messages in media.
advertise.ex: The company advertises its new products on television and social media.

advertisement.label: CEFR A2
advertisement.def: A public notice or announcement that tries to persuade people to buy something or use a service.
advertisement.ex: I saw an advertisement for a new phone on the television this morning.

advertising.label: CEFR A2
advertising.def: Using pictures, words, or videos to tell people about products and encourage them to buy
advertising.ex: I saw advertising for new shoes on the television yesterday.

advice.label: CEFR A2
advice.def: suggestions or recommendations about what someone should do
advice.ex: My teacher gave me advice about how to study for the exam.

advise.label: CEFR A2
advise.def: To tell someone what you think they should do.
advise.ex: My teacher advised me to study harder for the exam.

adviser/advisor.label: CEFR B1
adviser/advisor.def: A person who gives professional guidance or recommendations to help someone make decisions.
adviser/advisor.ex: My university adviser helped me choose which courses to take for my degree.

aerobics.label: CEFR B1
aerobics.def: Physical exercise done to music, usually in a group class, to improve heart and lung health.
aerobics.ex: She attends aerobics classes three times a week to stay fit and have fun with her friends.

affair.label: CEFR A2
affair.def: A matter or situation that needs attention or is being dealt with
affair.ex: The manager handled the difficult affair with the client very carefully.

affection.label: CEFR B1
affection.def: A feeling of liking or loving someone or something very much.
affection.ex: She showed affection towards her grandmother by hugging her warmly every morning.

affectionate.label: CEFR B2
affectionate.def: Showing feelings of liking or love for someone through words, gestures, or behaviour.
affectionate.ex: She gave her grandmother an affectionate hug when she arrived at the care home.

affectionately.label: CEFR B2
affectionately.def: In a way that shows warmth, fondness, or love towards someone or something.
affectionately.ex: She affectionately ruffled her son's hair as he left for school, a habit she had maintained since his childhood.

afford.label: CEFR B1
afford.def: To have enough money to buy or pay for something.
afford.ex: We can't afford a new car right now because we're saving for a house.

affordable.label: CEFR B2
affordable.def: Not expensive; priced low enough for most people to buy without financial difficulty
affordable.ex: The new housing development offers affordable apartments for young professionals entering the job market.

afraid.label: CEFR A1
afraid.def: Feeling fear or worry about something or someone.
afraid.ex: She was afraid of the dark when she was a little girl.

after.label: CEFR A2
after.def: At a time following something; later than something else
after.ex: I usually have a cup of tea after lunch.

afternoon.label: CEFR A1
afternoon.def: The time of day between noon and evening, usually until about 6 PM.
afternoon.ex: I have football practice every afternoon after school.

afterward.label: CEFR B1
afterward.def: At a time after something else happens or is mentioned
afterward.ex: We watched the film, and afterward we went to a café to discuss it.

afterwards.label: CEFR B1
afterwards.def: At a later time; after something else happens or is finished
afterwards.ex: We watched the film, and afterwards we went to a café for coffee.

again.label: CEFR A1
again.def: one more time; something that happens a second time
again.ex: Can you read the story again? I want to hear it.

age.label: CEFR A1
age.def: How many years old a person is.
age.ex: My sister is 15 years old, but I don't know her exact age.

aged.label: CEFR A2
aged.def: Very old; having lived or existed for many years
aged.ex: My aged grandmother still enjoys walking in the park every morning.

ageless.label: CEFR B2
ageless.def: Appearing to remain young or unchanged by the passage of time.
ageless.ex: Her ageless beauty and timeless style have made her an icon throughout her decades-long career.

agency.label: CEFR A2
agency.def: A business that helps people with travel plans, finding jobs, or getting services.
agency.ex: We booked our holiday flights through a travel agency in the city centre.

agenda.label: CEFR B1
agenda.def: A list of items to be discussed or tasks to be completed in a meeting or event.
agenda.ex: The manager shared the agenda for tomorrow's team meeting, which included three important topics.

agent.label: CEFR A2
agent.def: A person who works for a company and helps customers buy or sell something
agent.ex: The travel agent helped us book our holiday to Spain.

aggressive.label: CEFR B1
aggressive.def: Quick to attack or start conflict; behaving in a forceful or angry way towards others
aggressive.ex: The aggressive dog barked loudly when strangers approached the fence.

aggressively.label: CEFR B2
aggressively.def: In a forceful or hostile manner, showing strong determination or intent to attack.
aggressively.ex: The company aggressively pursued market dominance through strategic acquisitions and competitive pricing.

ago.label: CEFR A1
ago.ex: I saw that film two weeks ago.

agonize/agonise.label: CEFR B2
agonize/agonise.def: To worry intensely or suffer mentally about something difficult or uncertain.
agonize/agonise.ex: She agonised over the decision for weeks before finally accepting the job offer.

agony.label: CEFR B2
agony.def: Extreme physical or emotional pain and suffering that is intense and prolonged.
agony.ex: The protagonist endured years of agony after losing everything in the war.

agree.label: CEFR A1
agree.def: To have the same opinion or say yes to something
agree.ex: I agree with you that chocolate is delicious.

agreement.label: CEFR B1
agreement.def: A decision or arrangement that two or more people accept and decide to follow.
agreement.ex: The manager and the employees reached an agreement about working hours and pay.

agricultural.label: CEFR B1
agricultural.def: Related to farming and the production of crops and animals for food.
agricultural.ex: The agricultural industry in the region produces wheat, corn, and dairy products.

agriculture.label: CEFR B1
agriculture.def: The practice of growing crops and raising animals for food and other products.
agriculture.ex: Modern agriculture uses technology to produce more food with fewer workers.

ahead.label: CEFR A2
ahead.def: In front of someone or something; further forward in space or time
ahead.ex: The car ahead of us stopped suddenly, so we had to brake.

aid.label: CEFR B1
aid.def: To help someone or something, especially by giving support or resources.
aid.ex: The international organisation aids developing countries by providing education and healthcare.

aim.label: CEFR B2
aim.def: To point or direct something towards a target with intention to hit or achieve it.
aim.ex: The government aims to reduce carbon emissions by 50% within the next decade.

air.label: CEFR A2
air.def: The invisible gas all around us that we breathe to stay alive
air.ex: I opened the window to let fresh air into the bedroom.

air conditioning.label: CEFR B1
air conditioning.def: A system that cools and controls the temperature and humidity inside a building or vehicle.
air conditioning.ex: The office has strong air conditioning, so it's always cold in summer.

air force.label: CEFR B1
air force.def: Military organisation that fights wars using aircraft and operates in the sky.
air force.ex: My brother joined the air force after finishing secondary school and now pilots fighter jets.

aircraft.label: CEFR B2
aircraft.def: A vehicle designed to fly through the air, carrying passengers or cargo.
aircraft.ex: The aircraft was delayed due to bad weather conditions at the airport.

aircrew.label: CEFR B2
aircrew.def: The group of people who operate and work on an aircraft during flight.
aircrew.ex: The aircrew conducted safety checks before boarding passengers for the long-haul flight to Tokyo.

airline.label: CEFR B1
airline.def: A company that provides air transportation services for passengers and cargo.
airline.ex: I booked my flight with a budget airline to save money on my holiday.

airplane/aeroplane.label: CEFR A1
airplane/aeroplane.def: A large vehicle with wings that flies through the air and carries passengers or goods.
airplane/aeroplane.ex: I took an aeroplane to visit my grandmother in France last summer.

airport.label: CEFR A1
airport.def: A place where aeroplanes land and take off, and passengers get on and off.
airport.ex: We arrived at the airport two hours before our flight.

aisle.label: CEFR A2
aisle.def: A narrow passage between rows of seats or shelves in a plane, train, shop, or theatre.
aisle.ex: The flight attendant walked down the aisle to serve drinks to passengers.

alarm.label: CEFR B1
alarm.def: To make someone feel worried or frightened about something.
alarm.ex: The sudden loud noise alarmed the passengers on the train.

alarm clock.label: CEFR A2
alarm clock.def: A clock that makes a loud sound at a time you set to wake you up.
alarm clock.ex: I set my alarm clock for 7 o'clock every morning before school.

album.label: CEFR A1
album.def: A book or container that holds a collection of photographs or pictures.
album.ex: I keep my holiday photos in a photo album on the shelf.

alcohol.label: CEFR B1
alcohol.def: A drink that contains a substance making you feel relaxed or drunk, like beer or wine.
alcohol.ex: Many people enjoy having a glass of wine or beer with dinner, but alcohol affects your body and mind.

alcoholic.label: CEFR B2
alcoholic.def: A person who is unable to control their drinking and depends on alcohol regularly
alcoholic.ex: The documentary follows several recovering alcoholics as they rebuild their lives through support groups and therapy.

alcoholism.label: CEFR B2
alcoholism.def: A medical condition where someone is physically and psychologically dependent on alcohol and cannot control their drinking.
alcoholism.ex: The charity launched a campaign to raise awareness about alcoholism and provide support to those struggling with addiction.

alert.label: CEFR B2
alert.def: To make someone aware of a possible danger or problem quickly.
alert.ex: The security system alerted us to the unauthorised access attempt before any data was compromised.

alike.label: CEFR B1
alike.def: In the same way; similarly or equally
alike.ex: The two sisters look alike and often dress alike, so people frequently mistake them for twins.

alive.label: CEFR A2
alive.ex: The fish are still alive in the tank.

all.label: CEFR A2
all.def: Every person or thing in a group; the total amount or number
all.ex: I ate all of my breakfast this morning.

all right.label: CEFR A1
all right.ex: The food was all right, but I preferred yesterday's dinner.

allegation.label: CEFR B2
allegation.def: A claim or assertion that someone has done something wrong, without proof yet provided.
allegation.ex: The allegations against the politician were investigated by the police, but no evidence was found to support them.

allergic.label: CEFR B1
allergic.def: Having a bad physical reaction to a substance that does not affect most people.
allergic.ex: My brother is allergic to peanuts, so we have to be careful when preparing meals at home.

alliance.label: CEFR B2
alliance.def: A formal agreement between countries or groups to work together for shared goals.
alliance.ex: The two nations formed a strategic alliance to strengthen their defence against external threats.

allow.label: CEFR A2
allow.def: To permit someone to do something; to let someone do something.
allow.ex: My parents allow me to stay up late on Friday nights.

allowance.label: CEFR B1
allowance.def: Money given regularly to someone, usually a child, by a parent or guardian.
allowance.ex: My parents give me a weekly allowance of £10 to spend on things I want.

ally.label: CEFR B2
ally.def: A country or group that joins with another to support a shared cause or goal.
ally.ex: The government sought allies from neighbouring countries to help address the regional security crisis.

almost.label: CEFR A1
almost.def: Nearly; not quite but very close.
almost.ex: I almost finished my homework, but I need ten more minutes.

alone.label: CEFR A1
alone.ex: She sat alone in the library and read her book.

along.label: CEFR A1
along.def: Moving or going forward in a direction next to something
along.ex: We walked along the beach and found some nice shells.

aloud.label: CEFR B1
aloud.def: Speaking or reading so that other people can hear your voice clearly.
aloud.ex: The teacher asked the student to read the poem aloud to the whole class.

alphabet.label: CEFR B1
alphabet.def: The set of letters used to write a language, arranged in a fixed order.
alphabet.ex: Children learn the alphabet before they start learning to read words.

alphabetical.label: CEFR B2
alphabetical.def: Arranged in the order of letters from A to Z
alphabetical.ex: The dictionary lists words in alphabetical order so you can find them easily.

already.label: CEFR A1
already.def: Before now; in the past, before this moment
already.ex: I already ate breakfast this morning.

alright.label: CEFR A2
alright.def: Satisfactory or acceptable; not good but okay.
alright.ex: The film was alright, but I enjoyed the book more.

also.label: CEFR A1
also.def: too; in addition to something else
also.ex: I like coffee. I also like tea.

altar.label: CEFR B2
altar.def: A table or raised structure in a religious building where ceremonies and sacrifices are performed.
altar.ex: In the novel, the priest knelt before the altar and prayed for the village's salvation.

alternate.label: CEFR B2
alternate.def: To switch repeatedly between two or more things, one after another in a pattern.
alternate.ex: The speaker alternated between English and French throughout the conference to accommodate all attendees.

alternatively.label: CEFR B2
alternatively.def: As another possibility or option instead of something else mentioned
alternatively.ex: You can take the train to the city centre, or alternatively, you could drive and pay for parking.

altitude.label: CEFR B2
altitude.def: The height of something above sea level or ground level, measured in metres or feet.
altitude.ex: The aeroplane reached an altitude of 10,000 metres before the pilot noticed a problem with the engines.

altogether.label: CEFR B1
altogether.def: In total; when you add everything together
altogether.ex: The repairs to the house cost £5,000 altogether.

aluminum/aluminium.label: CEFR B2
aluminum/aluminium.def: A lightweight, silvery metal widely used in construction, packaging, and manufacturing industries.
aluminum/aluminium.ex: The aerospace engineering course teaches students how aluminium is used to build aircraft frames due to its strength and light weight.

always.label: CEFR A1
always.ex: I always eat breakfast in the morning.

amaze.label: CEFR B2
amaze.def: To surprise someone greatly by being unexpected or impressive.
amaze.ex: The architect's innovative design never ceased to amaze the city's residents.

amazed.label: CEFR B1
amazed.def: Very surprised and impressed by something unexpected or impressive.
amazed.ex: I was amazed by how quickly she learned to speak French.

amazement.label: CEFR B2
amazement.def: A feeling of great surprise and wonder at something unexpected or impressive.
amazement.ex: She watched in amazement as the magician made the car disappear from the locked garage.

amazing.label: CEFR B1
amazing.def: Extremely surprising or impressive in a way that causes wonder or astonishment.
amazing.ex: The view from the top of the mountain was absolutely amazing.

amazingly.label: CEFR B2
amazingly.def: In a way that surprises or impresses you greatly; unexpectedly and remarkably.
amazingly.ex: The new treatment was amazingly effective, reducing symptoms in just two weeks.

ambassador.label: CEFR B2
ambassador.def: An official representative of a country's government working in another country.
ambassador.ex: The ambassador met with the foreign minister to discuss trade relations between the two nations.

ambiguity.label: CEFR B2
ambiguity.def: The quality of being unclear or having more than one possible meaning or interpretation.
ambiguity.ex: The ambiguity in the contract's language led to disputes between the two companies about their obligations.

ambition.label: CEFR A2
ambition.def: A strong desire to succeed and achieve something important in life.
ambition.ex: Her ambition is to become a doctor and help people in her community.

ambitious.label: CEFR B1
ambitious.def: Having a strong desire to succeed or achieve important goals in life or work
ambitious.ex: She is an ambitious student who wants to become a doctor and help people in developing countries.

ambulance.label: CEFR B1
ambulance.def: A vehicle that carries sick or injured people to the hospital quickly.
ambulance.ex: When the old man fell down the stairs, someone called an ambulance immediately.

ammonia.label: CEFR B2
ammonia.def: A colourless gas with a pungent smell, commonly used in cleaning products and fertilisers.
ammonia.ex: The chemistry students conducted an experiment to demonstrate how ammonia reacts with acids in the laboratory.

amount.label: CEFR B1
amount.def: The total quantity or sum of something that can be measured or counted.
amount.ex: The amount of rainfall this month was higher than usual for this time of year.

ample.label: CEFR B2
ample.def: Enough or more than enough; plentiful and generous in quantity or size.
ample.ex: The hotel provides ample parking space for all its guests, so you won't have any difficulty finding a spot.

amplifier.label: CEFR B2
amplifier.def: An electronic device that increases the strength or volume of an electrical signal or sound.
amplifier.ex: The guitarist plugged his electric instrument into a powerful amplifier to fill the concert hall with sound.

amplify.label: CEFR B2
amplify.def: To increase the strength, intensity, or effect of something; to make something louder or more noticeable.
amplify.ex: The news media amplified the political tensions by giving extensive coverage to the controversial statement.

amuse.label: CEFR B2
amuse.def: To make someone laugh or feel entertained and happy.
amuse.ex: The comedian's jokes amused the audience so much that they laughed throughout the entire performance.

amused.label: CEFR A2
amused.def: Entertained or finding something funny and pleasant.
amused.ex: She was amused by the funny video her friend showed her.

amusement.label: CEFR A2
amusement.def: Enjoyment and laughter from something entertaining or funny
amusement.ex: The children played games at the amusement park all day.

amusing.label: CEFR B1
amusing.def: Funny and enjoyable; making you laugh or smile
amusing.ex: The comedian's amusing jokes kept the audience laughing throughout the show.

analysis.label: CEFR B1
analysis.def: Detailed examination of something to understand its parts and how they work together.
analysis.ex: The teacher asked us to write an analysis of the poem, explaining what each stanza meant.

analyst.label: CEFR B2
analyst.def: A person who examines something carefully to understand its nature, causes, or effects.
analyst.ex: The financial analyst predicted that interest rates would rise within the next quarter.

analyze/analyse.label: CEFR B1
analyze/analyse.def: To examine something carefully by looking at its parts separately to understand it better.
analyze/analyse.ex: The teacher asked us to analyse the poem to understand what the author really meant.

ancestor.label: CEFR A2
ancestor.def: A person in your family from a long time ago, before your parents or grandparents.
ancestor.ex: My ancestors came from Italy many years ago.

anchor.label: CEFR B2
anchor.def: A heavy object dropped from a ship to keep it in one place in water
anchor.ex: The captain ordered the crew to drop anchor as the storm approached the harbour.

anchorage.label: CEFR B2
anchorage.def: A place where ships can safely stop and stay using an anchor in water.
anchorage.ex: The yacht found good anchorage in the sheltered bay, protected from the strong Atlantic winds.

anchorman.label: CEFR B2
anchorman.def: The lead presenter of a television or radio news broadcast who coordinates coverage and interviews other reporters.
anchorman.ex: The anchorman introduced the evening news and then handed over to correspondents reporting from around the world.

anchorperson.label: CEFR B2
anchorperson.def: A journalist who presents news on television or radio from a studio.
anchorperson.ex: The anchorperson introduced the story about the Olympic Games with enthusiasm and professionalism.

ancient.label: CEFR A2
ancient.def: Very old, from a long time ago, especially thousands of years old.
ancient.ex: The ancient Romans built impressive buildings that still stand today.

angel.label: CEFR A2
angel.def: A spiritual being believed to be good and helpful, often shown with wings in pictures
angel.ex: In the children's book, a kind angel helps the lost girl find her way home.

anger.label: CEFR B1
anger.def: A strong feeling of displeasure or annoyance when something upsets you
anger.ex: He struggled to control his anger when he discovered the broken window.

angle.label: CEFR B1
angle.def: The space between two lines or surfaces that meet at a point, measured in degrees.
angle.ex: The ladder is leaning against the wall at a 45-degree angle.

angrily.label: CEFR A2
angrily.def: In a way that shows you are very upset or annoyed about something.
angrily.ex: She angrily slammed the door and refused to speak to him.

angry.label: CEFR A1
angry.def: Feeling very annoyed or upset about something.
angry.ex: He was angry when his friend broke his toy.

anguish.label: CEFR B2
anguish.def: Severe emotional or physical pain causing distress and suffering.
anguish.ex: The parents' anguish at their child's disappearance was evident in their tearful appeals to the public.

animal.label: CEFR A1
animal.def: A living creature that can move and eat food, such as dogs, cats, birds, or insects.
animal.ex: My favourite animal is a dog.

animate.label: CEFR B2
animate.def: To give life, energy, or motion to something; to make something seem alive or active
animate.ex: The director used computer graphics to animate the characters in the film, making them move and speak naturally.

animated.label: CEFR B1
animated.def: Full of energy, enthusiasm, and lively movement or expression
animated.ex: She gave an animated presentation about her research, using hand gestures and speaking with great passion.

animation.label: CEFR B1
animation.def: The process of creating moving pictures by displaying a series of still images in quick sequence.
animation.ex: She studies animation at university because she loves creating cartoon films on her computer.

ankle.label: CEFR A2
ankle.def: The joint where your foot connects to your leg.
ankle.ex: She twisted her ankle while playing football and had to sit down.

anniversary.label: CEFR A2
anniversary.def: The date each year when something important happened in the past, such as a wedding.
anniversary.ex: My parents celebrated their 25th wedding anniversary last month with a special dinner.

announce.label: CEFR B1
announce.def: To make something known or tell people about something publicly or officially
announce.ex: The company announced that they would be opening a new office in London next year.

announcement.label: CEFR B1
announcement.def: An official or public statement that gives people information about something important.
announcement.ex: The company made an announcement about their new product at the press conference yesterday.

annoy.label: CEFR A2
annoy.def: To make someone feel a little angry or impatient.
annoy.ex: The loud noise from next door annoys me when I'm trying to sleep.

annoyance.label: CEFR B1
annoyance.def: A feeling of mild anger or irritation caused by something or someone.
annoyance.ex: The constant noise from the construction site was a source of annoyance for the neighbours.

annoyed.label: CEFR B1
annoyed.def: Slightly angry or irritated about something that bothers you.
annoyed.ex: My sister was annoyed when I forgot to return her book.

annoying.label: CEFR A2
annoying.def: Making someone feel irritated or impatient; causing mild frustration.
annoying.ex: The annoying noise from the construction site made it hard to concentrate.

annually.label: CEFR B1
annually.def: Once every year; happening or done each year
annually.ex: The company holds an annual meeting annually to discuss its financial results with shareholders.

anonymous.label: CEFR B2
anonymous.def: Not identified by name; the person or source is kept secret or unknown.
anonymous.ex: The survey was anonymous, so respondents could answer honestly without fear of being identified.

answer.label: CEFR A1
answer.def: To say or write something in response to a question.
answer.ex: She answered the teacher's question in class.

ant.label: CEFR B1
ant.def: A small insect that lives in large groups underground or in colonies, known for carrying heavy objects.
ant.ex: The ant carried a piece of bread back to its nest to share with the colony.

anti.label: CEFR B1
anti.def: Opposed to something; against a particular idea, group, or policy.
anti.ex: The government introduced new anti-corruption laws to fight bribery in public institutions.

antiaircraft.label: CEFR B2
antiaircraft.def: Designed to defend against or destroy enemy aircraft and missiles
antiaircraft.ex: The military base was equipped with antiaircraft guns positioned on the perimeter.

antibacterial.label: CEFR B2
antibacterial.def: Designed to kill or stop the growth of bacteria.
antibacterial.ex: Many hospitals use antibacterial soap to reduce the risk of infection among staff and patients.

anticancer.label: CEFR B2
anticancer.def: Treatment or medication designed to destroy cancer cells or stop cancer from growing
anticancer.ex: The hospital's oncology department offers several anticancer therapies, including chemotherapy and radiation treatment.

anticipation.label: CEFR B2
anticipation.def: The feeling of excitement or nervousness about something expected to happen soon.
anticipation.ex: In anticipation of the Olympic Games, the city renovated its stadiums and transport systems.

antipollution.label: CEFR B2
antipollution.def: Measures and actions designed to reduce or prevent environmental contamination and damage.
antipollution.ex: The government introduced strict antipollution regulations to improve air quality in major cities.

antique.label: CEFR B1
antique.def: A very old object, usually valuable because of its age and rarity.
antique.ex: My grandmother's antique vase from the 1800s is now displayed in our living room.

antistatic.label: CEFR B2
antistatic.def: Designed to prevent or reduce the buildup of electrical charge on surfaces or objects.
antistatic.ex: The technician wore antistatic gloves while repairing the sensitive computer components.

antivirus.label: CEFR B2
antivirus.def: Software that detects and removes harmful programs designed to damage computers or steal information.
antivirus.ex: Before travelling abroad, I installed a reliable antivirus programme to protect my laptop from malware on public Wi-Fi networks.

antonym.label: CEFR B1
antonym.def: A word that has the opposite meaning of another word.
antonym.ex: The antonym of 'hot' is 'cold', so students learn them together in vocabulary class.

anxiety.label: CEFR B1
anxiety.def: A feeling of worry or nervousness about something that might happen.
anxiety.ex: She felt anxiety before her first day at the new job.

anxious.label: CEFR A2
anxious.def: Worried or nervous about something that might happen
anxious.ex: She felt anxious before her first day at the new school.

anxiously.label: CEFR B1
anxiously.def: In a worried or nervous way, unable to relax because you are concerned about something.
anxiously.ex: She anxiously checked her phone every few minutes while waiting for the exam results.

anyhow.label: CEFR B1
anyhow.def: In any case; regardless of what was said or happened before
anyhow.ex: The weather looked bad, but we decided to go to the beach anyhow.

anymore.label: CEFR A2
anymore.def: No longer; not now as before or in the past
anymore.ex: I don't smoke anymore because it is bad for my health.

anyway.label: CEFR A2
anyway.def: Used to continue talking about something, or to show what matters most in a situation.
anyway.ex: The film was long and boring, but anyway, I'm glad we went together.

anywhere.label: CEFR A2
anywhere.def: In any place; it does not matter which place
anywhere.ex: You can sit anywhere in the classroom today.

apart.label: CEFR A2
apart.def: separated by distance or space; not together
apart.ex: My best friend moved away and now we live 100 kilometres apart.

apartment.label: CEFR A2
apartment.def: A set of rooms for living in, usually in a building with other similar homes.
apartment.ex: She lives in a small apartment in the city centre with her two friends.

ape.label: CEFR B1
ape.def: A large primate with long arms, no tail, and high intelligence; includes gorillas, chimpanzees, and orangutans.
ape.ex: In biology class, we learned how apes share about 98% of their DNA with humans.

apologize/apologise.label: CEFR A2
apologize/apologise.def: To tell someone you are sorry for doing something wrong or hurtful.
apologize/apologise.ex: She apologised for being late to the meeting.

apology.label: CEFR B1
apology.def: A statement saying you are sorry for something wrong you did.
apology.ex: She gave a sincere apology for arriving late to the meeting.

apostrophe.label: CEFR B2
apostrophe.def: A punctuation mark (') used to show possession or omitted letters in contractions.
apostrophe.ex: You need to add an apostrophe in the word 'don't' to show that the 'o' is missing.

apparently.label: CEFR A2
apparently.def: As it seems to be true, based on what you can see or hear, though you are not completely sure
apparently.ex: She apparently left the office early yesterday, but I didn't see her go.

appeal.label: CEFR B2
appeal.def: To make a serious or emotional request for something you want or need badly.
appeal.ex: The charity appealed to the government for funding to help homeless people in the city.

appear.label: CEFR A2
appear.def: To become visible or come into sight
appear.ex: The sun appeared from behind the clouds this morning.

appearance.label: CEFR A2
appearance.def: The way someone or something looks.
appearance.ex: She changed her appearance by cutting her hair short.

appetite.label: CEFR B1
appetite.def: A feeling of wanting to eat food; the desire to eat
appetite.ex: After exercising, I had a huge appetite and ate a large dinner.

applaud.label: CEFR B1
applaud.def: To show approval or praise by clapping your hands loudly.
applaud.ex: The audience applauded enthusiastically when the pianist finished her performance.

applause.label: CEFR B1
applause.def: Clapping hands by many people to show they enjoyed a performance or speech.
applause.ex: The audience gave loud applause when the pianist finished playing.

apple.label: CEFR A1
apple.def: A round, hard fruit that is red, green, or yellow and grows on trees.
apple.ex: I eat an apple for breakfast every morning.

applicant.label: CEFR B2
applicant.def: A person who makes a formal request to be considered for a position, course, or place.
applicant.ex: The university received over 5,000 applicants for the engineering programme this year.

application.label: CEFR B1
application.def: A formal request for a job, place at university, or permission to do something.
application.ex: She submitted her job application to the company last week and is waiting to hear back.

apply.label: CEFR A2
apply.def: To make a formal request for something, usually a job or course at a school
apply.ex: She applied for a job at the hospital last month.

appoint.label: CEFR B1
appoint.def: To choose someone officially for a job or position.
appoint.ex: The company appointed a new manager to lead the sales team.

appointment.label: CEFR A2
appointment.def: A planned meeting with someone at a specific time.
appointment.ex: I have a dentist appointment on Tuesday at 2 o'clock.

appreciate.label: CEFR A2
appreciate.def: To understand the value or importance of something; to be grateful for.
appreciate.ex: I appreciate your help with my homework yesterday.

appreciation.label: CEFR B1
appreciation.def: Recognition and gratitude for something valuable or helpful that someone has done.
appreciation.ex: She showed her appreciation by writing a thank-you letter to the teacher who helped her pass the exam.

appreciative.label: CEFR B2
appreciative.def: Feeling or showing gratitude and recognition of someone's efforts or qualities.
appreciative.ex: The audience gave an appreciative round of applause after the pianist's outstanding performance.

appropriately.label: CEFR B2
appropriately.def: In a way that is suitable or proper for a particular situation or context.
appropriately.ex: She dressed appropriately for the formal dinner, wearing an elegant black evening gown.

approval.label: CEFR B1
approval.def: Permission or agreement that something is acceptable or good enough.
approval.ex: The manager gave her approval for the new project to start next month.

approve.label: CEFR B1
approve.def: To officially accept or say yes to something; to think something is good or right.
approve.ex: The bank approved our loan application after reviewing our financial documents.

approximately.label: CEFR B1
approximately.def: Close to a particular number or time, but not exactly
approximately.ex: The journey takes approximately two hours by train.

april.label: CEFR A1
april.def: The fourth month of the year, between March and May.

apron.label: CEFR A1
apron.def: A piece of cloth worn over your clothes to protect them while cooking or working.
apron.ex: She wore a blue apron while making dinner in the kitchen.

arch.label: CEFR B2
arch.def: A curved structure that spans an opening and supports the weight above it, common in buildings and bridges.
arch.ex: The ancient Roman arch in the city centre has been carefully restored by the local authority.

archaeologist/archeologist.label: CEFR B2
archaeologist/archeologist.def: A scientist who studies human history and cultures by examining ancient remains, artefacts, and sites.
archaeologist/archeologist.ex: The archaeologist discovered pottery fragments that revealed how people lived thousands of years ago.

architect.label: CEFR B1
architect.def: A person who designs buildings and oversees their construction.
architect.ex: The architect presented detailed plans for the new hospital to the city council.

architecture.label: CEFR A2
architecture.def: The design and style of buildings and how they are constructed.
architecture.ex: The old building has beautiful architecture with tall windows and stone walls.

arctic.label: CEFR B2
arctic.def: Relating to the extremely cold region around the North Pole with ice, snow, and very low temperatures.
arctic.ex: Scientists are studying how arctic ice is melting due to climate change.

arena.label: CEFR B2
arena.def: A large enclosed space where sports, entertainment, or public events take place.
arena.ex: The concert was held at the city's new arena, which can accommodate over 10,000 people.

argue.label: CEFR A2
argue.def: To disagree with someone and express different opinions about something.
argue.ex: My brother and I argued about which film to watch last night.

argument.label: CEFR A2
argument.def: A disagreement or discussion where people express different opinions strongly.
argument.ex: My sister and I had an argument about who should wash the dishes.

arise.label: CEFR B1
arise.def: To appear or happen, often unexpectedly or as a result of something else.
arise.ex: Several problems arose during the construction of the new building.

aristocracy.label: CEFR B2
aristocracy.def: A system of government where power is held by a small group of people from high-ranking families.
aristocracy.ex: The aristocracy in eighteenth-century France controlled most of the country's wealth and political influence.

arithmetic.label: CEFR B1
arithmetic.def: The basic mathematics of adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing numbers.
arithmetic.ex: My son struggles with arithmetic, so his teacher gives him extra help with division problems.

arm.label: CEFR B1
arm.def: To provide someone with weapons or equipment for fighting or defence.
arm.ex: The government decided to arm the security forces with new technology to combat crime.

armchair.label: CEFR A2
armchair.def: A comfortable chair with sides and a back, usually for one person to sit in.
armchair.ex: After dinner, my father relaxes in his armchair and reads the newspaper.

armed.label: CEFR A2
armed.def: Carrying or holding weapons like guns or knives.
armed.ex: The armed police officers stood at the entrance of the bank.

armful.label: CEFR B2
armful.def: The amount of something that a person can hold in their arms at once
armful.ex: She carried an armful of documents into the conference room for the meeting.

army.label: CEFR B1
army.def: A large organized group of soldiers trained to fight in wars and protect a country.
army.ex: My brother joined the army last year and now works as a soldier in Germany.

around.label: CEFR A2
around.def: On all sides of something; in a circle or surrounding position
around.ex: There are trees around the house.

arouse.label: CEFR B2
arouse.def: To wake someone up or to cause strong feelings or interest in someone.
arouse.ex: The controversial speech aroused heated debate among politicians and the public alike.

arrange.label: CEFR B1
arrange.def: To plan or organize something, or to put things in a particular order or position
arrange.ex: She arranged a meeting with her boss for next Tuesday afternoon.

arrangement.label: CEFR B1
arrangement.def: A plan or agreement about how something will be done or organized.
arrangement.ex: We made an arrangement to meet at the café every Saturday morning.

arrest.label: CEFR B1
arrest.def: To take someone into custody because they are suspected of breaking the law.
arrest.ex: The police arrested the suspect after finding evidence at the crime scene.

arrival.label: CEFR B1
arrival.def: The moment when someone or something reaches a place after travelling
arrival.ex: We had to wait two hours at the airport for my cousin's arrival from Paris.

arrive.label: CEFR A1
arrive.def: To reach or get to a place.
arrive.ex: The bus arrives at 3 o'clock every afternoon.

arrogant.label: CEFR B2
arrogant.def: Behaving in an unpleasantly proud way, showing excessive confidence in your own importance.
arrogant.ex: The arrogant billionaire dismissed all criticism and refused to listen to anyone's advice.

arrow.label: CEFR B1
arrow.def: A thin pointed stick shot from a bow as a weapon or in the sport of archery.
arrow.ex: She pulled back the bowstring and released the arrow, which flew straight towards the target.

art.label: CEFR A1
art.def: Creative work such as painting, drawing, or sculpture made to be beautiful or express ideas.
art.ex: My sister loves art and spends hours drawing pictures in her notebook.

artery.label: CEFR B2
artery.def: A blood vessel that carries oxygen-rich blood from the heart to the rest of the body.
artery.ex: A blocked artery can increase the risk of heart attack or stroke.

article.label: CEFR A1
article.def: A single thing you buy or sell in a shop.
article.ex: I need to buy a new article of clothing for the party.

articulate.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
articulate.def: To express thoughts or ideas clearly and effectively in speech or writing.
articulate.ex: The researcher articulated her findings in a way that made complex data accessible to the general audience.

artificial.label: CEFR A2
artificial.def: Made by people, not found in nature.
artificial.ex: She wore artificial flowers in her hair because real ones die quickly.

artificially.label: CEFR B2
artificially.def: In a way that is made by humans rather than occurring naturally in nature.
artificially.ex: The strawberries were artificially ripened in the warehouse before being shipped to supermarkets.

artist.label: CEFR B1
artist.def: A person who creates art such as paintings, sculptures, or drawings.
artist.ex: The artist displayed her paintings at the gallery exhibition last weekend.

artistic.label: CEFR B1
artistic.def: Related to art or showing skill and creativity in making or appreciating beautiful things.
artistic.ex: Her artistic talent was clear from the beautiful paintings she created in class.

artistry.label: CEFR B2
artistry.def: Skilled and creative ability to produce beautiful or impressive works of art.
artistry.ex: The dancer's artistry was evident in every graceful movement across the stage.

ash.label: CEFR B2
ash.def: Grey powder that remains after something burns completely.
ash.ex: The volcanic ash from the eruption spread across several countries and disrupted air travel.

ashamed.label: CEFR B1
ashamed.def: Feeling embarrassed or guilty about something you did or about yourself.
ashamed.ex: She felt ashamed when she realised she had forgotten her friend's birthday.

aside.label: CEFR B1
aside.def: To the side; away from the main area or person
aside.ex: She pulled him aside to tell him the secret before the meeting started.

ask.label: CEFR A1
ask.def: To say something as a question because you want to know the answer.
ask.ex: Can you ask your teacher about the homework?

asleep.label: CEFR A2
asleep.def: Resting with eyes closed, not conscious or awake.
asleep.ex: My baby was asleep in her cot when I came home from work.

aspiration.label: CEFR B2
aspiration.def: A strong desire to achieve something important or to succeed in life
aspiration.ex: Her aspiration to become a doctor motivated her to study hard throughout university.

aspire.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
aspire.def: To want to achieve or become something important or ambitious in the future
aspire.ex: She aspires to become a successful journalist and travel the world reporting on important stories.

aspirin.label: CEFR B1
aspirin.def: A common medicine taken to reduce pain, fever, or inflammation.
aspirin.ex: She took an aspirin for her headache before going to work.

assault.label: CEFR B2
assault.def: A violent physical attack on another person, or the crime of attacking someone violently.
assault.ex: The police are investigating an assault that occurred outside the nightclub late last night.

assembly.label: CEFR B2
assembly.def: A gathering of people, often for a formal meeting or school event where students listen to speakers
assembly.ex: The headmaster addressed the whole school at the morning assembly in the gymnasium.

assert.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
assert.def: To state something as true with confidence, often without providing proof.
assert.ex: The company asserts that its new product is the safest on the market, though independent testing is still pending.

assessment.label: CEFR B2
assessment.def: A systematic evaluation or test to measure someone's knowledge, skills, or progress.
assessment.ex: The teacher used a combination of written exams and project work for the final assessment of the students' learning.

assignment.label: CEFR B1
assignment.def: A piece of work that a teacher gives students to do, usually outside the classroom.
assignment.ex: I spent two hours last night completing my history assignment about the French Revolution.

assistance.label: CEFR B1
assistance.def: Help or support given to someone to do something they need.
assistance.ex: The new employee received assistance from her manager during her first week.

assistant.label: CEFR B1
assistant.def: A person who helps someone else do their job or tasks.
assistant.ex: She works as a teaching assistant at the secondary school, helping students with their homework.

associate.label: CEFR B1
associate.def: To connect or think of something together with something else.
associate.ex: Many people associate the colour red with love and passion.

association.label: CEFR A2
association.def: A group of people who work together for a shared purpose or interest.
association.ex: My mother joined the local shopkeepers' association to help improve the town centre.

assumption.label: CEFR B2
assumption.def: Something believed to be true without proof or verification
assumption.ex: The study's results challenged the common assumption that teenagers need nine hours of sleep every night.

assurance.label: CEFR B2
assurance.def: A promise or guarantee that something will happen or is true, giving confidence or certainty.
assurance.ex: The manager gave the team the assurance that their jobs would be secure despite the company's restructuring.

assure.label: CEFR B2
assure.def: To tell someone something will definitely happen or is definitely true to remove their doubt.
assure.ex: The doctor assured her patients that the new treatment was safe and effective.

astonish.label: CEFR B2
astonish.def: To surprise someone greatly or shock them with something unexpected or remarkable.
astonish.ex: The magician's final illusion astonished the entire audience, leaving them speechless.

astonished.label: CEFR B2
astonished.def: Extremely surprised and impressed by something unexpected or remarkable.
astonished.ex: The critics were astonished by the young pianist's technically flawless performance.

astonishing.label: CEFR B2
astonishing.def: Extremely surprising or impressive, causing someone to feel amazed or shocked
astonishing.ex: The scientist's astonishing discovery of a new species in the Amazon rainforest made headlines around the world.

astonishment.label: CEFR B2
astonishment.def: A feeling of great surprise or amazement at something unexpected or remarkable.
astonishment.ex: To her astonishment, she discovered that her childhood friend had become a famous actor.

astronaut.label: CEFR A2
astronaut.def: A person trained to travel and work in space.
astronaut.ex: The astronaut wore a special suit before boarding the spacecraft.

astronomer.label: CEFR B1
astronomer.def: A scientist who studies stars, planets, and space using telescopes and instruments.
astronomer.ex: The astronomer discovered a new planet orbiting a distant star using the observatory's powerful telescope.

astronomy.label: CEFR B2
astronomy.def: The scientific study of stars, planets, galaxies, and other objects in space.
astronomy.ex: She chose astronomy as her specialisation because she had always been fascinated by the structure of the universe.

athlete.label: CEFR A2
athlete.def: A person who plays sports or competes in physical competitions.
athlete.ex: The athlete trained every day to prepare for the Olympic Games.

athletic.label: CEFR B1
athletic.def: Strong and good at sports or physical activities; relating to athletes or athletics.
athletic.ex: She has an athletic build and plays on the school's netball team.

athletics.label: CEFR B2
athletics.def: Competitive sports involving running, jumping, and throwing events, typically organised in stadiums.
athletics.ex: She competed in athletics throughout university, specialising in the 1500-metre race.

atlantic.label: CEFR B1
atlantic.def: Related to the Atlantic Ocean, the large ocean between Europe and North America.
atlantic.ex: Many Atlantic fish species are threatened by overfishing in recent years.

atmosphere.label: CEFR B1
atmosphere.def: The mood or feeling that a place or situation creates
atmosphere.ex: The restaurant had a relaxing atmosphere with soft lighting and quiet music.

atom.label: CEFR A2
atom.def: The smallest piece of matter that makes up everything in the world.
atom.ex: Everything around us, like water and wood, is made of tiny atoms we cannot see.

atomic.label: CEFR B1
atomic.def: Relating to atoms, or so extremely small it seems indivisible.
atomic.ex: Scientists used atomic microscopes to study the structure of the material at the smallest possible scale.

attach.label: CEFR B1
attach.def: To fasten or join something to another thing securely
attach.ex: Please attach your CV to the email before sending it to the employer.

attached.label: CEFR B2
attached.def: emotionally connected to someone or something; feeling love or loyalty towards them
attached.ex: She became very attached to her childhood home and found it difficult to leave when her family moved abroad.

attachment.label: CEFR B1
attachment.def: A feeling of love or strong connection to someone or something.
attachment.ex: She has a strong attachment to her childhood home and visits it every summer.

attack.label: CEFR A2
attack.def: To try to hurt or damage someone or something using force.
attack.ex: The dog attacked the mailman when he came to the door.

attainable.label: CEFR B2
attainable.def: Able to be achieved or reached with reasonable effort or skill.
attainable.ex: With a structured training plan, running a marathon is an attainable goal for most people.

attainment.label: CEFR B2
attainment.def: The achievement or accomplishment of a goal, skill, or level of success through effort.
attainment.ex: The school's focus on literacy attainment has resulted in improved reading scores across all year groups.

attempt.label: CEFR B1
attempt.def: To try to do something, even if it might be difficult or you might not succeed.
attempt.ex: She attempted to climb the mountain despite the bad weather, but had to turn back halfway.

attend.label: CEFR B1
attend.def: To be present at an event or place; to go to something
attend.ex: I will attend the meeting tomorrow afternoon at 2 o'clock.

attendance.label: CEFR B2
attendance.def: The number or proportion of people present at an event, meeting, or institution.
attendance.ex: The conference's attendance exceeded expectations, with over two thousand delegates from forty countries participating in the three-day programme.

attention.label: CEFR A2
attention.def: The act of focusing your mind or eyes on something
attention.ex: The teacher asked for the students' attention before starting the lesson.

attentive.label: CEFR B2
attentive.def: Watching or listening carefully and noticing things; showing interest or concern for someone
attentive.ex: The attentive audience listened intently as the speaker described her groundbreaking research.

attentively.label: CEFR B2
attentively.def: In a way that shows careful focus and interest in something or someone.
attentively.ex: She listened attentively to the lecture, taking notes on every key point the professor mentioned.

attract.label: CEFR B1
attract.def: To make someone interested in something or draw them towards it.
attract.ex: The colourful decorations in the shop window attracted many customers.

attraction.label: CEFR B1
attraction.def: A person or place that many people like to visit because it is interesting or fun
attraction.ex: The theme park is one of the city's main attractions, with rides and shows for the whole family.

attractive.label: CEFR A2
attractive.def: Pleasing to look at or interesting to someone.
attractive.ex: She wore an attractive blue dress to the party.

attractiveness.label: CEFR B2
attractiveness.def: The quality of being appealing or interesting to people; the power to draw attention or interest.
attractiveness.ex: The attractiveness of the city lies in its historic architecture and vibrant cultural scene.

atypical.label: CEFR B2
atypical.def: Not following the usual pattern or characteristics; different from what is expected or normal.
atypical.ex: The patient's atypical symptoms made it difficult for doctors to reach a quick diagnosis.

aubergine.label: CEFR B2
aubergine.def: A large purple vegetable with smooth skin, commonly used in Mediterranean and Asian cooking.
aubergine.ex: She roasted slices of aubergine with olive oil and garlic to make a delicious side dish.

audience.label: CEFR A2
audience.def: The people who watch or listen to a performance, film, or programme
audience.ex: The audience clapped loudly when the concert finished.

audio.label: CEFR A2
audio.def: Related to sound or listening that you can hear
audio.ex: The audio quality of the film on this old TV is not very good.

audit.label: CEFR B2
audit.def: An official examination of an organisation's accounts or procedures to check they are correct and legal.
audit.ex: The company underwent a financial audit last month to ensure all records were accurate.

audition.label: CEFR B2
audition.def: A performance or test given by a performer to demonstrate their skills and suitability for a role
audition.ex: She attended the audition for the West End musical, performing two contrasting songs for the judges.

august.label: CEFR A1
august.def: The eighth month of the year, between July and September.
august.ex: My birthday is in August, and I turn seven years old.

aunt.label: CEFR A1
aunt.def: Your parent's sister, or your uncle's wife.
aunt.ex: My aunt bakes the best birthday cake for our family celebrations.

author.label: CEFR A2
author.def: A person who writes books, stories, or articles.
author.ex: My favourite author wrote five novels before becoming famous.

authoritative.label: CEFR B2
authoritative.def: Having real power or knowledge, so people trust and respect what you say or decide.
authoritative.ex: The report from the World Health Organisation is considered the most authoritative source on vaccine safety.

authorize/authorise.label: CEFR B2
authorize/authorise.def: To give official permission or power to someone to do something.
authorize/authorise.ex: The manager authorised the purchase of new equipment for the department.

automatic.label: CEFR A2
automatic.def: Working by itself without a person having to control it
automatic.ex: The automatic doors at the supermarket open when you walk towards them.

automatically.label: CEFR A2
automatically.def: Without needing to think about it or do anything; it happens by itself.
automatically.ex: The door automatically closes when you walk through it.

autumn.label: CEFR A1
autumn.def: The season between summer and winter when leaves fall from trees.
autumn.ex: In autumn, the leaves turn red and yellow.

auxiliary.label: CEFR B1
auxiliary.def: Providing extra help or support to the main person or thing
auxiliary.ex: The hospital hired auxiliary nurses to help during the busy winter months.

availability.label: CEFR B2
availability.def: The state of being able to be used or obtained when needed.
availability.ex: The availability of public transport in rural areas has improved significantly over the past decade.

avenue.label: CEFR B1
avenue.def: A wide street in a town or city, often with trees along the sides.
avenue.ex: The new shopping centre is located on the main avenue, next to the park.

average.label: CEFR A2
average.def: The number you get by adding amounts together and dividing by how many there are
average.ex: The average temperature in summer is 25 degrees Celsius.

avoid.label: CEFR A2
avoid.def: To stay away from something or someone because you do not want to meet it or deal with it.
avoid.ex: I avoid eating sweets because they are bad for my teeth.

await.label: CEFR B2
await.def: To wait for someone or something to happen or arrive.
await.ex: The results of the investigation await publication next month.

awake.label: CEFR A1
awake.def: Not sleeping; with eyes open and aware of things around you
awake.ex: I was awake all night because I was very excited.

award.label: CEFR B1
award.def: To give something to someone as a prize or in recognition of their achievement
award.ex: The university awarded her a scholarship for her excellent grades.

awareness.label: CEFR B1
awareness.def: Knowledge and understanding of something, especially what is happening around you
awareness.ex: The campaign raised awareness about climate change among young people.

away.label: CEFR A1
away.def: To or at a distance from a place or person.
away.ex: My friend moved away to London last year.

awe.label: CEFR B2
awe.def: A feeling of wonder and respect mixed with slight fear when facing something impressive or grand
awe.ex: The astronauts gazed in awe at the vast expanse of Earth from space.

awesome.label: CEFR B1
awesome.def: Extremely good or impressive; causing a feeling of wonder or surprise
awesome.ex: That concert last night was awesome—the best performance I've ever seen!

awful.label: CEFR A2
awful.def: Very bad or unpleasant; much worse than normal or expected.
awful.ex: The weather was awful yesterday, so we stayed inside all day.

awkward.label: CEFR B1
awkward.def: Uncomfortable or embarrassing; making you feel nervous or unsure what to do
awkward.ex: There was an awkward silence at the dinner table after he made that joke.

ax/axe.label: CEFR B2
ax/axe.def: A tool with a heavy blade on a long handle, used for chopping wood or trees.
ax/axe.ex: The gardener used a sharp axe to split the logs for the fireplace.

axis.label: CEFR B2
axis.def: A line around which something rotates, or a reference line used to measure positions in space.
axis.ex: The Earth rotates on its axis, completing one full turn every 24 hours.

baby.label: CEFR A1
baby.def: A very young child, from birth to about one or two years old.
baby.ex: My baby sister cries when she is hungry.

babysit.label: CEFR B1
babysit.def: To take care of a child while their parents are away from home.
babysit.ex: My sister babysits for the neighbours' children every weekend to earn extra money.

babysitter.label: CEFR B1
babysitter.def: A person employed to care for children while parents are away from home.
babysitter.ex: We hired a babysitter to look after our two children while we went out to dinner.

back.label: CEFR B1
back.def: To support or help someone by providing assistance or agreement.
back.ex: My parents backed my decision to study abroad, even though they were worried.

backache.label: CEFR B1
