backache.def: Pain in the lower or middle part of your back, often caused by bad posture or strain
backache.ex: After sitting at my desk all day, I developed a terrible backache that made it difficult to stand up.

background.label: CEFR A2
background.def: Someone's family, experiences, and education from their past
background.ex: She comes from a musical background because her parents are both musicians.

backpack.label: CEFR B1
backpack.def: A bag worn on your back with straps over both shoulders, used to carry belongings.
backpack.ex: She packed her backpack with books and a water bottle before hiking up the mountain.

backpacker.label: CEFR B1
backpacker.def: A traveller who carries belongings in a bag on their back and travels cheaply, often staying in hostels.
backpacker.ex: Many backpackers spend months travelling through Southeast Asia on a tight budget, visiting different countries.

backpacking.label: CEFR B1
backpacking.def: Travelling with a backpack, staying in budget accommodation and moving between different places.
backpacking.ex: Many students enjoy backpacking across Europe during their summer holidays because it is affordable and flexible.

backup.label: CEFR B2
backup.def: A copy of important data or files kept in case the original is lost or damaged.
backup.ex: The company keeps daily backups of all customer information on secure servers.

backward/backwards.label: CEFR B2
backward/backwards.def: In the direction opposite to the way you are facing or moving; towards the rear.
backward/backwards.ex: She walked backwards down the corridor whilst maintaining eye contact with the audience.

backyard.label: CEFR B2
backyard.def: The area of land directly behind a house, typically enclosed by a fence or garden.
backyard.ex: They installed a swimming pool in their backyard to enjoy during the summer months.

bacon.label: CEFR B1
bacon.def: Thin slices of salt-cured pork meat, usually cooked until crispy and eaten for breakfast.
bacon.ex: She fried some bacon and eggs for breakfast this morning.

bad.label: CEFR A1
bad.def: Not good; unpleasant or of poor quality
bad.ex: That was a bad film. I didn't enjoy it.

badge.label: CEFR B2
badge.def: A small piece of metal, plastic, or cloth worn to show rank, achievement, or membership in an organisation
badge.ex: She earned her Duke of Edinburgh gold badge after completing the three-year programme.

badly.label: CEFR A2
badly.def: in a way that is not good or not well
badly.ex: She played badly in the tennis match yesterday.

badminton.label: CEFR A2
badminton.def: A sport where two or four players hit a small feathered ball over a net using rackets.
badminton.ex: My friends and I played badminton in the park on Saturday afternoon.

bad-tempered.label: CEFR B2
bad-tempered.def: Inclined to become angry or irritable quickly; having a tendency to be hostile or impatient
bad-tempered.ex: The bad-tempered manager made it clear that any mistakes would not be tolerated in his department.

bag.label: CEFR A1
bag.def: A container made of cloth, plastic, or leather for carrying things
bag.ex: She put her shopping in a plastic bag at the supermarket.

baggage.label: CEFR B1
baggage.def: Suitcases, bags, and other things you carry when travelling
baggage.ex: I checked my baggage at the airport before boarding the flight.

bake.label: CEFR A2
bake.def: To cook food using dry heat in an oven
bake.ex: My mother bakes bread every Sunday morning.

baker.label: CEFR B1
baker.def: A person whose job is to make and cook bread and cakes in a bakery or kitchen.
baker.ex: The baker starts work very early in the morning to prepare fresh bread for customers.

bakery.label: CEFR B1
bakery.def: A shop where bread, cakes, and pastries are made and sold.
bakery.ex: I bought fresh croissants from the bakery this morning before work.

balance.label: CEFR B2
balance.def: To keep something in a stable position, or to distribute weight or attention equally between different things.
balance.ex: She struggled to balance her demanding career with her responsibilities as a parent.

balanced.label: CEFR B2
balanced.def: Having different parts or elements in the right proportion or harmony with each other.
balanced.ex: A balanced diet includes proteins, vegetables, fruits, and grains in appropriate amounts.

balcony.label: CEFR A2
balcony.def: A platform that sticks out from the side of a building, with a railing around it.
balcony.ex: We sat on the balcony of our hotel room and watched the sunset over the sea.

bald.label: CEFR B1
bald.def: Having little or no hair on the head.
bald.ex: My grandfather went bald in his thirties, but he never minded it.

balky.label: CEFR B2
balky.def: Unwilling to move forward or cooperate; resistant to doing what is expected or required
balky.ex: The balky negotiations between the two companies finally broke down after weeks of disagreement over contract terms.

ball.label: CEFR A1
ball.def: A round object used in sports and games.
ball.ex: The children played with a ball in the park.

ballad.label: CEFR B2
ballad.def: A narrative poem or song that tells a story, often about love, adventure, or historical events.
ballad.ex: The folk singer performed a traditional Scottish ballad about a doomed love affair that captivated the entire audience.

ballet.label: CEFR B2
ballet.def: A formal style of dance with precise movements, often telling a story through music and choreography.
ballet.ex: She has been training in ballet since she was five years old and now performs professionally with a national company.

balloon.label: CEFR A2
balloon.def: A flexible bag filled with gas that floats in the air or can be blown up with your mouth.
balloon.ex: The child held a red balloon at the funfair and watched it float into the sky.

ballot.label: CEFR B2
ballot.def: A paper or electronic system used to record votes in an election or formal decision
ballot.ex: Each voter received a ballot listing all the candidates standing for parliament.

ban.label: CEFR B2
ban.def: To officially prohibit or forbid something by law or authority.
ban.ex: The government has banned the use of single-use plastic bags in all shops across the country.

banana.label: CEFR A1
banana.def: A long yellow fruit with soft, sweet flesh inside a thick skin.
banana.ex: I eat a banana for breakfast every morning.

band.label: CEFR A1
band.def: A strip of material worn around part of your body, like your wrist or head.
band.ex: She wore a blue band around her hair.

bandage.label: CEFR B1
bandage.def: A strip of material used to cover and protect a wound or injury on the body.
bandage.ex: After he cut his hand, the nurse applied a clean bandage to stop the bleeding.

bang.label: CEFR B1
bang.def: To hit something hard and make a loud noise
bang.ex: He banged on the door loudly until someone answered.

bank.label: CEFR A2
bank.def: To deposit money in a bank or have an account with a bank.
bank.ex: I bank with the same company as my parents.

bank account.label: CEFR B1
bank account.def: An arrangement with a bank where you can deposit and withdraw money safely.
bank account.ex: Before travelling abroad, I opened a bank account so I could access my money easily.

banker.label: CEFR B2
banker.def: A person who works in a bank, managing money and financial services for customers and businesses.
banker.ex: The banker advised her clients on the best investment options for their retirement savings.

banking.label: CEFR A2
banking.def: The business of managing money, accounts, and financial services at a bank.
banking.ex: I went to the bank for banking services because I needed to open a new account.

bankrupt.label: CEFR B2
bankrupt.def: To make someone unable to pay their debts; cause financial ruin.
bankrupt.ex: The unexpected legal costs threatened to bankrupt the small publishing company.

bankruptcy.label: CEFR B2
bankruptcy.def: Legal situation where a person or company cannot pay their debts and their assets are distributed to creditors.
bankruptcy.ex: The retail company filed for bankruptcy after years of declining sales and mounting losses.

bar.label: CEFR B1
bar.def: To prevent or stop someone from doing something or going somewhere.
bar.ex: The security guard barred him from entering the building without a valid ID.

barbecue.label: CEFR B1
barbecue.def: To cook food slowly over fire or hot coals outdoors.
barbecue.ex: We barbecued chicken and vegetables in the garden for our friends last Saturday.

barber.label: CEFR A2
barber.def: A person whose job is to cut men's hair and shave their faces.
barber.ex: I go to the barber every month to get my hair cut.

barbershop.label: CEFR B2
barbershop.def: A shop where men get haircuts and shaving services, typically offering grooming treatments.
barbershop.ex: He visits the barbershop every three weeks to maintain his neat appearance and discuss current events with the barber.

bare.label: CEFR B2
bare.def: To uncover or expose something that was hidden or covered before.
bare.ex: The archaeologists carefully bared the ancient ruins by removing layers of soil.

barely.label: CEFR B1
barely.def: Almost not; only just; with difficulty or by a small amount
barely.ex: She barely passed the exam because she didn't study enough.

bargain.label: CEFR B2
bargain.def: To negotiate the terms of a deal, especially to try to obtain a lower price or better conditions.
bargain.ex: The customer bargained with the car salesman for twenty minutes before agreeing on a final price.

bark.label: CEFR B2
bark.def: To speak loudly and harshly, or to shout orders in a commanding manner.
bark.ex: The sergeant barked instructions at the recruits during the morning drill.

barley.label: CEFR B2
barley.def: A cereal grain with a hard outer layer, used in brewing and cooking.
barley.ex: The brewery uses locally grown barley to produce their award-winning ales.

barman.label: CEFR B1
barman.def: A man who serves drinks to customers in a bar or pub.
barman.ex: The barman at our local pub knows everyone's favourite drink by heart.

barn.label: CEFR B2
barn.def: A large farm building used for storing crops, hay, and farm equipment or keeping animals.
barn.ex: The farmer stored thousands of tonnes of grain in the old barn before winter arrived.

barometer.label: CEFR B2
barometer.def: An instrument that measures atmospheric pressure to predict weather changes.
barometer.ex: The barometer in the weather station showed that pressure was dropping, indicating a storm was approaching.

barrel.label: CEFR B1
barrel.def: A large cylindrical container, usually made of wood or metal, used for storing liquids or other materials.
barrel.ex: The winery stored the wine in wooden barrels for several years before bottling it.

barren.label: CEFR B2
barren.def: Land or soil unable to produce crops or vegetation; also means unproductive or lacking creative output.
barren.ex: The barren landscape of the desert stretched endlessly, with no signs of life or green vegetation.

barrier.label: CEFR B2
barrier.def: Something physical or non-physical that prevents movement, access, or communication between people or places
barrier.ex: The new trade barrier between the two countries has made it difficult for businesses to export their products.

bartender.label: CEFR B2
bartender.def: A person who prepares and serves alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks in a bar or restaurant.
bartender.ex: The bartender expertly mixed cocktails whilst maintaining a friendly conversation with customers throughout the evening.

base.label: CEFR B1
base.def: To use something as a foundation or starting point for something else
base.ex: The author based her novel on real events from her grandmother's life.

baseball.label: CEFR A1
baseball.def: A popular sport played between two teams with a bat, ball, and bases.
baseball.ex: My brother plays baseball every Saturday in the park.

basement.label: CEFR B1
basement.def: A room or level in a building that is below ground level.
basement.ex: They converted the basement into a home gym with exercise equipment and mirrors.

basic.label: CEFR A2
basic.def: Simple, fundamental, and containing only the most necessary things.
basic.ex: The hotel room was basic, but it had a bed, shower, and chair.

basically.label: CEFR A2
basically.def: In the most important or simple way; really or simply put
basically.ex: The job is basically the same as before, just with different hours.

basin.label: CEFR B1
basin.def: A large bowl or container used to hold water or other liquids.
basin.ex: She filled the basin with warm water to wash her hands.

basis.label: CEFR B1
basis.def: The main reason or fact that something is built on or depends on
basis.ex: The basis of this course is learning how to write clearly and confidently.

basket.label: CEFR A2
basket.def: A container made of woven material, used to hold or carry things.
basket.ex: She put the fresh apples in a basket and took them home.

basketball.label: CEFR A1
basketball.def: A sport where two teams throw a ball through a high net to score points.
basketball.ex: My brother plays basketball at school every Tuesday afternoon.

bat.label: CEFR B1
bat.def: To hit a ball with a stick or bat in sports like cricket or baseball.
bat.ex: She batted the ball hard and it flew over the fielder's head.

batch.label: CEFR A2
batch.def: A group of similar things made or dealt with together at the same time.
batch.ex: The baker made a fresh batch of bread this morning.

bath.label: CEFR A1
bath.def: A large container filled with water for washing your whole body while sitting or lying down
bath.ex: I take a hot bath every evening to relax before bed.

bathe.label: CEFR B1
bathe.def: To wash your body or someone else's body in water, usually in a bath or river.
bathe.ex: After playing football, he bathed in the shower to remove all the dirt and sweat.

bathhouse.label: CEFR B2
bathhouse.def: A public building where people pay to bathe, relax, and use facilities like saunas or hot springs.
bathhouse.ex: During her visit to Japan, she spent a relaxing afternoon at a traditional bathhouse with natural hot spring water.

bathroom.label: CEFR A1
bathroom.def: A room in a house with a toilet and usually a sink and shower or bath.
bathroom.ex: I wash my hands in the bathroom before eating dinner.

battery.label: CEFR A2
battery.def: A device that provides electrical power to make things work.
battery.ex: I need to buy new batteries for my remote control.

battle.label: CEFR B1
battle.def: A violent fight between armed groups or armies, often involving many people.
battle.ex: The soldiers fought a fierce battle to control the city during the war.

battlefield.label: CEFR B2
battlefield.def: An area of land where a military battle takes place or has taken place.
battlefield.ex: Journalists reported from the battlefield as the conflict intensified in the region.

bay.label: CEFR A2
bay.def: A large area of water surrounded by land on three sides.
bay.ex: We swam in the bay near the small coastal town.

beach.label: CEFR A1
beach.def: A sandy or rocky area by the sea where people swim and relax.
beach.ex: We played football on the beach last summer.

beagle.label: CEFR B2
beagle.def: A small hunting dog with short legs, long ears, and typically brown and white markings.
beagle.ex: The beagle's excellent sense of smell made it perfect for detecting contraband at the airport.

beak.label: CEFR B2
beak.def: The hard, pointed mouth parts of birds and some other animals used for eating and manipulating objects.
beak.ex: The eagle's sharp beak tore at the fish it had caught from the river.

beam.label: CEFR B2
beam.def: A long, thick piece of wood or metal used to support the structure of a building or bridge.
beam.ex: The wooden beams in the old farmhouse were over two hundred years old and still supporting the roof.

bean.label: CEFR A1
bean.def: A small, hard seed from a plant, cooked and eaten as food.
bean.ex: I like to eat beans with rice for lunch.

bear.label: CEFR A2
bear.def: To carry or hold something; to accept or endure a difficult situation
bear.ex: She bears a heavy bag of shopping every day from the market.

beard.label: CEFR B1
beard.def: Hair that grows on a man's chin and lower face
beard.ex: He decided to grow a beard for the first time in his life.

bearing.label: CEFR B2
bearing.def: A person's manner or deportment; the way someone carries or conducts themselves
bearing.ex: Despite the difficult circumstances, she maintained a dignified bearing throughout the hearing.

beast.label: CEFR B1
beast.def: A large, dangerous, or powerful animal, or a cruel or unkind person.
beast.ex: The zoo keeper warned us that the lion was a wild beast and we should stay behind the barrier.

beat.label: CEFR B1
beat.def: To defeat someone in a game or competition; to move repeatedly up and down or side to side
beat.ex: Our football team beat the rivals 3-1 in the final match.

beautiful.label: CEFR A1
beautiful.def: Very attractive or pleasing to look at.
beautiful.ex: She wore a beautiful blue dress to the party.

beautifully.label: CEFR B1
beautifully.def: In a way that is very attractive, pleasing, or done with great skill and care.
beautifully.ex: The garden was beautifully arranged with colourful flowers and stone pathways.

beautify.label: CEFR B2
beautify.def: To make something more attractive or pleasing to look at.
beautify.ex: The city council decided to beautify the park by planting new trees and installing modern sculptures.

beauty.label: CEFR A2
beauty.def: The quality of being very pleasing to look at or attractive.
beauty.ex: The beauty of the mountain landscape made her want to paint it.

beaver.label: CEFR B1
beaver.def: A large furry animal with a flat tail that builds dams across rivers and streams.
beaver.ex: The beaver used branches and mud to construct a dam that changed the river's flow.

become.label: CEFR A1
become.def: To start to be something or someone different.
become.ex: She studied hard and became a doctor.

bed.label: CEFR A1
bed.def: A piece of furniture you sleep on at night.
bed.ex: I go to bed at 10 o'clock every evening.

bedroom.label: CEFR A1
bedroom.def: A room in a house where people sleep.
bedroom.ex: I have a bed, a desk, and a wardrobe in my bedroom.

bedside.label: CEFR B1
bedside.def: The area beside a bed, especially in a bedroom or hospital
bedside.ex: She placed a glass of water on the bedside table before going to sleep.

bee.label: CEFR A1
bee.def: A small flying insect that makes honey and lives in large groups.
bee.ex: The bee flew from flower to flower in the garden.

beef.label: CEFR A1
beef.def: Red meat from a cow, used as food.
beef.ex: My father cooked beef for dinner last night.

beer.label: CEFR A2
beer.def: A cold alcoholic drink made from grains, water, and hops.
beer.ex: On Friday nights, my friends and I often go to the pub for a beer.

before.label: CEFR A1
before.def: At an earlier time than something else.
before.ex: I ate breakfast before I went to school.

beg.label: CEFR A2
beg.def: To ask someone for something in a sincere, urgent, or emotional way.
beg.ex: The child begged her mother for a new toy at the shop.

begin.label: CEFR A1
begin.ex: The lesson begins at nine o'clock in the morning.

beginner.label: CEFR A2
beginner.def: A person who is just starting to learn or do something new.
beginner.ex: She is a beginner at playing the piano, so she practices every day.

beginning.label: CEFR A2
beginning.def: The time or place when something starts or first appears.
beginning.ex: At the beginning of the school day, the teacher takes attendance.

behalf.label: CEFR B1
behalf.def: Someone's interests or benefit; acting or speaking for another person.
behalf.ex: The lawyer spoke on behalf of his client in the courtroom.

behave.label: CEFR B1
behave.def: To act or conduct yourself in a particular way, especially showing good or bad manners.
behave.ex: The children behaved very well during the school trip and the teachers were impressed.

behavior/behaviour.label: CEFR A2
behavior/behaviour.def: The way a person or animal acts or does things.
behavior/behaviour.ex: The teacher praised the children for their good behaviour in class.

behind.label: CEFR A1
behind.def: At the back of something or someone; not in front.
behind.ex: The cat is hiding behind the sofa.

being.label: CEFR A2
being.def: The state of existing or living as a person or creature
being.ex: She is a kind being who helps everyone around her.

belief.label: CEFR B1
belief.def: Something you think is true or right, even without proof
belief.ex: Her belief that hard work leads to success has helped her achieve her goals.

believe.label: CEFR A1
believe.def: To think that something is true.
believe.ex: I believe that cats are very intelligent animals.

bell.label: CEFR A1
bell.def: A metal object that makes a ringing sound when struck
bell.ex: The school bell rings at nine o'clock in the morning.

bellow.label: CEFR B2
bellow.def: To shout in a loud, deep voice, often expressing anger or pain.
bellow.ex: The angry coach bellowed at his players from the sideline during the match.

belly.label: CEFR A2
belly.def: The front part of your body between your chest and legs.
belly.ex: The baby rubbed his belly after eating lunch.

belong.label: CEFR A2
belong.def: To be a member of a group or to be the right place for something.
belong.ex: I belong to a swimming club at school.

belongings.label: CEFR B2
belongings.def: Personal possessions and items that someone owns and typically carries or keeps with them.
belongings.ex: She carefully packed her belongings into her suitcase before leaving for university.

beloved.label: CEFR B1
beloved.def: Loved deeply by someone; very dear and important to a person
beloved.ex: She visited her beloved grandmother every weekend without fail.

below.label: CEFR A1
below.def: At a lower level or position than something else.
below.ex: The water is very cold below the ice.

belt.label: CEFR A2
belt.def: A strip of material worn around your waist to hold up trousers or as decoration
belt.ex: He wore a leather belt with his new jeans.

bench.label: CEFR A2
bench.def: A long seat made of wood or metal, usually for two or more people.
bench.ex: We sat on a bench in the park and watched people shopping at the market.

bend.label: CEFR A2
bend.def: To move something so it is no longer straight, or to move your body in a curved way.
bend.ex: She bent down to pick up her keys from the floor.

beneficial.label: CEFR B2
beneficial.def: Producing good results; helpful or advantageous to someone or something.
beneficial.ex: Regular exercise has beneficial effects on both physical and mental health.

benevolence.label: CEFR B2
benevolence.def: The quality of being kind and generous, showing genuine care for others' wellbeing.
benevolence.ex: The charity's benevolence towards vulnerable communities has transformed thousands of lives across the region.

benevolent.label: CEFR B2
benevolent.def: Kind and generous, showing concern for others' welfare without expecting reward
benevolent.ex: The benevolent philanthropist donated millions to establish schools in underprivileged communities.

bent.label: CEFR B1
bent.def: Curved or no longer straight; determined to do something at any cost
bent.ex: The metal rod was bent after the accident, but the coach was bent on winning the championship.

berry.label: CEFR B2
berry.def: Small, sweet fruit with seeds, often used in cooking or eaten fresh.
berry.ex: The hedgerow was lined with blackberry bushes heavy with ripe fruit in late summer.

besides.label: CEFR A2
besides.def: In addition to; also, or as well as something else
besides.ex: I like swimming, and besides, it's good exercise.

best.label: CEFR A1
best.def: In the most good or excellent way; better than all others.
best.ex: She speaks English best in our class.

bestow.label: CEFR B2
bestow.def: To give something valuable or an honour to someone in a formal or generous way
bestow.ex: The university bestowed an honorary degree on the renowned scientist at the graduation ceremony.

bestseller.label: CEFR B2
bestseller.def: A book or product that sells more copies than almost all others in its category during a particular period.
bestseller.ex: Her debut novel became an international bestseller, selling over five million copies in the first year.

bet.label: CEFR B1
bet.def: To risk money or something valuable on the result of an uncertain event.
bet.ex: He bet £50 on his favourite horse in the race yesterday.

betray.label: CEFR B2
betray.def: To reveal someone's secrets or break their trust, often to help their enemies.
betray.ex: The spy betrayed his country by selling classified documents to a foreign government.

better.label: CEFR A2
better.def: In a way that is more good or more successful than before or than something else
better.ex: She plays tennis better than her brother.

bewilder.label: CEFR B2
bewilder.def: To confuse someone so much that they don't know what to think or do.
bewilder.ex: The complex instructions bewildered most of the students, leaving them unsure where to start.

bewilderment.label: CEFR B2
bewilderment.def: A state of confusion where someone is unsure what to do or think because something is unclear.
bewilderment.ex: The sudden change in government policy left economists in bewilderment about what would happen to the economy.

bicycle.label: CEFR A1
bicycle.def: A vehicle with two wheels, pedals, and handlebars that you ride for transport or fun
bicycle.ex: My brother rides his bicycle to school every day.

bid.label: CEFR B2
bid.def: To make an offer of a price for something, especially at an auction or in competition.
bid.ex: Several collectors bid aggressively for the rare painting at the auction house last night.

bidding.label: CEFR B2
bidding.def: The process of offering a price or terms to win a contract or project competition.
bidding.ex: Several construction companies submitted their bidding proposals for the new hospital building project.

bike.label: CEFR A1
bike.def: A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pushing the pedals with your feet.
bike.ex: I ride my bike to school every day.

bikini.label: CEFR B2
bikini.def: A two-piece swimsuit consisting of a top and bottom worn by women for swimming.
bikini.ex: She packed her new bikini for the beach holiday in Greece.

bilingual.label: CEFR B1
bilingual.def: Able to speak and understand two languages fluently and equally well.
bilingual.ex: She grew up in a bilingual household where her parents spoke French and English every day.

bill.label: CEFR B1
bill.def: To send someone an official request for payment for goods or services provided
bill.ex: The company will bill you monthly for your internet subscription.

billion.label: CEFR B2
billion.def: The number 1,000,000,000, or in British English, one million million.
billion.ex: The government invested several billion pounds into improving public transport infrastructure across the country.

bin.label: CEFR B1
bin.def: A container used for storing or throwing away waste and rubbish
bin.ex: I threw the empty bottle into the recycling bin outside.

bind.label: CEFR B2
bind.def: To tie something tightly with rope, string, or another material; to fasten together.
bind.ex: The carpenter used strong tape to bind the wooden planks together before applying the glue.

bio.label: CEFR B2
bio.def: A short written account of a person's life, education, and professional achievements.
bio.ex: The professor's bio on the university website highlighted her research in molecular biology and numerous publications.

biochemistry.label: CEFR B1
biochemistry.def: The scientific study of chemical substances and reactions inside living organisms.
biochemistry.ex: She chose to study biochemistry at university because she was fascinated by how the human body processes food.

biography.label: CEFR B1
biography.def: A written account of a person's life, including important events and experiences.
biography.ex: We read a biography of Marie Curie in our history class to learn about her scientific discoveries.

biological.label: CEFR B2
biological.def: Related to living things or the study of life and living organisms.
biological.ex: Scientists are researching biological processes to understand how diseases develop in the human body.

biology.label: CEFR B1
biology.def: The science that studies living things, including plants, animals, and how they work.
biology.ex: She chose biology as her main subject because she wants to become a doctor.

biotechnology.label: CEFR B2
biotechnology.def: The use of living organisms or their parts to develop new products or solve problems in medicine and agriculture.
biotechnology.ex: Biotechnology has revolutionised the production of insulin, allowing diabetics to access affordable treatment.

bird.label: CEFR A1
bird.def: A small animal with feathers, wings, and a beak that flies in the sky.
bird.ex: The bird sang a beautiful song in the tree this morning.

birdcage.label: CEFR B2
birdcage.def: A cage constructed to confine and house pet birds, typically made of wire or wood with small openings.
birdcage.ex: She decorated the birdcage with colourful perches and mirrors to keep her parrots entertained.

birth.label: CEFR A1
birth.def: The time when a baby comes out of its mother's body and is born.
birth.ex: My sister's birth was on a cold winter morning in December.

birthday.label: CEFR A1
birthday.def: The day each year when you were born; the anniversary of your birth.
birthday.ex: My birthday is in March, and I always have a party with my friends.

birthplace.label: CEFR B2
birthplace.def: The town or country where someone was born.
birthplace.ex: She has always been proud of her birthplace, a small village in rural Ireland.

biscuit.label: CEFR A1
biscuit.def: A hard, dry, sweet or savoury snack that is baked and crisp.
biscuit.ex: I eat a biscuit with my tea every afternoon.

bishop.label: CEFR B1
bishop.def: A senior religious leader in some Christian churches who oversees a district and its priests.
bishop.ex: The bishop visited our parish church last month to confirm the young people.

bit.label: CEFR A2
bit.def: A small piece or amount of something.
bit.ex: I ate a bit of cake after dinner.

bite.label: CEFR B1
bite.def: To cut or press something with your teeth
bite.ex: The dog tried to bite the postman's leg, but the man jumped back quickly.

bitter.label: CEFR B1
bitter.def: Having a strong, unpleasant taste; or feeling angry and unhappy about something unfair.
bitter.ex: She felt bitter about losing the job after working there for ten years.

bitterly.label: CEFR B2
bitterly.def: In a way that shows strong anger, resentment, or deep sadness about something.
bitterly.ex: She bitterly complained that her years of hard work had been completely ignored by management.

bizarre.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
bizarre.def: Extremely strange or unusual in a surprising and often unsettling way.
bizarre.ex: The artist's bizarre sculptures made from recycled materials attracted international attention at the gallery exhibition.

black.label: CEFR A1
black.def: The darkest colour, like the night sky or coal
black.ex: She wore a black dress to the party.

blackboard.label: CEFR A2
blackboard.def: A dark surface in a classroom where teachers write with chalk for students to see
blackboard.ex: The teacher wrote the maths problem on the blackboard and we all copied it in our notebooks.

blade.label: CEFR B2
blade.def: The flat, sharp part of a knife, sword, or other cutting tool
blade.ex: The surgeon carefully selected a fresh blade for the delicate operation.

blame.label: CEFR A2
blame.def: To say that someone is responsible for something bad that happened.
blame.ex: She blamed her brother for breaking the vase.

blank.label: CEFR A1
blank.def: Empty with nothing written or drawn on it.
blank.ex: I wrote my name on the blank piece of paper.

blanket.label: CEFR A2
blanket.def: A thick piece of cloth used to keep you warm in bed or when sitting outside.
blanket.ex: On cold nights, I pull my blanket up to my chin to stay warm.

blast.label: CEFR B2
blast.def: To forcefully criticise someone or something publicly and strongly.
blast.ex: Environmental groups blasted the government's decision to approve the new coal plant.

blaze.label: CEFR B2
blaze.def: To burn brightly and intensely, or to move very quickly with great energy and determination.
blaze.ex: The fire blazed through the forest, destroying everything in its path within hours.

bleed.label: CEFR B1
bleed.def: To lose blood from your body through a cut or injury.
bleed.ex: He cut his finger badly and it started to bleed, so he put a bandage on it.

blend.label: CEFR B1
blend.def: To mix two or more things together until they combine completely.
blend.ex: She blended the fruit and yoghurt together to make a smoothie.

bless.label: CEFR B1
bless.def: To ask God to help or protect someone; to make something holy or sacred.
bless.ex: The priest blessed the couple at their wedding ceremony.

blessing.label: CEFR B1
blessing.def: Something good that you are grateful for; a gift or benefit you receive with happiness
blessing.ex: Having a supportive family is a real blessing in difficult times.

blind.label: CEFR B1
blind.def: Unable to see because of damaged eyes or lack of light
blind.ex: After the accident, he was blind in one eye but could still see with the other.

blindly.label: CEFR B2
blindly.def: Without thinking carefully or checking facts; accepting something without questioning or understanding it
blindly.ex: She blindly followed her manager's advice without considering whether it was actually in her best interest.

blindness.label: CEFR B2
blindness.def: The inability to see, either complete or partial loss of vision.
blindness.ex: The organisation provides support to people living with blindness and visual impairment.

blink.label: CEFR B2
blink.def: To close and open your eyes very quickly, often without thinking about it.
blink.ex: She didn't blink during the entire horror film, too frightened to look away.

block.label: CEFR B1
block.def: To stop something from moving forward or prevent something from happening.
block.ex: The fallen tree blocked the road, so we had to take a different route.

blog.label: CEFR B1
blog.def: A website where someone regularly writes articles, photos, or videos about their interests or experiences.
blog.ex: She started a blog about her travels and now has thousands of readers from around the world.

blogger.label: CEFR B1
blogger.def: A person who writes regular posts about their interests or experiences on the internet.
blogger.ex: She started a fashion blogger account to share her style tips with thousands of followers.

blonde.label: CEFR A2
blonde.def: Having light yellow or pale hair colour.
blonde.ex: She has blonde hair and blue eyes.

blood.label: CEFR A2
blood.def: Red liquid that flows through your body and carries oxygen to keep you alive
blood.ex: When you cut your finger, a little blood comes out.

bloodstream.label: CEFR B2
bloodstream.def: The flow of blood through veins and arteries that carries oxygen and nutrients throughout the body.
bloodstream.ex: When you take medication, it enters your bloodstream and circulates to reach affected areas of your body.

bloody.label: CEFR B2
bloody.def: Used for emphasis to express annoyance or surprise; very or extremely.
bloody.ex: The new software update was bloody awful and crashed every time I tried to use it.

bloom.label: CEFR A2
bloom.def: A flower or the flowers on a plant or tree.
bloom.ex: The roses in the garden are in full bloom this month.

blooming.label: CEFR B2
blooming.def: Flowering or developing well; thriving and showing promise or health.
blooming.ex: The blooming tech sector has attracted thousands of young entrepreneurs to the city.

blossom.label: CEFR B2
blossom.def: Flowers that appear on trees and plants, especially in spring.
blossom.ex: The cherry blossom festival attracts thousands of visitors to the town's parks each year.

blow.label: CEFR A1
blow.def: To move air from your mouth with force
blow.ex: I blow out the candles on my birthday cake.

blue.label: CEFR A1
blue.def: A colour like the sky or the sea on a clear day.
blue.ex: The sky is blue and the sun is shining.

blur.label: CEFR B2
blur.def: To make something unclear or difficult to see by making edges less sharp and defined.
blur.ex: The camera blurred the background to focus attention on the main subject in the photograph.

blurt.label: CEFR B2
blurt.def: A sudden, uncontrolled statement or remark made without thinking first.
blurt.ex: Her angry blurt about the project's failures surprised everyone in the meeting.

blush.label: CEFR B2
blush.def: To become red in the face, typically from embarrassment or shame
blush.ex: She blushed deeply when he complimented her in front of the entire team.

board.label: CEFR B1
board.def: To get on a plane, ship, or other vehicle to travel somewhere
board.ex: We boarded the flight to Paris early in the morning.

board game.label: CEFR A2
board game.def: A game played on a board with pieces, dice, or cards, usually with two or more players.
board game.ex: My family loves playing board games on Sunday afternoons, especially Monopoly and Scrabble.

boast.label: CEFR B1
boast.def: To talk proudly about something you have done or own, in a way that annoys others.
boast.ex: He was always boasting about his new car at work, which made his colleagues uncomfortable.

boat.label: CEFR A1
boat.def: A small vehicle that floats on water and carries people or goods.
boat.ex: We took a boat across the lake on holiday.

body.label: CEFR A1
body.def: The physical form of a person or animal, including all parts like arms, legs, and head.
body.ex: She exercises to keep her body healthy and strong.

boil.label: CEFR A2
boil.def: To heat liquid until it reaches 100°C and bubbles rapidly.
boil.ex: I need to boil some water to make tea.

boiled.label: CEFR A2
boiled.def: Cooked in very hot water until soft or done
boiled.ex: I had boiled eggs and toast for breakfast this morning.

boiling.label: CEFR B2
boiling.def: Extremely hot or angry; at or above the temperature where liquid turns to gas
boiling.ex: After waiting two hours in boiling traffic, she arrived at the meeting feeling frustrated and irritated.

bold.label: CEFR B2
bold.def: A typeface style where letters appear thicker and darker than normal text.
bold.ex: The heading was printed in bold to make it stand out from the rest of the paragraph.

boldly.label: CEFR B1
boldly.def: In a confident and brave way, without fear or hesitation.
boldly.ex: She boldly walked into the meeting and shared her unpopular opinion with the team.

bolt.label: CEFR B2
bolt.def: To run away suddenly and quickly, usually out of fear or urgency
bolt.ex: When the fire alarm sounded, several audience members bolted from the theatre before the evacuation procedure began.

bomb.label: CEFR B1
bomb.def: To drop bombs on a place from an aircraft, or to attack something with bombs.
bomb.ex: During the war, enemy planes bombed the city several times, destroying many buildings.

bombard.label: CEFR B2
bombard.def: Heavy continuous attack or criticism directed at someone or something repeatedly.
bombard.ex: Students often face a bombardment of notifications from social media while trying to study.

bomber.label: CEFR B2
bomber.def: A person who carries out bomb attacks, or a military aircraft that drops bombs on targets.
bomber.ex: The terrorist bomber was arrested before he could carry out the planned attack on the city centre.

bombing.label: CEFR B2
bombing.def: An attack in which bombs are dropped or set off to destroy buildings or kill people.
bombing.ex: The bombing campaign during the war caused severe damage to the city's infrastructure and displaced thousands of civilians.

bond.label: CEFR B1
bond.def: A strong feeling of connection or friendship between people or groups
bond.ex: The shared experience of travelling together created a strong bond between the two friends.

bone.label: CEFR A1
bone.def: Hard white part inside your body that holds you up and protects your organs.
bone.ex: The doctor said my broken bone will heal in six weeks.

bonus.label: CEFR A2
bonus.def: Extra money given to an employee in addition to their normal salary
bonus.ex: My boss gave me a bonus of £500 for working hard this year.

bony.label: CEFR B2
bony.def: Containing a lot of bones or having bones that are very noticeable under the skin.
bony.ex: The fish was bony and difficult to eat, requiring careful attention to avoid choking on small bones.

book.label: CEFR B1
book.def: To arrange or reserve something in advance, like a hotel room or flight ticket.
book.ex: I booked a table at the restaurant for 7 o'clock tomorrow evening.

bookcase.label: CEFR A2
bookcase.def: A piece of furniture with shelves for storing and displaying books.
bookcase.ex: She put all her favourite novels on the wooden bookcase in her bedroom.

booking.label: CEFR B1
booking.def: A reservation you make in advance for a hotel, flight, restaurant, or other service
booking.ex: I made a booking at the hotel for three nights next month.

bookish.label: CEFR B2
bookish.def: Interested in reading and studying books rather than outdoor or social activities.
bookish.ex: His bookish nature meant he preferred spending weekends in the library rather than at parties with friends.

booklet.label: CEFR B2
booklet.def: A small, thin book with a paper cover, usually containing information or instructions on one topic.
booklet.ex: The airline provided a safety booklet explaining emergency procedures to all passengers before takeoff.

bookmark.label: CEFR B2
bookmark.def: To save a website address in your browser so you can access it quickly later.
bookmark.ex: I bookmarked several research articles to read for my essay this weekend.

bookshelf.label: CEFR A2
bookshelf.def: A piece of furniture with shelves for storing and displaying books.
bookshelf.ex: She put her favourite novels on the bookshelf in her bedroom.

bookshop.label: CEFR A2
bookshop.def: A shop where books are sold.
bookshop.ex: I went to the bookshop after school to buy a new novel.

bookstore.label: CEFR A1
bookstore.def: A shop where you can buy books.
bookstore.ex: I went to the bookstore to buy a new English dictionary.

boom.label: CEFR B1
boom.def: A sudden increase or period of rapid growth in business, economy, or activity
boom.ex: The tech industry experienced a boom in the 1990s when many new companies were created.

boost.label: CEFR B2
boost.def: To increase or improve something significantly, often quickly or suddenly
boost.ex: The new advertising campaign boosted sales by 30% in just three months.

boot.label: CEFR B1
boot.def: A strong shoe that covers your ankle and part of your leg.
boot.ex: She wore black leather boots with her winter coat to keep her feet warm and dry.

booth.label: CEFR B2
booth.def: A small enclosed or semi-enclosed space or structure, typically used for a specific purpose or activity.
booth.ex: She entered the photo booth at the shopping centre to take some passport pictures.

border.label: CEFR B1
border.def: The line that separates two countries or regions.
border.ex: She had to show her passport when crossing the border between France and Spain.

bore.label: CEFR B1
bore.def: To make someone feel tired and uninterested by being dull or repetitive
bore.ex: The long presentation started to bore the audience after the first twenty minutes.

bored.label: CEFR A2
bored.def: Not interested in something because it is not exciting or fun
bored.ex: I was bored during the long film, so I fell asleep.

boredom.label: CEFR B1
boredom.def: The feeling of being tired and uninterested because something is dull or not exciting.
boredom.ex: After watching the same film for the third time, he couldn't hide his boredom.

boring.label: CEFR A1
boring.ex: The film was boring, so I fell asleep.

borrow.label: CEFR A1
borrow.def: To take something from someone and return it later.
borrow.ex: Can I borrow your pen? I'll give it back tomorrow.

boss.label: CEFR A2
boss.def: A person who manages other workers and makes important decisions at work.
boss.ex: My boss asked me to finish the report by Friday morning.

bossy.label: CEFR B2
bossy.def: Tending to give orders to others and expecting them to obey without question.
bossy.ex: Her bossy attitude made it difficult for team members to contribute their own ideas during meetings.

botany.label: CEFR B2
botany.def: The scientific study of plants, including their structure, growth, reproduction, and relationships with their environment.
botany.ex: She chose botany as her specialist subject because she wanted to understand how plants adapt to climate change.

both.label: CEFR A1
both.def: Two people or things together, not just one.
both.ex: I like both coffee and tea.

bother.label: CEFR A2
bother.def: To cause someone trouble, worry, or inconvenience; to annoy.
bother.ex: I don't want to bother you, but could you help me with this?

bothered.label: CEFR B2
bothered.def: Feeling troubled, annoyed, or worried about something; having your attention or peace disturbed.
bothered.ex: She seemed bothered by the criticism, even though her colleagues praised her presentation overall.

bothersome.label: CEFR B2
bothersome.def: Causing slight annoyance or inconvenience; mildly irritating or troublesome.
bothersome.ex: The constant noise from the construction site next door has been bothersome for weeks, making it difficult to concentrate on work.

bottle.label: CEFR B2
bottle.def: To put liquid into bottles for storage or sale.
bottle.ex: The winery bottles its wine in the spring after fermentation is complete.

bottom.label: CEFR A1
bottom.def: The lowest part of something; the surface you sit on.
bottom.ex: I sat on the bottom of the chair.

bounce.label: CEFR B1
bounce.def: To move up and down repeatedly after hitting a surface
bounce.ex: The children were bouncing a ball against the wall in the playground.

bound.label: CEFR B1
bound.def: Certain to happen or arrive; very likely to occur
bound.ex: If you keep studying hard, you're bound to pass the exam.

bourbon.label: CEFR B2
bourbon.def: American whiskey made from corn and aged in charred wooden barrels, traditionally from Kentucky.
bourbon.ex: The bartender recommended a premium bourbon that had been aged for at least twelve years.

bow.label: CEFR B2
bow.def: To bend forward from the waist or head as a sign of respect or greeting.
bow.ex: The musicians bowed deeply to acknowledge the applause from the audience.

bowl.label: CEFR B1
bowl.def: To roll a heavy ball along the ground towards pins in a sport game.
bowl.ex: We decided to go bowling last Friday, and I managed to bowl three strikes in a row.

box.label: CEFR A1
box.def: A container with flat sides, usually made of cardboard or wood, used to hold things.
box.ex: I put my toys in a big box under my bed.

boxer.label: CEFR B2
boxer.def: A professional athlete who fights opponents using fists in a sport with strict safety rules.
boxer.ex: The boxer trained for months to prepare for the championship fight next month.

boxing.label: CEFR B1
boxing.def: A sport where two people fight each other with their fists while wearing protective gloves.
boxing.ex: My brother has been training in boxing at the gym for six months now and he loves it.

boy.label: CEFR A1
boy.ex: The boy is playing football with his friends.

boyfriend.label: CEFR A1
boyfriend.def: A man or boy that someone is in a romantic relationship with.
boyfriend.ex: My boyfriend and I go to the cinema together every weekend.

bra.label: CEFR A2
bra.def: A piece of clothing worn under a shirt to support a woman's chest.
bra.ex: She put on a clean bra before getting dressed for work.

bracelet.label: CEFR B1
bracelet.def: A piece of jewellery worn around the wrist, usually made of metal, beads, or string
bracelet.ex: She wore a gold bracelet on her left wrist at the wedding.

bracket.label: CEFR B2
bracket.def: A supporting structure, usually metal or plastic, that holds something firmly in place or attaches it to a wall
bracket.ex: The shelf was secured to the wall with heavy-duty steel brackets that could support up to fifty kilograms.

brag.label: CEFR B2
brag.def: A statement in which someone talks too proudly about their achievements or possessions.
brag.ex: His constant bragging about his expensive car annoyed everyone at the office.

brain.label: CEFR A1
brain.def: The organ inside your head that controls your thoughts and movements.
brain.ex: Use your brain to solve this math problem.

brainstorm.label: CEFR A2
brainstorm.def: A group activity where people quickly share many ideas without judging them.
brainstorm.ex: We had a brainstorm in class to find ideas for our school project.

brainstorming.label: CEFR B1
brainstorming.def: A group activity where people share ideas quickly without judging them to solve a problem.
brainstorming.ex: During the brainstorming session, the team came up with several creative solutions for the marketing campaign.

brake.label: CEFR B2
brake.def: To slow down or stop a vehicle by using mechanical pressure
brake.ex: The driver braked suddenly when a child ran into the street.

branch.label: CEFR B1
branch.def: To divide into separate parts or directions, or to start a new division of a business.
branch.ex: The company decided to branch out into the technology sector last year.

brand.label: CEFR A2
brand.def: A company name or product name that people know and recognise.
brand.ex: Nike is a famous brand that makes sports shoes and clothes.

brand-new.label: CEFR B1
brand-new.def: Completely new, never used before, just made or produced recently.
brand-new.ex: She drove her brand-new car carefully on the first day of ownership.

brass.label: CEFR B1
brass.def: A yellow metal made by mixing copper and zinc; also wind instruments made of this metal
brass.ex: The orchestra's brass section includes trumpets, trombones, and French horns.

brave.label: CEFR A2
brave.def: Not afraid; having courage to do something difficult or dangerous
brave.ex: The brave firefighter entered the burning building to save the family.

bravely.label: CEFR B1
bravely.def: In a way that shows courage despite fear or danger.
bravely.ex: She bravely spoke up in the meeting even though she was nervous about disagreeing with her boss.

bravery.label: CEFR B2
bravery.def: Courage to face danger, fear, or difficulty without showing weakness.
bravery.ex: The firefighter's bravery in rescuing the family from the burning building earned her widespread recognition.

bread.label: CEFR A1
bread.def: A common food made from flour, water, and yeast, baked until brown
bread.ex: I eat bread and butter for breakfast every morning.

break.label: CEFR A1
break.def: To cause something to separate into pieces or stop working.
break.ex: Be careful not to break the glass when you wash it.

breakable.label: CEFR B2
breakable.def: Likely to break or be damaged easily; fragile
breakable.ex: The museum clearly marked the breakable items in storage to ensure they were handled with extra care.

breakdown.label: CEFR B2
breakdown.def: A sudden failure or collapse of something physical, mental, or organisational; a detailed analysis of information into parts.
breakdown.ex: The company's financial breakdown showed that costs had increased significantly in the last quarter.

breakfast.label: CEFR A1
breakfast.def: The first meal you eat in the morning
breakfast.ex: I eat breakfast at 7 o'clock before school.

breakthrough.label: CEFR B1
breakthrough.def: An important discovery or sudden progress after a long period of difficulty
breakthrough.ex: Scientists announced a breakthrough in cancer treatment that could save millions of lives.

breakup.label: CEFR B2
breakup.def: The end of a romantic relationship between two people.
breakup.ex: After a difficult breakup, she decided to focus on her career and personal growth.

breast.label: CEFR B1
breast.def: Either of the two soft parts on a person's chest that produce milk after childbirth.
breast.ex: She was advised to have regular mammogram screenings to check her breast health.

breath.label: CEFR B1
breath.def: The air you take in and out of your lungs when you breathe
breath.ex: After running up the stairs, she was out of breath and needed to sit down.

breathe.label: CEFR A1
breathe.def: To take air in and out of your lungs
breathe.ex: I breathe fresh air when I walk in the park.

breathless.label: CEFR B1
breathless.def: Unable to breathe normally, usually because of physical effort or strong emotion
breathless.ex: After running up the stairs, she was breathless and had to sit down for a moment.

breathtaking.label: CEFR B2
breathtaking.def: Extremely impressive or surprising, causing a strong emotional reaction of wonder or shock.
breathtaking.ex: The breathtaking views of the mountain range made the difficult hike entirely worthwhile.

breed.label: CEFR B1
breed.def: To keep animals such as dogs or cattle for producing young animals or products.
breed.ex: The farmer breeds cattle to sell at the market every year.

breeding.label: CEFR B1
breeding.def: The process of producing young animals or plants, or training and developing skills in people.
breeding.ex: The school focuses on the breeding of creative thinking skills in all its students.

breeze.label: CEFR A2
breeze.def: A soft, gentle wind that is pleasant and not very strong
breeze.ex: On a hot summer day, we sat outside and enjoyed the cool breeze.

breezy.label: CEFR B2
breezy.def: Light and pleasant wind; or relaxed and casual in manner or tone.
breezy.ex: The breezy conversation between old friends felt effortless and made everyone smile.

brewery.label: CEFR B2
brewery.def: A factory where beer is manufactured and processed before being bottled or sold.
brewery.ex: The local brewery offers guided tours where visitors can see how they produce their award-winning ales.

brick.label: CEFR B1
brick.def: A small rectangular block of hard material used to build walls and buildings.
brick.ex: The old house was built from red brick and had a traditional English charm.

bride.label: CEFR A2
bride.def: A woman on her wedding day or who is getting married.
bride.ex: The bride looked beautiful in her white dress.

bridge.label: CEFR A1
bridge.def: A structure that goes over a river or road so people and cars can cross it.
bridge.ex: We walked across the bridge to get to the other side of the river.

brief.label: CEFR B1
brief.def: Lasting for a short time; short in duration or length
brief.ex: She gave a brief presentation about the company's new marketing strategy.

briefly.label: CEFR B1
briefly.def: For a short time; not in much detail or length
briefly.ex: The manager briefly explained the new company policy before the meeting ended.

bright.label: CEFR A1
bright.def: Full of light or shining strongly; easy to see clearly.
bright.ex: The bright sun made me wear sunglasses.

brightly.label: CEFR B1
brightly.def: In a way that produces or reflects a lot of light; shining strongly and clearly.
brightly.ex: The sun shone brightly through the classroom windows, making it hard to see the whiteboard.

brilliant.label: CEFR A2
brilliant.def: Very bright or shining strongly; also means very clever or excellent
brilliant.ex: She had a brilliant idea for solving the maths problem.

brilliantly.label: CEFR B2
brilliantly.def: In a way that shows exceptional intelligence, skill, or impressive quality.
brilliantly.ex: The lawyer argued her case brilliantly, persuading the jury with clear logic and compelling evidence.

bring.label: CEFR A1
bring.def: To carry or move something from one place to another place.
bring.ex: Please bring your book to class tomorrow.

broad.label: CEFR B1
broad.def: Wide, covering a large area or range of different things
broad.ex: The report gives a broad overview of the company's performance across all departments.

broadband.label: CEFR B2
broadband.def: High-speed internet connection that transmits data much faster than traditional dial-up connections.
broadband.ex: Many rural areas still lack access to reliable broadband infrastructure, limiting educational opportunities for students.

broadcast.label: CEFR B2
broadcast.def: To transmit a programme or information to a wide audience via television, radio, or internet.
broadcast.ex: The BBC will broadcast the football match live on Saturday evening.

broadcaster.label: CEFR B2
broadcaster.def: A person or organisation that transmits television or radio programmes to a wide audience.
broadcaster.ex: The broadcaster announced a new documentary series about climate change that will air next month.

broaden.label: CEFR B2
broaden.def: To make something wider in range, scope, or understanding.
broaden.ex: Studying abroad helped broaden her perspective on different cultures and ways of life.

broadly.label: CEFR B2
broadly.def: In general terms; without focusing on specific details or exceptions
broadly.ex: Broadly speaking, the new policy aims to improve healthcare access across all regions.

broccoli.label: CEFR B1
broccoli.def: A green vegetable with thick stems and small flower-like tops that you can eat
broccoli.ex: I steamed the broccoli for five minutes until it was tender but still crunchy.

brochure.label: CEFR B2
brochure.def: A printed booklet containing information, usually used for advertising or promoting products, services, or destinations.
brochure.ex: The travel agency gave me a colourful brochure describing all the available holiday packages.

broken.label: CEFR A1
broken.def: Damaged and not working properly anymore.
broken.ex: My toy car is broken, so I cannot play with it.

bronze.label: CEFR B1
bronze.def: A hard metal made by mixing copper and tin together.
bronze.ex: The museum displayed an ancient bronze statue from Greece that was over two thousand years old.

brother.label: CEFR A1
brother.def: A male child of the same parents as you.
brother.ex: My brother and I play football together after school.

brotherhood.label: CEFR B1
brotherhood.def: A strong feeling of friendship and loyalty shared between people, especially men, who support each other.
brotherhood.ex: The soldiers developed a deep brotherhood during their time serving together in the army.

brother-in-law.label: CEFR B2
brother-in-law.def: The brother of your spouse, or the husband of your sister.
brother-in-law.ex: My brother-in-law is a software engineer who works in London with my sister.

brown.label: CEFR A2
brown.def: A dark colour between red and yellow, like soil or wood
brown.ex: She wore a brown jacket to keep warm in autumn.

browse.label: CEFR B2
browse.def: To look through something casually without a specific purpose or plan.
browse.ex: She browsed the bookshop shelves for an hour, picking up novels that caught her interest.

browser.label: CEFR B2
browser.def: A software application used to access and view websites and other content on the internet.
browser.ex: Most people use Chrome or Firefox as their web browser to search for information online.

bruise.label: CEFR B2
bruise.def: A dark mark on the skin caused by an injury where blood vessels break beneath the surface.
bruise.ex: After falling off her bike, she noticed a large purple bruise forming on her knee.

brush.label: CEFR A1
brush.def: To clean or make smooth something with a brush
brush.ex: I brush my teeth every morning before breakfast.

bubble.label: CEFR B1
bubble.def: A ball of gas or air inside a liquid or on its surface.
bubble.ex: She blew bubbles through a straw into her glass of lemonade.

bucket.label: CEFR A1
bucket.def: A container with a handle, used to carry and store liquids or other materials.
bucket.ex: She filled the bucket with water to wash the car.

bud.label: CEFR B2
bud.def: A small, tight growth on a plant that develops into a leaf or flower.
bud.ex: The rose buds on the plant will bloom into beautiful flowers within a few days.

buddy.label: CEFR B1
buddy.def: A close friend or someone you spend time with regularly and trust.
buddy.ex: My buddy from university helped me move house last weekend.

budget.label: CEFR A2
budget.def: A plan for how much money you will spend on different things.
budget.ex: The company's budget for next year includes money for new computers and staff training.

bug.label: CEFR A2
bug.def: A small insect with six legs.
bug.ex: There was a bug crawling on the kitchen wall this morning.

build.label: CEFR A1
build.def: To make or construct something by putting pieces or materials together.
build.ex: We build a sandcastle on the beach.

builder.label: CEFR B1
builder.def: A person whose job is to construct buildings and other structures.
builder.ex: The builder used concrete and steel to create a strong foundation for the new house.

building.label: CEFR A1
building.def: A large structure with walls and a roof, used for living or working.
building.ex: The shopping centre is a big building in the town centre.

bulb.label: CEFR A2
bulb.def: A round glass part that gives light when electricity passes through it.
bulb.ex: I changed the light bulb in my bedroom lamp yesterday.

bulimia.label: CEFR B2
bulimia.def: An eating disorder involving episodes of binge eating followed by deliberate purging to avoid weight gain.
bulimia.ex: Healthcare professionals now recognise bulimia as a serious mental health condition requiring specialised treatment and psychological support.

bull.label: CEFR B1
bull.def: A large male cow used for farming or breeding purposes.
bull.ex: The farmer kept a strong bull in the field to breed with the cows.

bullet.label: CEFR B1
bullet.def: A small metal projectile fired from a gun or rifle as a weapon.
bullet.ex: The investigator found a bullet embedded in the wall of the crime scene.

bulletin.label: CEFR B1
bulletin.def: A short official statement or report giving news or information about something important.
bulletin.ex: The hospital issued a medical bulletin about the patient's condition after the surgery.

bully.label: CEFR B2
bully.def: To repeatedly threaten, frighten, or physically harm someone who is weaker or unable to defend themselves.
bully.ex: The investigation revealed that older students had been bullying younger children in the school corridors for months.

bum.label: CEFR B2
bum.def: The bottom part of your body that you sit on; buttocks.
bum.ex: He fell on his bum while skating and had to sit out the rest of the game.

bump.label: CEFR B1
bump.def: To hit or push something or someone accidentally or with force.
bump.ex: I bumped my head on the low doorway when I walked into the attic.

bumper.label: CEFR B2
bumper.def: A metal or plastic bar on a vehicle designed to absorb impact in a collision.
bumper.ex: The car's front bumper was badly dented after the minor accident in the car park.

bun.label: CEFR B2
bun.def: A small bread roll, often slightly sweet, used for serving food or eaten alone.
bun.ex: She ordered a hamburger bun without sesame seeds at the bakery counter.

bunch.label: CEFR B1
bunch.def: A group of similar things held or growing together; a lot of people or things.
bunch.ex: I bought a bunch of bananas and a bunch of flowers for my mum's birthday.

burden.label: CEFR B2
burden.def: To load someone with a heavy responsibility or worry; to trouble or weigh down mentally
burden.ex: The manager didn't want to burden her team with extra work during the holiday season.

burdensome.label: CEFR B2
burdensome.def: Causing difficulty, worry, or stress; heavy and hard to deal with
burdensome.ex: The burdensome regulations made it difficult for small businesses to operate efficiently.

bureau.label: CEFR B1
bureau.def: An office or department that provides a particular service or handles official business
bureau.ex: I went to the tourist bureau to get information about hotels in the city.

bureaucracy.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
bureaucracy.def: System of government or organization with many rules, departments, and official processes.
bureaucracy.ex: The hospital's bureaucracy made it difficult to get approval for the new medical equipment quickly.

burger.label: CEFR A1
burger.def: A hot sandwich made with a cooked meat patty inside a soft bread roll
burger.ex: I eat a burger with cheese and tomato for lunch.

burglar.label: CEFR B1
burglar.def: A person who illegally enters buildings to steal things.
burglar.ex: The burglar was caught on security cameras breaking into the jewellery shop last night.

burglary.label: CEFR B2
burglary.def: The crime of illegally entering a building with the intention of stealing something.
burglary.ex: Police are investigating a series of burglaries that occurred in the city centre last month.

burgle.label: CEFR B2
burgle.def: To enter a building illegally and steal things from it.
burgle.ex: The jewellery shop was burgled last night, and the police are investigating the theft.

burial.label: CEFR B2
burial.def: The act of placing a dead body in the ground or a tomb, usually as part of a funeral ceremony.
burial.ex: The burial took place on a grey morning in the village churchyard, with family and friends gathered to pay their respects.

burn.label: CEFR B1
burn.def: To damage or destroy something with fire or heat.
burn.ex: I accidentally burned the toast while making breakfast this morning.

burning.label: CEFR B1
burning.def: Very hot or causing intense heat and pain.
burning.ex: She felt a burning sensation in her legs after running the marathon.

burst.label: CEFR B1
burst.def: To suddenly break open or explode, often with force and noise.
burst.ex: The balloon burst when my sister stepped on it accidentally.

bury.label: CEFR A2
bury.def: To put something in the ground and cover it with soil.
bury.ex: The dog wanted to bury its bone in the garden.

bus.label: CEFR A1
bus.def: A large vehicle that carries many passengers along a fixed route for money
bus.ex: I take the bus to school every morning.

bus station.label: CEFR A2
bus station.def: A place where buses stop to pick up and drop off passengers.
bus station.ex: I waited at the bus station for thirty minutes before my bus arrived.

bus stop.label: CEFR A2
bus stop.def: A place where buses stop to let passengers get on and off.
bus stop.ex: I waited at the bus stop for fifteen minutes before the bus arrived.

bush.label: CEFR A2
bush.def: A plant with many stems and leaves, smaller than a tree.
bush.ex: There is a green bush near the entrance of the shop.

business.label: CEFR A1
business.def: A company or shop that sells things or provides services to make money.
business.ex: My mother owns a small business that sells coffee and cakes.

businessman.label: CEFR A2
businessman.def: A man who owns or manages a company or business.
businessman.ex: The businessman travelled to London for an important meeting with his clients.

businesswoman.label: CEFR A2
businesswoman.def: A woman who owns or manages a company or works in an important job in business.
businesswoman.ex: She is a successful businesswoman who runs three hotels in London.

bust.label: CEFR B1
bust.def: To break something into pieces, usually by accident or with force.
bust.ex: He accidentally bust the vase when he knocked it off the table.

bustle.label: CEFR B2
bustle.def: To move energetically and with purpose, often in a busy or crowded environment.
bustle.ex: The kitchen bustled with activity as chefs prepared dinner for the large wedding reception.

bustling.label: CEFR B2
bustling.def: Full of energetic activity and movement; busy and lively with many people
bustling.ex: The bustling market in the city centre was crowded with shoppers and vendors selling fresh produce.

busy.label: CEFR A1
busy.def: Having a lot of things to do; not free or available right now.
busy.ex: My mother is busy cooking dinner, so she cannot play with me now.

butcher.label: CEFR B2
butcher.def: To kill animals for meat, or to kill people in a brutal and violent way
butcher.ex: The documentary revealed how the regime had butchered thousands of civilians during the conflict.

butter.label: CEFR A1
butter.def: A soft, yellow food made from milk that you spread on bread or use for cooking.
butter.ex: I like to put butter on my toast in the morning.

butterfly.label: CEFR A1
butterfly.def: A colourful insect with four big wings that flies around gardens and flowers.
butterfly.ex: I saw a beautiful yellow butterfly in the garden this morning.

button.label: CEFR A1
button.def: A small, round object sewn onto clothing that you push through a hole to fasten it
button.ex: I cannot find my shirt button, so I sewed a new one on.

buy.label: CEFR A1
buy.def: To give money to get something you want.
buy.ex: I buy apples at the supermarket every week.

buyer.label: CEFR B1
buyer.def: A person who purchases goods or services from a seller or shop.
buyer.ex: The buyer negotiated a lower price for the car before signing the contract.

buzz.label: CEFR B2
buzz.def: To make a continuous humming sound; or to be filled with excited activity and energy.
buzz.ex: The office buzzed with anticipation as employees waited to hear the announcement about the merger.

bye.label: CEFR A1
bye.def: A short word you say when leaving someone or ending a conversation.
bye.ex: She waved and said bye before getting on the bus.

cab.label: CEFR A2
cab.def: A car with a driver you pay to take you somewhere
cab.ex: I called a cab to go to the airport because I didn't want to drive.

cabbage.label: CEFR B1
cabbage.def: A large round vegetable with thick green or purple leaves, eaten as food.
cabbage.ex: My grandmother grows cabbage in her garden and makes delicious soup with it.

cabin.label: CEFR B1
cabin.def: A small room on a ship or aircraft where passengers sleep or rest
cabin.ex: We booked a cabin on the cruise ship with a window overlooking the ocean.

cabinet.label: CEFR B2
cabinet.def: A group of senior government ministers who advise the head of government and control key departments.
cabinet.ex: The Prime Minister reshuffled her cabinet to include new ministers from different regions of the country.

cable.label: CEFR B2
cable.def: A thick rope made of metal wires twisted together, or a thick cord used to transmit electricity or signals.
cable.ex: The technician buried the underground cable to connect the new building to the power grid.

cafe/caf\u00e9.label: CEFR A1
cafe/caf\u00e9.def: A small restaurant where you can buy drinks and light meals or snacks.
cafe/caf\u00e9.ex: I like to go to the café near my house for a coffee in the morning.

cafeteria.label: CEFR A2
cafeteria.def: A place in a school or workplace where people can buy and eat food.
cafeteria.ex: I eat lunch in the school cafeteria with my friends every day.

cage.label: CEFR B1
cage.def: An enclosure made of metal bars or wires used to keep animals.
cage.ex: The bird escaped from its cage and flew out of the window.

cake.label: CEFR A1
cake.def: A sweet food made from flour, eggs, sugar, and butter, baked in an oven.
cake.ex: I ate a chocolate cake at my birthday party.

calculate.label: CEFR B1
calculate.def: To find an answer using numbers or mathematics.
calculate.ex: She calculated the total cost of the trip by adding up all the expenses.

calculation.label: CEFR B1
calculation.def: Using numbers and maths to find an answer or result.
calculation.ex: The calculation of the project's cost took several hours because we had to include all materials.

calculator.label: CEFR B1
calculator.def: A small electronic device that performs mathematical operations like addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
calculator.ex: I used my calculator to check the total cost of the groceries before going to the checkout.

calendar.label: CEFR A2
calendar.def: A chart showing all the days, weeks, and months of a year.
calendar.ex: I mark my important dates on the calendar so I don't forget them.

calf.label: CEFR B1
calf.def: A young cow, or the muscular part at the back of your lower leg
calf.ex: The farmer's calf was born in spring and will grow into a large cow within two years.

call.label: CEFR A2
call.def: To phone someone, or to say someone's name loudly so they hear you.
call.ex: I will call my friend tomorrow to ask about the party.

caller.label: CEFR B1
caller.def: A person who makes a telephone call to someone or to a business.
caller.ex: The caller asked to speak with the manager about their complaint.

calm.label: CEFR B1
calm.def: Peaceful and not worried or excited; relaxed and in control.
calm.ex: After the meeting was postponed, she felt much calmer about the situation.

calmly.label: CEFR B2
calmly.def: In a peaceful, controlled manner without worry, anger, or excitement.
calmly.ex: She calmly explained the situation to the upset customer, using a steady voice and measured words.

calmness.label: CEFR B1
calmness.def: A peaceful state of mind without worry, fear, or strong emotion.
calmness.ex: The calmness of the garden helped her feel relaxed after a stressful day at work.

camel.label: CEFR B1
camel.def: A large desert animal with one or two humps on its back, used for transport.
camel.ex: The Bedouin traders crossed the Sahara on camels, carrying spices and silk to distant markets.

camera.label: CEFR A1
camera.def: A device you use to take photographs or record videos.
camera.ex: She took a photo with her camera at the beach.

camp.label: CEFR A2
camp.def: To stay in a tent or outdoor shelter for a short time, usually for leisure or adventure.
camp.ex: We camped in the mountains last summer and slept in a tent.

campaign.label: CEFR B2
campaign.def: A series of organised activities designed to achieve a political, social, or commercial goal.
campaign.ex: The environmental campaign successfully pressured the government to ban single-use plastics.

camping.label: CEFR A2
camping.def: Staying outside in a tent for holidays or adventure, usually in nature.
camping.ex: We went camping in the mountains last summer and slept in a tent.

campsite.label: CEFR B1
campsite.def: A place where people can set up tents or park caravans for camping holidays.
campsite.ex: We booked a campsite near the lake for our summer holiday and spent two weeks hiking and fishing.

campus.label: CEFR A2
campus.def: The area of land and buildings where a school or university is located.
campus.ex: Students walk across the campus to get to their classes.

can.label: CEFR A2
can.def: A metal container with a lid, used for storing drinks or food.
can.ex: I opened a can of beans for dinner.

canal.label: CEFR B1
canal.def: An artificial waterway built for boats to travel through or to move water to fields
canal.ex: The gondolas glided silently along the canal in Venice, carrying tourists under ancient bridges.

cancel.label: CEFR B1
cancel.def: To decide that a planned event or service will not happen.
cancel.ex: The football match was cancelled because of heavy rain.

cancer.label: CEFR B1
cancer.def: A serious disease where harmful cells grow uncontrollably in the body
cancer.ex: Her mother was diagnosed with cancer last year, but she has responded well to treatment.

candidate.label: CEFR B2
candidate.def: A person who is applying for a job, position, or has been nominated for election.
candidate.ex: The leading candidate for the presidency outlined her economic plans during the televised debate.

candle.label: CEFR B1
candle.def: A stick of wax with a string through it that burns to give light and sometimes pleasant smell.
candle.ex: She lit a candle on the dining table to make the room feel more romantic.

candy.label: CEFR A1
candy.def: Sweet food made from sugar, often in small pieces you eat for pleasure.
candy.ex: The children received candy for being good.

cane.label: CEFR B2
cane.def: A walking stick, or a tall woody plant stem used to make furniture and other products.
cane.ex: The elderly man walked slowly through the garden using a carved wooden cane.

canned.label: CEFR B1
canned.def: Preserved and stored in a metal container for later use
canned.ex: I made a quick soup using canned tomatoes and beans from my cupboard.

cannonball.label: CEFR B2
cannonball.def: A heavy metal sphere fired from a cannon as a weapon, or a dive into water with knees drawn to chest.
cannonball.ex: The old fort's walls were scarred by cannonball impacts from the naval bombardment during the siege.

canon.label: CEFR B2
canon.def: The body of work by an author considered most important and widely accepted as genuine.
canon.ex: Shakespeare's canon includes thirty-seven plays that scholars have authenticated over centuries.

canteen.label: CEFR B1
canteen.def: A place in a school, factory, or office where people can buy and eat food and drinks.
canteen.ex: I usually have lunch at the school canteen instead of bringing a packed lunch from home.

cap.label: CEFR A1
cap.def: A hat with a stiff front part that shades your eyes.
cap.ex: He wore a blue cap to protect his face from the sun.

capability.label: CEFR B2
capability.def: The power or skill to do something; what someone is able to accomplish.
capability.ex: The company's capability to produce electric vehicles has improved significantly in recent years.

capital.label: CEFR A2
capital.def: The most important city in a country where the government is located.
capital.ex: Paris is the capital of France.

capital letter.label: CEFR A2
capital letter.def: A large letter used at the start of a sentence or name, like A, B, C
capital letter.ex: Every sentence starts with a capital letter in English.

capsule.label: CEFR B1
capsule.def: A small container, usually made of plastic or metal, holding a medicine or substance inside.
capsule.ex: She swallowed a vitamin capsule with her breakfast every morning.

captain.label: CEFR A2
captain.def: The person in charge of a ship, aircraft, or sports team.
captain.ex: The captain of the football team scored the winning goal yesterday.

caption.label: CEFR B2
caption.def: Text that describes or explains a picture, diagram, or video scene.
caption.ex: The caption beneath the photograph identified the historical site and the year it was built.

captive.label: CEFR B2
captive.def: A person held prisoner or kept confined against their will, especially by an enemy or captor.
captive.ex: The negotiators worked to secure the release of the captives held by the militant group.

capture.label: CEFR B1
capture.def: To take someone prisoner or to catch something with difficulty
capture.ex: The police managed to capture the escaped prisoner after a three-day search.

car.label: CEFR A1
car.def: A vehicle with four wheels for carrying people on roads.
car.ex: I go to the shops by car every Saturday.

car park.label: CEFR A2
car park.def: An area where people can leave their cars while they shop, work, or visit
car park.ex: I left my car in the car park near the supermarket this morning.

caravan.label: CEFR B2
caravan.def: A group of vehicles or people travelling together, especially across deserts; also a mobile home for holidays.
caravan.ex: The caravan of tourists crossed the Sahara with experienced guides and sufficient water supplies.

carbon.label: CEFR B2
carbon.def: A chemical element found in all living things and fossil fuels, essential for life on Earth.
carbon.ex: Scientists are studying ways to reduce carbon emissions from vehicles to slow climate change.

carbon dioxide.label: CEFR B2
carbon dioxide.def: A colourless gas produced when people and animals breathe, and when fuel burns; a major greenhouse gas.
carbon dioxide.ex: Scientists warn that carbon dioxide emissions from factories and vehicles are contributing significantly to climate change.

carbon footprint.label: CEFR B2
