germ.ex: Scientists are studying the germ responsible for the widespread infection in the hospital.

gesture.label: CEFR B1
gesture.def: A movement of your hand, arm, or head to show what you mean or how you feel
gesture.ex: She made a welcoming gesture by opening her arms wide when her friend arrived at the station.

get.label: CEFR A1
get.def: To receive, obtain, or come to have something.
get.ex: I get a birthday present every year.

getaway.label: CEFR B2
getaway.def: A short holiday or escape from everyday life, often taken quickly or secretly.
getaway.ex: The couple booked a weekend getaway to the countryside to escape the stress of city life.

ghetto.label: CEFR B2
ghetto.def: A poor, crowded urban area where a particular group of people is forced or chooses to live separately.
ghetto.ex: The film examines how educational inequality persists in urban ghettos across the country.

ghost.label: CEFR A1
ghost.def: The spirit of a dead person that some people believe they can see
ghost.ex: Children say they saw a ghost in the old, empty house at night.

giant.label: CEFR B1
giant.def: extremely large, much bigger than normal or expected
giant.ex: The giant wave crashed against the shore during the storm.

gift.label: CEFR A1
gift.def: Something you give to someone because you like them or for a special occasion.
gift.ex: I bought a nice gift for my mother's birthday.

gifted.label: CEFR B1
gifted.def: Having natural ability or intelligence that is much higher than average
gifted.ex: The gifted students in her class were selected for an advanced mathematics programme.

giggle.label: CEFR B1
giggle.def: A light, nervous laugh, often repeated quickly and quietly.
giggle.ex: The children couldn't stop their giggles during the serious exam.

ginger.label: CEFR B1
ginger.def: A hot-tasting plant root used as a spice or flavouring in cooking and drinks.
ginger.ex: She added fresh ginger to the curry to give it a spicy kick.

giraffe.label: CEFR B1
giraffe.def: A very tall African animal with a long neck, long legs, and brown spots on its body.
giraffe.ex: The giraffe stretched its neck to reach the leaves at the top of the tree.

girl.label: CEFR A1
girl.def: A young female person, from birth until she becomes a woman.
girl.ex: The girl is playing with her doll in the park.

girlfriend.label: CEFR A1
girlfriend.def: A female romantic partner or female friend you have a close relationship with.
girlfriend.ex: My girlfriend and I like to go to the cinema together on weekends.

give.label: CEFR A1
give.def: To hand something to someone; to let them have it.
give.ex: I give my friend a book for her birthday.

giveaway.label: CEFR B2
giveaway.def: Something that reveals a secret or hidden truth, often unintentionally.
giveaway.ex: Her nervous laugh was a dead giveaway that she hadn't told us the whole story.

given.label: CEFR A2
given.def: Considering or assuming something is true as a starting point
given.ex: Given the weather, we decided to stay indoors and play board games.

glacis.label: CEFR B2
glacis.def: A gentle slope extending downward from a fortification toward open ground for defensive purposes.
glacis.ex: The castle's glacis provided clear sightlines that made it nearly impossible for enemies to approach without being detected.

glad.label: CEFR A1
glad.ex: I am glad to see you after so long.

glamorous/glamourous.label: CEFR B2
glamorous/glamourous.def: Attractive and fascinating, often involving elegance, luxury, or celebrity appeal.
glamorous/glamourous.ex: The glamorous film star arrived at the premiere in a designer gown, surrounded by photographers.

glance.label: CEFR B1
glance.def: To look quickly at something for a very short time.
glance.ex: She glanced at her phone to check the time during the meeting.

glare.label: CEFR B2
glare.def: A strong, harsh light that shines directly into your eyes and makes it difficult to see
glare.ex: The glare from the snow-covered mountains was so intense that we had to wear dark sunglasses.

glass.label: CEFR A1
glass.def: A transparent material used to make windows and drinking containers.
glass.ex: I drink water from a glass every morning.

glasses.label: CEFR A1
glasses.def: Two clear lenses in a frame worn on your face to help you see better.
glasses.ex: My father wears glasses when he reads the newspaper.

gleam.label: CEFR B2
gleam.def: A bright flash of light, or something shining with a soft, reflected brightness.
gleam.ex: There was a gleam of hope in her eyes as she received the good news.

glee.label: CEFR B2
glee.def: Great happiness or delight, often shown in an obvious or enthusiastic way
glee.ex: The children sang with glee when they learned they had won the competition.

glide.label: CEFR B1
glide.def: A smooth, continuous movement without effort or interruption.
glide.ex: The ice skater performed a beautiful glide across the frozen rink.

glimpse.label: CEFR B1
glimpse.def: A quick, brief look at something, usually by chance or not for very long.
glimpse.ex: I caught a glimpse of the film star as she walked past the café.

glint.label: CEFR B1
glint.def: A sudden bright flash of light that reflects off a shiny surface
glint.ex: She saw a glint of sunlight on the water as the boat sailed past.

global.label: CEFR B1
global.def: Affecting or involving the whole world or all countries
global.ex: Climate change is a global problem that requires action from every nation.

global warming.label: CEFR B2
global warming.def: Long-term increase in Earth's average temperature caused mainly by human activities releasing greenhouse gases.
global warming.ex: Scientists warn that global warming is causing sea levels to rise and threatening coastal communities worldwide.

globally.label: CEFR B2
globally.def: Throughout the world; affecting or involving all countries and regions
globally.ex: Climate change is a globally significant issue that requires international cooperation.

globe.label: CEFR A2
globe.def: A round object that shows all countries and oceans on Earth's surface.
globe.ex: I found a small globe in my classroom that shows where all the cities are.

glocal.label: CEFR B2
glocal.def: Combining global reach with local relevance and adaptation to specific communities or markets.
glocal.ex: The company adopted a glocal strategy, promoting its products worldwide whilst customising them to suit regional preferences and cultures.

gloomy.label: CEFR B2
gloomy.def: Dark and miserable; lacking brightness or cheerfulness
gloomy.ex: The gloomy weather forecast did nothing to lift the team's spirits before the important match.

glorify.label: CEFR B2
glorify.def: To praise someone or something excessively or make them seem more important than they really are
glorify.ex: The film glorifies violence by showing criminals as heroic rather than criminal.

glorious.label: CEFR B1
glorious.def: Beautiful, impressive, and deserving great praise or respect.
glorious.ex: The glorious sunset over the mountains filled us with wonder and joy.

glory.label: CEFR B1
glory.def: Great honour and respect that someone receives for their achievements.
glory.ex: The athlete basked in glory after winning the Olympic gold medal.

glove.label: CEFR A2
glove.def: A piece of clothing you wear on your hand to keep it warm or protected.
glove.ex: I wear gloves when it is cold outside in winter.

glow.label: CEFR B2
glow.def: A steady, soft light produced by something hot or luminous.
glow.ex: The glow from the campfire lit up the faces of everyone gathered around it.

go.label: CEFR A1
go.def: To move or travel from one place to another
go.ex: I go to school every morning.

goal.label: CEFR A1
goal.def: Something you want to achieve or reach in the future.
goal.ex: My goal is to speak English fluently by next year.

goalkeeper.label: CEFR B1
goalkeeper.def: A player in sports like football who protects the goal and tries to prevent the other team from scoring.
goalkeeper.ex: The goalkeeper made an excellent save in the final minutes of the match.

goat.label: CEFR B2
goat.def: A farm animal with horns and hooves, raised for milk, meat, and hair.
goat.ex: The farmer kept several goats in the pasture to produce milk for making cheese.

god.label: CEFR B2
god.def: A supernatural being worshipped in religion, often believed to be all-powerful and creator of the universe.
god.ex: In Homer's Iliad, the god Zeus controls the fate of mortals during the Trojan War.

goddess.label: CEFR B1
goddess.def: A female divine being or supreme power, often worshipped in religions and mythology.
goddess.ex: In Greek mythology, Athena was the goddess of wisdom and warfare.

godly.label: CEFR B2
godly.def: Devout, pious, and devoted to religious belief and moral virtue.
godly.ex: She led a godly life, attending church regularly and serving her community with compassion.

gold.label: CEFR A2
gold.def: A valuable yellow metal used to make jewellery and money.
gold.ex: She wore a gold ring on her wedding day.

golden.label: CEFR A2
golden.def: Made of or coloured like gold; yellow and shiny
golden.ex: She wore a beautiful golden necklace on her wedding day.

golf.label: CEFR A2
golf.def: A sport where you hit a small ball into holes on a grass field using special sticks.
golf.ex: My father plays golf every Saturday morning at the local club.

golfer.label: CEFR B1
golfer.def: A person who plays golf as a sport or profession.
golfer.ex: The professional golfer won the tournament after playing eighteen holes in excellent conditions.

good.label: CEFR B2
good.def: Something that is beneficial, useful, or morally right; a commodity or product.
good.ex: The government's new policy is expected to produce long-term goods for the entire economy.

good-looking.label: CEFR A2
good-looking.def: Attractive and pleasant to look at; having a nice appearance.
good-looking.ex: The good-looking actor was popular with many fans.

goods.label: CEFR B1
goods.def: Products or items that are made or sold, especially physical things you can buy.
goods.ex: The shop sells a wide range of goods, from clothing to electronics.

goodwill.label: CEFR B2
goodwill.def: Friendly feelings and desire to help others; positive attitude towards a person or organisation
goodwill.ex: The charity's work has built considerable goodwill among local communities, encouraging more people to volunteer.

gorge.label: CEFR B2
gorge.def: A deep, narrow valley with steep rocky sides, often formed by a river over time.
gorge.ex: The hikers walked through the spectacular limestone gorge, admiring the towering cliffs on either side.

gorgeous.label: CEFR B1
gorgeous.def: Extremely beautiful and attractive to look at.
gorgeous.ex: We watched the gorgeous sunset over the ocean yesterday evening.

gorilla.label: CEFR B1
gorilla.def: A large, powerful ape found in African forests, with dark fur and long arms.
gorilla.ex: The gorilla at the zoo is incredibly strong and can lift heavy objects with ease.

gossip.label: CEFR B2
gossip.def: To talk about other people's private affairs, often spreading unverified information or rumours.
gossip.ex: She was gossiping with her colleagues about the manager's sudden resignation.

govern.label: CEFR B1
govern.def: To make decisions and create laws for a country or organization.
govern.ex: The Prime Minister governs the country with help from Parliament.

government.label: CEFR A2
government.def: The group of people who run a country or region and make laws.
government.ex: The government decided to build a new hospital in our town.

governmental.label: CEFR B2
governmental.def: Relating to the system or organisation that runs a country or region and makes official decisions.
governmental.ex: The new governmental policies aim to reduce pollution by encouraging the use of renewable energy sources.

governor.label: CEFR B1
governor.def: A person elected or appointed to lead and make decisions for a state, region, or province.
governor.ex: The governor announced a new policy to improve schools across the state.

gown.label: CEFR B1
gown.def: A long, formal dress worn for special occasions or professional purposes.
gown.ex: The bride wore a beautiful white gown at her wedding ceremony.

grab.label: CEFR B1
grab.def: To take hold of something quickly and firmly with your hand.
grab.ex: She grabbed her coat and ran out of the house to catch the bus.

grace.label: CEFR A2
grace.def: Beauty and smoothness in the way someone moves or behaves
grace.ex: The dancer moved across the stage with such grace that everyone watched in silence.

graceful.label: CEFR B1
graceful.def: Moving or looking elegant and smooth without awkwardness
graceful.ex: The ballerina's graceful movements impressed everyone in the audience.

gracefulness.label: CEFR B2
gracefulness.def: The quality of moving or behaving with smooth, controlled, and elegant movements.
gracefulness.ex: The dancer's gracefulness captivated the audience as she glided across the stage.

grade.label: CEFR B2
grade.def: To assess and assign a mark or score to work or performance based on quality or achievement.
grade.ex: The teacher graded all the exam papers over the weekend and returned them on Monday.

gradual.label: CEFR B2
gradual.def: Happening or changing slowly over a period of time, not suddenly or quickly.
gradual.ex: The gradual increase in temperatures over the past decade has raised concerns among climate scientists.

gradually.label: CEFR A2
gradually.def: Slowly and by small amounts over time, not all at once.
gradually.ex: She gradually got better at playing the piano after practising every day.

graduate.label: CEFR A2
graduate.def: To finish school or university and receive a diploma or degree.
graduate.ex: My sister graduated from university last year and now works as a teacher.

graduation.label: CEFR B1
graduation.def: The ceremony and completion of studies at a school or university, receiving a diploma or degree.
graduation.ex: My graduation from secondary school took place in July, and my family attended the ceremony.

grain.label: CEFR B2
grain.def: Small hard seeds of cereal plants like wheat, rice, or corn, used as food or animal feed.
grain.ex: The baker sources her grain from local farmers who use sustainable agricultural practices.

gram/gramme.label: CEFR A2
gram/gramme.def: A unit of weight used to measure light objects, equal to one thousandth of a kilogram.
gram/gramme.ex: This packet of coffee weighs 250 grammes.

grammar.label: CEFR A1
grammar.def: The rules for how to put words together correctly in a language.
grammar.ex: My teacher helps us learn grammar so we can write correct sentences.

grand.label: CEFR B2
grand.def: Impressive in size, scale, or appearance; magnificent and impressive.
grand.ex: The grand opening of the new museum attracted thousands of visitors from across the country.

grandchild.label: CEFR A2
grandchild.def: A child of your son or daughter.
grandchild.ex: My grandchild is five years old and loves to visit me on weekends.

granddad.label: CEFR A2
granddad.def: Your father's father or your mother's father; an older male family member.
granddad.ex: My granddad taught me how to fish when I was five years old.

granddaughter.label: CEFR A2
granddaughter.def: Your daughter's daughter, or your son's daughter.
granddaughter.ex: My granddaughter is five years old and loves visiting me on weekends.

grandfather.label: CEFR A1
grandfather.def: Your mother's or father's father; the father of your parent.
grandfather.ex: My grandfather tells me stories about when he was young.

grandma.label: CEFR A1
grandma.ex: I visit my grandma every Sunday and she makes delicious cookies.

grandmother.label: CEFR A1
grandmother.def: Your mother's or father's mother; the parent of your parent.
grandmother.ex: My grandmother makes delicious cakes every Sunday.

grandpa.label: CEFR A1
grandpa.def: Your father's or mother's father; the male parent of your parent.
grandpa.ex: My grandpa tells me stories about when he was young.

grandparent.label: CEFR A1
grandparent.def: The mother or father of your mother or father.
grandparent.ex: My grandparents visit us every Sunday for dinner.

grandson.label: CEFR A2
grandson.ex: My grandson is five years old and he loves playing football with his dad.

granny.label: CEFR A2
granny.def: Your mother's or father's mother; your grandmother.
granny.ex: My granny lives in a small cottage by the seaside.

grant.label: CEFR B1
grant.def: To give or allow something that someone has asked for.
grant.ex: The university granted her a scholarship to study engineering.

grape.label: CEFR A1
grape.def: A small, sweet fruit that grows in bunches on vines and is green, red, or purple.
grape.ex: I like to eat grapes as a healthy snack.

graph.label: CEFR B1
graph.def: A diagram showing the relationship between two or more sets of numbers or measurements using lines, bars, or points.
graph.ex: The graph shows how students' test scores improved throughout the academic year.

graphic.label: CEFR B1
graphic.def: Showing or describing something in a very clear and detailed way, often shocking or disturbing.
graphic.ex: The film contained graphic violence that made many viewers uncomfortable.

graphically.label: CEFR B2
graphically.def: In a vivid, detailed way that creates a clear mental image or visual impression.
graphically.ex: The documentary graphically depicted the environmental damage caused by deforestation.

graphics.label: CEFR B1
graphics.def: Pictures, images, or drawings, especially created with a computer or used to show information visually
graphics.ex: The graphics in that video game are incredibly realistic and detailed.

grasp.label: CEFR B2
grasp.def: Understanding of something; the ability to comprehend or know about a subject
grasp.ex: Her grasp of quantum physics impressed the examination board at university.

grass.label: CEFR A1
grass.def: Green plant with thin leaves that covers the ground in parks and gardens.
grass.ex: The children play on the grass in the park.

grateful.label: CEFR A2
grateful.def: Feeling or showing thanks for something good that someone did for you.
grateful.ex: I am very grateful for your help with my homework yesterday.

gratitude.label: CEFR B1
gratitude.def: The feeling of appreciation and thanks for something good someone did or gave you.
gratitude.ex: She expressed her gratitude to the teacher for helping her prepare for the exam.

grave.label: CEFR B1
grave.def: A hole in the ground where a dead person is buried.
grave.ex: The family visited the grave every Sunday to leave flowers for their grandmother.

gravestone.label: CEFR B2
gravestone.def: A stone monument placed on a grave to mark where a dead person is buried.
gravestone.ex: The old gravestone in the churchyard had names and dates carved into it, dating back to the 1800s.

gravitation.label: CEFR B2
gravitation.def: The natural force that pulls objects towards each other, especially towards Earth's centre.
gravitation.ex: Newton's law of gravitation explains why planets orbit the Sun in predictable paths.

gravy.label: CEFR B1
gravy.def: A thick liquid sauce made from meat juices and flour, served with hot food.
gravy.ex: She poured the hot gravy over the mashed potatoes and roasted chicken.

gray/grey.label: CEFR A1
gray/grey.def: A colour between black and white, like the colour of rain clouds.
gray/grey.ex: The sky was grey this morning, so I took an umbrella.

great.label: CEFR A1
great.def: Very good or impressive; of high quality or importance.
great.ex: She is a great teacher and her students love her lessons.

greatly.label: CEFR A2
greatly.def: Very much; to a large degree or extent.
greatly.ex: She greatly enjoyed her holiday in Spain last summer.

greatness.label: CEFR B2
greatness.def: The quality of being exceptionally skilled, important, or impressive in a significant way.
greatness.ex: Historians debate whether Shakespeare's greatness stems from his innovative language or his universal understanding of human nature.

greedy.label: CEFR A2
greedy.def: Wanting more of something than you need; not willing to share or give.
greedy.ex: The greedy child ate all the biscuits without offering any to his sister.

green.label: CEFR A2
green.def: The colour of grass, leaves, and trees
green.ex: She wore a bright green dress to the party.

greenhouse.label: CEFR B1
greenhouse.def: A glass building where plants are grown in controlled warm conditions.
greenhouse.ex: The local garden centre has a large greenhouse where they grow tropical plants year-round.

greet.label: CEFR A1
greet.def: To say hello to someone when you meet them.
greet.ex: She greeted her friends at the school gate this morning.

greeting.label: CEFR B1
greeting.def: A polite word or gesture you use when you meet someone or start communicating with them
greeting.ex: She gave a friendly greeting when her colleague arrived at the office.

grief.label: CEFR B2
grief.def: Deep sadness and emotional pain caused by the loss of someone or something important.
grief.ex: After her mother's death, she experienced intense grief that lasted many months.

grieve.label: CEFR B2
grieve.def: To feel deep sadness and emotional pain, especially after losing someone close to you.
grieve.ex: She took time away from work to grieve the loss of her mother.

grill.label: CEFR B1
grill.def: To cook food over or under direct heat, usually over a fire or on a metal rack.
grill.ex: We're planning to grill some chicken and vegetables for the barbecue this Saturday.

grilled.label: CEFR A2
grilled.def: Cooked using direct heat from below or above, usually on a metal frame
grilled.ex: We had grilled chicken and vegetables for dinner last night.

grim.label: CEFR B2
grim.def: Dark, depressing, or suggesting something unpleasant or difficult ahead
grim.ex: The grim reality of climate change has finally prompted governments to take action.

grin.label: CEFR B2
grin.def: A wide smile showing teeth, often expressing happiness or amusement.
grin.ex: She broke into a broad grin when she saw her old friend at the airport.

grip.label: CEFR B2
grip.def: To hold something firmly with your hand or hands, or to have a strong effect on someone's attention.
grip.ex: The actor's powerful performance gripped the entire audience from the opening scene.

grocery store.label: CEFR A2
grocery store.def: A shop where you buy food and things for your home.
grocery store.ex: I go to the grocery store every Saturday to buy milk, bread, and vegetables.

groom.label: CEFR B1
groom.def: A man who is getting married or has just married someone.
groom.ex: The groom waited nervously at the altar as his bride walked down the aisle.

gross.label: CEFR B2
gross.def: Extremely unpleasant or offensive; morally or physically repulsive and unacceptable
gross.ex: The hygiene conditions in that restaurant were absolutely gross, and it was shut down by health inspectors.

ground.label: CEFR A1
ground.def: The surface of the earth that we walk on.
ground.ex: The shop is on the ground floor, near the street.

ground floor.label: CEFR B1
ground floor.def: The level of a building at street level, with no steps down from outside.
ground floor.ex: The café is located on the ground floor of the shopping centre, so it's easy to access.

group.label: CEFR B1
group.def: To gather or arrange people or things together into a collection.
group.ex: The teacher grouped the students by age to make reading activities easier.

grouping.label: CEFR B1
grouping.def: A collection of people or things arranged or classified together based on shared characteristics
grouping.ex: The teacher created different groupings of students based on their reading level.

grow.label: CEFR A1
grow.ex: Plants grow when you give them water and sunlight.

growing.label: CEFR B2
growing.def: Increasing in size, amount, or importance over time.
growing.ex: There is a growing concern among scientists about the effects of climate change on polar regions.

grown-up.label: CEFR B2
grown-up.def: An adult person, especially one with responsibility and maturity.
grown-up.ex: The grown-ups decided to have their conversation in the other room while the children played.

growth.label: CEFR B1
growth.def: When something becomes larger, stronger, or more developed over time.
growth.ex: The company's growth has been impressive, with sales increasing by 30% this year.

guard.label: CEFR B2
guard.def: To protect someone or something from harm, danger, or unwanted access.
guard.ex: Security guards were stationed at the entrance to guard against unauthorised entry to the building.

guerrilla.label: CEFR B2
guerrilla.def: A member of a small armed group fighting against an official army or government using surprise attacks.
guerrilla.ex: The news report described how guerrilla fighters used unconventional tactics to resist the occupying forces.

guess.label: CEFR A1
guess.def: To give an answer when you are not sure if it is correct.
guess.ex: I don't know the answer, so I will guess.

guest.label: CEFR A1
guest.def: A person who is invited to visit someone's home or attend an event.
guest.ex: We have guests coming to dinner tonight.

guidance.label: CEFR B1
guidance.def: Help or advice given by someone more experienced to direct a person's actions or decisions.
guidance.ex: The teacher provided guidance on how to write a good essay for the exam.

guide.label: CEFR B1
guide.def: To lead or direct someone by showing them the way or giving advice.
guide.ex: The tour guide guided us through the ancient ruins, explaining the history of each building.

guidebook.label: CEFR A2
guidebook.def: A book that gives information and advice about a place or how to do something
guidebook.ex: She bought a guidebook before travelling to Italy so she could learn about the best museums and restaurants.

guideline.label: CEFR B2
guideline.def: A general rule or principle intended to advise on how something should be done.
guideline.ex: The government released new guidelines on remote working to help companies protect their employees' wellbeing.

guilt.label: CEFR B1
guilt.def: A feeling of shame and responsibility for doing something wrong or bad.
guilt.ex: The suspect admitted his guilt in court after the evidence was presented to the judge.

guilty.label: CEFR B1
guilty.def: Feeling responsible for doing something wrong or illegal
guilty.ex: She felt guilty after shouting at her friend, so she apologised the next day.

guitar.label: CEFR A1
guitar.def: A musical instrument with strings that you play with your fingers or a pick.
guitar.ex: He plays the guitar every evening in his bedroom.

guitarist.label: CEFR B1
guitarist.def: A person who plays the guitar, either professionally or as a hobby.
guitarist.ex: The guitarist from the band performed an impressive solo during the concert last night.

gulp.label: CEFR B2
gulp.def: A large amount of liquid swallowed quickly in one action.
gulp.ex: He took a gulp of water after finishing his run.

gum.label: CEFR B2
gum.def: A sticky substance that remains on the teeth or gums after chewing food, or a sweet product chewed for flavour.
gum.ex: After eating the sticky cake, she had to brush her teeth to remove the gum that had hardened on her molars.

gun.label: CEFR A2
gun.def: A weapon that fires bullets by using gunpowder or explosive force.
gun.ex: The police officer carried a gun to protect people in the city.

gunshot.label: CEFR B2
gunshot.def: The sound or act of firing a gun, or a wound caused by a bullet from a gun.
gunshot.ex: The gunshot wound required immediate surgical intervention at the hospital.

guts.label: CEFR B2
guts.def: The internal organs in your stomach area; also means courage or determination to do something difficult.
guts.ex: She had the guts to quit her job and start her own business, despite the financial risks.

guy.label: CEFR A1
guy.def: A man or boy; a person, usually male.
guy.ex: That guy over there is my friend Tom.

gym.label: CEFR B1
gym.def: A building or room with equipment for physical exercise and fitness activities.
gym.ex: I go to the gym three times a week to lift weights and use the treadmill.

gymnastic.label: CEFR B2
gymnastic.def: Relating to physical exercises that develop strength, balance, and flexibility using the body or equipment.
gymnastic.ex: The gymnastic routines performed by the athletes at the Olympic Games demonstrated years of intensive training and remarkable body control.

gymnastics.label: CEFR B1
gymnastics.def: Sport involving physical exercises and movements on equipment like bars, beams, and mats.
gymnastics.ex: Maria trains at gymnastics three times a week to improve her balance and strength.

habit.label: CEFR A1
habit.def: Something you do regularly and automatically, almost without thinking.
habit.ex: I have a habit of drinking coffee every morning before work.

habitant.label: CEFR B2
habitant.def: A person who lives in or occupies a particular place or region.
habitant.ex: The habitants of the remote mountain village had maintained their traditional customs for centuries.

habitat.label: CEFR B1
habitat.def: The natural environment where a plant or animal normally lives and grows.
habitat.ex: The rainforest is the habitat of many endangered species that we study in biology class.

hail.label: CEFR B2
hail.def: To greet or acclaim someone or something enthusiastically and with approval
hail.ex: Critics hailed the director's latest film as a masterpiece of contemporary cinema.

hair.label: CEFR A1
hair.def: Thin threads growing from your head and body

haircut.label: CEFR A1
haircut.def: When someone cuts your hair shorter.
haircut.ex: I got a new haircut yesterday and it looks really nice.

hairdresser.label: CEFR B1
hairdresser.def: A person whose job is to cut, style, and treat hair in a salon.
hairdresser.ex: My hairdresser suggested a shorter cut that would be easier to maintain.

hairdryer.label: CEFR B1
hairdryer.def: An electric device that blows hot air to dry wet hair quickly
hairdryer.ex: She used the hairdryer after her shower to style her hair.

hairy.label: CEFR B2
hairy.def: Covered with a lot of hair; or involving difficulty and risk
hairy.ex: The negotiations became increasingly hairy as both sides refused to compromise on key issues.

half.label: CEFR A1
half.def: One of two equal parts of something divided into two.
half.ex: I ate half of my apple and gave the other half to my friend.

half-price.label: CEFR A2
half-price.def: At a cost that is fifty percent less than the usual price
half-price.ex: The shop is selling winter coats half-price this week, so I bought one for £30 instead of £60.

hall.label: CEFR A1
hall.def: A large room or space in a building where people gather or walk through.
hall.ex: We walked through the shopping hall looking at all the different shops.

ham.label: CEFR A2
ham.def: Meat from a pig's leg, usually preserved with salt and often smoked.
ham.ex: She made sandwiches with ham and cheese for lunch.

hamburger.label: CEFR A1
hamburger.def: A hot food made of cooked meat between two pieces of bread.
hamburger.ex: I ate a hamburger and chips for lunch.

hammer.label: CEFR B2
hammer.def: To hit something repeatedly with force, or to work hard to achieve a goal despite difficulties.
hammer.ex: The team hammered out a new agreement after weeks of negotiation.

hand.label: CEFR A2
hand.def: To give something to someone by passing it to them with your hand.
hand.ex: Please hand me that pen from your desk.

handbag.label: CEFR A2
handbag.def: A bag that women carry to hold personal items like money, keys, and phone
handbag.ex: She put her wallet and phone in her handbag before leaving the house.

handball.label: CEFR B1
handball.def: A sport where two teams throw a ball into a goal using their hands.
handball.ex: She joined the handball team at her school and now practises three times a week.

hand-held.label: CEFR B1
hand-held.def: Small and light enough to carry and use in your hands
hand-held.ex: The nurse used a hand-held device to measure the patient's blood pressure.

handicap.label: CEFR B2
handicap.def: A physical or mental condition that makes certain activities more difficult for a person.
handicap.ex: Despite having a hearing handicap, she became a successful architect through determination and modern technology.

handicapped.label: CEFR A2
handicapped.def: Unable to do some things easily because of a physical or mental condition.
handicapped.ex: My grandfather is handicapped and uses a wheelchair to move around.

handicraft.label: CEFR B2
handicraft.def: Skilled activity making decorative or useful items by hand, using traditional techniques and artistic ability.
handicraft.ex: The village is known for its traditional handicrafts, particularly hand-woven textiles that are exported worldwide.

handkerchief.label: CEFR B1
handkerchief.def: A small piece of cloth used to wipe your nose or face, often kept in a pocket or bag
handkerchief.ex: She pulled a handkerchief from her bag to dry her eyes after the sad film.

handle.label: CEFR B1
handle.def: To deal with something, manage a situation, or touch something with your hands.
handle.ex: She handled the difficult customer complaint professionally and found a solution.

handout.label: CEFR B2
handout.def: Printed material or money given free to people, usually for information or assistance purposes
handout.ex: The professor distributed a handout summarising the key points from yesterday's lecture.

handrail.label: CEFR B2
handrail.def: A rail fixed to a wall or staircase that people hold for support and safety.
handrail.ex: She gripped the handrail tightly as she descended the steep metal staircase into the underground station.

handshake.label: CEFR B1
handshake.def: A greeting where two people clasp each other's right hand firmly as a sign of agreement or respect.
handshake.ex: After the business meeting, the two managers exchanged a firm handshake to confirm their partnership.

handsome.label: CEFR A1
handsome.def: Attractive and pleasant to look at, usually used for men or boys.
handsome.ex: The handsome actor was very popular with the audience.

handwriting.label: CEFR B1
handwriting.def: The style of writing by hand that is personal and unique to each person.
handwriting.ex: The teacher marked the student's work but said her handwriting was difficult to read because the letters were too small.

handy.label: CEFR B1
handy.def: Useful and easy to use; conveniently located or available
handy.ex: A small dictionary is handy to keep on your desk when you're learning English.

hang.label: CEFR B1
hang.def: To attach or hold something from above so it stays suspended in the air.
hang.ex: I need to hang my coat on the hook by the door when I arrive home.

hanging.label: CEFR B1
hanging.def: Something that is suspended or attached at the top, with the rest extending downward.
hanging.ex: The hanging lamp cast a soft light over the dinner table.

happily.label: CEFR A2
happily.def: In a way that shows you are pleased and content.
happily.ex: The children played happily in the park all afternoon.

happiness.label: CEFR A2
happiness.def: A feeling of joy and contentment when things are good in your life.
happiness.ex: She felt great happiness when her family arrived for a surprise visit.

happy.label: CEFR A1
happy.def: Feeling pleased and enjoying yourself; not sad.
happy.ex: I am happy when I play with my friends.

harbor/harbour.label: CEFR B1
harbor/harbour.def: A sheltered area of water where ships can stop and stay safely
harbor/harbour.ex: The fishing boats returned to the harbour at the end of the day to sell their catch.

hard.label: CEFR A1
hard.def: With a lot of effort or force; not gently or lightly.
hard.ex: She worked hard to pass her English test.

hard drive.label: CEFR B2
hard drive.def: A computer component that stores files, programs, and operating system data permanently using magnetic storage.
hard drive.ex: She backed up all her important documents to an external hard drive before replacing her laptop.

hardly.label: CEFR A2
hardly.ex: I hardly slept last night because I was too excited about my trip.

hardship.label: CEFR B1
hardship.def: A difficult or painful situation that causes suffering or struggle
hardship.ex: Many families faced serious hardship during the economic crisis and struggled to pay their bills.

hardware.label: CEFR B2
hardware.def: Physical computer equipment such as machines, devices, and components that you can touch.
hardware.ex: The company invested in new server hardware to improve its network performance and data processing capacity.

hard-working.label: CEFR B1
hard-working.def: Someone who puts a lot of effort and time into their work or tasks.
hard-working.ex: She is a hard-working student who studies every evening to improve her grades.

harm.label: CEFR A2
harm.def: To cause pain, injury, or damage to someone or something.

harmful.label: CEFR A2
harmful.def: Causing damage or injury to someone or something.
harmful.ex: Smoking is harmful to your health.

harmless.label: CEFR B2
harmless.def: Not able to cause injury, damage, or negative effects to anyone or anything.
harmless.ex: The scientist assured the committee that the new additive was completely harmless to human health.

harmony.label: CEFR A2
harmony.def: When things work well together without disagreement or conflict.
harmony.ex: The colours in the painting are in perfect harmony with each other.

harness.label: CEFR B1
harness.def: A set of straps worn around the body to hold something in place or keep someone safe.
harness.ex: The construction worker wore a safety harness before climbing up the tall building.

harrow.label: CEFR B2
harrow.def: A farming tool with sharp points or blades, dragged over soil to break it up and prepare it for planting.
harrow.ex: The farmer used a harrow to break up the clumps of earth before sowing the seeds in spring.

harvest.label: CEFR A2
harvest.def: The time when crops are gathered from the fields, or the crops that are gathered.
harvest.ex: The farmers worked hard during the harvest to collect all the wheat before winter.

haste.label: CEFR B2
haste.def: Excessive speed or hurried action, often without proper thought or care
haste.ex: In her haste to finish the project before the deadline, she overlooked several important details.

hastily.label: CEFR B2
hastily.def: Done quickly and without careful thought or planning.
hastily.ex: She hastily packed her bag and left for the airport without checking her passport.

hasty.label: CEFR B1
hasty.def: Done quickly without careful thought or planning
hasty.ex: He made a hasty decision to quit his job without looking for another one first.

hat.label: CEFR A1
hat.def: A piece of clothing you wear on your head to keep warm or look nice.
hat.ex: She wears a red hat when it is cold outside.

hatch.label: CEFR B1
hatch.def: An opening in a floor, roof, or wall with a door or cover that can be opened and closed
hatch.ex: The sailor climbed through the hatch to go down to the lower deck of the ship.

hatching.label: CEFR B2
hatching.def: Fine parallel lines drawn close together to create shading or texture in drawings or prints.
hatching.ex: The artist used cross-hatching to add depth and shadow to the portrait's face.

hate.label: CEFR A2
hate.def: To dislike something or someone very strongly.
hate.ex: I hate getting up early in the morning.

hatred.label: CEFR B1
hatred.def: A very strong feeling of dislike or anger towards someone or something.
hatred.ex: The hatred between the two families had lasted for generations, making peace seem impossible.

haunt.label: CEFR B2
haunt.def: To visit someone as a ghost, or to remain persistently in someone's thoughts or memory.
haunt.ex: The memory of his childhood trauma continued to haunt him years after the accident.

haunting.label: CEFR B2
haunting.def: Lingering in the mind persistently, often creating a feeling of sadness or unease.
haunting.ex: The haunting melody from the film stayed with her for weeks after she watched it.

have to.label: CEFR A1
have to.def: Must do something; it is necessary or required
have to.ex: I have to go to school every day.

haven.label: CEFR B1
haven.def: A place where someone feels safe, comfortable, or can escape from problems
haven.ex: The shopping centre became a haven for teenagers on rainy weekends.

hay.label: CEFR B2
hay.def: Dried grass used as animal feed, typically stored in bales or stacks.
hay.ex: The farmer stored bales of hay in the barn to feed the cattle during winter.

hazard.label: CEFR B1
hazard.def: A danger or risk that could cause harm or damage.
hazard.ex: Wet floors are a hazard in the kitchen, so we should clean them quickly.

hazardous.label: CEFR B2
hazardous.def: Dangerous and likely to cause harm or injury.
hazardous.ex: Workers handling hazardous chemicals must wear protective equipment at all times.

head.label: CEFR A2
head.def: To move towards a particular direction or place.
head.ex: After school, I headed home to do my homework.

headache.label: CEFR A1
headache.def: Pain in your head that makes you feel uncomfortable or sick
headache.ex: I had a terrible headache after the long flight, so I took medicine and rested.

headline.label: CEFR B1
headline.def: The title of a news story, printed in large letters at the top of an article
headline.ex: The headline in today's newspaper announced that the government had approved a new education policy.

headphone.label: CEFR A2
headphone.def: Small speakers you wear over your ears to listen to music or audio privately
headphone.ex: She put on her headphones and listened to music while doing her homework.

headquarters.label: CEFR B2
headquarters.def: The main office or centre of control for an organisation, company, or government.
headquarters.ex: The company's headquarters are located in the city centre, where all major decisions are made.

headteacher.label: CEFR A2
headteacher.def: The person in charge of a school who manages teachers and students.
headteacher.ex: The headteacher spoke to all the students about the new school rules.

heal.label: CEFR B1
heal.def: To become healthy again or make someone healthy again after illness or injury.
heal.ex: The wound on her arm took three weeks to heal completely.

healing.label: CEFR B1
healing.def: Having the power to help someone recover from physical or emotional pain.
healing.ex: After her divorce, she found that spending time in nature was a healing experience that helped her feel better.

health.label: CEFR A1
health.def: The condition of your body and mind; being well and not sick.
health.ex: I exercise every day to maintain good health.

health care.label: CEFR B2
health care.def: Services and systems that prevent, diagnose, and treat illness or injury in people.
health care.ex: The government has invested significantly in improving healthcare access for rural communities.

healthy.label: CEFR A1
healthy.def: In good physical condition; not sick.
healthy.ex: Eating vegetables and fruits is healthy for your body.

heap.label: CEFR B2
heap.def: To pile things up in a large, untidy mass or accumulation.
heap.ex: She heaped praise on the young musician's outstanding performance at the concert.

hear.label: CEFR A1
hear.def: To receive sound with your ears.
hear.ex: I can hear birds singing outside my window.

hearing.label: CEFR B1
hearing.def: The ability to perceive sound, or an official meeting where someone presents their case
hearing.ex: She has excellent hearing and can detect sounds that others miss, or The court scheduled a hearing to listen to both sides of the dispute.

heart.label: CEFR A1
heart.def: The organ inside your chest that pumps blood around your body.
heart.ex: The doctor listened to my heart with a stethoscope.

heart attack.label: CEFR B1
heart attack.def: A sudden, serious medical emergency when blood flow to the heart stops, causing severe chest pain.
heart attack.ex: His father had a heart attack last year, but he recovered after surgery and rehabilitation.

heartbreaking.label: CEFR B2
heartbreaking.def: Causing deep sadness or emotional pain; extremely disappointing or upsetting.
heartbreaking.ex: The heartbreaking decision to close the local school devastated the community that had relied on it for generations.

hearted.label: CEFR B1
hearted.def: Having a particular kind of feelings or character, usually shown by a compound word like kind-hearted or cold-hearted.
hearted.ex: She is a warm-hearted person who always helps her neighbours with their problems.

heartwarming/heart-warming.label: CEFR B2
heartwarming/heart-warming.def: Causing feelings of warmth, happiness, and hope by showing human kindness or goodness
heartwarming/heart-warming.ex: The heartwarming story of the elderly man reunited with his lost dog after twenty years moved audiences to tears.

hearty.label: CEFR B1
hearty.def: Warm, friendly, and showing genuine feeling or enthusiasm
hearty.ex: She gave him a hearty welcome when he arrived at her house after many years.

heat.label: CEFR B1
heat.def: To make something hotter by applying warmth or energy to it.
heat.ex: Please heat the water to 100 degrees before adding the pasta.

heater.label: CEFR B1
heater.def: A device that produces heat to warm a room or building.
heater.ex: We turned on the heater in the living room because it was very cold this morning.

heating.label: CEFR A2
heating.def: A system that makes a building or room warm when it is cold
heating.ex: We turned on the heating because the house was very cold in winter.

heaven.label: CEFR B1
heaven.def: In religion, the place where God lives and where good people go after death.
heaven.ex: Many people believe that heaven is a peaceful place of eternal happiness.

heavenly.label: CEFR B1
heavenly.def: Extremely beautiful, pleasant, or enjoyable; like heaven or the sky
heavenly.ex: The heavenly aroma of fresh bread baking in the oven made everyone hungry.

heavily.label: CEFR A2
heavily.def: In a way that weighs a lot or has great force or intensity.
heavily.ex: It was raining heavily, so we decided to stay inside.

heavy.label: CEFR A1
heavy.def: Weighing a lot; hard to lift or carry
heavy.ex: This suitcase is very heavy, so I need help carrying it.

hedge.label: CEFR B2
hedge.def: A row of bushes or small trees planted close together to form a boundary or barrier.
hedge.ex: The farmer planted a thick hedge around his property to keep livestock from straying onto the neighbouring land.

heel.label: CEFR B1
heel.def: The raised part at the back of a shoe or boot that supports your foot.
heel.ex: She wore black leather boots with high heels to the party.

height.label: CEFR B1
height.def: The measurement of how tall something or someone is from bottom to top
height.ex: The height of the building is 150 metres, making it one of the tallest in the city.

helicopter.label: CEFR B2
helicopter.def: An aircraft with rotating blades on top that allow it to rise vertically and hover in the air.
helicopter.ex: The rescue team used a helicopter to reach the stranded climbers on the mountain.

hell.label: CEFR B2
hell.def: In Christian and other religious beliefs, the place where evil people suffer punishment after death.
hell.ex: Many religious texts describe hell as a place of eternal fire and torment for those who reject God.

hello.label: CEFR A1
hello.def: A greeting you say when you meet someone or answer the phone.
hello.ex: She said hello to her friend at the bus stop.

helmet.label: CEFR B1
helmet.def: A hard hat worn to protect your head from injury during sports or work.
helmet.ex: The cyclist always wears a helmet when riding on busy roads.

help.label: CEFR A1
help.def: To make something easier for someone or do something for them.
help.ex: Can you help me carry these bags?

helper.label: CEFR B2
helper.def: A person who assists or supports someone in completing a task or achieving a goal.
helper.ex: She hired a helper to manage the office while she was away on maternity leave.

helpful.label: CEFR A2
helpful.def: Willing to help others; doing something that makes things easier or better.
helpful.ex: My teacher was very helpful when I didn't understand the maths problem.

helpless.label: CEFR B1
helpless.def: Unable to do anything to help yourself or improve a bad situation.
helpless.ex: When the car broke down in the middle of nowhere, Sarah felt completely helpless without her mobile phone.

hemp.label: CEFR B2
hemp.def: A tough plant fibre used to make rope, cloth, and other materials; also refers to the plant itself.
hemp.ex: The farmer cultivated hemp for its strong fibres, which were processed into durable rope for maritime use.

herb.label: CEFR B2
herb.def: A plant used in cooking to add flavour, or medicinally to treat illness.
herb.ex: The chef used fresh basil and other herbs to prepare the Italian pasta dish.

herd.label: CEFR B1
herd.def: A large group of animals of the same type that live and move together.
herd.ex: The farmer watched over a herd of cattle grazing in the meadow.

here.label: CEFR A1
here.def: In this place or at this location
here.ex: I am here at the school now.

heritage.label: CEFR B2
heritage.def: Important cultural, historical, or natural features passed down from previous generations to the present.
heritage.ex: The ancient temples are part of the country's architectural heritage and attract millions of visitors each year.

hero.label: CEFR A2
hero.def: A person who is brave and does something very good or important to help others.
hero.ex: The firefighter was a hero because she saved the family from the burning house.

hesitate.label: CEFR B1
hesitate.def: To pause or delay before doing something because you are uncertain or nervous.
hesitate.ex: She hesitated for a moment before answering the difficult question.

hesitation.label: CEFR B2
hesitation.def: A pause or delay caused by uncertainty about what to do or say.
hesitation.ex: After a moment of hesitation, she decided to accept the job offer despite her doubts.

hidden.label: CEFR B1
hidden.def: Not able to be seen or found; kept secret or concealed
hidden.ex: The hidden camera recorded everything that happened in the room.

hide.label: CEFR A1
hide.def: To put something where people cannot see it or find it
hide.ex: The children hide their toys under the bed so their parents cannot find them.

high.label: CEFR A2
high.def: At or to a great distance above the ground or a surface
high.ex: The bird flew high in the sky above the trees.

high school.label: CEFR A2
high school.def: A school for teenagers, usually from ages 14 to 18, where students study before university.
high school.ex: My sister goes to high school in the centre of town and takes the bus every morning.

highlight.label: CEFR B1
highlight.def: To mark or emphasize something to make it stand out or seem important.
highlight.ex: The teacher highlighted the key points in the textbook with a yellow marker.

highly.label: CEFR B1
highly.def: Very much; to a great degree or extent.
highly.ex: She is a highly skilled surgeon who has trained for many years.

high-tech/hi-tech.label: CEFR B2
high-tech/hi-tech.def: Using the most recent and advanced technology or equipment available.
high-tech/hi-tech.ex: The hospital invested in high-tech medical equipment to improve diagnostic accuracy and patient care.

highway.label: CEFR A2
highway.def: A wide, important road that connects cities and towns
highway.ex: We drove on the highway for two hours to reach the next city.

hijack.label: CEFR B2
hijack.def: To seize control of a vehicle or situation by force, often for a criminal purpose.
hijack.ex: Terrorists attempted to hijack the aircraft, but security personnel managed to prevent the incident.

hike.label: CEFR A2
hike.def: A long walk in the countryside or mountains for exercise or pleasure.
hike.ex: We went on a hike through the forest last weekend and saw beautiful views.

hiking.label: CEFR A2
hiking.def: Walking a long distance in nature or mountains for exercise or pleasure.
hiking.ex: We went hiking in the mountains last weekend and saw beautiful views.

hilarious.label: CEFR B2
hilarious.def: Extremely funny and causing uncontrollable laughter.
hilarious.ex: The comedian's hilarious jokes about everyday life had the entire audience laughing throughout the show.

hill.label: CEFR A1
hill.def: A raised area of land that is higher than the flat ground around it.
hill.ex: The shop is at the top of the hill, so we have to walk up some steps.

hint.label: CEFR B2
hint.def: A subtle suggestion or clue that helps someone understand or guess something without stating it directly
hint.ex: The teacher gave us a hint about the answer, but we still had to work out the problem ourselves.

hip.label: CEFR B1
hip.def: The area on the side of your body below your waist where your leg joins your body.
hip.ex: She put her hands on her hips and looked at him with frustration.

hip hop/hip-hop/hiphop.label: CEFR A2
hip hop/hip-hop/hiphop.def: A style of popular music with spoken words and strong beats, originated in the 1970s.
hip hop/hip-hop/hiphop.ex: My brother loves hip hop music and listens to it every day on his phone.

hire.label: CEFR B1
hire.def: To employ someone or pay them to work for you.
hire.ex: The company decided to hire five new engineers for their technology department.

historian.label: CEFR B1
historian.def: A person who studies and writes about past events and their causes and effects.
historian.ex: The historian discovered new documents that changed our understanding of the revolution.

historic.label: CEFR B1
historic.def: Important or famous because of its connection to past events
historic.ex: The historic castle attracts thousands of tourists each year.

historical.label: CEFR B1
historical.def: Connected with or relating to events and people from the past.
historical.ex: The museum displays historical artefacts from ancient Rome, including coins and pottery.

history.label: CEFR A1
history.def: The study of important events and people from the past.
history.ex: My favourite subject at school is history because I like learning about ancient Egypt.

hit.label: CEFR A2
hit.def: To strike or touch something or someone with force using your hand or an object
hit.ex: The baseball player hit the ball over the fence.

hiv.label: CEFR B1
hiv.def: A virus that damages the body's ability to fight infections and diseases.
hiv.ex: Many countries now provide free HIV testing at clinics and hospitals.

hobby.label: CEFR A1
hobby.def: Activity you do for fun in your free time.
hobby.ex: My hobby is collecting stamps from different countries.

hockey.label: CEFR A2
hockey.def: A sport played on ice where two teams hit a puck with sticks to score goals
hockey.ex: My brother plays hockey on the school team and has matches every weekend.

hold.label: CEFR A2
hold.def: To have something in your hands or arms; to keep something in a position
hold.ex: Can you hold my bag while I tie my shoes?

holder.label: CEFR B2
holder.def: A person or organisation that legally possesses or owns something, such as a document, title, or qualification.
holder.ex: As the holder of a valid passport, she was able to travel freely throughout Europe.

hole.label: CEFR A1
hole.def: An empty space or opening in something solid.
hole.ex: There is a hole in my shoe, so water comes in when it rains.

holiday.label: CEFR A1
holiday.def: A day or period when you do not work and can rest or travel for fun.
holiday.ex: We went to the beach for our summer holiday.

hollow.label: CEFR B2
hollow.def: Empty inside; having a space or cavity within rather than being solid throughout
hollow.ex: The archaeologists discovered a hollow log that had served as a shelter for ancient peoples.

holocaust.label: CEFR B2
holocaust.def: Destruction or slaughter on a massive scale, especially the Nazi genocide of European Jews during World War II.
holocaust.ex: The novel explores the psychological impact of the Holocaust on survivors and their descendants.

holy.label: CEFR B1
holy.def: Connected to religion or God; deserving respect because of spiritual importance
holy.ex: Many pilgrims travel to holy sites in Jerusalem every year.

home.label: CEFR A1
home.def: The place where you live with your family.
home.ex: I go home after school every day.

homeless.label: CEFR B1
homeless.def: Without a permanent place to live; having nowhere safe to sleep at night.
homeless.ex: The charity helps homeless people find affordable housing and support services.

hometown.label: CEFR A1
hometown.def: The city or town where you were born or grew up.
hometown.ex: My hometown is Manchester, and I miss it when I travel.

homeward.label: CEFR B2
homeward.def: Moving towards or directed back to one's home.
homeward.ex: After three months working abroad, she boarded the homeward flight with great anticipation.

homework.label: CEFR A1
homework.def: School work that students must do at home after school or in the evening.
homework.ex: I have a lot of homework tonight, so I cannot play outside.

hone.label: CEFR B2
hone.def: A fine-grained stone used to sharpen tools and blades to a keen edge.
hone.ex: The craftsman regularly used a whetstone hone to maintain his chisels in perfect condition.

honest.label: CEFR B1
honest.def: Telling the truth and not trying to trick or deceive anyone
honest.ex: She gave an honest answer about her mistakes at work instead of making excuses.

honestly.label: CEFR B1
honestly.def: In a truthful way; saying what you really think or believe without lying.
honestly.ex: I honestly think that film was better than the first one, even though many people disagreed.

honesty.label: CEFR B1
honesty.def: The quality of being truthful and not lying or cheating.
honesty.ex: She appreciated his honesty when he admitted he had made a mistake at work.

honey.label: CEFR A2
honey.def: Sweet, thick liquid made by bees from flower nectar, used as food.
honey.ex: She put honey in her tea to make it sweet.

honeymoon.label: CEFR B1
honeymoon.def: A holiday taken by a newly married couple to celebrate their wedding.
honeymoon.ex: After the wedding ceremony, they flew to Greece for their honeymoon.

honor/honour.label: CEFR B2
honor/honour.def: To show special respect or recognition for someone or something they have done.
honor/honour.ex: The university honoured her outstanding contributions to medical research with an honorary doctorate.

honorable/honourable.label: CEFR B2
honorable/honourable.def: Worthy of respect and admiration because of high moral standards and honest behaviour.
honorable/honourable.ex: She pursued an honourable career in public service, always putting citizens' needs before personal gain.

hood.label: CEFR B2
hood.def: A covering for the head and neck, often part of a coat or worn separately for protection or warmth
hood.ex: She pulled her hood up over her head as the rain began to fall heavily.

hook.label: CEFR B2
hook.def: To catch something with a curved device; to fasten or attach something securely.
hook.ex: The fisherman hooked a large salmon and reeled it in carefully.

hop.label: CEFR B1
hop.def: A short jump or leap, especially on one foot or made by a small animal
hop.ex: The rabbit moved across the garden in a series of quick hops.

hope.label: CEFR B1
hope.def: To want something to happen and believe it is possible.
hope.ex: I hope the weather will be good for our picnic next weekend.

hopeful.label: CEFR B1
hopeful.def: Feeling optimistic that something good will happen in the future
hopeful.ex: She felt hopeful about her job interview and believed she would get the position.

hopefully.label: CEFR B1
hopefully.def: It is hoped that something will happen; expressing a wish that something positive will occur
hopefully.ex: Hopefully, the weather will improve tomorrow and we can go to the park.

hopeless.label: CEFR B1
hopeless.def: Without any chance of success or improvement; impossible to fix or solve.
hopeless.ex: After losing 5-0, the team felt hopeless about winning the match.

horizon.label: CEFR B1
horizon.def: The line where the earth or sea appears to meet the sky in the distance.
horizon.ex: As the sun set, the sky turned orange along the horizon.

horn.label: CEFR B1
horn.def: A hard, pointed growth on the head of some animals like cows or sheep.
horn.ex: The bull lowered its horns and charged at the fence.

horrible.label: CEFR B1
horrible.def: Very unpleasant or bad; causing discomfort, fear, or disgust
horrible.ex: The weather was horrible yesterday, so we stayed inside all day.

horrify.label: CEFR B1
horrify.def: To shock or upset someone very much by something extremely unpleasant or disturbing
horrify.ex: The news of the accident horrified everyone in the office, and we all went home early.

horror.label: CEFR A2
horror.def: A very strong feeling of fear or shock caused by something terrible or unpleasant.
horror.ex: She watched the horror film and couldn't sleep that night because she was so scared.

horse.label: CEFR A1
horse.def: A large animal with four legs, a mane, and a tail, often ridden by people.
horse.ex: The girl rode her horse in the countryside.

hospice.label: CEFR B2
hospice.def: A facility providing care and support for people with terminal illnesses, focusing on comfort rather than cure.
hospice.ex: After her cancer diagnosis, she decided to spend her final months at a local hospice where she could be with family and receive palliative care.

hospitable.label: CEFR B2
hospitable.def: Friendly and welcoming to guests; generous in offering food, drinks, and accommodation
hospitable.ex: The hotel staff were exceptionally hospitable, offering us complimentary refreshments and local recommendations throughout our stay.

hospital.label: CEFR A1
hospital.def: A large building where doctors treat sick and injured people.
hospital.ex: My grandmother stayed in the hospital for three days after her operation.

hospitality.label: CEFR B2
hospitality.def: The friendly and generous treatment of guests or visitors with food, drink, and accommodation.
hospitality.ex: The hotel's hospitality made guests feel welcome from the moment they arrived at reception.

hospitalize/hospitalise.label: CEFR B2
hospitalize/hospitalise.def: To admit and keep a patient in a hospital for medical treatment and care.
hospitalize/hospitalise.ex: After the accident, she was hospitalised for three weeks to recover from her injuries.

host.label: CEFR A2
host.def: A person who invites and welcomes guests to their home or event.
host.ex: The host prepared dinner for all the dinner party guests.

hostel.label: CEFR B1
hostel.def: A cheap accommodation where guests share rooms and facilities with other travellers.
hostel.ex: After backpacking across Europe for two months, she stayed in a hostel in Berlin where she made friends from all over the world.

hot.label: CEFR A1
hot.def: Having a high temperature; very warm.
hot.ex: The tea is too hot to drink right now.

hotel.label: CEFR A1
hotel.def: A building where people pay to sleep and eat when travelling
hotel.ex: We stayed at a nice hotel near the beach for our holiday.

hour.label: CEFR A1
hour.def: A period of time equal to sixty minutes.
hour.ex: The class lasts one hour every morning.

hourly.label: CEFR B2
hourly.def: Occurring or calculated for each hour, or paid based on the number of hours worked.
hourly.ex: The factory workers receive their hourly wages every Friday, based on the number of hours they logged.

house.label: CEFR A1
house.def: A building where people live with their family.
house.ex: My house is near the shops in the town.

household.label: CEFR B1
household.def: All the people who live together in one home, or the home and everything in it.
household.ex: My household includes my parents, my sister, and our two dogs.

housekeeper.label: CEFR B2
housekeeper.def: A person employed to manage household tasks such as cleaning, cooking, and laundry.
housekeeper.ex: The wealthy family hired a live-in housekeeper to maintain their large country estate.

housemaster.label: CEFR B2
housemaster.def: A teacher, usually male, who manages a house at a boarding school and acts as a mentor to students living there.
housemaster.ex: My son's housemaster at boarding school has been instrumental in helping him adjust to dormitory life and providing pastoral care.

housewife.label: CEFR B2
housewife.def: A married woman whose primary occupation is managing the household and family.
housewife.ex: She decided to become a housewife after her children were born, though she maintained freelance work from home.

housework.label: CEFR B1
housework.def: Regular cleaning and cooking tasks done to maintain a home
housework.ex: My mother spends several hours each week doing housework, including cooking meals and washing clothes.

hover.label: CEFR B2
hover.def: To remain suspended in the air without moving, or to stay near something waiting uncertainly.
hover.ex: The helicopter hovered above the landing pad whilst the pilot waited for clearance to descend.

how.label: CEFR A1
how.def: In what way or by what method.

however.label: CEFR A2
however.def: used to show a different or opposite idea from what was just said
however.ex: The weather was cold, however we still went to the beach.

hug.label: CEFR A2
hug.def: To hold someone close to your body with your arms to show love or comfort.
hug.ex: She hugged her friend when she arrived at the airport.

huge.label: CEFR B1
huge.def: extremely large in size or amount
huge.ex: The new shopping centre is huge and has over 200 shops inside.

hugely.label: CEFR B2
hugely.def: To a very great extent; extremely or significantly
hugely.ex: The new technology has hugely improved productivity in manufacturing industries across Europe.

human.label: CEFR B1
human.def: A person; a member of the species that can think, speak, and use tools.
human.ex: Every human has the right to education and healthcare.

human rights.label: CEFR B2
human rights.def: Basic freedoms and protections that all people deserve, such as fair treatment and freedom of speech.
human rights.ex: The organisation campaigns to protect human rights in countries where people face political oppression.

humane.label: CEFR B2
humane.def: Showing compassion and concern for people or animals; treating others with dignity and kindness.
humane.ex: The prison reform advocate argued for more humane treatment of inmates, including access to education and healthcare.

humanist.label: CEFR B2
humanist.def: A person who believes in human potential and reason rather than religious faith or tradition.
humanist.ex: The Renaissance humanist studied classical texts to understand human nature and achievement.

humanity.label: CEFR B1
humanity.def: All human beings considered together, or the quality of being kind and sympathetic to others.
humanity.ex: The charity's mission is to improve humanity by providing food and shelter to people in need.

humanize/humanise.label: CEFR B2
humanize/humanise.def: To make something more considerate of human needs, feelings, or values; to treat as human rather than mechanical.
humanize/humanise.ex: The documentary aims to humanise refugees by sharing their personal stories rather than just presenting statistics.

humble.label: CEFR B2
humble.def: Not arrogant; modest about one's abilities or achievements despite possessing talent or status
humble.ex: Despite winning the award, she remained humble and credited her success to her team's hard work.

humbug.label: CEFR B2
humbug.def: Deceptive or dishonest talk intended to trick or mislead people.
humbug.ex: The politician's promises were dismissed as humbug by voters who had heard similar claims before.

humid.label: CEFR B1
humid.def: Containing a lot of moisture in the air; damp and warm.
humid.ex: The weather was so humid that my clothes felt wet even though it wasn't raining.

humiliate.label: CEFR B2
humiliate.def: To make someone feel ashamed or lose respect in front of others.
humiliate.ex: The coach's harsh criticism in front of the entire team humiliated the young player.

humiliating.label: CEFR B2
humiliating.def: Causing someone to feel ashamed or embarrassed in front of others.
humiliating.ex: Losing to a much weaker team was a humiliating experience for the defending champions.

humiliation.label: CEFR B2
humiliation.def: A painful feeling of shame or loss of dignity in front of others.
humiliation.ex: The public humiliation of being criticised by her boss in the meeting left her feeling embarrassed for weeks.

humor/humour.label: CEFR B1
humor/humour.def: The ability to find something funny or make others laugh; comedy or jokes.
humor/humour.ex: Her sense of humour made everyone in the office feel more relaxed during stressful meetings.

humorous.label: CEFR B1
humorous.def: Funny and intended to make people laugh or smile.
humorous.ex: The comedian told humorous stories about his childhood that made the whole audience laugh.

hunger.label: CEFR B1
hunger.def: A strong physical need or desire to eat food.
hunger.ex: The charity works to reduce hunger in developing countries by providing meals to families in need.

hungry.label: CEFR A1
hungry.ex: I am hungry and want to eat lunch now.

hunt.label: CEFR B1
hunt.def: To search for and try to catch or kill wild animals, often for food or sport.
hunt.ex: The lion hunts zebras on the African savanna.

hunter.label: CEFR A2
hunter.def: A person who goes into wild areas to catch or kill animals for food or sport.
hunter.ex: The hunter carried his gun into the forest early in the morning.

hunting.label: CEFR B2
hunting.def: The activity of chasing and killing wild animals for food, sport, or population control.
hunting.ex: The government has introduced new regulations to regulate hunting in national forests.

hurriedly.label: CEFR B1
hurriedly.def: In a quick or rushed manner, without taking time to be careful or thorough.
hurriedly.ex: She hurriedly packed her suitcase before the taxi arrived to take her to the airport.

hurry.label: CEFR A2
hurry.def: To move or do something quickly because you do not have much time.
hurry.ex: We need to hurry to catch the bus before it leaves.

hurt.label: CEFR A1
hurt.def: To cause physical pain or injury to someone or something.
hurt.ex: I fell off my bike and hurt my knee badly.

husband.label: CEFR A1
husband.def: A married man; a woman's partner in marriage.
husband.ex: My husband and I went to the cinema last night.

hush.label: CEFR B2
hush.def: To make someone stop talking or become quiet, often by saying 'hush' or making a gesture.
hush.ex: The teacher hushed the class before the headmaster entered the room.

hut.label: CEFR B1
hut.def: A small, simple building, usually made of basic materials like wood or metal.
hut.ex: We bought hot chocolate from a small hut in the market square.

hybrid.label: CEFR B2
hybrid.def: Combining two different types or elements to create something new and improved.
hybrid.ex: The company's hybrid approach to education combines online learning with in-person classes.

hygiene.label: CEFR B2
hygiene.def: Practices and conditions that promote health by preventing disease and maintaining cleanliness.
hygiene.ex: The school's new hygiene protocol requires students to wash their hands between lessons and after using the toilets.

hyperbole.label: CEFR B2
hyperbole.def: An extreme exaggeration used for emphasis or dramatic effect, not meant to be taken literally.
hyperbole.ex: When she said she'd told him a million times to clean his room, that was pure hyperbole.

hyphen.label: CEFR B2
hyphen.def: A short line connecting two words or parts of a word to show they are related.
hyphen.ex: The phrase 'mother-in-law' requires a hyphen between the words to be written correctly.

ice.label: CEFR A1
ice.def: Frozen water that is cold and hard
ice.ex: I put ice in my drink to keep it cold.

ice cream.label: CEFR A1
ice cream.def: A cold, sweet frozen dessert made from milk, cream, and sugar.
ice cream.ex: On hot days, I like to eat ice cream with my friends at the park.

ice hockey.label: CEFR B1
ice hockey.def: A fast team sport played on ice where players use sticks to hit a puck into the opposite goal.
ice hockey.ex: My brother plays ice hockey every weekend at the local rink during winter.

ice skating.label: CEFR B1
ice skating.def: The sport or activity of moving across frozen water on special boots with blades attached underneath.
ice skating.ex: During winter, many families enjoy ice skating at the outdoor rink in the town centre.

iceman.label: CEFR B2
iceman.def: A person whose job is to deliver or sell ice, especially historically or in specific trades.
iceman.ex: The iceman delivered blocks of ice to homes before electric refrigerators became common in the 1950s.

icon.label: CEFR B2
icon.def: A person or thing that represents or is widely recognised as a symbol of something important.
icon.ex: Marilyn Monroe became a cultural icon whose image remains instantly recognisable decades after her death.

icy.label: CEFR B2
icy.def: Very cold and covered with ice; unfriendly or unwelcoming in manner
icy.ex: The icy conditions on the motorway forced many drivers to slow down considerably.

id.label: CEFR A2
id.def: A document or card that proves who you are
id.ex: You need to show your ID when you check into the hotel.

id card.label: CEFR A2
id card.def: Official document with your name, photo, and personal information that proves who you are.
id card.ex: I need to bring my ID card to the airport when I travel.

idea.label: CEFR A1
idea.def: A thought or plan in your mind.
idea.ex: I have a good idea for a fun game to play with my friends.

ideal.label: CEFR A1
ideal.def: Perfect or exactly what you want; the best possible thing
ideal.ex: Summer is the ideal time to go to the beach because it is warm and sunny.

ideally.label: CEFR B2
ideally.def: In the best possible circumstances or according to what would be perfect
ideally.ex: Ideally, the project should be completed by the end of the month, though we may need more time.

identical.label: CEFR B2
identical.def: Exactly the same in every way; having no differences whatsoever.
identical.ex: The twins wore identical uniforms and were often mistaken for each other.

identification.label: CEFR B2
identification.def: The process of recognising who or what someone or something is, or proof of who you are.
identification.ex: The police officer asked for identification before allowing her to enter the secure area.

identity.label: CEFR B1
identity.def: Who someone is, including their name, character, and the groups they belong to.
identity.ex: Many teenagers struggle with their identity as they grow up and change.

identity card.label: CEFR B1
identity card.def: An official document with your photograph and personal information, used to prove who you are.
identity card.ex: I had to show my identity card at the airport before boarding the plane.

idiom.label: CEFR B1
idiom.def: A phrase or expression whose meaning cannot be understood from the individual words alone.
idiom.ex: The idiom 'raining cats and dogs' means heavy rain, not actual animals falling from the sky.

idiot.label: CEFR B2
idiot.def: A person who lacks intelligence or behaves in a very foolish manner.
idiot.ex: Calling someone an idiot during an argument is disrespectful and unlikely to resolve the disagreement.

idle.label: CEFR B2
idle.def: Not working or being used; doing nothing or lacking purpose or occupation
idle.ex: The factory's idle machinery will be sold off as the company relocates to a cheaper location.

idol.label: CEFR B1
idol.def: A person or thing that is loved and admired very much by many people.
idol.ex: Many teenagers consider famous film stars their idols and try to copy their style.

idolize/idolise.label: CEFR B2
idolize/idolise.def: To admire and love someone so much that you consider them perfect or almost perfect.
idolize/idolise.ex: Many young fans idolise the famous footballer and try to copy everything he does.

ignorance.label: CEFR B2
ignorance.def: Lack of knowledge or awareness about something; not knowing facts or information.
ignorance.ex: Her ignorance of the company's financial problems meant she was shocked when the business suddenly closed.

ignorant.label: CEFR B2
ignorant.def: Lacking knowledge or awareness about something; not informed or educated about a particular subject.
ignorant.ex: He made ignorant remarks about climate change because he hadn't read any scientific research on the topic.

ill.label: CEFR A2
ill.def: Feeling sick or unwell in your body or mind.
ill.ex: My sister felt ill yesterday, so she stayed home from school.

illegal.label: CEFR A2
illegal.def: Against the law; not allowed by the government or legal rules.
illegal.ex: Parking your car in a no-parking zone is illegal.

illegally.label: CEFR B1
illegally.def: In a way that breaks the law or is not allowed by rules.
illegally.ex: The company was fined for illegally dumping waste into the river.

illness.label: CEFR B1
illness.def: A condition in which your body or mind is not healthy or working normally.
illness.ex: She had to cancel her trip because of a sudden illness.

illuminate.label: CEFR B1
illuminate.def: To light up a place or object so you can see it clearly.
illuminate.ex: The street lamps illuminate the pathway at night so pedestrians can walk safely.

illustration.label: CEFR B2
illustration.def: A picture or drawing used to explain or decorate a book, article, or other text.
illustration.ex: The medical textbook included detailed illustrations of the human anatomy to help students understand complex structures.

image.label: CEFR A2
image.def: A picture or photograph of something or someone.
image.ex: She took an image of the sunset with her camera.

imagery.label: CEFR B2
imagery.def: Vivid descriptions or pictures in words that help readers see, hear, or feel something in their mind.
imagery.ex: The poet's powerful imagery of the storm made readers feel the wind and rain as if they were really there.

imaginary.label: CEFR B1
imaginary.def: Existing only in the mind, not real or actually present.
imaginary.ex: When she was a child, she had an imaginary friend named Sophie who she played with every day.

imagination.label: CEFR A2
imagination.def: The ability to create pictures or ideas in your mind that are not real.
imagination.ex: My daughter has a wonderful imagination and loves to invent stories about magical creatures.

imagine.label: CEFR A1
imagine.def: To picture something in your mind that is not real or does not exist yet
imagine.ex: Can you imagine what you want to be when you grow up?

imitate.label: CEFR B1
imitate.def: To copy or reproduce someone's actions, speech, or behaviour very closely.
imitate.ex: The child began to imitate his father's way of walking and speaking.

imitation.label: CEFR B2
imitation.def: A copy or reproduction of something, often made to look like the original but of lower quality.
imitation.ex: The leather handbag was an imitation, not genuine designer merchandise.

immediate.label: CEFR B1
immediate.def: Happening or needing action right now, without any delay
immediate.ex: The immediate problem is that we don't have enough staff to finish the project this week.

immediately.label: CEFR B1
immediately.def: At once, without any delay or waiting time
immediately.ex: When the fire alarm went off, everyone left the building immediately.

immerse.label: CEFR B2
immerse.def: To involve oneself deeply in an activity or environment; to absorb completely in something.
immerse.ex: She decided to immerse herself in French culture by spending a year living in Paris.

immigrant.label: CEFR B2
immigrant.def: A person who comes to live permanently in a country where they were not born.
immigrant.ex: Many immigrants contribute significantly to their new country's economy and culture.

immigrate.label: CEFR B1
