immigrate.def: To come to a country to live there permanently or for a long time.
immigrate.ex: Her parents immigrated to Canada from Poland in the 1990s to find better job opportunities.

immigration.label: CEFR B1
immigration.def: The process of moving to a different country to live permanently or for a long period.
immigration.ex: Immigration to Canada has increased over the past decade as more people seek better job opportunities.

immoral.label: CEFR B2
immoral.def: Not following accepted standards of right and wrong behaviour; unethical.
immoral.ex: Many people consider it immoral to exploit workers for profit without fair wages.

immortal.label: CEFR B2
immortal.def: Living or existing forever; never dying or ceasing to exist
immortal.ex: Shakespeare's immortal works continue to influence writers and audiences more than four centuries after his death.

immortality.label: CEFR B2
immortality.def: The state of living or existing forever without ever dying or ending.
immortality.ex: The ancient Greeks believed their gods possessed immortality and could never be destroyed by time or disease.

impair.label: CEFR B1
impair.def: To make something weaker or less effective than before.
impair.ex: Lack of sleep can impair your ability to concentrate at work.

impartial.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
impartial.def: Treating all people or sides equally and fairly, without supporting one side more than others
impartial.ex: The judge must remain impartial throughout the trial, considering both the prosecution and defence arguments carefully.

impatient.label: CEFR A2
impatient.def: Not wanting to wait; annoyed when things take time
impatient.ex: She was impatient to open her birthday present.

impatiently.label: CEFR B2
impatiently.def: In a way that shows you cannot wait calmly or want something to happen quickly.
impatiently.ex: She tapped her foot impatiently while waiting for the meeting to begin.

imperative.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
imperative.def: Something that must be done; a necessary requirement or duty that cannot be avoided.
imperative.ex: Reducing carbon emissions has become an environmental imperative for governments worldwide.

impermanent.label: CEFR B2
impermanent.def: Lasting only a short time; not permanent or lasting forever.
impermanent.ex: The beauty of cherry blossoms is impermanent, which is why many people travel to Japan each spring to see them before they fade.

implication.label: CEFR B2
implication.def: Something that is suggested or indicated indirectly without being stated openly.
implication.ex: The implication of her resignation is that she disagreed with the company's new direction.

impolite.label: CEFR B2
impolite.def: Lacking respect or courtesy in behaviour or speech towards others.
impolite.ex: It would be impolite to interrupt someone while they are speaking.

impolitely.label: CEFR B2
impolitely.def: In a manner that is rude or disrespectful to others.
impolitely.ex: He interrupted her impolitely during the meeting, which upset several colleagues.

import.label: CEFR B2
import.def: To bring goods into a country from abroad for sale or use.
import.ex: France imports significant quantities of oil from the Middle East each year.

importance.label: CEFR A2
importance.def: How much something matters or how much it affects people or situations.
importance.ex: Learning English has great importance for finding a good job.

important.label: CEFR A1
important.def: Matters a lot; has great value or influence.
important.ex: Learning English is important for my future.

importantly.label: CEFR A2
importantly.def: In a way that is serious or has great significance.
importantly.ex: Importantly, you must wear a seatbelt when driving a car.

impose.label: CEFR B2
impose.def: To force someone to accept something they may not want, such as a rule, tax, or punishment.
impose.ex: The government imposed new restrictions on plastic bags to protect the environment.

impossibility.label: CEFR B2
impossibility.def: Something that cannot happen or be done; a situation that is impossible to achieve or solve.
impossibility.ex: The team faced an impossibility: they had to win by ten points in just two minutes.

impossible.label: CEFR A2
impossible.def: Cannot happen or be done; not possible
impossible.ex: It is impossible to learn a language in just one day.

impractical.label: CEFR B2
impractical.def: Not sensible or realistic to do; difficult to carry out in practice despite seeming possible
impractical.ex: Building a railway through the mountains proved impractical due to extreme costs and engineering challenges.

impress.label: CEFR A2
impress.def: To make someone feel admiration or respect for you.
impress.ex: She impressed her teacher with her excellent homework.

impressed.label: CEFR B2
impressed.def: Feeling admiration or respect because someone or something is excellent or surprising.
impressed.ex: The hiring manager was impressed by the candidate's innovative approach to solving complex problems.

impression.label: CEFR B1
impression.def: The effect or feeling that something or someone creates in your mind
impression.ex: My first impression of the new teacher was that she was very strict, but I soon realised she was kind.

impressionism.label: CEFR B2
impressionism.def: An art movement emphasising light, colour, and momentary impressions rather than detailed realism or precise forms.
impressionism.ex: The painter's use of loose brushstrokes and soft colours shows the influence of impressionism on her work.

impressionist.label: CEFR B2
impressionist.def: An artist who paints light and colour as they appear in a moment, focusing on impression rather than detail.
impressionist.ex: Monet was a famous Impressionist painter whose water lily series explored how light changed throughout the day.

impressive.label: CEFR B1
impressive.def: Making a strong positive effect on someone because of being very good or remarkable
impressive.ex: Her impressive performance in the exam impressed all her teachers and parents.

imprison.label: CEFR B2
imprison.def: To keep someone in prison as a legal punishment for a crime.
imprison.ex: The court decided to imprison the criminal for ten years following his conviction.

imprisonment.label: CEFR B2
imprisonment.def: The state of being held in prison as a legal punishment for a crime.
imprisonment.ex: The court sentenced him to five years' imprisonment for fraud.

improbable.label: CEFR B2
improbable.def: Unlikely to happen or be true based on the available evidence or logic.
improbable.ex: It seems improbable that they would leave town without telling anyone, given how organised they usually are.

improper.label: CEFR B1
improper.def: Not suitable, correct, or acceptable for a particular situation or purpose.
improper.ex: Wearing improper clothing to a formal dinner showed a lack of respect for the event.

improperly.label: CEFR B2
improperly.def: In a way that is not suitable, correct, or following the rules or standards expected.
improperly.ex: The evidence was improperly collected without a warrant, which led to its exclusion from the trial.

improve.label: CEFR A2
improve.def: To make something better or good than before.
improve.ex: If you practise every day, your English will improve.

improved.label: CEFR B2
improved.def: Made better in quality, performance, or condition than before.
improved.ex: The improved version of the software includes better security features and faster processing speeds.

improvement.label: CEFR B1
improvement.def: The process of making something better or becoming better than before.
improvement.ex: There has been significant improvement in her English speaking skills since she started taking lessons.

impulse.label: CEFR B2
impulse.def: A sudden strong desire or urge to do something without thinking first
impulse.ex: She had an impulse to call him, but she decided to wait until morning instead.

impulsive.label: CEFR B2
impulsive.def: Acting or done suddenly without thinking carefully about consequences first.
impulsive.ex: Her impulsive decision to quit her job without another offer lined up turned out to be a mistake.

in.label: CEFR A2
in.def: inside or within a place or container
in.ex: She put her books in the bag before leaving for school.

inaccurate.label: CEFR B2
inaccurate.def: Not correct or precise; containing errors or mistakes in detail or fact.
inaccurate.ex: The inaccurate weather forecast failed to predict the sudden storm that affected the region.

inadequate.label: CEFR B2
inadequate.def: Not enough in quality or quantity to meet a need or requirement
inadequate.ex: The hospital complained that funding was inadequate to provide proper care for all patients.

inanimate.label: CEFR B2
inanimate.def: Not alive or not having life; lacking consciousness, movement, or growth
inanimate.ex: The artist created a still life painting of inanimate objects like fruit and bottles.

inborn.label: CEFR B2
inborn.def: A quality or characteristic that you have naturally from birth, not learned or developed later.
inborn.ex: Her inborn talent for music was evident from the age of three, when she could play melodies after hearing them once.

incense.label: CEFR B2
incense.def: A substance that produces a fragrant smoke when burned, often used in religious ceremonies or to perfume a space.
incense.ex: The temple was filled with the sweet aroma of incense burning during the evening prayer service.

inch.label: CEFR B2
inch.def: To move slowly and gradually in a particular direction, often with difficulty or hesitation.
inch.ex: The traffic inched forward during rush hour as commuters waited patiently to leave the city.

incident.label: CEFR B1
incident.def: An event or situation that happens, often something unusual or unpleasant.
incident.ex: There was a minor incident on the train this morning when two passengers started arguing.

include.label: CEFR A2
include.def: To have something as part of a whole; to contain.
include.ex: The holiday package includes flights, hotel, and meals.

inconvenient.label: CEFR B1
inconvenient.def: Causing difficulty or problems; not suitable for your needs or plans
inconvenient.ex: I wanted to visit my friend, but she said it was an inconvenient time because she was working late.

incorrect.label: CEFR B1
incorrect.def: Not right or accurate; containing mistakes or wrong information
incorrect.ex: The incorrect answer on the test showed that I hadn't studied the material properly.

increase.label: CEFR A2
increase.def: To become larger or greater in number, size, or amount

increasingly.label: CEFR B1
increasingly.def: More and more; to a greater degree with each passing time
increasingly.ex: As cities grow, traffic problems are becoming increasingly difficult to solve.

incredible.label: CEFR B1
incredible.def: Extremely good or surprising; hard to believe because it is so amazing
incredible.ex: The view from the mountain top was incredible, and we took dozens of photographs.

incredibly.label: CEFR B1
incredibly.def: In a way that is hard to believe; extremely or surprisingly
incredibly.ex: The new smartphone is incredibly fast and has an amazing camera.

incur.label: CEFR B2
incur.def: To become liable for or experience something unwanted as a result of an action
incur.ex: The company will incur significant costs if it fails to meet the new environmental regulations.

incurable.label: CEFR B2
incurable.def: Unable to be cured or healed, especially referring to a disease or illness
incurable.ex: The patient was diagnosed with an incurable form of cancer, so doctors focused on managing her symptoms and improving her quality of life.

indebted.label: CEFR B2
indebted.def: Owing money or gratitude to someone; feeling obligated because of help or kindness received.
indebted.ex: The researcher felt deeply indebted to her mentor for guidance throughout the project.

indeed.label: CEFR A2
indeed.def: Used to confirm or emphasize that something is true or correct.
indeed.ex: The weather is very cold today, and indeed, it started snowing this morning.

indefinite article.label: CEFR B1
indefinite article.def: The words 'a' or 'an' used before a noun when referring to something non-specific or mentioned for the first time.
indefinite article.ex: In the sentence 'I need a pen', the indefinite article 'a' shows the pen is not a particular one.

indent.label: CEFR B2
indent.def: To start a line of text further from the left margin than other lines.
indent.ex: Remember to indent the first line of each paragraph in your essay by half an inch.

independence.label: CEFR A2
independence.def: The state of being free and able to make your own choices without help.
independence.ex: When she turned eighteen, she moved to her own flat and enjoyed her independence.

independent.label: CEFR B1
independent.def: Able to do things without help from other people or things.
independent.ex: After university, she moved into her own flat and became more independent.

independently.label: CEFR B2
independently.def: Without help, support, or influence from other people or things.
independently.ex: She decided to move out and live independently after finishing university.

indescribable.label: CEFR B2
indescribable.def: Too extreme or unusual to put into words; impossible to describe adequately
indescribable.ex: The feeling of winning the championship was indescribable—she had never experienced such joy before.

indifferent.label: CEFR B2
indifferent.def: Not interested in or concerned about something; showing no preference or care.
indifferent.ex: She seemed indifferent to the criticism, continuing with her work as if nothing had happened.

indignity.label: CEFR B2
indignity.def: Treatment that is disrespectful or humiliating, making someone feel ashamed or insulted.
indignity.ex: The former president felt the indignity of being arrested and paraded in front of cameras.

indirect.label: CEFR B1
indirect.def: Not direct; going around something in a way that is not straight or obvious
indirect.ex: She gave an indirect answer to the question instead of saying yes or no directly.

indirectly.label: CEFR B1
indirectly.def: In a way that is not direct or straightforward; by suggesting or implying rather than stating openly.
indirectly.ex: She indirectly suggested that I should consider applying for the job by mentioning how well I'd be suited for it.

indispensable.label: CEFR B2
indispensable.def: Absolutely necessary and impossible to do without; essential for something to work or succeed.
indispensable.ex: A good dictionary is indispensable for anyone learning a foreign language.

indoor.label: CEFR A2
indoor.def: Happening or used inside a building, not outside.
indoor.ex: We played indoor games when it was raining outside.

indoors.label: CEFR B1
indoors.def: inside a building rather than outside
indoors.ex: We decided to play indoors because the weather was too rainy.

indulge.label: CEFR B2
indulge.def: To allow yourself to enjoy something you want, often something that is considered a pleasure or treat.
indulge.ex: After working hard all week, she decided to indulge in a relaxing spa treatment.

indulgent.label: CEFR B2
indulgent.def: Allowing yourself or others to enjoy something pleasurable without restraint or guilt.
indulgent.ex: She treated herself to an indulgent weekend away at a luxury spa after months of hard work.

industrial.label: CEFR B1
industrial.def: Related to factories, manufacturing, or large-scale production of goods.
industrial.ex: The industrial area of the city has many factories and warehouses.

industrialize/industrialise.label: CEFR B2
industrialize/industrialise.def: To develop an economy or region by establishing factories and large-scale manufacturing industries.
industrialize/industrialise.ex: The country began to industrialise rapidly during the 1950s, transforming from an agricultural society into a manufacturing powerhouse.

industry.label: CEFR B1
industry.def: All the companies and businesses that make or provide one type of product or service
industry.ex: My father works in the automotive industry, designing parts for car manufacturers.

ineffective.label: CEFR B2
ineffective.def: Not producing the desired result or having little or no useful effect.
ineffective.ex: The government's ineffective response to the crisis drew criticism from opposition parties.

inert.label: CEFR B2
inert.def: Not moving, reacting, or showing activity; chemically stable and unlikely to combine with other substances
inert.ex: The inert gases like helium and argon are used in many industrial applications because they don't react with other elements.

inevitable.label: CEFR B1
inevitable.def: Certain to happen; impossible to prevent or avoid
inevitable.ex: After missing so many classes, his poor exam results were inevitable.

inexpensive.label: CEFR A2
inexpensive.ex: These inexpensive shoes are comfortable and last a long time.

inexperienced.label: CEFR B2
inexperienced.def: Lacking knowledge or skill from not having done something before
inexperienced.ex: The inexperienced driver made several mistakes during the test, but the examiner was patient with her.

infant.label: CEFR B2
infant.def: A very young child, usually from birth until around one or two years old.
infant.ex: The infant was sleeping peacefully in her cot whilst her mother prepared dinner.

infect.label: CEFR B2
infect.def: To cause disease or illness to spread into a person, animal, or plant through germs or virus
infect.ex: Contaminated water can infect thousands of people with cholera if not properly treated.

infected.label: CEFR B2
infected.def: Contaminated with harmful bacteria, viruses, or other pathogens that cause disease.
infected.ex: The infected wound required antibiotics to prevent serious complications.

infection.label: CEFR B1
infection.def: Illness caused when harmful bacteria or viruses enter and multiply in your body.
infection.ex: The doctor prescribed antibiotics to treat the bacterial infection in his wound.

infectious.label: CEFR B2
infectious.def: Able to spread from one person to another through contact or air; causing disease in others
infectious.ex: The doctor warned that the patient's infectious disease could spread rapidly through the hospital if proper precautions weren't taken.

inference.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
inference.def: A conclusion you reach based on evidence or information, without being directly told
inference.ex: From the dark clouds and wind, we made the inference that a storm was coming.

infinitive.label: CEFR B1
infinitive.def: The basic form of a verb, often preceded by 'to' in English, showing no tense or subject.
infinitive.ex: In the sentence 'I want to learn Spanish', the infinitive is 'to learn'.

inflammation.label: CEFR B2
inflammation.def: Redness, heat, and swelling in body tissue caused by infection or injury
inflammation.ex: The inflammation in her knee gradually reduced after she rested and applied ice for several days.

inflammatory.label: CEFR B2
inflammatory.def: Causing or likely to cause anger, strong feelings, or violent disagreement among people.
inflammatory.ex: The politician's inflammatory remarks about immigration sparked heated debate across the country.

inflate.label: CEFR B2
inflate.def: To fill something with air or gas, causing it to expand and become larger.
inflate.ex: The government's policies have inflated house prices significantly over the past decade.

inflation.label: CEFR B2
inflation.def: General rise in prices of goods and services over time, reducing money's purchasing power
inflation.ex: The central bank raised interest rates to control inflation and prevent the cost of living from rising further.

influence.label: CEFR B2
influence.def: To have an effect on someone's thoughts, behaviour, or decisions
influence.ex: Social media has increasingly influenced how young people view themselves and others.

influential.label: CEFR B2
influential.def: Having the power to affect or change people's opinions, behaviour, or decisions.
influential.ex: The influential economist's predictions about inflation shaped government policy for years.

influenza.label: CEFR B2
influenza.def: A contagious viral illness causing fever, body aches, and respiratory symptoms.
influenza.ex: Several passengers on the cruise ship contracted influenza during the voyage, prompting the captain to implement enhanced health protocols.

inform.label: CEFR B1
inform.def: To tell someone about something; to give information or knowledge to someone.
inform.ex: The teacher informed the class that the exam would be postponed until next week.

informal.label: CEFR B2
informal.def: Relaxed and casual in style or manner; not formal or official.
informal.ex: She wore informal clothes to the family gathering instead of dressing up.

information.label: CEFR A1
information.def: Facts or details about something that help you learn or understand it.
information.ex: I found information about the weather on the news website.

informative.label: CEFR B1
informative.def: Containing useful facts or knowledge that helps you understand something better.
informative.ex: The documentary was very informative and taught me about marine life conservation.

infringe.label: CEFR B2
infringe.def: To break or violate a law, right, or agreement, usually without permission.
infringe.ex: The company was sued for infringing on the patent rights of their competitor.

infringement.label: CEFR B2
infringement.def: A violation or unauthorised use of someone's legal rights or property.
infringement.ex: The software company sued for trademark infringement after another business used their logo without permission.

ingredient.label: CEFR B1
ingredient.def: One of the foods or substances that you mix together to make a dish or product.
ingredient.ex: The main ingredients in a chocolate cake are flour, eggs, butter, and cocoa powder.

inhabit.label: CEFR B2
inhabit.def: To live or reside in a place as a permanent or long-term resident.
inhabit.ex: Thousands of species inhabit the rainforest, from insects to large mammals.

inhabitant.label: CEFR B1
inhabitant.def: A person who lives in a particular place, such as a town or country.
inhabitant.ex: The inhabitants of the small town gathered at the market square to celebrate the festival.

inhale.label: CEFR B1
inhale.def: To breathe air into your lungs through your nose or mouth.
inhale.ex: She took a deep breath and inhaled slowly before speaking to the large audience.

inherit.label: CEFR B2
inherit.def: To receive money, property, or characteristics from a parent or relative after they die
inherit.ex: She inherited her grandmother's jewellery collection when she passed away last year.

initially.label: CEFR B1
initially.def: At the beginning or at first, before anything else happens.
initially.ex: Initially, she was nervous about the new job, but soon she felt more confident.

initiative.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
initiative.def: A new plan or project started by someone to improve or change something.
initiative.ex: The government launched a new initiative to reduce carbon emissions by 50% within ten years.

inject.label: CEFR B1
inject.def: To put a liquid, especially medicine, into the body using a needle and syringe
inject.ex: The nurse will inject the vaccine into your arm to protect you from the disease.

injection.label: CEFR B2
injection.def: A liquid medicine forced into the body through a needle to treat illness or disease.
injection.ex: The nurse gave him an injection to prevent malaria before his trip to Africa.

injured.label: CEFR B2
injured.def: Having received physical harm or damage to the body as a result of an accident or attack.
injured.ex: The injured passenger was taken to hospital immediately after the train derailment.

injury.label: CEFR B1
injury.def: Physical damage or harm to your body caused by an accident or attack
injury.ex: He suffered a serious leg injury when he fell off his bicycle during the holiday.

ink.label: CEFR B1
ink.def: Coloured liquid used for writing or printing with a pen or printer
ink.ex: She ran out of ink while writing her essay, so she had to buy a new cartridge for her printer.

innate.label: CEFR B2
innate.def: Present from birth; natural ability or quality you have from the start, not learned
innate.ex: She has an innate talent for music that developed even before formal training.

inner.label: CEFR A2
inner.def: On the inside or interior of something, not on the outside.
inner.ex: She keeps her inner feelings private and doesn't tell many people about them.

innermost.label: CEFR B1
innermost.def: Deepest or furthest inside; closest to the centre or core
innermost.ex: She kept her innermost feelings hidden from everyone except her best friend.

innocent.label: CEFR B1
innocent.def: Not guilty of a crime or wrongdoing; having done nothing wrong.
innocent.ex: The court found the defendant innocent of all charges after reviewing the evidence.

innocently.label: CEFR B2
innocently.def: In a way that shows you did not know or intend any harm or wrongdoing.
innocently.ex: She innocently asked about his past, not realising the question would upset him.

innovation.label: CEFR B2
innovation.def: A new idea, method, or product that is different from what existed before.
innovation.ex: The company's latest innovation in smartphone technology has impressed both critics and consumers.

innovative.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
innovative.def: Using new ideas or methods that are different from traditional approaches.
innovative.ex: The company's innovative approach to sustainable packaging has reduced waste significantly.

innovator.label: CEFR B1
innovator.def: A person who creates new ideas, methods, or products that did not exist before.
innovator.ex: Steve Jobs was an innovator who completely changed how people use computers and phones.

input.label: CEFR B2
input.def: Information or data that you put into a system, machine, or process for it to work with
input.ex: The quality of the software's output depends entirely on the quality of the input provided by users.

inquire/enquire.label: CEFR B2
inquire/enquire.def: To ask questions in order to get information or learn more about something
inquire/enquire.ex: She decided to enquire about the job vacancy at the reception desk.

inquiry/enquiry.label: CEFR B1
inquiry/enquiry.def: An official question or investigation to find out information about something.
inquiry/enquiry.ex: I made an enquiry at the hotel reception to ask about available rooms for next week.

insane.label: CEFR B1
insane.def: Completely crazy or mentally ill; extremely foolish or unreasonable.
insane.ex: He drove at insane speeds down the motorway, which terrified his passengers.

inscribe.label: CEFR B1
inscribe.def: To write or carve words onto a surface, such as stone, metal, or paper.
inscribe.ex: The artist inscribed her name on the back of the painting to show it was her work.

inscription.label: CEFR B1
inscription.def: Words or letters carved, written, or printed on a surface like a stone, coin, or building
inscription.ex: The inscription on the old gravestone was difficult to read because it was so worn.

insect.label: CEFR A2
insect.def: A small animal with six legs, wings, and a body divided into three parts.
insect.ex: I saw a butterfly, which is a beautiful insect, flying in the garden.

inseparable.label: CEFR B2
inseparable.def: So closely connected or dependent that they cannot be parted or considered separately.
inseparable.ex: The twins were inseparable throughout their childhood, doing everything together and rarely spending time apart.

inside.label: CEFR B2
inside.def: The interior or inner part of something; the space contained within an object or place.
inside.ex: The inside of the old mansion revealed ornate decorations that had been hidden from public view for decades.

insist.label: CEFR B1
insist.def: To demand something firmly and repeatedly, even if others disagree.
insist.ex: She insisted on paying for the meal herself, even though her friend offered to help.

inspect.label: CEFR B2
inspect.def: To examine something carefully and thoroughly to check its condition or quality.
inspect.ex: The building inspector will inspect the new construction site next week to ensure it meets safety standards.

inspection.label: CEFR B1
inspection.def: A careful examination of something to check if it is good or safe.
inspection.ex: The building failed its safety inspection because the fire exits were blocked.

inspector.label: CEFR B2
inspector.def: An official who examines things carefully to check they meet required standards or rules.
inspector.ex: The building inspector found several safety violations that needed to be corrected before the shop could open.

inspiration.label: CEFR A2
inspiration.def: A good idea or feeling that makes you want to do something creative or exciting.
inspiration.ex: The beautiful sunset gave her inspiration to paint a picture.

inspirational.label: CEFR B2
inspirational.def: Causing someone to feel motivated, encouraged, or stimulated to do something creative or ambitious.
inspirational.ex: Her inspirational speech about overcoming adversity motivated thousands of young people to pursue their dreams.

inspire.label: CEFR B1
inspire.def: To make someone want to do something or feel excited about an idea.
inspire.ex: Her courage and determination inspired many young people to pursue their dreams.

install/instal.label: CEFR B1
install/instal.def: To put equipment or software in place so it is ready to use.
install/instal.ex: We need to install new security cameras in the office building next week.

instant.label: CEFR A2
instant.ex: I got an instant reply to my email just five seconds after I sent it.

instantly.label: CEFR B2
instantly.def: Immediately, without any delay or hesitation whatsoever.
instantly.ex: When she saw the accident, she instantly called emergency services.

instead.label: CEFR A2
instead.def: In place of something else; as an alternative to something
instead.ex: I don't like coffee, so I drink tea instead.

instill/instil.label: CEFR B2
instill/instil.def: To gradually introduce or develop an idea, value, or skill in someone's mind over time.
instill/instil.ex: Teachers work to instil a love of reading in their students from an early age.

instinct.label: CEFR B2
instinct.def: A natural feeling or behaviour that happens without thinking or learning
instinct.ex: Her instinct told her something was wrong with the investment, so she decided not to participate.

institution.label: CEFR B2
institution.def: A large, formal organization or building established to serve a specific purpose, like education, healthcare, or government.
institution.ex: The university is one of the oldest educational institutions in Europe, founded over three centuries ago.

instruct.label: CEFR B2
instruct.def: To teach someone how to do something or provide them with detailed information about a task.
instruct.ex: The manager instructed the new employees on the company's safety procedures during their first week.

instruction.label: CEFR B1
instruction.def: Teaching or guidance that tells someone how to do something or explains a subject.
instruction.ex: The teacher gave clear instructions before the students started their chemistry experiment.

instructor.label: CEFR B1
instructor.def: A person who teaches a skill or subject to a group of people or an individual.
instructor.ex: My driving instructor taught me how to parallel park safely before my test.

instrument.label: CEFR A2
instrument.def: A tool or device designed to do a particular job or create music.
instrument.ex: She plays the violin, which is a stringed musical instrument.

insufficient.label: CEFR B2
insufficient.def: Not enough in quantity or quality to meet a need or requirement.
insufficient.ex: The company decided not to proceed with the project due to insufficient funding from investors.

insult.label: CEFR B2
insult.def: To say something rude or disrespectful that offends someone's dignity or honour.
insult.ex: He insulted the chef by calling the meal tasteless and refusing to pay for it.

insurance.label: CEFR B1
insurance.def: A service where you pay money regularly so a company will pay you if something bad happens.
insurance.ex: Before the trip, I bought travel insurance to cover medical costs if I got sick abroad.

integrate.label: CEFR B2
integrate.def: To combine different parts into a unified whole that works together effectively.
integrate.ex: The company is working to integrate new technologies into their existing systems.

intellectual.label: CEFR B2
intellectual.def: A person who uses their mind professionally, such as a scholar, writer, or academic.
intellectual.ex: The conference brought together intellectuals from different fields to discuss contemporary social issues.

intelligence.label: CEFR A2
intelligence.def: The ability to learn, understand, and think about things quickly and well.
intelligence.ex: She has high intelligence and always gets good marks in her tests.

intelligent.label: CEFR A2
intelligent.def: Smart and able to learn and understand things quickly.
intelligent.ex: My sister is very intelligent and always gets good marks at school.

intend.label: CEFR B1
intend.def: To plan or want to do something; to have a purpose in mind.
intend.ex: I intend to finish my degree before moving abroad next year.

intense.label: CEFR B1
intense.def: Very strong or extreme in degree or feeling
intense.ex: The intense heat during summer makes it difficult to exercise outdoors.

intensive.label: CEFR B1
intensive.def: Very thorough and using a lot of time, effort, or resources in a short period
intensive.ex: She completed an intensive English course over three weeks to prepare for her exam.

intent.label: CEFR B2
intent.def: Showing strong purpose and serious attention to something; focused and determined.
intent.ex: She gazed at the painting with an intent expression, studying every detail carefully.

intention.label: CEFR B1
intention.def: A plan or purpose that you want to achieve or do
intention.ex: Her intention was to finish university before starting a family.

intentionally.label: CEFR B1
intentionally.def: On purpose; deliberately choosing to do something rather than by accident.
intentionally.ex: She intentionally arrived late to the meeting to make a statement about her disagreement with the decision.

interaction.label: CEFR B1
interaction.def: Communication or activity between two or more people that affects each other.
interaction.ex: The interaction between the teacher and students improved when they started working in small groups.

interactive.label: CEFR B2
interactive.def: Involving communication or action between two or more people or things; allowing exchange of information.
interactive.ex: The museum's interactive exhibits encouraged visitors to touch displays and answer questions about the artworks.

interest.label: CEFR B1
interest.def: To make someone want to know or learn more about something
interest.ex: The documentary about space really interested my younger brother, and now he wants to be an astronomer.

interested.label: CEFR A1
interested.def: Wanting to know or learn more about something
interested.ex: She is interested in learning to play the guitar.

interesting.label: CEFR A1
interesting.def: Catches your attention and makes you want to know more about something.
interesting.ex: That book is interesting and I cannot stop reading it.

interfere.label: CEFR B2
interfere.def: To become involved in someone else's affairs without being asked or wanted.
interfere.ex: The manager decided not to interfere in the dispute between the two team members, allowing them to resolve it themselves.

interior.label: CEFR B2
interior.def: The inside part or inner surface of something, especially a building or room.
interior.ex: The architect designed a minimalist interior with clean lines and neutral colours.

intermediate.label: CEFR B1
intermediate.def: Between two levels or stages; neither basic nor advanced
intermediate.ex: She has an intermediate level of Spanish, so she can read simple books and hold conversations.

intermission.label: CEFR B1
intermission.def: A short break during a performance, film, or event where activity stops temporarily.
intermission.ex: During the theatre intermission, we went to the bar to get some drinks before the second half started.

international.label: CEFR A2
international.def: Involving more than one country or people from different countries.
international.ex: The international football match was between England and France.

internationally.label: CEFR B1
internationally.def: In a way that involves or affects multiple countries around the world.
internationally.ex: The film was successful internationally, earning money from cinemas in over fifty countries.

internet/internet.label: CEFR A1
internet/internet.def: A system connecting computers worldwide, allowing people to share information and communicate.
internet/internet.ex: I use the internet every day to watch videos and talk to my friends.

interpersonal.label: CEFR B2
interpersonal.def: Relating to relationships and interactions between different people.
interpersonal.ex: Good interpersonal skills are essential for success in most modern workplaces.

interpretation.label: CEFR B2
interpretation.def: An explanation of the meaning or significance of something, based on one's understanding or analysis.
interpretation.ex: The director's interpretation of the classic novel was very different from previous film versions.

interpreter.label: CEFR B2
interpreter.def: A person who translates spoken words from one language into another during conversations or meetings.
interpreter.ex: The interpreter helped the foreign diplomat communicate with the government officials during the treaty negotiations.

interrupt.label: CEFR B1
interrupt.def: To stop someone speaking or doing something by acting or speaking suddenly
interrupt.ex: I didn't mean to interrupt you, but I had an important question about the meeting.

interruption.label: CEFR B2
interruption.def: When someone stops another person from continuing what they were saying or doing.
interruption.ex: The meeting suffered from constant interruptions, making it difficult for the presenter to finish her points.

intersection.label: CEFR B2
intersection.def: A place where two or more roads, lines, or things cross each other.
intersection.ex: The accident happened at a busy intersection near the city centre where four main roads meet.

interval.label: CEFR B1
interval.def: A space of time between two events or points
interval.ex: The train leaves every thirty-minute interval throughout the day.

interview.label: CEFR B1
interview.def: To ask someone questions to learn about them, often for a job or news story.
interview.ex: The journalist interviewed the mayor about the new community centre project.

interviewee.label: CEFR B1
interviewee.def: A person who answers questions in a formal meeting or conversation with another person.
interviewee.ex: The interviewee answered all the job interview questions confidently and impressed the panel.

interviewer.label: CEFR A1
interviewer.def: A person who asks questions to another person, usually to get information or to check if they are right for a job.
interviewer.ex: The interviewer asked me about my experience working with children.

intimate.label: CEFR B2
intimate.def: Very close and personal, involving deep knowledge or strong emotional connection between people.
intimate.ex: They shared an intimate conversation about their fears and dreams late into the night.

intransitive.label: CEFR B2
intransitive.def: Describing a verb that does not need a direct object to complete its meaning.
intransitive.ex: The verb 'sleep' is intransitive because you can say 'She slept' without naming what she slept.

intravenous.label: CEFR B2
intravenous.def: Delivered or injected directly into a vein, usually through a needle or tube.
intravenous.ex: The patient received intravenous antibiotics to treat the severe infection more quickly.

introduce.label: CEFR A1
introduce.def: To tell someone who another person is for the first time
introduce.ex: I introduced my friend Sarah to my parents yesterday.

introduction.label: CEFR B1
introduction.def: The act of presenting someone to another person, or the beginning part of a book or speech.
introduction.ex: The introduction to the novel explained the author's inspiration for writing the story.

intrude.label: CEFR B2
intrude.def: To enter or interrupt someone's space, time, or privacy without permission or welcome.
intrude.ex: The journalist's questions began to intrude on the politician's personal life, causing significant controversy.

intruder.label: CEFR B2
intruder.def: A person who enters a place illegally or without permission, often with harmful intent.
intruder.ex: The security system detected an intruder in the building at midnight and immediately alerted the police.

intrusion.label: CEFR B2
intrusion.def: The act of entering a place or situation where you are not wanted or have no right to be.
intrusion.ex: The new motorway was seen by local residents as an unwelcome intrusion into their quiet countryside community.

invade.label: CEFR A2
invade.def: To enter a country or place with an army to take control of it.
invade.ex: The soldiers invaded the country during the war.

invariable.label: CEFR B2
invariable.def: Never changing or varying; always the same in all situations or circumstances
invariable.ex: Her response to criticism was invariable: she would listen carefully, then explain her reasoning calmly.

invariably.label: CEFR B2
invariably.def: In every case or situation; always without exception.
invariably.ex: The train is invariably delayed during rush hour, so commuters have learned to expect it.

invasion.label: CEFR B1
invasion.def: The act of entering a country or region by military force, often causing harm and disruption to people living there
invasion.ex: The invasion of the country by neighbouring forces led to thousands of people fleeing their homes.

invent.label: CEFR A2
invent.def: To create something completely new that did not exist before.
invent.ex: Thomas Edison invented the electric light bulb in 1879.

invention.label: CEFR A2
invention.def: A new thing that someone creates or designs for the first time.
invention.ex: The light bulb was an important invention that changed how people lived.

inventor.label: CEFR B2
inventor.def: A person who creates or designs something completely new that has never existed before.
inventor.ex: Thomas Edison, the famous inventor of the light bulb, conducted thousands of experiments before achieving success.

inverted commas.label: CEFR B2
inverted commas.def: Punctuation marks used to show the beginning and end of a direct quotation or quoted text.
inverted commas.ex: The journalist wrote that the minister said, in inverted commas, 'the economy is improving faster than expected.'

investigation.label: CEFR B1
investigation.def: A careful examination or study of something to discover facts or truth about it.
investigation.ex: The police investigation into the crime took several months before they found evidence.

investigator.label: CEFR B2
investigator.def: A person who conducts a systematic examination to discover facts or evidence about something.
investigator.ex: The investigator interviewed witnesses and examined the crime scene carefully.

investment.label: CEFR B2
investment.def: Money or resources put into a business or project with the aim of making a profit.
investment.ex: The company made a significant investment in new technology to improve productivity.

investor.label: CEFR B2
investor.def: A person or organisation that provides money to a business or project expecting financial returns.
investor.ex: The property investor purchased several houses with the intention of renting them out for profit.

invisible.label: CEFR B2
invisible.def: Unable to be seen, either because it is very small or because it is hidden from view.
invisible.ex: The harmful effects of air pollution are often invisible, but they damage our health over time.

invitation.label: CEFR B1
invitation.def: A request asking someone to come to an event or visit a place.
invitation.ex: I received an invitation to Sarah's birthday party next Saturday.

invite.label: CEFR A2
invite.def: To ask someone to come to an event or to your home.
invite.ex: I invited my friends to my birthday party next Saturday.

invitingly.label: CEFR B2
invitingly.def: In a way that makes something seem attractive and worth doing or experiencing.
invitingly.ex: The restaurant's warm lighting and aroma of fresh bread smelt invitingly through the open doorway.

invoke.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
invoke.def: To cite or appeal to something, such as a law, authority, or emotion, to support an argument or action.
invoke.ex: The lawyer invoked the constitutional right to free speech in her defence of the defendant.

involuntarily.label: CEFR B1
involuntarily.def: Done without willing or choosing to do it; happening against your wishes.
involuntarily.ex: She involuntarily shuddered when she saw the cold water in the swimming pool.

involved.label: CEFR B1
involved.def: Taking part in something; connected with or affected by something.
involved.ex: She felt nervous about the involved process of applying to university, but her teacher helped her understand each step.

involvement.label: CEFR B2
involvement.def: The state of being engaged or taking part in something, often with effort or commitment.
involvement.ex: Her involvement in the environmental charity has made a real difference to the local community.

iron.label: CEFR B1
iron.def: To press clothes with a hot iron to remove wrinkles and make them smooth and neat.
iron.ex: I need to iron my shirt before the job interview tomorrow.

ironically.label: CEFR B2
ironically.def: In a way that is amusingly or strikingly opposite to what you would expect.
ironically.ex: Ironically, the fire safety officer's house was destroyed by a fire last year.

ironing.label: CEFR B1
ironing.def: The process of pressing clothes with a hot device to remove wrinkles and creases
ironing.ex: My sister does the ironing every Sunday afternoon to prepare clothes for the week ahead.

irrational.label: CEFR B2
irrational.def: Not based on logic or reason; driven by emotion rather than clear thinking.
irrational.ex: Her irrational fear of flying prevented her from accepting the job offer abroad.

irregular.label: CEFR B1
irregular.def: Not happening at regular times or following a consistent pattern; unpredictable or uneven
irregular.ex: His irregular sleep schedule made it difficult for him to concentrate at work.

irresistible.label: CEFR B2
irresistible.def: So attractive or compelling that you cannot refuse or stop yourself from wanting it
irresistible.ex: The smell of fresh bread from the bakery was irresistible, so she decided to buy a loaf.

irresistibly.label: CEFR B2
irresistibly.def: In a way that is impossible to resist or refuse; with compelling force or attraction.
irresistibly.ex: The dessert smelled irresistibly delicious, so everyone at the dinner decided to have a portion.

irresponsible.label: CEFR B2
irresponsible.def: Not showing enough care about the effects or consequences of your actions on others.
irresponsible.ex: Driving after drinking alcohol is irresponsible and puts lives at risk.

irritate.label: CEFR B1
irritate.def: To make someone feel annoyed or frustrated.
irritate.ex: The constant noise from the construction site irritates many people in the neighbourhood.

irritated.label: CEFR B2
irritated.def: annoyed or frustrated, often by something repeated or persistent
irritated.ex: She felt irritated by her colleague's constant interruptions during the important meeting.

irritating.label: CEFR B2
irritating.def: Causing annoyance or slight anger; making someone feel uncomfortable or impatient.
irritating.ex: The constant notifications on his phone were irritating, so he finally turned them off.

irritation.label: CEFR B2
irritation.def: A feeling of annoyance or impatience; physical discomfort or redness of the skin
irritation.ex: The constant noise from the construction site caused irritation among the residents, who filed complaints with the local council.

island.label: CEFR A1
island.def: A piece of land completely surrounded by water.
island.ex: Japan is an island country in Asia.

islander.label: CEFR B2
islander.def: A person who lives on or comes from an island.
islander.ex: The islanders of the Caribbean have developed a unique culture influenced by both African and European traditions.

isle.label: CEFR B1
isle.def: A piece of land surrounded completely by water, smaller than a continent.
isle.ex: The Isle of Wight is a popular holiday destination for families in southern England.

isolation.label: CEFR B1
isolation.def: The state of being separated from other people or things.
isolation.ex: During the pandemic, many people experienced isolation while working from home.

it.label: CEFR A2
it.def: Information technology; computers and systems used to store, process, and share information
it.ex: She works in IT and helps fix computer problems at the office.

italicize/italicise.label: CEFR B2
italicize/italicise.def: To print or write text in italic style, slanting to the right, typically for emphasis or distinction.
italicize/italicise.ex: When writing an essay, remember to italicise the titles of books and journals to make them stand out from the main text.

itch.label: CEFR B2
itch.def: To feel an uncomfortable sensation on your skin that makes you want to scratch.
itch.ex: My skin begins to itch whenever I wear wool sweaters for too long.

ivory.label: CEFR B1
ivory.def: Hard, creamy-white material from elephant tusks, now illegal to trade
ivory.ex: The antique piano had keys made of ivory, which would be illegal to sell today.

jacket.label: CEFR A1
jacket.def: A piece of clothing with sleeves that you wear over your shirt to stay warm.
jacket.ex: I put on my blue jacket because it was cold outside.

jade.label: CEFR B1
jade.def: A hard, smooth green stone used to make jewellery and decorative objects.
jade.ex: She wore a beautiful jade bracelet that her grandmother had given her.

jail/gaol.label: CEFR B1
jail/gaol.def: A building where people are kept as punishment for breaking the law.
jail/gaol.ex: The criminal was sent to gaol for five years after the trial.

jam.label: CEFR B2
jam.def: To push or squeeze something forcefully into a space that is too small or tight.
jam.ex: The printer jammed again when I tried to load too many sheets of paper at once.

january.label: CEFR A1
january.def: The first month of the year, between December and February.
january.ex: My birthday is in January, so it's very cold.

jar.label: CEFR B1
jar.def: A cylindrical glass or plastic container with a lid, used for storing food or other items
jar.ex: She kept her homemade jam in glass jars on the kitchen shelf.

jaw.label: CEFR B2
jaw.def: The lower part of the face that moves to allow eating and speaking.
jaw.ex: The boxer received a powerful punch to the jaw and fell to the canvas.

jazz.label: CEFR B1
jazz.def: A type of American music with strong rhythm, improvisation, and syncopated beats, originating from African American culture.
jazz.ex: The club features live jazz performances every Friday night, with talented musicians playing saxophone and drums.

jealous.label: CEFR B1
jealous.def: Unhappy or angry because someone else has something you want, or afraid of losing someone's attention.
jealous.ex: She felt jealous when her best friend started spending all her time with a new boyfriend.

jealously.label: CEFR B2
jealously.def: In a way that shows worry about losing someone's attention or affection to another person.
jealously.ex: She jealously guarded her position as the team's lead researcher, resisting any collaboration with external specialists.

jeans.label: CEFR A1
jeans.def: Casual trousers made from thick blue cotton material.
jeans.ex: I wear my jeans every day because they are comfortable.

jelly.label: CEFR A2
jelly.def: A soft, sweet food made from fruit juice and gelatine that wobbles when you move it
jelly.ex: The children enjoyed eating red jelly and ice cream at the birthday party.

jeopardize/jeopardise.label: CEFR B2
jeopardize/jeopardise.def: To put something valuable at risk of being lost or damaged; to endanger.
jeopardize/jeopardise.ex: The scandal jeopardised the company's reputation and market position.

jet.label: CEFR A1
jet.def: An aeroplane that flies very fast using powerful engines.
jet.ex: We took a jet to Paris and arrived in just two hours.

jewel.label: CEFR B1
jewel.def: A precious stone or piece of expensive jewellery, often used as decoration or worn as a valuable possession
jewel.ex: She inherited her grandmother's jewels, including a diamond ring and a ruby necklace.

jeweler/jeweller.label: CEFR B2
jeweler/jeweller.def: A person who makes, repairs, or sells jewellery and other decorative items made from precious metals and stones.
jeweler/jeweller.ex: The jeweller examined the damaged ring carefully before deciding it could be restored to its original condition.

jewelry/jewellery.label: CEFR A1
jewelry/jewellery.def: Pretty things made of gold, silver, or gems that people wear on their body
jewelry/jewellery.ex: She wore a beautiful necklace and earrings to the party.

jog.label: CEFR B1
jog.def: To run at a slow, steady pace, usually for exercise or fitness.
jog.ex: She jogs in the park every morning before work.

jogging.label: CEFR B1
jogging.def: Running at a steady, comfortable pace for exercise and fitness.
jogging.ex: She goes jogging in the park every morning before work to stay healthy.

join.label: CEFR A1
join.def: To connect with someone or something; to become part of a group or activity.
join.ex: I want to join the football club at school.

joint.label: CEFR B2
joint.def: A place where two bones or structures connect, allowing movement in the body.
joint.ex: He injured his knee joint while playing football and had to undergo surgery.

joke.label: CEFR B1
joke.def: To say something funny or not serious to make people laugh.
joke.ex: He was joking about his terrible cooking skills when he said he could become a chef.

journalism.label: CEFR B2
journalism.def: The profession of investigating, writing, and reporting news for newspapers, magazines, websites, or broadcast media.
journalism.ex: She pursued a career in journalism after studying media at university, covering political stories for a national newspaper.

journalist.label: CEFR B1
journalist.def: A person who writes or reports news stories for newspapers, magazines, websites, or television.
journalist.ex: The journalist interviewed the mayor about the new transport plan for the city.

journey.label: CEFR A2
journey.def: A trip from one place to another, often taking some time to complete
journey.ex: My journey to work takes about thirty minutes by bus.

joy.label: CEFR A2
joy.def: A feeling of great happiness and pleasure.
joy.ex: When she saw her family at the airport, her face was full of joy.

judge.label: CEFR A1
judge.def: To make a decision about something or someone after thinking carefully.
judge.ex: The teacher will judge which student has the best drawing.

judgment/judgement.label: CEFR B1
judgment/judgement.def: An opinion or decision made after careful thought about something or someone.
judgment/judgement.ex: The court's judgement on the case will be announced next week.

judo.label: CEFR B2
judo.def: A Japanese martial art and Olympic sport in which opponents try to throw each other to the ground.
judo.ex: She has been training in judo for three years and recently competed in the national championships.

jug.label: CEFR B1
jug.def: A container with a handle and a spout, used for holding and pouring liquids like water or milk.
jug.ex: She filled the jug with cold water and put it on the kitchen table.

juice.label: CEFR A1
juice.def: A liquid from fruit or vegetables that you can drink.
juice.ex: I drink orange juice for breakfast every morning.

juicy.label: CEFR B1
juicy.def: Containing a lot of liquid or moisture; full of flavour and interest
juicy.ex: The juicy orange was refreshing on a hot summer day.

july.label: CEFR A1
july.def: The seventh month of the year, between June and August.
july.ex: My birthday is in July, so I have a summer party with my friends.

jump.label: CEFR A1
jump.def: To push yourself up into the air with your legs.
jump.ex: The children jump on the trampoline in the garden.

jumper.label: CEFR B1
jumper.def: A piece of clothing you wear on top, usually made of wool or cotton, pulled over your head
jumper.ex: I wore a warm jumper to work because it was cold outside.

june.label: CEFR A1
june.def: The sixth month of the year, between May and July.
june.ex: My birthday is in June, so we go to the beach to celebrate.

jungle.label: CEFR B1
jungle.def: A thick forest with many trees and plants, usually in a hot, wet climate.
jungle.ex: The explorers walked through the dense jungle, listening to the sounds of wildlife all around them.

junior.label: CEFR A2
junior.def: Lower in rank, position, or level than someone or something else
junior.ex: My brother is a junior footballer, so he plays with younger children than me.

junk.label: CEFR A2
junk.def: Old or broken things that are not wanted or useful anymore
junk.ex: There is a lot of junk in the garage—old toys, broken furniture, and magazines.

junk food.label: CEFR B2
junk food.def: Food high in calories, sugar, and fat but low in nutritional value; eaten for convenience rather than health.
junk food.ex: Nutritionists warn that regularly consuming junk food increases the risk of obesity and cardiovascular disease.

juror.label: CEFR B2
juror.def: A member of a jury chosen to listen to evidence and decide the guilt or innocence of a defendant in a court.
juror.ex: Each juror was sworn to make a fair decision based solely on the facts presented during the trial.

jury.label: CEFR B2
jury.def: A group of ordinary people who listen to evidence in court and decide if someone is guilty or not guilty
jury.ex: The jury deliberated for three days before reaching a unanimous verdict in the high-profile fraud case.

just.label: CEFR A1
just.def: A very short time ago; recently

justice.label: CEFR B1
justice.def: Fair treatment according to law and what is morally right.
justice.ex: The court worked hard to ensure that justice was served for the victim of the crime.

kangaroo.label: CEFR B2
kangaroo.def: Large Australian marsupial that moves by jumping on its powerful hind legs.
kangaroo.ex: The wildlife documentary featured kangaroos hopping across the outback at surprising speeds.

karaoke.label: CEFR B2
karaoke.def: Entertainment where people sing popular songs to recorded music using a microphone.
karaoke.ex: After dinner, we went to a karaoke bar downtown where my friend belted out three classic rock songs.

keen.label: CEFR B2
keen.def: Enthusiastic and eager to do something; showing strong interest or desire.
keen.ex: She was keen to learn more about renewable energy and attended every workshop available.

keep.label: CEFR A1
keep.def: To have something and not give it away or throw it away.
keep.ex: I want to keep this toy because it is my favourite.

keeper.label: CEFR B2
keeper.def: A person whose job is to care for, protect, or manage something or someone regularly
keeper.ex: The zookeeper feeds the animals and monitors their health every single day.

kerosene.label: CEFR B2
kerosene.def: A thin, flammable oil used as fuel for lamps, heaters, and some aircraft engines.
kerosene.ex: The village still relies on kerosene lamps in areas where electricity supply remains unreliable.

kettle.label: CEFR B1
kettle.def: A container with a handle and spout used for boiling water, usually on a stove or electric base.
kettle.ex: I'll put the kettle on to make us some tea.

key.label: CEFR A1
key.def: A small metal object you use to open or close a lock.
key.ex: I need my key to open the front door of my house.

keyboard.label: CEFR B1
keyboard.def: A set of buttons with letters, numbers, and symbols that you press to type on a computer or phone.
keyboard.ex: She spilled coffee on her keyboard and had to buy a new one for her laptop.

keystroke.label: CEFR B2
keystroke.def: A single press of a key on a keyboard, or the act of pressing it.
keystroke.ex: The programmer recorded every keystroke to monitor productivity and identify security breaches in the system.

kick.label: CEFR A1
kick.def: To hit something with your foot.
kick.ex: The children kick the ball in the park after school.

kid.label: CEFR B1
kid.def: To joke or tease someone in a playful way, not seriously
kid.ex: Don't worry, I was only kidding about the broken vase—it's still in one piece!

kill.label: CEFR A1
kill.def: To make someone or something stop living or die.
kill.ex: The hunter killed a deer in the forest.

killer.label: CEFR A2
killer.def: A person or animal that causes someone's death
killer.ex: The police are searching for the killer who escaped from the city.

killing.label: CEFR B1
killing.def: The act of causing someone's death, especially violently or unlawfully.
killing.ex: The killing of the prominent journalist sparked international outrage and demands for an investigation.

kilo.label: CEFR A2
kilo.def: A unit of weight equal to one thousand grams.
kilo.ex: A kilo of apples costs about three pounds at the market.

kilogram/kilogramme.label: CEFR A2
kilogram/kilogramme.def: A unit of weight equal to 1,000 grams, used to measure how heavy something is.
kilogram/kilogramme.ex: A bag of flour usually weighs about one kilogram.

kilometer/kilometre.label: CEFR A2
kilometer/kilometre.def: A unit of distance equal to 1,000 metres.
kilometer/kilometre.ex: The nearest town is about 5 kilometres away from our village.

kind.label: CEFR A1
kind.def: A type or variety of something.
kind.ex: What kind of fruit do you like best?

kindly.label: CEFR B1
kindly.def: In a pleasant and helpful way; showing that you care about others
kindly.ex: The teacher kindly offered to help me with my homework after class.

kindness.label: CEFR B1
kindness.def: The quality of being friendly, generous, and caring about others' feelings and needs.
kindness.ex: Her kindness towards the new student made him feel welcome on his first day at school.

king.label: CEFR A1
king.def: A male ruler of a country or kingdom
king.ex: The king of Spain lives in a beautiful palace in Madrid.

kingdom.label: CEFR A2
kingdom.def: A country ruled by a king or queen.
kingdom.ex: The United Kingdom includes England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

kiss.label: CEFR B1
kiss.def: To touch someone with your lips to show love or greet them.
kiss.ex: She kissed her mother goodbye before leaving for university.

kit.label: CEFR A2
kit.def: A set of things you need for a particular purpose or activity
kit.ex: I bought a first aid kit to keep at home in case of emergencies.

kitchen.label: CEFR A1
kitchen.def: A room where you prepare and cook food.
kitchen.ex: I cook breakfast in the kitchen every morning.

kite.label: CEFR A1
kite.def: A light toy made of paper or cloth that flies in the wind on a string.
kite.ex: The children flew their kites in the park on a windy day.

kitten.label: CEFR B1
kitten.def: A young domestic cat, usually from birth until about one year old.
kitten.ex: The kitten was playing with a ball of wool on the kitchen floor.

knee.label: CEFR A1
knee.def: The joint in the middle of your leg where it bends.
knee.ex: I fell down and hurt my knee.

kneel.label: CEFR B2
kneel.def: To lower yourself onto your knees, resting your weight on them.
kneel.ex: The athlete chose to kneel during the national anthem to protest racial injustice.

knife.label: CEFR A1
knife.def: A tool with a sharp metal blade used for cutting food or other things.
knife.ex: I used a knife to cut the bread for breakfast.

knit.label: CEFR B1
knit.def: To make cloth or clothing by looping yarn together with needles or a machine.
knit.ex: My grandmother taught me how to knit a scarf when I was eight years old.

knock.label: CEFR B1
knock.def: To hit a door or surface with your knuckles to get someone's attention.
knock.ex: I knocked on the office door before entering the meeting.

knot.label: CEFR B2
knot.def: A tight fastening made by tying rope, string, or cord, or a unit measuring nautical speed.
knot.ex: The sailor tied a secure knot in the rope before securing the boat to the dock.

know.label: CEFR A1
know.def: To have information or understand something in your mind.
know.ex: I know my friend's telephone number.

knowingly.label: CEFR B2
knowingly.def: Done deliberately or intentionally, with full awareness of what you are doing.
knowingly.ex: She knowingly submitted false information to the insurance company, which led to criminal charges.

knowledge.label: CEFR A2
knowledge.def: Information and understanding that you learn or know about something.
knowledge.ex: She has good knowledge of English grammar after studying for two years.

known.label: CEFR B2
known.def: Recognized or familiar to many people; famous or well-established
known.ex: She is a known expert in renewable energy and frequently advises government policy makers.

lab.label: CEFR B1
lab.def: A room where scientific experiments and research are performed using special equipment.
lab.ex: She worked in the chemistry lab to test different compounds and record the results.

labor/labour.label: CEFR A2
labor/labour.def: Hard physical or mental work, especially over a long time period.
labor/labour.ex: The construction workers' labour was difficult, but they were paid well for their efforts.

laboratory.label: CEFR B1
laboratory.def: A room or building where scientific experiments and tests are done by trained professionals.
laboratory.ex: The chemistry laboratory at our school has all the equipment we need for our experiments.

laborer/labourer.label: CEFR B2
laborer/labourer.def: A person who does physical work, often unskilled or semi-skilled, typically paid by the hour.
laborer/labourer.ex: The construction site employed dozens of labourers to excavate the foundations for the new building.

lack.label: CEFR B2
lack.def: To not have something that is needed or wanted.
lack.ex: The project failed because it lacked sufficient funding and experienced staff.

ladder.label: CEFR B1
ladder.def: A structure with steps for climbing up or down between different heights.
ladder.ex: She climbed the ladder to reach the books on the top shelf of the library.

lady.label: CEFR A1
lady.def: A woman, especially one who is polite or comes from a good family.
lady.ex: The lady at the shop helped me find my size.

lake.label: CEFR A2
lake.def: A large area of water surrounded by land, smaller than a sea or ocean.
lake.ex: We went swimming in the lake near our village last summer.

lamb.label: CEFR B1
lamb.def: A young sheep, or meat from a young sheep used as food.
lamb.ex: We ordered lamb chops at the restaurant, and they were cooked perfectly with herbs and garlic.

lame.label: CEFR B2
lame.def: Unable to walk normally due to injury or illness affecting the leg or foot.
lame.ex: After his fall from the horse, he was lame for several weeks and needed physiotherapy to recover.

lamp.label: CEFR A2
lamp.def: A device that produces light, usually with a bulb powered by electricity.
lamp.ex: She turned on the lamp beside her bed to read her book.

lance.label: CEFR B2
lance.def: A long, pointed spear weapon historically used by soldiers on horseback in battle
lance.ex: The medieval knight gripped his lance tightly as he charged towards his opponent on the tournament field.

land.label: CEFR A2
land.def: To come down from the sky and touch the ground.
land.ex: The aeroplane will land at the airport in ten minutes.

landing.label: CEFR B2
landing.def: The act of coming down and touching ground after flying or jumping through the air.
landing.ex: The pilot made a smooth landing despite the strong winds at the airport.

landlady.label: CEFR B2
landlady.def: A woman who owns a building or property and rents it to tenants for payment.
landlady.ex: The landlady raised our rent by £50 per month, giving us three months' notice as required by law.

landlord.label: CEFR B1
landlord.def: A person who owns a building or land and rents it to other people for money
landlord.ex: My landlord increased the rent on my flat by £50 per month starting next year.

landscape.label: CEFR A2
landscape.def: The scenery of a large area of land, including hills, trees, fields, and buildings
landscape.ex: The landscape of the Scottish mountains is beautiful in autumn.

landslide.label: CEFR B2
landslide.def: A sudden fall of earth or rocks down a steep slope, often causing damage.
landslide.ex: The heavy rainfall triggered a landslide that blocked the mountain road for several days.

lane.label: CEFR A2
lane.def: A narrow road or path, often in a town or between buildings
lane.ex: The old market is down a quiet lane near the church.

language.label: CEFR A1
language.def: Words and grammar that people in a country use to speak and write.
language.ex: English is the language spoken in Britain.

lap.label: CEFR B2
lap.def: The upper surface of the thighs and lower torso when sitting down.
lap.ex: She sat in the armchair with her laptop resting on her lap.

laptop.label: CEFR B2
laptop.def: A portable personal computer with a screen and keyboard that folds together for easy transport.
laptop.ex: She balanced her laptop on her knee whilst working on the train during her commute.

large.label: CEFR A1
large.ex: She lives in a large house with five bedrooms.

largely.label: CEFR B1
largely.def: mostly or mainly; to a great degree or extent
largely.ex: The project succeeded largely because of her hard work and dedication.

lark.label: CEFR B2
lark.def: A small wild bird known for its beautiful song, or a playful adventure undertaken for fun and amusement.
lark.ex: The students decided to skip school as a lark, but they were caught and given detention.

laser.label: CEFR B2
laser.def: A device that produces a very narrow beam of intense light, used in medicine, industry, and technology.
laser.ex: The surgeon used a laser to precisely remove the tumour without damaging surrounding tissue.

last.label: CEFR B1
last.def: To continue existing or being in use for a particular length of time.
last.ex: The battery should last for about eight hours before needing to be recharged.

last minute.label: CEFR B2
last minute.def: The final moment before a deadline or event when something is done or decided.
last minute.ex: She submitted her assignment at the last minute, just before the midnight deadline.

last name.label: CEFR A1
last name.def: Your family name, shared with other family members.
last name.ex: My last name is Smith, and my first name is Emma.

lasting.label: CEFR B1
lasting.def: Continuing for a long time or having a strong permanent effect.
lasting.ex: The book made a lasting impression on her, and she still thinks about it years later.

lastly.label: CEFR B2
lastly.def: Finally; at the end, after mentioning other things previously.
lastly.ex: The report covered three main issues: budget constraints, staffing problems, and lastly, the need for improved infrastructure.

last-minute.label: CEFR B2
last-minute.def: Happening or done very close to a deadline, with little time to prepare or plan.
last-minute.ex: She made a last-minute decision to change her university choice just before the application deadline.

late.label: CEFR A1
late.def: After the expected or planned time; not on time
late.ex: The bus arrived late this morning.

latecomer.label: CEFR B1
latecomer.def: A person who arrives after the expected or scheduled time.
latecomer.ex: As a latecomer to the meeting, she missed the important announcement about the project deadline.

lately.label: CEFR B1
lately.def: Recently; in the time period not long ago
lately.ex: She hasn't been feeling well lately, so she stayed home from work yesterday.

later.label: CEFR A1
later.def: At a time in the future, not now
later.ex: I'm busy now, but I can help you later.

latest.label: CEFR A2
latest.def: Most recent; the newest or most up-to-date version
latest.ex: Have you seen the latest episode of that TV show?

latter.label: CEFR A2
latter.def: The second of two people or things just mentioned
latter.ex: You can choose tea or coffee; I prefer the latter.

laugh.label: CEFR A1
laugh.def: To make happy sounds from your mouth when something is funny
laugh.ex: The children laugh when they watch the cartoon.

laughter.label: CEFR B1
laughter.def: The sound people make when they find something funny or amusing.
laughter.ex: The audience's laughter filled the theatre during the comedian's funniest jokes.

launch.label: CEFR B1
launch.def: To start or introduce something new, like a business, product, or project, officially
launch.ex: The company will launch its new smartphone next month with a big presentation.

laundry.label: CEFR B2
laundry.def: Clothes and other items that need washing, or the act of washing them.
laundry.ex: She sorted her laundry into whites and colours before putting a load in the washing machine.

lava.label: CEFR B2
lava.def: Molten rock that flows from a volcano onto Earth's surface during an eruption.
lava.ex: Scientists study lava flows to understand volcanic behaviour and predict future eruptions.

law.label: CEFR A2
law.def: An official rule that everyone in a country or place must follow.
law.ex: The new law says that drivers must wear seatbelts in cars.

lawful.label: CEFR B1
lawful.def: Allowed or approved by the law; not against legal rules.
lawful.ex: The company's business practices are lawful and follow all government regulations.

lawmaker.label: CEFR B2
lawmaker.def: A person elected to a government position who creates and votes on laws for a country or region.
lawmaker.ex: The lawmakers debated the new environmental legislation for several hours before taking a vote.

lawn.label: CEFR B2
lawn.def: An area of grass that is regularly mowed, typically in front of or around a house.
lawn.ex: The family spent the afternoon playing croquet on their manicured lawn whilst the sun shone.

lawsuit.label: CEFR B2
lawsuit.def: A legal case brought by one person or organisation against another in court to settle a disagreement.
lawsuit.ex: The company filed a lawsuit against its former employee for breach of contract.

lawyer.label: CEFR A2
lawyer.def: A person who studies and practises law, helping people with legal problems and giving legal advice.
lawyer.ex: My sister wants to be a lawyer because she enjoys helping people solve their problems.

lay.label: CEFR B1
lay.def: To place or put something down in a flat or horizontal position
lay.ex: She laid the blanket on the grass for the picnic.

layabout.label: CEFR B2
layabout.def: A person who avoids work and spends their time doing nothing productive.
layabout.ex: He was dismissed from his job for being a lazy layabout who never completed his assignments.

laziness.label: CEFR A2
laziness.def: Not wanting to work or make effort; being unwilling to do things.
laziness.ex: His laziness meant he never finished his homework on time.

lazy.label: CEFR A1
lazy.def: Not wanting to work or make effort; preferring to rest or do nothing.
lazy.ex: He is lazy and does not want to help with the homework.

lead.label: CEFR B1
lead.def: To guide or direct someone, or to be in first place in a competition or activity.
lead.ex: The captain will lead the team to victory in tomorrow's match.

leader.label: CEFR A1
leader.def: A person who is in charge of a group, team, or organization.
leader.ex: The team leader helped us finish the project on time.

leadership.label: CEFR B1
leadership.def: The skill of guiding and influencing a group of people toward a goal.
leadership.ex: Her strong leadership helped the team win the competition despite many difficulties.

leading.label: CEFR B1
leading.def: Most important or in first position among others; having the main role or influence.
leading.ex: She plays the leading role in the school play, and everyone is excited to watch her perform.

leaf.label: CEFR A1
leaf.def: A green flat part of a plant or tree that grows from branches.
leaf.ex: In autumn, the leaves turn brown and fall from the trees.

leaflet.label: CEFR B2
leaflet.def: A small printed paper, usually folded, containing information or advertisement about something.
leaflet.ex: The campaign leaflet was distributed to thousands of households during the election.

league.label: CEFR B1
league.def: A group of sports teams that compete against each other in a formal competition.
league.ex: Manchester United plays in the Premier League, which is the top football division in England.

leak.label: CEFR B2
leak.def: To accidentally allow liquid, gas, or information to escape through a hole or crack.
leak.ex: The confidential report leaked to the press before the official announcement was made.

lean.label: CEFR B2
lean.def: To incline or move the upper body towards something; to rest against something at an angle.
lean.ex: She leaned against the wall, watching the crowd pass by.

leap.label: CEFR B1
leap.def: To jump a long distance or high into the air with force.
leap.ex: The athlete leaped over the high jump bar with ease.

learn.label: CEFR A1
learn.def: To get knowledge or skill by studying or practising.
learn.ex: I learn English at school every day.

learner.label: CEFR A2
learner.def: A person who is studying or gaining knowledge about something.
learner.ex: She is a quick learner and understands new things very easily.

learning.label: CEFR B2
learning.def: The process of acquiring knowledge, skills, or understanding through study, experience, or instruction.
learning.ex: Her learning of Japanese improved significantly after she spent a year living in Tokyo.

lease.label: CEFR B2
lease.def: A legal contract allowing you to rent property or equipment for a fixed period
lease.ex: She signed a three-year lease for the flat before moving to London.

leash.label: CEFR B2
leash.def: A rope or strap attached to a collar to control and restrain a dog or other animal.
leash.ex: The dog pulled hard on its leash as we walked past the park where other dogs were playing.

least.label: CEFR B1
least.def: The smallest amount or degree; used to say something is minimum or lowest
least.ex: You should at least read the instructions before starting the test.

leather.label: CEFR A2
leather.def: Material made from animal skin, used for making bags, shoes, and jackets.
leather.ex: She bought a new leather jacket for the winter.

leave.label: CEFR A1
leave.def: To go away from a place or person
leave.ex: I leave home at 8 o'clock every morning.

lecture.label: CEFR B1
lecture.def: A formal talk given by an expert to teach students about a subject.
lecture.ex: The professor's lecture on European history was so interesting that nobody fell asleep.

lecturer.label: CEFR B2
lecturer.def: A person who teaches university students, usually on a specific subject at scheduled times.
lecturer.ex: The chemistry lecturer explained the complex molecular structures using detailed diagrams on the whiteboard.

leek.label: CEFR B2
leek.def: A vegetable with white and light green parts, similar to an onion but with a milder flavour.
leek.ex: The chef added finely sliced leeks to the potato soup for a subtle, onion-like taste.

left.label: CEFR A2
left.def: The side of your body opposite to your right side.
left.ex: She held her bag in her left hand.

left-hand.label: CEFR A2
