left-hand.def: On or for the side of your body where your left hand is.
left-hand.ex: He writes with his left hand, so he needs a left-hand desk at school.

leg.label: CEFR A1
leg.def: One of two body parts below your hips that you use to walk and stand.
leg.ex: She broke her leg playing football.

legally.label: CEFR B1
legally.def: In a way that is allowed by the law or done according to legal rules.
legally.ex: She legally changed her name after getting married.

legend.label: CEFR B2
legend.def: A traditional story about a person or event, often considered partly historical but not entirely factual.
legend.ex: The novel retells the legend of King Arthur and his knights at Camelot.

legendary.label: CEFR B2
legendary.def: Famous throughout history; widely known and remembered for being exceptional or remarkable.
legendary.ex: The legendary footballer Pelé scored over 1,000 goals during his career.

legislation.label: CEFR B2
legislation.def: Laws or rules made officially by a government or parliament.
legislation.ex: The government proposed new legislation to reduce carbon emissions and combat climate change.

legislative.label: CEFR B2
legislative.def: Relating to the process of making or passing laws in government.
legislative.ex: The legislative branch of government is responsible for creating new laws that affect all citizens.

legislator.label: CEFR B2
legislator.def: A person elected or appointed to make and pass laws for a country or region.
legislator.ex: The legislator proposed a new bill to improve public transportation infrastructure across the city.

legislature.label: CEFR B2
legislature.def: The body of elected officials responsible for making and passing laws in a country or region.
legislature.ex: The state legislature debated the new education bill for several weeks before voting on it.

legitimate.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
legitimate.def: Valid, lawful, or based on proper authority and fair reasoning.
legitimate.ex: The company's concerns about data privacy are legitimate and deserve serious consideration.

leisure.label: CEFR A2
leisure.def: Free time when you are not working and can enjoy yourself.
leisure.ex: On weekends, I have more leisure time to spend with my family and friends.

leisurely.label: CEFR B2
leisurely.def: In a slow, relaxed manner without hurrying or feeling rushed.
leisurely.ex: After retiring, she began taking leisurely walks through the countryside every morning.

lemon.label: CEFR A2
lemon.def: A yellow citrus fruit with a sour taste, used in cooking and drinks
lemon.ex: I put a slice of lemon in my tea to make it taste better.

lemonade.label: CEFR A2
lemonade.def: A sweet cold drink made from lemon juice, water, and sugar.
lemonade.ex: On hot days, I like to drink homemade lemonade with ice.

lend.label: CEFR A2
lend.def: To give something to someone for a short time, expecting them to return it.
lend.ex: Can you lend me your pencil? I promise to give it back tomorrow.

length.label: CEFR B1
length.def: The measurement of how long something is from one end to the other.
length.ex: The length of the swimming pool is 50 metres, so it takes me about two minutes to swim from one end to the other.

lengthen.label: CEFR B2
lengthen.def: To make something longer or extend its duration.
lengthen.ex: The government decided to lengthen the school day by thirty minutes to improve student achievement.

lengthy.label: CEFR B2
lengthy.def: Taking a lot of time; long in duration or extent.
lengthy.ex: After a lengthy investigation, the police finally identified the suspect responsible for the robbery.

leopard.label: CEFR B2
leopard.def: A large wild cat with a spotted coat, found in Africa and Asia, known for climbing trees and hunting at night.
leopard.ex: The leopard dragged its prey into the tree to keep it safe from scavengers on the ground.

less.label: CEFR A2
less.def: a smaller amount or degree of something; not as much
less.ex: This coffee has less sugar than the other one.

lessen.label: CEFR B1
lessen.def: To make something smaller or less strong in amount or degree.
lessen.ex: Taking regular breaks can lessen your stress and help you work more effectively.

lesson.label: CEFR A1
lesson.def: A class or period of time when someone teaches you something
lesson.ex: My English lesson starts at nine o'clock in the morning.

let.label: CEFR A1
let.def: To allow someone to do something.
let.ex: My mum let me stay up late yesterday.

letter.label: CEFR A1
letter.def: A written or printed message sent to someone, usually in an envelope by post
letter.ex: I received a letter from my grandmother yesterday.

lettuce.label: CEFR B1
lettuce.def: A green vegetable with large leaves, commonly eaten raw in salads.
lettuce.ex: I made a fresh salad with lettuce, tomatoes, and a light vinaigrette for lunch.

level.label: CEFR B2
level.def: To make something flat, even, or at the same height as something else.
level.ex: The construction workers levelled the ground before building the new car park.

liar.label: CEFR B1
liar.def: A person who deliberately tells things that are not true.
liar.ex: He claimed he was sick, but his friends knew he was a liar because they saw him playing football.

liberate.label: CEFR B1
liberate.def: To free someone or something from control, imprisonment, or an oppressive situation.
liberate.ex: The army worked to liberate the occupied territory from enemy forces.

liberation.label: CEFR B1
liberation.def: The process of freeing people from oppression, control, or an unwanted situation
liberation.ex: The liberation of the country from foreign rule was celebrated with parades and festivals throughout the nation.

liberty.label: CEFR A2
liberty.def: The freedom to do what you want without restrictions from others or the government.
liberty.ex: In many countries, people have the liberty to choose their own religion.

librarian.label: CEFR B1
librarian.def: A person who works in a library, helping people find books and managing library resources.
librarian.ex: The librarian recommended several novels that matched my interests in science fiction.

library.label: CEFR A1
library.def: A place with many books that people can borrow and read.
library.ex: I go to the library every Saturday to get new books.

license/licence.label: CEFR A2
license/licence.def: To officially permit someone to do something or use something
license/licence.ex: The government licensed the new restaurant to serve alcohol.

lick.label: CEFR B2
lick.def: To pass your tongue over a surface, or to defeat someone completely in a competition.
lick.ex: The dog licked its owner's face excitedly when she came home from work.

lid.label: CEFR B2
lid.def: A removable or hinged cover that closes the opening of a container, pot, or box
lid.ex: She lifted the lid off the saucepan to check if the pasta was cooked.

lie.label: CEFR A2
lie.def: To say something that is not true.
lie.ex: He lied about his age when he applied for the job.

life.label: CEFR A1
life.def: The time from birth until death; being alive.
life.ex: She has lived a happy life with her family.

lifeguard.label: CEFR B1
lifeguard.def: A person trained to watch swimmers and rescue people in danger at beaches or swimming pools.
lifeguard.ex: The lifeguard sat on the high chair, scanning the beach for any swimmers who needed help.

lifeless.label: CEFR B2
lifeless.def: Without life, energy, or vitality; appearing dead or completely lacking animation
lifeless.ex: The abandoned factory stood lifeless in the grey morning light, its windows broken and machinery rusted.

lifelong.label: CEFR B1
lifelong.def: Continuing or lasting for the whole of someone's life.
lifelong.ex: Learning a new language can become a lifelong hobby that brings joy for many years.

lifestyle.label: CEFR A2
lifestyle.def: The way a person lives, including their daily habits, activities, and choices
lifestyle.ex: She has a very healthy lifestyle because she exercises every day and eats well.

lifetime.label: CEFR B2
lifetime.def: The period of time from someone's birth until their death.
lifetime.ex: She had achieved more success in her lifetime than most people could hope for.

lift.label: CEFR B1
lift.def: To raise or move something upward using force or effort.
lift.ex: She lifted the heavy box onto the shelf with difficulty.

light.label: CEFR B1
light.def: To start a fire or make something burn, or to turn on an electric lamp
light.ex: She lit the candles on the birthday cake and everyone sang.

lighten.label: CEFR B2
lighten.def: To make something less heavy, serious, or dark; to reduce the weight or burden of something.
lighten.ex: The government decided to lighten tax regulations for small businesses to help them grow.

lighter.label: CEFR B1
lighter.def: A small device used to produce a flame for lighting cigarettes or candles.
lighter.ex: He pulled out his lighter to light the candles on the birthday cake.

lighting.label: CEFR B2
lighting.def: Arrangement and use of lights in a space or artistic production to create effect
lighting.ex: The theatre's dramatic lighting transformed the simple stage into a mysterious forest.

lightly.label: CEFR B1
lightly.def: With little weight, force, or intensity; gently or delicately.
lightly.ex: She touched his arm lightly to get his attention without startling him.

lightning.label: CEFR B1
lightning.def: A bright flash of electricity in the sky during a storm, followed by thunder.
lightning.ex: The lightning struck a tree during the thunderstorm last night.

like.label: CEFR A1
like.def: To enjoy or find something pleasant
like.ex: I like chocolate ice cream very much.

likely.label: CEFR A2
likely.def: Probably going to happen or be true; having a good chance of occurring
likely.ex: It is likely to rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella with you.

likeness.label: CEFR B1
likeness.def: How similar two people or things are to each other in appearance or qualities.
likeness.ex: There is a strong likeness between the two sisters, though their personalities are quite different.

lily.label: CEFR A1
lily.def: A large flower with six petals, often white or colourful, with a pleasant smell.
lily.ex: My grandmother has beautiful white lilies in her garden.

limb.label: CEFR B2
limb.def: An arm or leg; a major body part extending from the trunk used for movement.
limb.ex: The accident left him with injuries to his right limb, requiring several months of physiotherapy.

limit.label: CEFR B1
limit.def: To set the greatest amount or number that is allowed.
limit.ex: The school limits the number of students in each class to thirty.

limitation.label: CEFR B1
limitation.def: A restriction or boundary that prevents something from being unlimited or perfect.
limitation.ex: The main limitation of this smartphone is its battery life, which only lasts one day.

limited.label: CEFR B1
limited.def: Restricted in amount, quantity, or extent; not large or unlimited
limited.ex: The restaurant has only limited seating, so you should book a table in advance.

limp.label: CEFR B1
limp.def: To walk with difficulty because one leg or foot is injured or painful
limp.ex: After spraining his ankle during football, he had to limp home slowly.

line.label: CEFR B2
line.def: To cover the inside surface of something with a protective or decorative layer.
line.ex: The baker lined the cake tin with parchment paper before pouring in the batter.

linen.label: CEFR B2
linen.def: A fabric made from the fibres of the flax plant, known for being cool and durable.
linen.ex: She chose a linen shirt for the summer because it's breathable and keeps her cool in hot weather.

liner.label: CEFR B2
liner.def: A large passenger ship that operates regular scheduled routes between ports.
liner.ex: She booked a cabin on the cruise liner for a two-week voyage across the Atlantic.

ling.label: CEFR B2
ling.def: A common sea fish with an elongated body, similar to cod, found in North Atlantic waters.
ling.ex: The fishmonger recommended fresh ling for making a delicious fish pie.

linger.label: CEFR B2
linger.def: To stay in a place longer than necessary, or to remain present in a slow, reluctant way.
linger.ex: The smell of fresh bread lingered in the kitchen long after breakfast was finished.

lion.label: CEFR A1
lion.def: A large, wild cat with golden fur, found in Africa.
lion.ex: The lion is the king of the jungle.

lip.label: CEFR A2
lip.def: Either of the two soft pink or red parts that form the edge of your mouth.
lip.ex: She wore red lipstick on her lips for the party.

liquid.label: CEFR B1
liquid.def: A substance that flows freely and takes the shape of its container, like water or milk.
liquid.ex: The doctor asked me to drink plenty of clear liquids after the surgery.

list.label: CEFR B2
list.def: To write down or mention a series of items or names one after another.
list.ex: The report lists all the major cities affected by the recent flooding.

listen.label: CEFR A1
listen.def: To pay attention to sound; to hear someone or something carefully.
listen.ex: Please listen to your teacher in class.

listener.label: CEFR B2
listener.def: A person who hears or pays attention to sound, especially music, podcasts, or radio broadcasts.
listener.ex: The radio station's evening programme attracts millions of listeners from across the country.

liter/litre.label: CEFR A2
liter/litre.def: A unit of measurement for liquids; about the amount of a large bottle of water.
liter/litre.ex: This bottle contains one litre of milk.

literal.label: CEFR B2
literal.def: Using the exact meaning of words without exaggeration or metaphor; actually true or real.
literal.ex: The term 'drowning in paperwork' is not literal; it simply means having too much work to do.

literally.label: CEFR B2
literally.def: In the exact sense of the word, without exaggeration or metaphor.
literally.ex: The company literally doubled its profits in just one year, according to the financial report.

literary.label: CEFR B1
literary.def: Relating to books, writing, and serious written works rather than everyday language
literary.ex: Her literary style is influenced by classic authors she studied at university.

literature.label: CEFR B1
literature.def: Written works such as novels, poems, and plays that are valued for their artistic and creative quality.
literature.ex: She studied English literature at university and learned about famous authors from different centuries.

litter.label: CEFR B2
litter.def: Rubbish or waste materials left lying scattered in a public place.
litter.ex: The park was full of litter after the festival, with discarded bottles and food wrappers everywhere.

little.label: CEFR A1
little.def: In a small amount; not much
little.ex: She drinks very little coffee in the morning.

live.label: CEFR A1
live.def: To have your home in a place; to exist or stay somewhere
live.ex: I live in London with my family.

lively.label: CEFR A2
lively.def: Full of energy, movement, and excitement; not quiet or still.
lively.ex: The party was very lively, with music, dancing, and lots of happy people.

liver.label: CEFR B1
liver.def: Large organ in your body that cleans your blood and helps digest food.
liver.ex: The doctor said he should eat more vegetables to keep his liver healthy.

living.label: CEFR B2
living.def: The money or income someone earns to pay for food, shelter, and other basic needs.
living.ex: She earns a comfortable living as a graphic designer in London.

living room.label: CEFR A1
living room.def: The main room in a house where a family sits together to relax and watch television.
living room.ex: We have a sofa and armchairs in our living room.

load.label: CEFR B2
load.def: To put cargo, goods, or people onto a vehicle, ship, or container for transport
load.ex: The workers loaded the truck with boxes of supplies before driving to the warehouse.

loaf.label: CEFR B1
loaf.def: A large piece of bread baked in a single mass, usually shaped like a rectangle.
loaf.ex: She bought a fresh loaf of bread from the bakery this morning.

loan.label: CEFR B2
loan.def: Money borrowed from a bank or lender that must be repaid with interest over time.
loan.ex: She took out a personal loan to finance her backpacking trip across Southeast Asia.

lobby.label: CEFR B2
lobby.def: A group seeking to influence politicians or decisions about a particular issue.
lobby.ex: Environmental lobbies have pressured the government to ban single-use plastics.

local.label: CEFR A2
local.def: Belonging to or affecting a particular area or town, not the whole country.
local.ex: I buy vegetables from the local market near my house.

location.label: CEFR B1
location.def: A particular place or position where something is situated or where an event happens.
location.ex: The hotel's location near the beach makes it very popular with tourists.

lock.label: CEFR B1
lock.def: To fasten something with a key or code so it cannot be opened.
lock.ex: Don't forget to lock the door when you leave the house.

lodge.label: CEFR B1
lodge.def: A small house or cottage, often in a rural or mountain area used for holidays or outdoor activities.
lodge.ex: We rented a cosy lodge in the Scottish Highlands for our winter skiing holiday.

log.label: CEFR B1
log.def: A thick piece of wood cut from a tree, or a written record of events and activities.
log.ex: The captain kept a detailed log of the ship's journey across the Atlantic.

logical.label: CEFR A2
logical.def: Following a clear and sensible pattern or reason; making sense.
logical.ex: It is logical to wear a coat when it is cold outside.

logo.label: CEFR B1
logo.def: A symbol or design that represents a company or organisation
logo.ex: The Nike logo is one of the most recognisable symbols in the world.

loneliness.label: CEFR B2
loneliness.def: The painful feeling of being isolated or lacking meaningful connection with others.
loneliness.ex: Despite living in a crowded city, she struggled with loneliness after her friends moved abroad.

lonely.label: CEFR A1
lonely.def: Sad because you are alone and have no friends with you.
lonely.ex: She felt lonely when all her classmates went home.

long.label: CEFR A2
long.def: For a great amount of time; lasting many hours or days
long.ex: She studied long into the night to prepare for her exam.

long-distance.label: CEFR B2
long-distance.def: Covering or involving a great physical distance or extended period of time between two points.
long-distance.ex: She maintained a long-distance relationship with her partner whilst studying abroad for two years.

long-term.label: CEFR B2
long-term.def: Continuing or planned to last for an extended period, often several years or more.
long-term.ex: The company has invested in long-term research to develop sustainable energy solutions.

look.label: CEFR A1
look.def: To turn your eyes towards something to see it.
look.ex: Look at that beautiful bird in the tree!

loose.label: CEFR A2
loose.def: Not tight or firmly fixed; able to move or come apart easily.
loose.ex: My shoe is loose so I need to tie the laces tighter.

loosely.label: CEFR B2
loosely.def: In a way that is not strictly accurate, precise, or firmly fastened; approximately.
loosely.ex: The term 'artificial intelligence' is loosely used to describe any computer system that mimics human reasoning.

lorry.label: CEFR B1
lorry.def: A large vehicle used to carry goods and materials over long distances.
lorry.ex: The lorry delivered boxes of supplies to the warehouse early this morning.

lose.label: CEFR A2
lose.def: To no longer have something because you cannot find it or it is taken away.
lose.ex: I lost my keys yesterday and had to search everywhere for them.

loss.label: CEFR B1
loss.def: The state of no longer having something that you had before, or the thing that is gone.
loss.ex: The company reported a significant financial loss after the factory fire destroyed their equipment.

lost.label: CEFR A2
lost.def: Unable to find your way or know where you are
lost.ex: I was lost in the city because I didn't have a map.

lot.label: CEFR A2
lot.def: A large amount or number of something.
lot.ex: She has a lot of books on her shelf.

lottery.label: CEFR B1
lottery.def: A game where people pay money for a chance to win a large prize by random selection.
lottery.ex: She bought a lottery ticket and dreamed of winning enough money to travel around the world.

loud.label: CEFR B1
loud.def: In a way that produces strong sound; with high volume or noise level
loud.ex: She spoke loudly during the meeting so everyone in the back could hear her clearly.

loudly.label: CEFR A2
loudly.def: With a high volume of sound; in a way that makes a lot of noise.
loudly.ex: She spoke loudly so everyone in the classroom could hear her.

loudspeaker.label: CEFR B1
loudspeaker.def: A device that makes sound much louder so people can hear it from far away
loudspeaker.ex: The teacher used a loudspeaker to announce the school assembly to all students in the playground.

lousy.label: CEFR B2
lousy.def: Very poor in quality; extremely bad or unpleasant
lousy.ex: The film received lousy reviews from critics, who complained about the weak plot and unconvincing acting.

love.label: CEFR A1
love.def: To have strong feelings of affection and care for someone or something.
love.ex: I love my family very much.

lovely.label: CEFR A1
lovely.def: Very nice, beautiful, or pleasant; makes you feel happy.
lovely.ex: We had a lovely day at the beach yesterday.

lover.label: CEFR A2
lover.def: A person with whom someone has a romantic or sexual relationship.
lover.ex: She introduced her lover to her family at the dinner party.

lovingly.label: CEFR B1
lovingly.def: In a way that shows care, affection, and tenderness towards someone or something.
lovingly.ex: She lovingly prepared her grandmother's favourite recipe, remembering every detail from her childhood.

low.label: CEFR A2
low.def: Not high; at or near the ground or bottom
low.ex: Speak in a low voice so you don't wake the baby.

lower.label: CEFR B2
lower.def: To move something down to a position that is not as high as before.
lower.ex: The government decided to lower interest rates to stimulate economic growth.

lowland.label: CEFR B2
lowland.def: Area of land that is much lower in elevation than surrounding hills or mountains.
lowland.ex: The lowlands of Scotland are known for their agricultural productivity and gentler terrain compared to the Highlands.

loyal.label: CEFR B1
loyal.def: Always supporting and faithful to someone or something over time.
loyal.ex: She has been a loyal customer of that shop for over ten years.

loyalty.label: CEFR B1
loyalty.def: Strong support for and faithfulness to a person, group, or organisation.
loyalty.ex: The company rewards employee loyalty with good salaries and career opportunities.

luck.label: CEFR A1
luck.def: When good or bad things happen to you by chance, not because you planned them.
luck.ex: I had good luck and found my lost keys under the sofa.

luckily.label: CEFR A2
luckily.def: by good chance; something good happened that could have been bad
luckily.ex: Luckily, I found my keys just before leaving for work.

lucky.label: CEFR A1
lucky.def: Having good things happen by chance, without planning or effort.
lucky.ex: I was lucky and found 5 pounds on the street.

lucrative.label: CEFR B2
lucrative.def: Producing or resulting in considerable profit or financial gain.
lucrative.ex: She left her teaching job to pursue a more lucrative career in management consulting.

luggage.label: CEFR B1
luggage.def: Bags and cases that you carry your clothes and belongings in when travelling
luggage.ex: When we arrived at the airport, we waited at baggage claim to collect our luggage from the conveyor belt.

lunch.label: CEFR A1
lunch.def: A meal you eat in the middle of the day, usually around midday.
lunch.ex: I eat lunch at school at 12 o'clock.

lunchtime.label: CEFR A2
lunchtime.def: The time during the middle of the day when people stop work or school to eat lunch.
lunchtime.ex: We usually play football with our friends at lunchtime.

lung.label: CEFR B1
lung.def: One of two organs in your chest that you use to breathe air in and out.
lung.ex: Smoking can seriously damage your lungs and cause health problems later in life.

lush.label: CEFR B2
lush.def: Containing abundant greenery or vegetation; thick and healthy in growth.
lush.ex: The lush gardens of the countryside were filled with colourful flowers and dense foliage.

luxurious.label: CEFR B2
luxurious.def: Expensive and very comfortable, designed to provide the highest quality and pleasure.
luxurious.ex: The luxurious hotel suite featured a marble bathroom, silk furnishings, and views of the Mediterranean.

luxury.label: CEFR B1
luxury.def: Something expensive and comfortable that is enjoyable but not necessary for living
luxury.ex: Staying in a five-star hotel is a luxury that most families can only afford on holiday.

lyric.label: CEFR A2
lyric.def: Related to a short poem or song; expressing personal feelings and emotions.
lyric.ex: She wrote a lyric poem about missing her family.

lyrics.label: CEFR B2
lyrics.def: The words of a song, typically written by a songwriter to be sung to music.
lyrics.ex: The lyrics of that protest song really resonated with audiences during the civil rights movement.

machine.label: CEFR A1
machine.def: A device with moving parts that does work using power or energy.
machine.ex: The washing machine cleans our clothes.

mad.label: CEFR A2
mad.def: Very angry; feeling strong anger about something.
mad.ex: She was mad at her brother because he broke her phone.

madam.label: CEFR B2
madam.def: A polite form of address for a woman, used in formal or professional contexts.
madam.ex: Madam, your reservation has been confirmed for 7 PM this evening.

madame.label: CEFR B2
madame.def: A polite form of address for a woman, especially in formal or professional contexts.
madame.ex: Madame, your reservation is ready at the restaurant.

madness.label: CEFR B2
madness.def: Extreme foolishness or a state of being mentally unwell and behaving irrationally.
madness.ex: The sudden market collapse sparked a madness of panic selling among investors.

maestro.label: CEFR B2
maestro.def: A highly skilled conductor or expert in classical music, especially one who leads orchestras with exceptional mastery.
maestro.ex: The renowned maestro conducted the symphony with such precision that every musician moved in perfect unison.

magazine.label: CEFR A1
magazine.def: A thick paper publication with pictures and stories, usually published every week or month.
magazine.ex: I read a magazine about animals on the bus.

magic.label: CEFR A2
magic.def: The use of special powers to make impossible things happen, like in tricks or stories.
magic.ex: The children watched the magician perform magic tricks at the birthday party.

magical.label: CEFR B1
magical.def: Having special, wonderful, or enchanting qualities that seem impossible or hard to explain.
magical.ex: The sunset over the ocean created a magical moment that we'll never forget.

magician.label: CEFR B1
magician.def: A person who performs tricks that seem to make impossible things happen for entertainment.
magician.ex: The magician pulled a rabbit out of his empty hat and amazed everyone in the audience.

magnificent.label: CEFR B1
magnificent.def: Extremely impressive, beautiful, or grand in appearance or quality.
magnificent.ex: The magnificent palace had high ceilings, golden decorations, and hundreds of rooms.

magnify.label: CEFR B2
magnify.def: To make something appear larger or more important than it actually is
magnify.ex: The microscope magnifies tiny cells so scientists can study them in detail.

mail.label: CEFR B1
mail.def: To send a letter or package by post office to someone's address
mail.ex: I mailed the application form yesterday, so it should arrive next week.

mailbox.label: CEFR B1
mailbox.def: A container where letters and packages are delivered and kept until collected
mailbox.ex: I checked my mailbox this morning and found a letter from the university.

main.label: CEFR B1
main.def: Most important or significant; the primary one among several.
main.ex: The main reason she moved to London was to find a better job.

main course.label: CEFR A2
main course.def: The largest and most important dish of a meal, served after the starter.
main course.ex: For the main course, we had grilled chicken with vegetables and rice.

mainly.label: CEFR A2
mainly.def: For the most part; mostly or in large amounts.
mainly.ex: I eat healthy food, mainly vegetables and fruit.

maintenance.label: CEFR B1
maintenance.def: Regular work done to keep something in good condition and prevent damage.
maintenance.ex: The building requires regular maintenance to prevent problems with the roof and walls.

majority.label: CEFR B1
majority.def: More than half of a total number or amount of people or things.
majority.ex: The majority of students in the class prefer online learning to studying in person.

make.label: CEFR A1
make.def: To create or produce something new.
make.ex: I make breakfast every morning for my family.

maker.label: CEFR A2
maker.def: A person who produces or creates something, often as their job.
maker.ex: My father is a furniture maker who builds tables and chairs in his workshop.

make-up.label: CEFR A2
make-up.def: Coloured powders and creams you put on your face to look better or different
make-up.ex: She wore make-up to the party because she wanted to look nice.

malady.label: CEFR B2
malady.def: A disease or illness, especially a chronic or serious one affecting health or society
malady.ex: The doctor diagnosed the patient's malady as a rare autoimmune disorder that had gone undetected for years.

malaria.label: CEFR B2
malaria.def: A serious infectious disease spread by mosquitoes, causing fever and often affecting the blood.
malaria.ex: Travellers heading to tropical regions should take precautions against malaria and consult their doctor before departure.

male.label: CEFR A2
male.def: A person or animal that is a man or boy, not a woman or girl.
male.ex: My brother is a male, and my sister is a female.

mall.label: CEFR A2
mall.def: A large building with many shops and restaurants in one place.
mall.ex: My friends and I go to the shopping mall on Saturday to buy clothes and eat lunch.

mama/mamma.label: CEFR B2
mama/mamma.def: A woman's mother, or a term of address used by children for their mother.
mama/mamma.ex: She called her mama every Sunday to ask how she was doing and share news about her week.

man.label: CEFR A1
man.ex: The man is wearing a blue shirt.

manage.label: CEFR A2
manage.def: To be in charge of something or make something work well.
manage.ex: She manages the team at the restaurant and decides what they do each day.

management.label: CEFR B1
management.def: The process of organising and controlling people or resources to achieve goals.
management.ex: Good management at the company helps employees work more efficiently and feel satisfied with their jobs.

manager.label: CEFR A2
manager.def: A person who is responsible for organising and directing the work of other employees.
manager.ex: The manager asked the team to finish the project by Friday.

mango.label: CEFR B2
mango.def: A tropical fruit with smooth skin, sweet orange flesh, and a large flat stone inside.
mango.ex: The mango harvest in India typically begins in late spring and produces some of the world's finest varieties.

mankind.label: CEFR B1
mankind.def: All human beings considered as one group, including both men and women.
mankind.ex: Throughout history, mankind has faced many challenges that required cooperation to overcome.

manner.label: CEFR A2
manner.def: The way someone behaves or does something.
manner.ex: She speaks in a friendly manner and everyone likes talking to her.

mansion.label: CEFR B2
mansion.def: A very large and impressive house, usually owned by a wealthy person.
mansion.ex: The billionaire's mansion overlooked the city with its marble columns and sprawling gardens.

manual.label: CEFR B2
manual.def: A book containing instructions on how to operate or use a machine or device.
manual.ex: She consulted the car manual to understand how to reset the warning light on the dashboard.

manufacture.label: CEFR B2
manufacture.def: To make goods in large quantities using machinery and labour in factories.
manufacture.ex: The company manufactures electronic components for smartphones in their facilities across Asia.

manufacturer.label: CEFR B2
manufacturer.def: A company or person that makes goods in large quantities, typically using machines and factories.
manufacturer.ex: The car manufacturer announced it would invest £500 million in developing electric vehicles over the next five years.

manufacturing.label: CEFR B2
manufacturing.def: The large-scale production of goods in factories using machinery and labour.
manufacturing.ex: The manufacturing sector in Germany employs over three million people across various industries.

map.label: CEFR A1
map.def: A drawing or picture showing the layout of a place or area from above.
map.ex: I looked at a map to find the way to the library.

maple.label: CEFR B1
maple.def: A large tree with colourful leaves in autumn, native to North America and Asia.
maple.ex: The maple trees in the park turn bright red and gold every October.

marathon.label: CEFR B2
marathon.def: A long-distance running race covering 42.195 kilometres, or any lengthy, exhausting undertaking.
marathon.ex: She trained for six months before completing her first marathon in under four hours.

marble.label: CEFR B1
marble.def: A hard stone used for building and sculpture, or a small coloured glass ball used in children's games.
marble.ex: The sculptor carved a beautiful statue from white marble.

march.label: CEFR B1
march.def: To walk with regular, measured steps, usually in a group or military formation.
march.ex: The soldiers marched down the street in perfect formation during the parade.

marine.label: CEFR B1
marine.def: Connected with the sea or relating to ships and ocean activities.
marine.ex: The marine biologist studied different species of fish and coral in the ocean.

mark.label: CEFR A2
mark.def: To make a visible sign or line on something with a pen, pencil, or similar tool
mark.ex: She marked the page with a red pen to show the important information.

marked.label: CEFR B1
marked.def: Very noticeable or obvious; having clear signs or features that stand out
marked.ex: There has been a marked improvement in his English pronunciation since he started taking lessons.

market.label: CEFR B1
market.def: To advertise and sell a product to customers in ways designed to attract buyers.
market.ex: The company is marketing their new smartphone through social media and television advertisements.

marketing.label: CEFR B2
marketing.def: Business activities designed to promote and sell products or services to customers.
marketing.ex: The company's marketing strategy focused on reaching younger audiences through social media campaigns.

marriage.label: CEFR B1
marriage.def: The legal and social relationship between two people who have agreed to live together as partners.
marriage.ex: My parents celebrated their marriage with a big wedding party and invited all their friends and family.

married.label: CEFR A2
married.def: Having a husband or wife; joined together as partners in a legal or religious ceremony.
married.ex: My parents have been married for thirty years.

marry.label: CEFR A2
marry.def: To become husband and wife by a formal ceremony.
marry.ex: They decided to marry next summer in a small church.

martial art.label: CEFR B2
martial art.def: A system of fighting techniques and physical training originating in Asia, like karate or judo.
martial art.ex: She has been studying martial arts for five years and recently earned her black belt in taekwondo.

marvelous/marvellous.label: CEFR A2
marvelous/marvellous.def: Extremely good or wonderful; amazing in a way that surprises you
marvelous/marvellous.ex: She had a marvellous time at the beach with her friends last summer.

mash.label: CEFR B1
mash.def: To crush something into a soft, pulpy mixture by pressing it hard.
mash.ex: She mashed the potatoes with butter and milk to make them creamy.

mask.label: CEFR B2
mask.def: A covering worn over the face to hide identity, protect against harm, or conceal true feelings
mask.ex: During the pandemic, many people wore protective masks when travelling on public transport.

mass.label: CEFR B2
mass.def: A large quantity or number of something gathered together; the amount of physical matter in an object.
mass.ex: The protest attracted a mass of demonstrators from across the country.

massacre.label: CEFR B2
massacre.def: The killing of a large number of people, typically unarmed and defenceless, in a violent attack.
massacre.ex: The international community condemned the massacre of civilians in the conflict zone.

massage.label: CEFR B2
massage.def: Rubbing and pressing muscles and soft body tissues to relieve tension or pain.
massage.ex: After her long flight, Sarah booked a therapeutic massage to ease the stiffness in her shoulders.

masseur.label: CEFR B2
masseur.def: A professional who manipulates muscles and tissues of the body to relieve tension and promote relaxation or healing.
masseur.ex: She hired a masseur to help with her back pain after the accident.

massive.label: CEFR B1
massive.def: extremely large in size, amount, or effect
massive.ex: The new shopping centre is massive—it has over 200 shops inside.

master.label: CEFR B2
master.def: A person who has complete knowledge or skill in a particular subject or activity.
master.ex: She became a master of classical piano after years of dedicated practice and study.

masterpiece.label: CEFR B2
masterpiece.def: A work of art or literature of outstanding excellence that is considered the finest example of an artist's work
masterpiece.ex: Many critics regard Picasso's 'Guernica' as a masterpiece of twentieth-century art that powerfully conveys the horrors of war.

mastery.label: CEFR B2
mastery.def: Complete skill and knowledge in a subject or activity through extensive practice and study
mastery.ex: After years of training, the musician achieved mastery of the classical piano repertoire.

match.label: CEFR B1
match.def: To be the same as something else, or to put two things together because they go well.
match.ex: The colour of your shoes matches your bag perfectly.

mate.label: CEFR B2
mate.def: A friend or companion, especially someone you work, study, or socialise with regularly.
mate.ex: My university mate and I decided to travel across Europe together after graduation.

material.label: CEFR A2
material.def: Cloth, paper, or other physical substance used to make things.
material.ex: She bought some blue material to make a new dress.

math/maths.label: CEFR A1
math/maths.def: The study of numbers, shapes, and amounts using calculations.
math/maths.ex: I have maths class every day at school.

mathematical.label: CEFR B2
mathematical.def: Relating to numbers, quantities, and calculations; based on logic and precise reasoning.
mathematical.ex: The engineer used a mathematical formula to calculate the bridge's structural strength.

mathematician.label: CEFR B1
mathematician.def: A person who studies or works with numbers, shapes, and mathematical problems.
mathematician.ex: The mathematician developed a new formula to solve complex equations.

mathematics.label: CEFR B1
mathematics.def: The study of numbers, shapes, and patterns using logic and calculation.
mathematics.ex: She enjoys mathematics and wants to study engineering at university.

matter.label: CEFR A2
matter.def: To be important or have importance in a particular situation.
matter.ex: Your opinion matters to me, so please tell me what you think.

mausoleum.label: CEFR B2
mausoleum.def: A large, impressive stone building constructed to hold a dead person's remains.
mausoleum.ex: The ancient mausoleum in the city centre attracts thousands of visitors each year who come to learn about the town's history.

max.label: CEFR A2
max.def: The largest amount, number, or value that is possible or allowed
max.ex: The max speed on this road is 50 kilometres per hour.

maximum.label: CEFR B1
maximum.def: The largest amount or highest level that is possible or allowed
maximum.ex: The maximum speed on this motorway is 70 miles per hour.

may.label: CEFR A1
may.def: The fifth month of the year, between April and June.
may.ex: We have a holiday in May and go to the beach.

maybe.label: CEFR A1
maybe.def: Perhaps; it is possible but not certain.
maybe.ex: Maybe it will rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella.

mayor.label: CEFR B1
mayor.def: The elected leader of a city or town government.
mayor.ex: The mayor announced new plans to improve public transport across the city.

meal.label: CEFR A1
meal.def: An occasion when you eat food, like breakfast, lunch, or dinner
meal.ex: I eat a healthy meal for lunch every day.

mean.label: CEFR A1
mean.def: To have as a purpose or intention; to intend something
mean.ex: I didn't mean to hurt your feelings—I was just joking.

meaning.label: CEFR A2
meaning.def: The thing that a word, sentence, or sign refers to; what something wants to communicate.
meaning.ex: The meaning of the word 'happy' is feeling joy and pleasure.

meaningful.label: CEFR B1
meaningful.def: Important and having a real purpose or value; significant.
meaningful.ex: She found her volunteer work very meaningful because it helped people in her community.

means.label: CEFR B2
means.def: Methods or resources used to achieve a goal or purpose.
means.ex: The charity uses various means to provide education to underprivileged children in rural areas.

meanwhile.label: CEFR B1
meanwhile.def: At the same time that something else is happening; in the period between now and later
meanwhile.ex: Sarah was studying for her exam, and meanwhile, her brother was watching television downstairs.

measure.label: CEFR B1
measure.def: To find the size, length, or amount of something using a tool or standard
measure.ex: The carpenter measured the wall before hanging the shelves.

measurement.label: CEFR B1
measurement.def: The size, length, or amount of something determined by comparing it to a standard unit.
measurement.ex: The tailor took careful measurements of my waist and shoulders before making the shirt.

meat.label: CEFR A1
meat.def: Food from animals like chicken, beef, and pork that people cook and eat.
meat.ex: I eat meat and vegetables for dinner every day.

mechanic.label: CEFR B1
mechanic.def: A person who repairs and maintains machines, especially cars and engines.
mechanic.ex: The mechanic spent two hours fixing the brakes on my car.

mechanical.label: CEFR B1
mechanical.def: Relating to machines or how machines work; operated by or using machines.
mechanical.ex: The mechanical failure of the engine caused the car to break down on the motorway.

mechanize/mechanise.label: CEFR B2
mechanize/mechanise.def: To replace human or animal labour with machines to do work more efficiently.
mechanize/mechanise.ex: The textile industry began to mechanise production in the 18th century, which transformed manufacturing.

medal.label: CEFR A2
medal.def: A metal disc or award given to the winner of a competition or someone very brave
medal.ex: She won a gold medal in the swimming competition at the Olympics.

media.label: CEFR B2
media.def: Organisations and methods that communicate news, information, and entertainment to large audiences.
media.ex: The media reported extensively on the government's new environmental policy changes.

medical.label: CEFR A2
medical.def: Relating to doctors, hospitals, and the treatment of illness or injury.
medical.ex: She had to go to the hospital for medical treatment after the accident.

medication.label: CEFR B2
medication.def: A substance or drug prescribed or taken to treat, cure, or prevent illness or disease.
medication.ex: The doctor prescribed new medication to help control her high blood pressure.

medicine.label: CEFR A1
medicine.def: A substance you take or apply to your body to treat illness or pain
medicine.ex: I took medicine when I had a bad cold.

medieval/mediaeval.label: CEFR B2
medieval/mediaeval.def: Relating to the Middle Ages, the period in European history from roughly the 5th to the 15th century.
medieval/mediaeval.ex: The medieval manuscript contained beautifully illustrated pages written in Latin by hand.

mediocre.label: CEFR B2
mediocre.def: Ordinary and not very good; lacking excellence or distinction in quality.
mediocre.ex: The film received mediocre reviews from critics who found it neither original nor particularly entertaining.

meditate.label: CEFR B1
meditate.def: To sit quietly and focus your mind on one thing to relax or find peace.
meditate.ex: Every morning, she meditates for twenty minutes to reduce stress before work.

meditation.label: CEFR B1
meditation.def: The practice of sitting quietly and focusing your mind to relax and become calmer.
meditation.ex: She does meditation every morning for ten minutes to start her day peacefully.

mediterranean.label: CEFR B1
mediterranean.def: Large sea between Europe, Africa, and Asia, surrounded by many countries and islands.
mediterranean.ex: Many tourists visit countries around the Mediterranean to enjoy the beaches and historical sites.

medium.label: CEFR B1
medium.def: A way of communicating or sharing information with people, such as television or newspapers.
medium.ex: Social media is now the most popular medium for young people to share news and stay connected.

meet.label: CEFR A1
meet.def: To come together with someone; to see and talk with another person.
meet.ex: I meet my friend at the café every Saturday.

meeting.label: CEFR A1
meeting.def: A time when two or more people come together to talk about something.
meeting.ex: We have a meeting at work tomorrow at 10 o'clock.

melody.label: CEFR B2
melody.def: A sequence of musical notes that forms the main tune of a song or piece of music.
melody.ex: The melody of that classical composition is so memorable that it stayed with me for days.

melon.label: CEFR A2
melon.def: A large round fruit with hard skin and sweet juicy flesh inside.
melon.ex: We bought a watermelon at the market and shared it with our friends.

melt.label: CEFR B1
melt.def: To turn from solid to liquid by heating; to become soft or liquid.
melt.ex: The snow melted quickly when the sun came out in spring.

member.label: CEFR A2
member.def: A person who belongs to a group, club, or organisation.
member.ex: She is a member of the tennis club and plays every weekend.

membership.label: CEFR B1
membership.def: The state of being part of a group, club, or organisation
membership.ex: She renewed her gym membership for another year so she could continue attending classes.

memorable.label: CEFR B1
memorable.def: Easy to remember because interesting, important, or special in some way
memorable.ex: The concert last night was memorable—the band played perfectly and the atmosphere was amazing.

memorial.label: CEFR B2
memorial.def: A structure or event created to remember and honour a person or historical event.
memorial.ex: The war memorial in the town centre displays the names of soldiers who died in the conflict.

memorize/memorise.label: CEFR B1
memorize/memorise.def: To learn something and remember it exactly, often by repeating it many times.
memorize/memorise.ex: She memorised the entire poem for the school competition last week.

memory.label: CEFR A1
memory.def: Your ability to remember things you have learned or experienced.
memory.ex: I have a good memory, so I can remember my friends' phone numbers easily.

mend.label: CEFR B1
mend.def: To repair something that is broken or damaged so it works again.
mend.ex: She took her torn jacket to a tailor to mend it before the party.

mentally.label: CEFR B1
mentally.def: In relation to the mind or thinking; concerning thoughts and emotions.
mentally.ex: She felt mentally prepared for the difficult exam after weeks of studying.

mention.label: CEFR B1
mention.def: To speak or write about something briefly, without giving many details
mention.ex: She mentioned in her email that she would be arriving on Friday afternoon.

mentor.label: CEFR B2
mentor.def: An experienced person who advises and guides someone with less experience over a period of time.
mentor.ex: Her university mentor helped her develop research skills and navigate academic challenges throughout her degree.

menu.label: CEFR A2
menu.def: A list of foods and drinks available at a restaurant or café.
menu.ex: The waiter gave us the menu so we could choose what to eat.

merchandise.label: CEFR B2
merchandise.def: Goods or products that are bought and sold, especially in shops or retail businesses.
merchandise.ex: The shop displays its merchandise in the window to attract customers walking past.

merchant.label: CEFR B1
merchant.def: A person or business that buys and sells goods, especially in large quantities.
merchant.ex: The merchant exported coffee beans to markets across Europe.

mercy.label: CEFR B2
mercy.def: Compassionate treatment shown to someone who deserves punishment or suffering.
mercy.ex: The judge showed mercy by reducing the defendant's sentence due to his difficult childhood.

mere.label: CEFR B1
mere.def: Only this, nothing more or better than what is stated.
mere.ex: Learning a new language is not a mere hobby; it opens many job opportunities.

merely.label: CEFR B1
merely.def: Only this, nothing more or else; used to emphasize something is simple or not important
merely.ex: She was merely asking for directions, not trying to be rude.

merge.label: CEFR B2
merge.def: To combine two or more things into one single unit or entity.
merge.ex: The two companies decided to merge in order to increase their market share and reduce operating costs.

merger.label: CEFR B2
merger.def: The joining of two companies or organisations into one larger business entity.
merger.ex: The merger between the two banks created the country's largest financial institution.

merry.label: CEFR A1
merry.def: Happy and full of joy, especially during celebrations.
merry.ex: We had a merry Christmas with family and lots of laughter.

mess.label: CEFR B2
mess.def: To make something untidy or dirty; to spoil or damage something.
mess.ex: She didn't mean to mess up the presentation by arriving late with the wrong files.

message.label: CEFR A1
message.def: Information or words that one person sends to another person
message.ex: I sent a message to my friend on her mobile phone.

message board.label: CEFR B1
message board.def: A website or application where people post and discuss messages about specific topics.
message board.ex: She found helpful advice about learning French on an online message board for language students.

messenger.label: CEFR B2
messenger.def: A person employed to carry messages, documents, or packages between places or people.
messenger.ex: The company hired a messenger to deliver contracts and important documents to clients across the city.

messy.label: CEFR B1
messy.def: Not tidy or organized; with things scattered or disorganized around
messy.ex: After the party, the living room was so messy that it took hours to clean up.

metal.label: CEFR A2
metal.def: A hard, shiny material like iron, copper, or gold found in nature
metal.ex: The door handle is made of metal and feels cold when you touch it.

metaphor.label: CEFR B2
metaphor.def: A comparison between two different things without using 'like' or 'as' to show similarities.
metaphor.ex: The teacher used the metaphor of a journey to describe the students' progress through the curriculum.

meter/metre.label: CEFR A2
meter/metre.def: A unit of length equal to 100 centimetres; used to measure distance or height.
meter/metre.ex: The swimming pool is 50 metres long.

metric.label: CEFR B2
metric.def: Relating to a system of measurement based on the metre, kilogram, and litre.
metric.ex: The European Union requires all member states to use metric units for official measurements and labelling.

micro.label: CEFR B2
micro.def: A very short skirt that ends well above the knee, typically several inches up the thigh.
micro.ex: She wore a black micro with tights and boots to the music festival.

microbe.label: CEFR B2
microbe.def: A tiny living organism, usually single-celled, visible only through a microscope.
microbe.ex: Scientists discovered a new microbe in the soil that helps plants absorb nutrients more efficiently.

microcomputer.label: CEFR B2
microcomputer.def: A small computer with a microprocessor, designed for individual users or small businesses.
microcomputer.ex: In the 1980s, microcomputers became affordable enough for families to have computers at home.

micrometer/micrometre.label: CEFR B2
micrometer/micrometre.def: A precision instrument used to measure very small distances or thicknesses, accurate to fractions of a millimetre.
micrometer/micrometre.ex: The engineer used a micrometre to ensure the metal component was exactly 5.23 millimetres thick.

microorganism.label: CEFR B2
microorganism.def: A tiny living creature visible only under a microscope, such as bacteria or viruses.
microorganism.ex: Scientists discovered a new microorganism in deep ocean water that produces its own light.

microphone.label: CEFR B2
microphone.def: A device that captures sound waves and converts them into electrical signals for recording or amplification
microphone.ex: The concert pianist adjusted the microphone before performing her classical repertoire to the large audience.

microscope.label: CEFR B2
microscope.def: An optical instrument that magnifies tiny objects so they can be seen and studied in detail.
microscope.ex: The biology students examined blood cells under a microscope to understand their structure.

microscopic.label: CEFR B2
microscopic.def: Extremely small; impossible to see without a microscope or magnifying device.
microscopic.ex: The microscopic organisms in the water sample were studied under the laboratory microscope to identify potential pathogens.

microscopy.label: CEFR B2
microscopy.def: The use of a microscope to examine objects or structures too small to see with the naked eye.
microscopy.ex: Advanced microscopy techniques have allowed scientists to observe individual cells and their internal structures in unprecedented detail.

microwave.label: CEFR B2
microwave.def: An electrical kitchen appliance that heats food quickly using electromagnetic radiation.
microwave.ex: She heated the leftovers in the microwave for three minutes before serving them.

midday.label: CEFR A2
midday.def: The middle of the day, around 12 o'clock when the sun is highest.
midday.ex: We usually have lunch at midday when we finish our morning work.

middle.label: CEFR A2
middle.def: The point or area that is equally far from all edges of something
middle.ex: I sat in the middle of the sofa between my two friends.

middle-aged.label: CEFR B1
middle-aged.def: Between young adult and old; typically between 40 and 60 years old
middle-aged.ex: My middle-aged parents enjoy hiking and travelling more than they did when they were younger.

midnight.label: CEFR A2
midnight.def: Twelve o'clock at night, when one day ends and the next day starts.
midnight.ex: The party finished at midnight, so we went home just after twelve o'clock.

midst.label: CEFR B2
midst.def: The middle or central part of something, especially a group of people or a situation
midst.ex: In the midst of the pandemic, many communities came together to support vulnerable neighbours.

might.label: CEFR A2
might.def: Shows that something is possible or may happen, but you are not certain.
might.ex: It might rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella with you.

mighty.label: CEFR B2
mighty.def: Possessing great power, strength, or influence; impressive in size or capability.
mighty.ex: The mighty oak tree in the village square had stood for over three hundred years, its enormous branches sheltering generations of residents.

migration.label: CEFR B2
migration.def: The movement of people or animals from one place to another, often for work, safety, or seasonal reasons.
migration.ex: The migration of refugees from conflict zones has created significant challenges for neighbouring countries.

mild.label: CEFR B1
mild.def: Not severe, strong, or extreme; gentle or moderate
mild.ex: The weather has been mild this winter, so we haven't needed heavy coats.

mile.label: CEFR B1
mile.def: A unit of distance equal to about 1.6 kilometres.
mile.ex: The nearest petrol station is five miles away from here.

military.label: CEFR A2
military.def: Related to the armed forces and soldiers
military.ex: My father works for the military and travels to different countries.

milk.label: CEFR A1
milk.def: A white liquid from cows or other animals that people drink and use for cooking.
milk.ex: I drink a glass of milk with my breakfast every morning.

mill.label: CEFR B2
mill.def: A building or machine that grinds grain into flour or processes other raw materials into finished products.
mill.ex: The old cotton mill employed hundreds of workers before it closed in the 1980s.

millimeter/millimetre.label: CEFR B2
millimeter/millimetre.def: A unit of length equal to one thousandth of a metre, used for measuring small distances.
millimeter/millimetre.ex: The engineer specified that the metal sheet should be precisely 2.5 millimetres thick to meet the safety standards.

millionaire.label: CEFR B2
millionaire.def: A person whose wealth or assets total at least one million pounds, dollars, or euros.
millionaire.ex: The tech millionaire donated £5 million to fund scholarships for underprivileged students.

mimic.label: CEFR B2
mimic.def: A person who copies another person's speech, movements, or behaviour, often to entertain or mock.
mimic.ex: The comedian was a brilliant mimic who could perfectly reproduce the prime minister's distinctive accent and mannerisms.

mind.label: CEFR A2
mind.def: To feel bothered or upset about something; to object to something.
mind.ex: I don't mind if you arrive a bit late.

mine.label: CEFR B2
mine.def: An excavation where valuable minerals or metals are extracted from beneath the Earth's surface.
mine.ex: The closure of the coal mine has affected thousands of workers in the region.

mineral.label: CEFR B1
mineral.def: A natural solid substance found in earth and rock, often used in industry or for health
mineral.ex: Iron is an important mineral that your body needs to stay healthy and energetic.

mineral water.label: CEFR A2
mineral water.def: Water that contains natural salts and other substances from rocks underground.
mineral water.ex: I bought a bottle of mineral water at the shop because it's healthier than tap water.

miniature.label: CEFR B2
miniature.def: Extremely small in size, but still a complete or detailed version of something larger
miniature.ex: The museum displayed a miniature replica of the 18th-century palace, hand-crafted down to the smallest window frame.

minimize/minimise.label: CEFR B1
minimize/minimise.def: To make something as small or as little as possible.
minimize/minimise.ex: The company is trying to minimise costs by using cheaper materials.

minimum.label: CEFR B1
minimum.def: The smallest amount, level, or quantity that is allowed or needed.
minimum.ex: The minimum wage in this country is £10.42 per hour.

minister.label: CEFR B2
minister.def: A senior government official responsible for a specific department or area of policy.
minister.ex: The health minister announced new funding for hospitals across the country.

minority.label: CEFR B1
minority.def: A smaller group of people within a larger population, often distinguished by race, religion, or culture.
minority.ex: The school has many students from ethnic minority backgrounds, and teachers make sure everyone feels included.

mint.label: CEFR B2
mint.def: A place where a government produces coins and banknotes; also an herb with a cool, fresh flavour
mint.ex: The Royal Mint produces all the coins circulating in the United Kingdom.

minute.label: CEFR A1
minute.def: A unit of time equal to sixty seconds.
minute.ex: The bus arrives in five minutes.

miracle.label: CEFR B1
miracle.def: A surprising and wonderful event that seems impossible to explain by natural laws
miracle.ex: Doctors called her recovery from the accident a miracle because no one thought she would survive.

mirror.label: CEFR A2
mirror.def: A smooth glass surface that shows a reflection of what you look at
mirror.ex: She looked at herself in the mirror before going to school.

mischief.label: CEFR B1
mischief.def: Playful or annoying behaviour that causes minor trouble or harm
mischief.ex: The children were always getting into mischief at school, hiding the teacher's markers and moving the classroom furniture.

misconception.label: CEFR B2
misconception.def: An incorrect belief or understanding about something that is widely held but false
misconception.ex: A common misconception is that people only use ten per cent of their brain; in fact, we use nearly all of it.

miserable.label: CEFR B1
miserable.def: Very unhappy and uncomfortable; feeling sad, sick, or in poor conditions.
miserable.ex: After losing the match, the team felt miserable despite their best effort.

misery.label: CEFR B1
misery.def: A state of being very unhappy and uncomfortable.
misery.ex: After losing his job, he fell into a period of misery and despair.

misfortune.label: CEFR B2
misfortune.def: Bad luck or an unfortunate event that causes difficulty or harm
misfortune.ex: Despite the misfortune of losing her job, she remained optimistic about finding new opportunities.

mislead.label: CEFR B1
mislead.def: To give someone wrong information that makes them believe something untrue.
mislead.ex: The advertisement misled customers about the product's quality and durability.

misleading.label: CEFR B2
misleading.def: Causing someone to believe something that is not true or incomplete.
misleading.ex: The advertisement's claim that the product cures all health problems is misleading and not supported by scientific evidence.

miss.label: CEFR A1
miss.def: To fail to catch, hit, or reach something you are trying to get.
miss.ex: I threw the ball to my friend, but she missed it.

missile.label: CEFR B2
missile.def: A weapon that is fired through the air and travels to a distant target before exploding.
missile.ex: The military announced that the new missile system could reach targets over 300 kilometres away.

missing.label: CEFR A2
missing.def: Not present or not found; something that should be there but is not
missing.ex: I cannot find my keys—they are missing.

mission.label: CEFR B1
mission.def: An important task or purpose that someone is sent to do or chooses to accomplish.
mission.ex: The astronauts' mission was to repair the space station and conduct scientific experiments.

mist.label: CEFR B2
mist.def: A cloud of tiny water droplets near the ground that reduces visibility but is lighter than fog.
mist.ex: The morning mist over the valley slowly cleared as the sun rose higher in the sky.

mistake.label: CEFR A2
mistake.def: Something you do wrong by accident, not on purpose.
mistake.ex: I made a mistake on my test and wrote the wrong answer.

mistreat.label: CEFR B2
mistreat.def: To treat someone or something badly or cruelly, without respect or care.
mistreat.ex: The investigation revealed that the animals had been mistreated by their former owners.

mistrustful.label: CEFR B2
mistrustful.def: Not trusting others; believing that people may be dishonest or have bad intentions
mistrustful.ex: The investor became mistrustful of the company's financial reports after discovering several accounting errors.

misty.label: CEFR B1
misty.def: Filled with mist; not clear because of thin clouds of water in the air
misty.ex: We could barely see the mountain top through the misty morning air.

misunderstand.label: CEFR B2
misunderstand.def: To interpret or understand something incorrectly or incompletely.
misunderstand.ex: She misunderstood his intentions and thought he was being rude when he was actually trying to help.

misunderstanding.label: CEFR B1
misunderstanding.def: A situation where people do not understand each other correctly or have different ideas about something.
misunderstanding.ex: There was a misunderstanding about the meeting time, so half the team arrived an hour late.

mix.label: CEFR A2
mix.def: To combine two or more things together so they become one.
mix.ex: She mixed flour and eggs to make the cake batter.

mixing bowl.label: CEFR B1
mixing bowl.def: A large, deep bowl used in cooking to combine ingredients together
mixing bowl.ex: She poured the flour and eggs into the mixing bowl to prepare the cake batter.

mixture.label: CEFR B2
mixture.def: A combination of two or more different substances or things mixed together.
mixture.ex: The chef created a mixture of spices and herbs to enhance the flavour of the dish.

mobile.label: CEFR A1
mobile.def: A phone that you can carry with you and use anywhere.
mobile.ex: I use my mobile to call my friends and send messages.

mobile phone.label: CEFR A1
mobile phone.def: A small electronic device used to make calls and send messages to other people.
mobile phone.ex: I use my mobile phone to call my mum every day.

mobility.label: CEFR B2
mobility.def: The ability to move freely or change position; also, movement of people between social classes or locations.
mobility.ex: Improved public transport has increased mobility for elderly residents in the city.

mock.label: CEFR B2
mock.def: Not real or genuine; made to imitate something for practice or testing purposes
mock.ex: The students took a mock exam before the real one to prepare themselves.

modal verb/modal.label: CEFR B1
modal verb/modal.def: A verb that shows possibility, necessity, ability, or permission, such as can, must, or should.
modal verb/modal.ex: The modal 'must' expresses obligation, while 'might' suggests something is possible.

model.label: CEFR B1
model.def: To create a representation or example of something to show how it works or looks.
model.ex: The engineer modelled the new building design using computer software before construction began.

modem.label: CEFR B1
modem.def: A device that connects your computer to the internet through a phone line or cable.
modem.ex: Our internet speed improved significantly after we replaced the old modem with a new one.

moderate.label: CEFR B1
moderate.def: Average in amount, level, or intensity; not extreme or excessive
moderate.ex: The doctor said she had moderate pain, not severe, so she didn't need strong medication.

modern.label: CEFR A2
modern.def: Made or designed recently; using the newest ideas or technology.
modern.ex: She lives in a modern apartment with new furniture and big windows.

modernization/modernisation.label: CEFR B2
modernization/modernisation.def: The process of updating systems, methods, or infrastructure to be more current and efficient.
modernization/modernisation.ex: The government's modernisation of the railway network has significantly improved travel times across the country.

modernize/modernise.label: CEFR B2
modernize/modernise.def: To update something with current technology, methods, or standards to make it more contemporary.
modernize/modernise.ex: The government has invested billions to modernise the country's aging railway infrastructure.

modest.label: CEFR B2
modest.def: Not claiming or drawing attention to one's abilities; humble and unassuming in behaviour or appearance.
modest.ex: Despite winning three Olympic gold medals, the athlete remained modest and credited her success to her coaches and team.

modification.label: CEFR B2
modification.def: A change or adjustment made to something to improve or adapt it
modification.ex: The engineer proposed several modifications to the design to make the product more efficient.

mogul.label: CEFR B2
mogul.def: A powerful and influential person in business, media, or entertainment.
mogul.ex: The property mogul built hotels across three continents before retiring.

moisture.label: CEFR B1
moisture.def: Water or dampness present in air, soil, or other materials.
moisture.ex: The high moisture in the bathroom makes the mirror foggy after a shower.

mold/mould.label: CEFR B2
mold/mould.def: A fungal growth that appears on damp surfaces, typically soft and colourless or greenish-black.
mold/mould.ex: The bathroom developed mould on the walls because of the constant moisture and poor ventilation.

molecule.label: CEFR B1
molecule.def: The smallest unit of a substance made of two or more atoms bonded together.
molecule.ex: In chemistry class, we studied how a water molecule consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

mom.label: CEFR A1
mom.ex: My mom makes delicious pancakes for breakfast.

moment.label: CEFR A1
moment.def: A very short period of time; an instant.
moment.ex: Wait a moment, please! I need to find my shoes.

momentarily.label: CEFR B2
momentarily.def: For a very short time; lasting only a brief moment or instant
momentarily.ex: The storm passed momentarily, but dark clouds remained on the horizon.

momentary.label: CEFR B2
momentary.def: Lasting for a very short time; brief and quickly passing.
momentary.ex: The film's momentary pause in dialogue created tension before the crucial revelation.

mommy/mommie.label: CEFR A1
mommy/mommie.def: A mother; a woman who has children, especially when spoken to or about by young children.
mommy/mommie.ex: My mommy helps me get ready for school every morning.

monastery.label: CEFR B2
monastery.def: A building or group of buildings where monks live and worship together as a religious community.
monastery.ex: The ancient monastery on the hilltop has welcomed pilgrims for over five hundred years.

monday.label: CEFR A1
monday.def: The first day of the working week, after Sunday.
monday.ex: I go to school on Monday morning.

money.label: CEFR A1
money.def: Paper and coins used to buy things.
money.ex: I need more money to buy a new shirt.

monk.label: CEFR B1
monk.def: A man who is a member of a religious community and lives according to strict spiritual rules.
monk.ex: The monk spent hours each day in prayer and meditation at the monastery.

monkey.label: CEFR A1
monkey.def: A small animal with a tail that lives in trees and eats fruit.
monkey.ex: The monkey climbed up the tree very quickly.

monster.label: CEFR B1
monster.def: A very large, frightening creature that is often imaginary or seen in stories.
monster.ex: The children were scared when the monster appeared on the cinema screen during the horror film.

month.label: CEFR A1
month.def: A period of time lasting about 30 days, one of the twelve parts of a year
month.ex: My birthday is in July, which is the seventh month of the year.

monthly.label: CEFR B1
monthly.def: Happening or done once every month, or relating to a single month
monthly.ex: I receive a monthly salary payment on the last day of each month.

monument.label: CEFR B1
monument.def: A large structure built to remember an important person or historical event
monument.ex: The monument in the town centre was built to honour the soldiers who died in the war.

mood.label: CEFR A2
mood.def: The way you feel at a particular time; your emotional state.
mood.ex: She was in a good mood after her birthday party.

moody.label: CEFR B2
moody.def: Having changeable emotions or a tendency to feel sad and withdrawn without clear reason.
moody.ex: After failing the exam, he became moody and barely spoke to anyone for days.

moon.label: CEFR A1
moon.def: The large, bright object that moves across the night sky and orbits Earth.
moon.ex: The moon is very bright tonight.

moonlight.label: CEFR B2
moonlight.def: Bright light from the moon that shines on Earth during the night.
moonlight.ex: The couple walked along the beach in the moonlight, enjoying the peaceful evening.

moonscape.label: CEFR B2
moonscape.def: A barren, desolate landscape resembling the surface of the moon, often rocky and lifeless.
moonscape.ex: The volcanic region created a harsh moonscape of black rock and ash stretching across the desert.

moral.label: CEFR B1
moral.def: A lesson or principle about what is right and wrong behaviour.
moral.ex: The moral of the story is that honesty is always the best choice.

morality.label: CEFR B2
morality.def: Principles concerning what is right and wrong behaviour.
morality.ex: The film explores questions of morality by showing characters facing difficult ethical choices.

morally.label: CEFR B2
morally.def: In relation to principles of right and wrong behaviour or ethics.
morally.ex: Many people argue that it is morally wrong to test cosmetics on animals.

more.label: CEFR B1
more.def: A greater amount or degree; additionally or to a higher extent.
more.ex: I need to study more if I want to pass the exam next week.

moreover.label: CEFR B1
moreover.def: In addition; also used to introduce a further point that supports what was said before
moreover.ex: The new software is more efficient, and moreover, it costs less than the previous version.

morning.label: CEFR A1
morning.def: The early part of the day from when the sun rises until noon.
morning.ex: I drink coffee every morning before work.

mortal.label: CEFR B2
mortal.def: Subject to death; causing death or severe harm if it happens.
mortal.ex: The boxer suffered a mortal wound during the fight and died shortly after.

mortar.label: CEFR B2
mortar.def: A mixture of cement, sand, and water used to join bricks or stones together in construction
mortar.ex: The bricklayer applied fresh mortar between each layer of stones to ensure the wall was structurally sound.

mortgage.label: CEFR B2
mortgage.def: A loan from a bank to buy property, repaid over many years with interest.
mortgage.ex: After saving for a deposit, they applied for a mortgage to purchase their first home.

mortify.label: CEFR B2
mortify.def: To cause someone to feel ashamed, embarrassed, or humiliated.
mortify.ex: She was mortified when she realised she had been giving the presentation with her blouse buttoned incorrectly.

mosque.label: CEFR A2
mosque.def: A building where Muslims go to pray and worship.
mosque.ex: Every Friday, my neighbour goes to the mosque to pray with his family.

mosquito.label: CEFR B1
mosquito.def: A small flying insect that bites people and animals to drink their blood.
mosquito.ex: We lit a candle to keep mosquitoes away from us while we sat outside in the garden.

moss.label: CEFR B1
moss.def: A small green plant without flowers that grows on damp rocks, trees, and soil.
moss.ex: After the heavy rain, thick moss covered the old stone wall in our garden.

most.label: CEFR A2
most.def: used to show the greatest amount or degree of something
most.ex: She studies the most in our class, so she gets the best grades.

mostly.label: CEFR A2
mostly.def: Almost completely; the largest or main part of something.
mostly.ex: My friends are mostly from school, but I have a few from football club too.

mother.label: CEFR A1
mother.def: A female parent; a woman who has children.
mother.ex: My mother makes delicious food for our family.

mother-in-law.label: CEFR B2
mother-in-law.def: The mother of your spouse or partner.
mother-in-law.ex: My mother-in-law gave us excellent advice about renovating the kitchen.

motherland.label: CEFR B1
motherland.def: A person's native country, the place where they were born or their family comes from.
motherland.ex: Many immigrants still feel a deep connection to their motherland even after living abroad for years.

motion.label: CEFR B2
motion.def: The act or process of moving from one place or position to another.
motion.ex: The ship's gentle motion across the calm sea made most passengers feel quite relaxed.

motionless.label: CEFR B1
motionless.def: Not moving at all; staying completely still without any movement.
motionless.ex: The cat sat motionless by the window, watching the birds outside for hours.

motivate.label: CEFR B1
motivate.def: To give someone a reason or desire to do something
motivate.ex: The coach's speech motivated the team to play better in the second half.

motivated.label: CEFR B2
motivated.def: Having a strong reason or desire to do something; driven to achieve a goal.
motivated.ex: The motivated students worked hard to prepare for their university entrance exams.

motivation.label: CEFR B1
motivation.def: The reason or desire that makes you want to do something
motivation.ex: Her motivation for studying hard was to get into a good university.

motor.label: CEFR B1
motor.def: An engine that produces power to make a machine or vehicle move
motor.ex: The car's motor needs to be serviced regularly to keep it running smoothly.

motorbike.label: CEFR B2
motorbike.def: A two-wheeled vehicle with an engine, typically faster and more agile than a car.
motorbike.ex: She commutes to work on her motorbike, which allows her to navigate through heavy traffic easily.

motorcycle.label: CEFR B1
motorcycle.def: A vehicle with two wheels, an engine, and seats for one or two people.
motorcycle.ex: He rides his motorcycle to work every day because it's faster than driving a car in the city.

motorist.label: CEFR B2
motorist.def: A person who drives a motor vehicle, especially a car.
motorist.ex: The motorist was fined for exceeding the speed limit on the motorway.

motorway.label: CEFR A2
motorway.def: A very wide road built for fast traffic between cities and towns.
motorway.ex: We drove on the motorway for three hours to reach London.

motto.label: CEFR B1
motto.def: A short phrase or sentence that expresses the main belief or purpose of a person, group, or organization.
motto.ex: The school's motto is 'Excellence through effort', which reminds students to always try their best.

mount.label: CEFR B2
mount.def: To climb onto something, or to prepare and organize something for presentation or action.
mount.ex: The museum decided to mount a major exhibition showcasing artifacts from ancient Rome.

mountain.label: CEFR A1
mountain.def: A very high area of land with steep sides.
mountain.ex: We climbed the mountain and saw beautiful views from the top.

mountaintop.label: CEFR B1
mountaintop.def: The highest point or peak of a mountain.
mountaintop.ex: From the mountaintop, we could see three countries in the distance.

mourn.label: CEFR B1
mourn.def: To feel and show deep sadness because someone has died.
mourn.ex: The whole nation mourned the loss of the beloved former president.

mournful.label: CEFR B2
mournful.def: Expressing or showing deep sadness, grief, or sorrow
mournful.ex: The mournful sound of the funeral bells echoed through the village as mourners gathered to pay their respects.

mouse.label: CEFR A1
mouse.def: A small animal with fur, a long tail, and big ears that lives in houses or fields
mouse.ex: The cat chased the mouse across the kitchen floor.

mouth.label: CEFR A1
mouth.def: The opening in your face where you eat and speak.
mouth.ex: She opened her mouth and took a big bite of apple.

move.label: CEFR A2
move.def: To change position or go from one place to another
move.ex: She moved to London last year to start a new job.

movement.label: CEFR B1
movement.def: The action or process of changing position or location from one place to another.
movement.ex: The movement of the clouds across the sky was slow and peaceful that afternoon.

mover.label: CEFR B2
mover.def: A person or company that transports furniture and belongings from one place to another.
mover.ex: We hired professional movers to help us relocate to our new house across the country.

movie.label: CEFR A1
