part.def: To separate or divide something into pieces; to stop being together with someone.
part.ex: The two companies decided to part ways after their partnership became unprofitable.

partial.label: CEFR B1
partial.def: Not complete or whole; only some of something, not all of it.
partial.ex: She made a partial payment towards her university fees and will pay the rest next month.

participant.label: CEFR B1
participant.def: A person who takes part in an activity, event, or process.
participant.ex: Each participant in the study was asked to complete a questionnaire about their habits.

participation.label: CEFR B2
participation.def: Active involvement or engagement in an activity, event, or decision-making process.
participation.ex: The student's participation in class discussions significantly improved her understanding of the subject matter.

participle.label: CEFR B1
participle.def: A verb form used as an adjective, like 'running' in 'a running child' or 'broken' in 'a broken window'.
participle.ex: In the sentence 'The smiling teacher entered the classroom', 'smiling' is a participle acting as an adjective.

particle.label: CEFR B2
particle.def: A very small piece or amount of something material or physical.
particle.ex: Scientists use powerful microscopes to study individual particles of dust and pollen in the air.

particular.label: CEFR B2
particular.def: Relating to one specific person, thing, or group rather than others or to people in general
particular.ex: The government has no particular plans to raise taxes in the coming year, despite ongoing budgetary pressures.

particularly.label: CEFR B1
particularly.def: To a greater degree than usual; especially or specifically.
particularly.ex: This restaurant is particularly good for seafood, so I recommend ordering the fish.

partisan.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
partisan.def: A strong supporter of a political party, cause, or group, often favouring their side over others.
partisan.ex: The partisan argued passionately that his party's economic policies were superior to all alternatives.

partly.label: CEFR A2
partly.def: Not completely or fully; some but not all
partly.ex: The cake was partly chocolate and partly vanilla.

partnership.label: CEFR B2
partnership.def: A formal business or professional relationship between two or more people or organisations working together.
partnership.ex: The technology company entered into a strategic partnership with a leading automotive manufacturer to develop self-driving vehicles.

part-time.label: CEFR B1
part-time.def: For fewer hours than a normal full-time job, usually less than 40 hours per week.
part-time.ex: She works part-time at the bookshop while studying for her degree at university.

party.label: CEFR A1
party.def: A social event where people gather to enjoy music, food, and conversation.
party.ex: I went to a birthday party at my friend's house last weekend.

pass.label: CEFR A2
pass.def: To move or go forward, or to give something to someone
pass.ex: Can you pass the salt to me, please?

passage.label: CEFR A2
passage.def: A short piece of text or writing from a book, article, or speech.
passage.ex: We read a difficult passage from our English textbook in class today.

passenger.label: CEFR A2
passenger.def: A person who travels in a vehicle, plane, or train but does not drive or operate it.
passenger.ex: The passengers got on the bus at the station this morning.

passion.label: CEFR B1
passion.def: A strong feeling of enthusiasm or love for something
passion.ex: Her passion for music started when she was five years old and learned to play the piano.

passionate.label: CEFR B2
passionate.def: Feeling or showing very strong emotion or enthusiasm about something
passionate.ex: She gave a passionate speech about climate change that moved the entire audience.

passionately.label: CEFR B2
passionately.def: With intense emotion or strong feeling about something you deeply care about.
passionately.ex: She argued passionately for stricter environmental regulations during the council meeting.

passive.label: CEFR B1
passive.def: Not active; accepting things without protest or resistance.
passive.ex: She took a passive role in the meeting and didn't share her opinions.

passport.label: CEFR B1
passport.def: An official document that proves who you are and allows you to travel to other countries.
passport.ex: You need to bring your passport when you fly to another country.

password.label: CEFR B1
password.def: A secret word or phrase you type to access a computer, email, or website account.
password.ex: You should change your password every few months to keep your email account secure.

past.label: CEFR A1
past.def: Gone by; no longer happening now
past.ex: I lived in London in the past.

pasta.label: CEFR A2
pasta.def: A food made from flour and water, shaped into long strings or tubes and cooked by boiling.
pasta.ex: We cooked pasta with tomato sauce for dinner.

pastor.label: CEFR B2
pastor.def: A member of the clergy who leads a Christian church or congregation and provides spiritual guidance to members.
pastor.ex: The pastor delivered an inspiring sermon about forgiveness during Sunday's service at the local church.

pastoral.label: CEFR B2
pastoral.def: Related to countryside life, farming, or peaceful rural landscapes and simple living.
pastoral.ex: The pastoral scenery of the Lake District, with its rolling hills and grazing sheep, inspired many Romantic poets.

patch.label: CEFR B2
patch.def: A small area or piece of something that is different from the surrounding surface or material.
patch.ex: The mechanic discovered a damp patch on the ceiling where water had been leaking from the roof.

path.label: CEFR A2
path.def: A narrow way or track that people walk on, usually in a park or garden.
path.ex: We walked along the path in the park after we finished shopping.

patience.label: CEFR B1
patience.def: The ability to stay calm and accept delays without becoming angry or annoyed.
patience.ex: Learning a new language requires patience; you will not become fluent in a few weeks.

patient.label: CEFR A2
patient.def: A person receiving medical care or treatment from a doctor or hospital.
patient.ex: The patient waited in the doctor's office for her appointment.

patiently.label: CEFR B2
patiently.def: In a calm way, accepting delay or difficulty without getting annoyed or angry.
patiently.ex: She patiently explained the complex theory to her students, answering each question thoroughly.

patriot.label: CEFR B2
patriot.def: A person who loves and strongly supports their own country and its interests.
patriot.ex: The patriot was willing to make sacrifices for the good of the nation.

patriotic.label: CEFR B2
patriotic.def: Feeling or showing strong love and support for one's own country.
patriotic.ex: The patriotic citizens gathered at the memorial to honour those who died in the war.

patriotism.label: CEFR B2
patriotism.def: Strong love and loyalty for one's own country and willingness to support it.
patriotism.ex: The athlete's patriotism was evident in the way she proudly represented her country at the Olympic Games.

patrol.label: CEFR B1
patrol.def: A group of people who move around an area regularly to watch for danger or problems
patrol.ex: The police patrol stopped the driver who was speeding through the residential neighbourhood.

patron.label: CEFR B2
patron.def: A person who gives financial support or protection to an artist, organisation, or cause
patron.ex: The wealthy art patron donated millions to establish a new wing at the contemporary art museum.

pattern.label: CEFR B1
pattern.def: A repeated arrangement of shapes, colours, or things that follows a regular order.
pattern.ex: The fabric had a beautiful floral pattern with blue and white flowers.

pause.label: CEFR B1
pause.def: To stop something temporarily, intending to continue it later
pause.ex: She paused the video to answer the phone call.

pave.label: CEFR B2
pave.def: To cover a surface with concrete, stones, or similar material to create a hard, flat level.
pave.ex: The council has paved the entire town square with new granite blocks.

pavement.label: CEFR B2
pavement.def: Hard surface layer covering a road or path where vehicles or pedestrians travel
pavement.ex: The old pavement on the high street was badly cracked and needed replacing.

paw.label: CEFR B2
paw.def: The foot of an animal such as a dog, cat, or bear, with claws or nails.
paw.ex: The cat's paws left muddy prints across the kitchen floor.

pawnbroker.label: CEFR B2
pawnbroker.def: A person who lends money in exchange for personal items left as security, which they can sell if not repaid.
pawnbroker.ex: She visited the pawnbroker to get a short-term loan, offering her grandmother's watch as collateral for the £500 she needed.

pawnshop.label: CEFR B2
pawnshop.def: A shop where people can borrow money by leaving personal possessions as security.
pawnshop.ex: He sold his grandfather's watch to a pawnshop when he urgently needed cash for rent.

pay.label: CEFR A1
pay.def: To give money for something you buy or a service you receive
pay.ex: I pay five pounds for this book.

payment.label: CEFR B2
payment.def: Money given to someone in exchange for goods, services, or as repayment of a debt.
payment.ex: The company processes online payments through a secure system to protect customer financial information.

pc.label: CEFR A2
pc.def: A personal computer; a machine you use for work, studies, or entertainment.
pc.ex: I use my PC every day to write emails and play games.

pea.label: CEFR A2
pea.def: A small, round, green vegetable that grows in a pod
pea.ex: I like to eat peas with rice and chicken for dinner.

peace.label: CEFR A1
peace.def: A time when there is no war or fighting.
peace.ex: After the war ended, there was finally peace in the country.

peaceful.label: CEFR A2
peaceful.def: Quiet and calm, without noise, trouble, or violence.
peaceful.ex: The beach was peaceful early in the morning, with only the sound of waves.

peacefully.label: CEFR B2
peacefully.def: In a calm manner without conflict, violence, or disturbance.
peacefully.ex: The protesters marched peacefully through the city centre, carrying signs and chanting slogans.

peach.label: CEFR B2
peach.def: A soft, sweet fruit with fuzzy skin, yellow or orange flesh, and a large central stone.
peach.ex: The farmer's market had fresh peaches at the peak of ripeness, perfect for making jam.

peak.label: CEFR B2
peak.def: The highest point or maximum level of something, whether a mountain or a period of success.
peak.ex: Tourist numbers reach their peak during the summer months when the weather is warmest.

peanut.label: CEFR B1
peanut.def: A small oval nut that grows in a shell underground, often eaten roasted or made into butter.
peanut.ex: My son is allergic to peanuts, so I always check the ingredients before buying snacks.

pear.label: CEFR A2
pear.def: A sweet fruit that is green or yellow, round at the bottom and narrower at the top.
pear.ex: I ate a ripe pear for breakfast this morning.

pearl.label: CEFR B2
pearl.def: A hard, lustrous white or cream-coloured gem formed inside oyster shells, used in jewellery and valuable items.
pearl.ex: She wore her grandmother's pearl necklace to the formal dinner, and it complemented her dress perfectly.

pebble.label: CEFR B2
pebble.def: A small, smooth stone, typically found on beaches or in riverbeds, worn round by water.
pebble.ex: She picked up a pebble from the shore and skipped it across the calm water.

peculiar.label: CEFR B1
peculiar.def: Strange or unusual in a way that surprises or interests people
peculiar.ex: She had a peculiar habit of drinking tea with salt instead of sugar.

pedal.label: CEFR B2
pedal.def: A foot-operated lever on a machine or vehicle that controls its operation or movement.
pedal.ex: She pressed the accelerator pedal to increase the car's speed on the motorway.

pedantic.label: CEFR B2
pedantic.def: Overly concerned with minor details and rules; fussy about accuracy in a way that seems excessive.
pedantic.ex: His pedantic approach to grammar made casual conversations feel tiresome and uncomfortable.

pedestrian.label: CEFR B2
pedestrian.def: A person who is walking, especially in an area with traffic or on a city street.
pedestrian.ex: The driver was fined for hitting a pedestrian who was crossing at the zebra crossing.

pee.label: CEFR B2
pee.def: To urinate; to pass urine from the body.
pee.ex: The toddler needed to pee before they could leave the house.

peel.label: CEFR B2
peel.def: To remove the outer skin or covering from fruit, vegetables, or other surfaces.
peel.ex: She peeled back the wallpaper to reveal the original floral pattern underneath.

peep.label: CEFR B2
peep.def: A quick or secret look, often done hurriedly or furtively without being seen
peep.ex: She took a peep through the curtains to see if the guests had arrived yet.

peer.label: CEFR B2
peer.def: A person of equal standing, status, or age in a particular group or society.
peer.ex: She felt nervous presenting her research to her peers at the academic conference.

peg.label: CEFR B2
peg.def: A pin or bolt used to fasten, hang, or secure something in place.
peg.ex: She hung her coat on the wooden peg by the door.

pen.label: CEFR A1
pen.def: A writing tool with ink that you use to write or draw on paper.
pen.ex: I write my homework with a blue pen.

penalize/penalise.label: CEFR B2
penalize/penalise.def: To impose a penalty or punishment on someone for breaking a rule or law.
penalize/penalise.ex: The referee penalised the team for excessive fouls, awarding a free kick to their opponents.

penalty.label: CEFR B2
penalty.def: A punishment given for breaking a rule or law, often a fine or loss of points in sports
penalty.ex: The company was ordered to pay a substantial penalty for violating environmental regulations.

pence.label: CEFR A2
pence.def: British money, equal to one hundredth of a pound

pencil.label: CEFR A1
pencil.def: A thin writing tool with a dark grey core inside wood, used for drawing or writing.
pencil.ex: I write my homework with a pencil and an eraser.

penetrate.label: CEFR B2
penetrate.def: To pass through or into something, often with force or difficulty.
penetrate.ex: The sharp needle penetrated the thick fabric without tearing it.

penfriend/pen friend.label: CEFR A2
penfriend/pen friend.def: A person in another country you write letters to in order to learn about their culture and make a friendship.
penfriend/pen friend.ex: My pen friend lives in France and we write letters to each other every month.

penguin.label: CEFR B2
penguin.def: A flightless seabird with black and white feathers that lives mainly in the Southern Hemisphere.
penguin.ex: The documentary followed a colony of emperor penguins as they trekked across the Antarctic ice to their breeding grounds.

penicillin.label: CEFR B2
penicillin.def: An antibiotic medicine derived from mould that kills bacteria and treats infections.
penicillin.ex: The doctor prescribed penicillin to clear up the patient's bacterial infection after surgery.

penniless.label: CEFR B1
penniless.def: Having no money at all; extremely poor.
penniless.ex: After losing his job, he became penniless and had to ask friends for help.

penny.label: CEFR B1
penny.def: A British coin worth one hundredth of a pound, or one cent in American money.
penny.ex: I found a penny on the pavement and put it in my pocket.

pension.label: CEFR B2
pension.def: Regular monthly payment given to a person who has retired from work or is unable to work.
pension.ex: After working for forty years, she received a generous pension that allowed her to travel in retirement.

pentagon.label: CEFR B2
pentagon.def: A shape or building with five straight sides and five angles.
pentagon.ex: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defence, located near Washington, DC.

people.label: CEFR A1
people.def: Human beings; men, women, and children.
people.ex: There are many people in the shopping centre on Saturday.

pepper.label: CEFR A2
pepper.def: A hot spice made from dried berries, used to flavour food
pepper.ex: She added black pepper to her soup to make it taste better.

percent/per cent.label: CEFR B1
percent/per cent.def: A number or amount expressed as a fraction of one hundred.
percent/per cent.ex: About 75 per cent of the students passed the exam on their first attempt.

percentage.label: CEFR B2
percentage.def: A number or amount expressed as a part of one hundred, shown with the % symbol.
percentage.ex: The survey showed that 87 percentage of respondents supported the new policy.

perception.label: CEFR B2
perception.def: The way you understand or interpret something based on what you see, hear, or experience.
perception.ex: Many people's perception of climate change has shifted after reading recent scientific reports.

perfect.label: CEFR A2
perfect.def: Extremely good with no faults or mistakes
perfect.ex: She got a perfect score on her English test yesterday.

perfectly.label: CEFR A2
perfectly.def: In a way that is completely correct or without any mistakes or problems.
perfectly.ex: She speaks English perfectly, without any accent.

perform.label: CEFR A2
perform.def: To do something in front of people, like sing, dance, or act.
perform.ex: She performed a song at the school concert last night.

performance.label: CEFR A2
performance.def: How well someone does a task or activity, or a show that entertains people.
performance.ex: Her performance in the school play was excellent, and everyone applauded.

performer.label: CEFR B1
performer.def: Someone who acts, sings, dances, or does other entertainment in front of an audience.
performer.ex: The street performer played guitar beautifully and attracted a large crowd in the city centre.

perfume.label: CEFR A2
perfume.def: A sweet-smelling liquid that people put on their body to smell nice.
perfume.ex: She wore her favourite perfume to the party.

perhaps.label: CEFR A2
perhaps.def: Maybe; possibly; used when something is not certain
perhaps.ex: Perhaps we can go to the beach tomorrow if the weather is good.

permanence.label: CEFR B2
permanence.def: The quality of lasting or continuing without changing or ending.
permanence.ex: The artist sought permanence by using materials that would survive for centuries.

permanent.label: CEFR B1
permanent.def: Lasting for a very long time or never changing; not temporary
permanent.ex: She got a permanent job at the hospital after working there for six months.

permanently.label: CEFR B1
permanently.def: In a way that lasts forever and cannot be changed or removed.
permanently.ex: After the accident, he was permanently disabled and had to use a wheelchair.

permeate.label: CEFR B2
permeate.def: To spread slowly through something; to penetrate and become present throughout
permeate.ex: The smell of fresh coffee permeated the entire office building within minutes.

permission.label: CEFR A2
permission.def: Official approval or consent to do something
permission.ex: You need to ask your teacher for permission before leaving the classroom.

permit.label: CEFR B1
permit.def: To allow someone to do something or to give official permission.
permit.ex: The school will permit students to leave early on Friday if they finish their exams.

perseverance.label: CEFR B2
perseverance.def: Continued effort to achieve something despite difficulties or delays.
perseverance.ex: Her perseverance through years of training finally paid off when she won the Olympic medal.

persistence.label: CEFR B2
persistence.def: Determination to continue doing something despite difficulty or opposition.
persistence.ex: Through hard work and persistence, she eventually became a successful surgeon despite facing many obstacles early in her career.

persistent.label: CEFR B2
persistent.def: Continuing firmly or stubbornly despite difficulty; refusing to give up or stop.
persistent.ex: Her persistent efforts to improve her English skills eventually paid off with fluency.

person.label: CEFR A1
person.def: A human being; a man, woman, or child.
person.ex: That person over there is my friend Sarah.

personal.label: CEFR A1
personal.def: Belonging to or about one particular person, not public or shared.
personal.ex: I keep my personal diary in my bedroom where nobody else can read it.

personality.label: CEFR A2
personality.def: The combination of qualities and characteristics that make a person unique and different from others.
personality.ex: My friend has a friendly personality and everyone likes talking to her.

personally.label: CEFR B1
personally.def: In a way that relates to your own individual opinion or experience, not official or general
personally.ex: Personally, I think this restaurant serves better food than the one near your house.

personification.label: CEFR B2
personification.def: A literary technique where human qualities or characteristics are given to non-human things or animals.
personification.ex: The poet used personification to describe the wind as a lonely wanderer moving through the empty streets.

personify.label: CEFR B2
personify.def: To give human characteristics or qualities to a non-human thing or abstract idea.
personify.ex: In the poem, the poet personifies the wind as a gentle companion that whispers through the trees.

personnel.label: CEFR B2
personnel.def: The people employed by an organisation or company to do its work.
personnel.ex: The hospital's personnel were well-trained to handle the emergency situation.

perspiration.label: CEFR B2
perspiration.def: Salty liquid released from skin, especially during physical activity or when hot.
perspiration.ex: After running a marathon, beads of perspiration dripped down the athlete's face.

persuade.label: CEFR B1
persuade.def: To make someone agree or believe something by giving good reasons.
persuade.ex: She persuaded her parents to let her study abroad by showing them her excellent grades.

persuasion.label: CEFR B2
persuasion.def: The act of convincing someone to believe something or do something through reasoning or argument.
persuasion.ex: The lawyer's persuasion of the jury was so effective that they reached a guilty verdict within hours.

persuasive.label: CEFR B1
persuasive.def: Able to make someone believe or agree with something through strong arguments or communication
persuasive.ex: Her persuasive speech convinced many voters to support the new environmental policy.

pessimist.label: CEFR B2
pessimist.def: A person who typically expects bad outcomes and focuses on negative possibilities rather than positive ones.
pessimist.ex: The pessimist in our team always warned that the project would fail, but we succeeded anyway.

pessimistic.label: CEFR B2
pessimistic.def: Tending to expect bad outcomes and focus on negative aspects of situations.
pessimistic.ex: The economist's pessimistic forecast suggested the economy would enter a recession within months.

pet.label: CEFR A1
pet.def: An animal that lives with you at home and you care for.
pet.ex: My sister has a pet cat named Mittens.

petrified.label: CEFR B2
petrified.def: Extremely frightened or so scared that you cannot move or think clearly.
petrified.ex: She was petrified when she saw the spider crawling across her bedroom floor.

petrol.label: CEFR A2
petrol.def: Liquid fuel used to make cars and other vehicles move.
petrol.ex: I need to stop at the petrol station to fill up my car.

petrol station.label: CEFR A2
petrol station.def: A place where you buy fuel for your car
petrol station.ex: I stopped at the petrol station to fill up my tank before the long drive.

petty.label: CEFR B2
petty.def: Small, unimportant, or trivial; concerned with minor details rather than larger matters
petty.ex: The manager's petty complaints about how employees dressed made her unpopular with the team.

ph.label: CEFR B2
ph.def: A measurement of how acidic or alkaline a substance is, on a scale from 0 to 14.
ph.ex: The pH of the soil in this region is too low for most crops to grow successfully.

phantom.label: CEFR B1
phantom.def: Not real or actually present; existing only in imagination or as a ghost
phantom.ex: The phantom pain in his leg continued even though the injury had healed months ago.

pharmacist.label: CEFR B2
pharmacist.def: A qualified professional who prepares and dispenses medications, and advises patients on their use.
pharmacist.ex: The pharmacist reviewed my prescriptions to ensure there were no dangerous interactions between the medications.

pharmacy.label: CEFR B1
pharmacy.def: A shop where medicines and health products are sold, usually run by a trained pharmacist.
pharmacy.ex: I need to go to the pharmacy to pick up my prescription for antibiotics.

phenomenon.label: CEFR B1
phenomenon.def: Something that happens or exists that is unusual, interesting, or important to notice
phenomenon.ex: The northern lights are a natural phenomenon that attracts visitors to Iceland every winter.

philosopher.label: CEFR B1
philosopher.def: A person who studies fundamental questions about life, knowledge, existence, and how to live well.
philosopher.ex: The ancient Greek philosopher Aristotle wrote extensively about ethics and human happiness.

philosophical.label: CEFR B2
philosophical.def: Relating to the study of knowledge, reality, and human values; thoughtful and reflective about deep questions.
philosophical.ex: She took a philosophical approach to the problem, considering its deeper meaning rather than seeking a quick solution.

phoenix.label: CEFR B1
phoenix.def: A legendary bird that burns and is born again from its ashes
phoenix.ex: The company rose like a phoenix from the ashes after bankruptcy, becoming successful once more.

phone.label: CEFR A1
phone.def: To call someone using a telephone to talk to them.
phone.ex: I will phone my mother this evening.

photo.label: CEFR A1
photo.def: A picture made by a camera.
photo.ex: I took a photo of my family at the beach.

photocopy.label: CEFR B1
photocopy.def: An exact paper copy of a document made by a machine using light and chemicals.
photocopy.ex: I need to make a photocopy of my passport for the application form.

photograph.label: CEFR B2
photograph.def: To take a picture of someone or something using a camera or similar device.
photograph.ex: The journalist photographed the protest to document what happened for her newspaper.

photographer.label: CEFR A2
photographer.def: A person whose job is taking pictures with a camera.
photographer.ex: The photographer took beautiful pictures at my sister's wedding.

photography.label: CEFR A2
photography.def: The art or skill of taking pictures using a camera.
photography.ex: My hobby is photography, and I love taking pictures of nature and animals.

phrasal verb.label: CEFR B1
phrasal verb.def: A verb combined with a preposition or adverb that creates a meaning different from the individual words.
phrasal verb.ex: When you 'give up' something, that phrasal verb means to stop trying, not just to hand something over.

phrase.label: CEFR B1
phrase.def: A group of words that form a unit of meaning but not a complete sentence.
phrase.ex: The phrase 'under the weather' means you feel a bit ill or unwell.

physically.label: CEFR A2
physically.def: In a way that involves the body rather than the mind.
physically.ex: He felt physically tired after running for an hour.

physician.label: CEFR B2
physician.def: A medical doctor trained and licensed to diagnose and treat illnesses and injuries in patients.
physician.ex: The physician examined the patient's test results and prescribed a course of antibiotics for the infection.

physics.label: CEFR B1
physics.def: The scientific study of matter, energy, and how objects move and interact.
physics.ex: She enjoys physics lessons because she finds experiments about forces fascinating.

pi.label: CEFR B2
pi.def: The ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter, represented by the symbol π and approximately equal to 3.14159.
pi.ex: Engineers use pi to calculate the volume of cylindrical pipes in construction projects.

piano.label: CEFR A1
piano.def: A large musical instrument with black and white keys that you press to make sounds.
piano.ex: My sister plays the piano every day after school.

pick.label: CEFR A1
pick.def: To take or choose something with your hand or fingers.
pick.ex: Can you pick an apple from the tree for me?

pickle.label: CEFR B2
pickle.def: A vegetable, usually a cucumber, preserved in a salty or vinegary liquid for storage and flavour.
pickle.ex: She opened a jar of pickles to serve alongside the sandwich at lunch.

picky.label: CEFR B2
picky.def: Hard to please; having strong preferences and rejecting many things as not good enough.
picky.ex: She's quite picky about which restaurants she'll eat at, preferring only those with excellent reviews.

picnic.label: CEFR A1
picnic.def: A meal eaten outdoors, usually in a park or countryside.
picnic.ex: We packed sandwiches and had a picnic by the lake on Sunday.

picture.label: CEFR B2
picture.def: To imagine or visualize something in your mind, or to describe something in words vividly.
picture.ex: Can you picture yourself living in a different country in five years' time?

picturesque.label: CEFR B2
picturesque.def: Visually attractive or charming, especially in a quaint or artistic way.
picturesque.ex: The picturesque village nestled in the valley had become a popular destination for landscape photographers.

pie.label: CEFR B1
pie.def: A baked dish with pastry covering fruit, meat, or vegetables inside
pie.ex: My grandmother makes the best apple pie for dessert on Sunday afternoons.

piece.label: CEFR A1
piece.def: A part or bit of something larger that has been separated or broken off
piece.ex: I ate a piece of cake after dinner.

piety.label: CEFR B1
piety.def: Devotion to religious beliefs and practices; showing respect and reverence towards God or religion
piety.ex: The monk's piety was evident in his daily prayers and his dedication to helping others in the community.

pig.label: CEFR A1
pig.def: A farm animal with a pink body, four short legs, and a curly tail.
pig.ex: The pig rolled in the mud to stay cool.

pile.label: CEFR A2
pile.def: A large amount of something stacked or heaped together in one place
pile.ex: There was a pile of dirty clothes on the bedroom floor that needed washing.

pilgrim.label: CEFR A2
pilgrim.def: A person who travels to a holy or important place for religious reasons.
pilgrim.ex: Many pilgrims visit the temple every year to pray and honour their faith.

pill.label: CEFR A2
pill.def: A small round tablet of medicine that you swallow to treat illness or pain
pill.ex: I took a pill for my headache before the flight.

pillow.label: CEFR B1
pillow.def: A soft bag filled with feathers or foam that you rest your head on in bed
pillow.ex: She placed an extra pillow behind her back to feel more comfortable while reading.

pillowcase.label: CEFR B2
pillowcase.def: A fabric cover that goes over a pillow to keep it clean and for decoration.
pillowcase.ex: She changed the white cotton pillowcase every Saturday as part of her regular cleaning routine.

pilot.label: CEFR A2
pilot.def: A person who operates and controls an aircraft during flight.
pilot.ex: The pilot announced that we would land in ten minutes.

pin.label: CEFR B1
pin.def: To fasten or hold something in place with a pin or similar object
pin.ex: She pinned the notice to the bulletin board so everyone could see it.

pine.label: CEFR B2
pine.def: A tall evergreen tree with long thin leaves called needles, valued for its wood and resin.
pine.ex: The cabin was built entirely from pine timber, which gives it a warm, rustic appearance.

pineapple.label: CEFR B1
pineapple.def: A large tropical fruit with rough, bumpy yellow skin and sweet yellow flesh inside.
pineapple.ex: She bought a fresh pineapple from the market and cut it into rings to serve for dessert.

pink.label: CEFR A2
pink.def: A light red colour made by mixing red and white
pink.ex: She wore a pink dress to the party.

pint.label: CEFR B2
pint.def: A unit of liquid measurement equal to approximately 568 millilitres in the UK.
pint.ex: He ordered a pint of bitter at the pub after work.

pipe.label: CEFR B1
pipe.def: A long, hollow tube used to carry liquids or gases from one place to another.
pipe.ex: The water pipe under the street burst, so they had to repair it.

piracy.label: CEFR B2
piracy.def: Illegal copying and distribution of copyrighted material such as films, music, or software.
piracy.ex: The film industry has invested millions in fighting online piracy through stricter digital security measures.

pirate.label: CEFR B2
pirate.def: A person who illegally copies and sells creative works like books, films, or software without permission.
pirate.ex: The publisher sued the website for distributing pirated copies of their bestselling novels.

pit.label: CEFR B1
pit.def: A deep hole in the ground, or the hard seed inside some fruits
pit.ex: Workers dug a pit to find coal beneath the earth.

pitch.label: CEFR B2
pitch.def: To present an idea or proposal to someone, hoping they will accept or support it.
pitch.ex: The startup founder pitched her innovative app to a room full of investors last week.

pity.label: CEFR A2
pity.def: A feeling of sadness for someone who is suffering or in a difficult situation.
pity.ex: I felt pity for the homeless man sleeping outside in the cold.

pizza.label: CEFR A1
pizza.def: Flat, round bread baked with cheese, tomato sauce, and other toppings.
pizza.ex: I eat pizza with my friends on Friday nights.

place.label: CEFR B1
place.def: To put something in a particular position or location.
place.ex: She placed the vase carefully on the shelf so it wouldn't fall.

plague.label: CEFR B2
plague.def: A widespread disease that infects many people or animals, causing serious illness or death.
plague.ex: The city implemented strict quarantine measures to control the plague spreading through the densely populated neighbourhoods.

plain.label: CEFR B1
plain.def: Simple, clear, and easy to understand; not complicated or decorated.
plain.ex: The instructions were written in plain language so everyone could follow them.

plan.label: CEFR B1
plan.def: To decide in advance what you will do and how you will do it.
plan.ex: We are planning a holiday to Spain next summer and have already booked our flights.

plane.label: CEFR A1
plane.def: A large aircraft that carries many passengers through the sky.
plane.ex: I took a plane to visit my cousin in another country.

planet.label: CEFR A2
planet.def: A large round object in space that moves around a star like Earth moves around the Sun.
planet.ex: Mars is a planet that scientists want to explore.

planning.label: CEFR B2
planning.def: The process of deciding what you want to do and how to organise it before you do it.
planning.ex: The success of the project depended largely on careful planning and detailed preparation by the team.

plant.label: CEFR B1
plant.def: To put seeds or a living plant in soil so it can grow.
plant.ex: We planted tomato seeds in the garden last spring, and now we have plenty of vegetables to harvest.

plastic.label: CEFR B1
plastic.def: A light, strong material made from chemicals, used to make many everyday objects.
plastic.ex: She packed her lunch in a plastic container to take to work.

plate.label: CEFR A2
plate.def: A flat, round dish used for serving or eating food
plate.ex: I put the sandwich on a plate and sat down to eat lunch.

platform.label: CEFR B1
platform.def: A raised flat surface, often used for standing or displaying things in shops.
platform.ex: The shop assistant arranged the new shoes on the platform in the window display.

play.label: CEFR A1
play.def: To do an activity for fun or enjoyment.
play.ex: The children play in the park after school.

player.label: CEFR A1
player.def: A person who plays a game or sport.
player.ex: He is a good football player on his school team.

playful.label: CEFR A2
playful.def: Wanting to have fun and make others laugh; not serious.
playful.ex: The playful dog jumped around and wagged its tail when we arrived home.

playground.label: CEFR A2
playground.def: An outdoor area with equipment and space where children play and have fun
playground.ex: After school, the children ran to the playground to play on the swings and slide.

playmate.label: CEFR B2
playmate.def: A child or person you regularly play with and spend leisure time with.
playmate.ex: She spent most of her childhood exploring the woods with her playmate from the neighbouring farm.

playwright.label: CEFR B2
playwright.def: A writer who creates scripts and stories for theatrical performances on stage.
playwright.ex: The renowned playwright Shakespeare wrote tragedies and comedies that are still performed worldwide today.

plead.label: CEFR B2
plead.def: To make an emotional or serious request; to beg or appeal strongly to someone.
plead.ex: The defendant decided to plead guilty to the charges rather than go to trial.

pleasant.label: CEFR A2
pleasant.def: Nice, enjoyable, and making you feel happy or satisfied
pleasant.ex: We had a pleasant day at the beach with our friends.

pleasantly.label: CEFR B1
pleasantly.def: In a way that is nice, friendly, or agreeable.
pleasantly.ex: She smiled pleasantly and offered us some tea when we arrived.

please.label: CEFR B1
please.def: To ask politely for something you want or need.
please.ex: Could you please help me carry these boxes to the other room?

pleased.label: CEFR A2
pleased.ex: She was pleased with her test results.

pleasing.label: CEFR A2
pleasing.def: Nice and enjoyable; making you happy or satisfied.
pleasing.ex: The pleasing smell of flowers filled the garden.

pleasure.label: CEFR A1
pleasure.def: A feeling of happiness or enjoyment
pleasure.ex: She felt great pleasure when she received her birthday present.

plenty.label: CEFR A2
plenty.def: A large or enough amount of something; more than you need.
plenty.ex: There is plenty of food at the party, so everyone can eat.

plot.label: CEFR B2
plot.def: The main story of a film, including what happens to the characters from beginning to end
plot.ex: The film's plot follows a detective investigating a mysterious disappearance in a small village.

pluck.label: CEFR B2
pluck.def: To pull or pick something quickly and forcefully with your fingers or hand.
pluck.ex: She plucked a guitar string to test if it was in tune before the performance.

plug.label: CEFR B2
plug.def: To promote or publicise something enthusiastically, often repeatedly, to increase its popularity.
plug.ex: The author spent the interview plugging her new novel to encourage people to buy it.

plum.label: CEFR B2
plum.def: A small round fruit with smooth purple or red skin, soft flesh, and a hard stone in the centre.
plum.ex: She bit into the ripe plum, enjoying its sweet juicy taste on a warm summer afternoon.

plumber.label: CEFR B2
plumber.def: A skilled worker who installs and repairs water pipes, drains, and bathroom or kitchen fixtures.
plumber.ex: We called a plumber to fix the leaking tap in our bathroom.

plural.label: CEFR A2
plural.def: The form of a word used when talking about two or more things

plus.label: CEFR B2
plus.def: An advantage or positive aspect that increases value or appeal of something.
plus.ex: One of the biggest pluses of working from home is having more time with your family.

pneumonia.label: CEFR B2
pneumonia.def: A serious lung infection that fills air sacs with fluid, making breathing difficult.
pneumonia.ex: After catching a cold during her business trip, she developed pneumonia and had to spend a week in hospital.

pocket.label: CEFR A1
pocket.def: A small bag sewn into or onto clothing to hold small items.
pocket.ex: I put my keys and phone in my jacket pocket.

pocket money.label: CEFR B1
pocket money.def: Small amount of money given regularly to a child by parents for personal spending
pocket money.ex: My parents give me £10 pocket money each week to spend on books or games.

poem.label: CEFR A1
poem.def: A piece of writing with words arranged for their sound and meaning, often with rhythm.
poem.ex: She wrote a beautiful poem about flowers for her school project.

poet.label: CEFR B1
poet.def: A person who writes poems, using creative language and emotions to express ideas.
poet.ex: The famous poet Maya Angelou wrote powerful verses about freedom and identity.

poetry.label: CEFR B1
poetry.def: Writing that uses words in a creative way to express feelings and ideas through rhythm and images.
poetry.ex: In English class, we studied romantic poetry from the 19th century and discussed what the poets meant.

point.label: CEFR A2
point.def: To show where something is by holding your finger towards it
point.ex: She pointed at the map to show me where the restaurant is.

point of view.label: CEFR B2
point of view.def: A particular way of thinking about something based on someone's position, experiences, or beliefs.
point of view.ex: From an environmental point of view, we should reduce plastic consumption significantly.

pointless.label: CEFR B2
pointless.def: Having no useful purpose or meaningful result; futile or without aim.
pointless.ex: After three failed attempts, continuing to negotiate seemed pointless without new proposals from either side.

poison.label: CEFR B2
poison.def: To give someone a harmful or toxic substance, usually in food or drink, causing illness or death.
poison.ex: The detective suspected that someone had poisoned the victim's morning coffee.

poisonous.label: CEFR B1
poisonous.def: Containing substance that can cause serious harm or death if eaten or touched.
poisonous.ex: Some mushrooms are poisonous and should never be eaten, even if they look nice.

polar bear.label: CEFR B2
polar bear.def: A large white bear found in Arctic regions, adapted to live on sea ice and hunt marine mammals.
polar bear.ex: Climate change is threatening polar bear populations as Arctic sea ice melts earlier each year.

pole.label: CEFR B1
pole.def: A long, thin, usually round stick or rod made of wood or metal.
pole.ex: He used a fishing pole to catch trout in the river.

police.label: CEFR A2
police.def: People whose job is to enforce laws and keep people safe in a community.
police.ex: When my bike was stolen, I called the police to report it.

police officer.label: CEFR A2
police officer.def: A person whose job is to enforce laws and keep people safe in a community.
police officer.ex: The police officer helped me find my lost dog yesterday.

police station.label: CEFR A2
police station.def: A building where police officers work and where people can report crimes.
police station.ex: When my bike was stolen, I went to the police station to tell the police.

policeman.label: CEFR A2
policeman.def: A man whose job is to protect people and enforce laws in a community.
policeman.ex: The policeman helped an old woman cross the busy street safely.

policewoman.label: CEFR A2
policewoman.def: A female police officer who works to protect people and enforce laws.
policewoman.ex: The policewoman helped the lost child find her parents at the train station.

polio.label: CEFR B2
polio.def: A serious infectious disease caused by a virus that can paralyse muscles and cause permanent disability or death.
polio.ex: The global polio eradication initiative has successfully reduced cases from hundreds of thousands annually to just a handful in recent years.

polish.label: CEFR B2
polish.def: A substance applied to a surface to make it smooth, shiny, and attractive.
polish.ex: She used furniture polish to restore the shine to the wooden table.

polite.label: CEFR A2
polite.def: Respectful and courteous in behaviour; showing good manners towards others.
polite.ex: She gave a polite smile and said thank you to the waiter.

politely.label: CEFR B1
politely.def: In a respectful and well-mannered way that shows consideration for others.
politely.ex: She politely asked if she could leave the meeting early.

politeness.label: CEFR B2
politeness.def: The quality of being respectful and considerate towards others through good behaviour and courteous language.
politeness.ex: She thanked him for his politeness when he offered her his seat on the crowded train.

political.label: CEFR A2
political.def: Related to government, elections, and how a country is run.
political.ex: My parents often discuss political news at dinner.

politically.label: CEFR B2
politically.def: In a way that relates to government, politics, or public policy and power.
politically.ex: The government's decision to raise taxes was politically controversial because many voters opposed it.

politician.label: CEFR B1
politician.def: A person who works in government and helps make decisions about laws and policies for a country or region.
politician.ex: The local politician promised to improve schools and roads in our neighbourhood.

politics.label: CEFR B1
politics.def: The activities and decisions involved in governing a country or making public policies.
politics.ex: Many young people are becoming more interested in politics and how their government makes decisions.

polity.label: CEFR B2
polity.def: An organised society with a system of government and shared political institutions.
polity.ex: The new polity established democratic institutions to ensure fair representation for all citizens.

pollutant.label: CEFR B1
pollutant.def: A substance that damages the air, water, or soil and harms living things.
pollutant.ex: The factory was fined for releasing dangerous pollutants into the river.

pollute.label: CEFR A2
pollute.def: To make air, water, or soil dirty and harmful.
pollute.ex: Cars and factories pollute the air in big cities.

pollution.label: CEFR A1
pollution.def: Dirty or harmful substances in the air, water, or soil that damage the environment and health.
pollution.ex: There is too much pollution in the city, so many people wear masks.

polo.label: CEFR B2
polo.def: A sport played on horseback between two teams who use mallets to hit a ball into goals.
polo.ex: She decided to take up polo after spending a summer at her cousin's estate in Argentina.

pond.label: CEFR B1
pond.def: A small body of water, usually artificial, found in parks or gardens.
pond.ex: The town park has a lovely pond where children feed the ducks on weekends.

ponder.label: CEFR B2
ponder.def: To think carefully and deeply about something for a period of time.
ponder.ex: She pondered the decision for several days before finally accepting the job offer.

pool.label: CEFR A1
pool.def: A place where people swim in water for exercise or fun
pool.ex: I go to the swimming pool every Saturday with my friends.

poor.label: CEFR A1
poor.def: Not having much money; without enough things you need.
poor.ex: The poor family did not have much food to eat.

pop.label: CEFR B2
pop.def: To burst suddenly with a sharp sound, or to move quickly and suddenly.
pop.ex: The balloon popped when she sat on it, startling everyone in the room.

popcorn.label: CEFR B1
popcorn.def: Corn kernels that are heated until they burst and become white, fluffy pieces eaten as a snack.
popcorn.ex: We bought a large bag of popcorn to eat while watching the film at the cinema.

popular.label: CEFR A2
popular.def: Liked or enjoyed by many people.
popular.ex: Pizza is a popular food with children around the world.

popularity.label: CEFR B2
popularity.def: The state of being liked, admired, or known by many people.
popularity.ex: The singer's popularity increased dramatically after she won several international awards.

popularize/popularise.label: CEFR B2
popularize/popularise.def: To make something well-known and liked by many people in society.
popularize/popularise.ex: Social media has helped to popularise plant-based diets among younger generations.

population.label: CEFR A2
population.def: The total number of people living in a place or country.
population.ex: The population of Japan is about 125 million people.

porch.label: CEFR B2
porch.def: A covered entrance to a building, typically with a roof and open or partially enclosed sides.
porch.ex: We sat on the porch swing in the evening, watching the sun set over the garden.

pork.label: CEFR B1
pork.def: Meat from a pig, used as food in cooking and meals.
pork.ex: She made a delicious pork chop dinner with roasted vegetables for her family.

port.label: CEFR B1
port.def: A coastal town or harbour where ships load and unload goods and passengers.
port.ex: The cargo ship arrived at the port early this morning to deliver thousands of containers.

portrait.label: CEFR A2
portrait.def: A painting, drawing, or photograph of a person's face or body
portrait.ex: The artist painted a beautiful portrait of the queen for the museum.

pose.label: CEFR B2
pose.def: A particular position of the body, especially when deliberately adopted for a photograph or artistic work.
pose.ex: The sculptor asked the model to hold a dramatic pose while she studied his proportions.

posh.label: CEFR B2
posh.def: Elegant and expensive, suggesting wealth and refined taste.
posh.ex: They decided to celebrate their anniversary at a posh restaurant in the city centre.

position.label: CEFR A2
position.def: The place where someone or something is located or situated.
position.ex: She sat in a comfortable position on the sofa and watched television.

positively.label: CEFR B1
positively.def: In a way that is certain, definite, or shows optimism and confidence.
positively.ex: She responded positively to the job offer and said she would start next month.

possess.label: CEFR B1
possess.def: To own or have something as yours.
possess.ex: She possesses several valuable paintings that she inherited from her grandmother.

possessed.label: CEFR B2
possessed.def: controlled or influenced strongly by an emotion, idea, or belief; appearing almost magical or supernatural
possessed.ex: She seemed possessed by an obsessive need to prove her theory correct, working late into the night every day.

possession.label: CEFR B1
possession.def: Something that you own or have with you.
possession.ex: She packed all her possessions into a small suitcase before moving to a new city.

possessive.label: CEFR B1
possessive.def: Wanting to keep someone or something for yourself and not wanting to share.
possessive.ex: He was possessive about his new car and didn't want anyone else to drive it.

possibility.label: CEFR B1
possibility.def: A thing that might happen or be true; a chance that something could occur.
possibility.ex: There is a possibility that the meeting could be postponed until next week.

possible.label: CEFR A2
possible.def: Able to happen or be done; something that might occur or exist
possible.ex: It is possible to learn English if you practise every day.

possibly.label: CEFR A2
possibly.def: Maybe; something that might happen or be true, but is not certain.
possibly.ex: It will possibly rain tomorrow, so bring an umbrella just in case.

post.label: CEFR A2
post.def: To put something on a website or social media for others to see
post.ex: She posted a photo of her holiday on Instagram yesterday.

post office.label: CEFR A2
post office.def: A building where you can send letters and packages, and buy stamps.
post office.ex: I went to the post office to mail a letter to my friend in Spain.

postal.label: CEFR B2
postal.def: Relating to the system of delivering letters and packages by mail.
postal.ex: The postal service has introduced new tracking systems to improve delivery times across the country.

postcard.label: CEFR B1
postcard.def: A small card with a picture on one side that you can send through the mail, usually from a holiday destination.
postcard.ex: She sent me a postcard from Barcelona showing the Sagrada Família cathedral.

poster.label: CEFR A1
poster.def: A large printed picture or notice you stick on a wall to advertise or decorate
poster.ex: There is a colourful poster of a new film in the shop window.

postman.label: CEFR B1
postman.def: A person whose job is to collect and deliver letters and parcels to people's homes and offices.
postman.ex: The postman delivered a package to our house this morning.

postpone.label: CEFR B2
postpone.def: To arrange for something to happen at a later time instead of the planned time.
postpone.ex: The concert was postponed until next month due to the lead singer's illness.

pot.label: CEFR B1
pot.def: A deep, round container made of metal or clay, used for cooking food or growing plants.
pot.ex: She heated water in a large pot to cook the pasta for dinner.

potato.label: CEFR A1
potato.def: A round or oval vegetable with brown, yellow, or red skin that grows underground.
potato.ex: I like to eat potato chips as a snack.

potent.label: CEFR B2
potent.def: Having great power or effect; able to produce strong results or influence.
potent.ex: The new drug proved potent in treating the disease, with patients showing significant improvement within weeks.

potentially.label: CEFR B2
potentially.def: possibly or likely to happen or become something in the future
potentially.ex: The new technology could potentially revolutionise how we treat cancer, though further research is needed.

pottery.label: CEFR B1
pottery.def: Objects made from clay and hardened by heat, such as bowls, vases, or plates.
pottery.ex: She visited a pottery workshop and learned how to make clay bowls on a wheel.

pound.label: CEFR B2
pound.def: To hit something repeatedly with force, often with a heavy object or fist.
pound.ex: She pounded on the door until someone finally answered.

pour.label: CEFR A2
pour.def: To make liquid flow from a container into something else
pour.ex: She poured milk into her coffee this morning.

poverty.label: CEFR B1
poverty.def: The state of being very poor and not having enough money for basic needs.
poverty.ex: Many families in the developing country struggle with poverty and lack access to clean water.

powder.label: CEFR B2
powder.def: To cover or dust a surface with fine, dry particles.
powder.ex: She powdered her face with makeup before the television interview.

power.label: CEFR A2
power.def: The ability to do something or make things happen.
power.ex: The government has the power to make new laws.

powerful.label: CEFR A2
powerful.def: Having great strength, energy, or effect; able to do things easily.
powerful.ex: The powerful engine in that car makes it very fast.

powerfully.label: CEFR B2
powerfully.def: In a way that has strong effect or influence; with great force or intensity.
powerfully.ex: The film's final scene powerfully illustrates the consequences of unchecked ambition.

practical.label: CEFR B1
practical.def: Useful and effective in real situations; focused on doing things rather than theory
practical.ex: She gave me practical advice about how to manage my time at university.

practicality.label: CEFR B2
practicality.def: The quality of being sensible and realistic; concerned with what actually works in real life.
practicality.ex: The practicality of the design was questionable because it required expensive materials that most people couldn't afford.

practically.label: CEFR B2
practically.def: In a way that is realistic and sensible rather than theoretical; almost or nearly.
practically.ex: The project is practically finished, with only minor details remaining to be confirmed.

practice.label: CEFR A1
practice.def: Doing an activity repeatedly to get better at it.
practice.ex: She does piano practice every evening after school.

practice/practise.label: CEFR A1
practice/practise.def: To do something many times to become better at it.
practice/practise.ex: I practise English every day to improve my speaking skills.

praise.label: CEFR B2
praise.def: To express strong approval or admiration for someone's achievements or qualities.
praise.ex: The coach praised the team's exceptional performance throughout the entire tournament.

prance.label: CEFR B2
prance.def: A movement where a horse springs forward with high, exaggerated steps, or a similar showy way of walking.
prance.ex: The dancer's prance across the stage demonstrated both strength and confidence to the audience.

prawn.label: CEFR B2
prawn.def: A shellfish with ten legs and a long body, similar to but larger than a shrimp.
prawn.ex: The restaurant's grilled prawns were served with garlic butter and fresh lemon.

pray.label: CEFR A1
pray.def: To speak to God or a god, asking for help or giving thanks.
pray.ex: She prays every morning before school.

prayer.label: CEFR B1
prayer.def: Words spoken to God or a religious figure, asking for help or giving thanks
prayer.ex: She said a prayer before dinner every evening with her family.

precede.label: CEFR B2
precede.def: To happen or occur before something else in time or order.
precede.ex: The opening ceremony will precede the main sporting events by two hours.

precious.label: CEFR B1
precious.def: Very valuable and important, either because of worth or emotional meaning.
precious.ex: She kept her grandmother's ring in a safe place because it was precious to her.

precisely.label: CEFR B2
precisely.def: In an exact way; accurately and with great attention to detail
precisely.ex: The surgeon needed to position the instrument precisely to avoid damaging nearby tissue.

predicament.label: CEFR B2
predicament.def: A difficult or unpleasant situation with no easy solution.
predicament.ex: The company found itself in a financial predicament after losing its largest customer.

predicate.label: CEFR B2
predicate.def: To base something on a particular condition, assumption, or fact; to assert or state something about a subject.
predicate.ex: The economist predicated her analysis on the assumption that interest rates would remain stable throughout the year.

predicative.label: CEFR B2
predicative.def: Describing a word or phrase that follows a linking verb and refers back to the subject
predicative.ex: In the sentence 'She is intelligent', the adjective 'intelligent' is in a predicative position following the verb 'is'.

predictable.label: CEFR B2
predictable.def: Happening or behaving in a way that you can expect or forecast in advance.
predictable.ex: The film followed a predictable plot with no surprising twists or unexpected revelations.

prediction.label: CEFR A2
prediction.def: A statement about what you think will happen in the future.
prediction.ex: The weather prediction says it will rain tomorrow.

predictive.label: CEFR B2
predictive.def: Relating to forecasting what will happen in the future based on available information or patterns.
predictive.ex: The company used predictive analytics to anticipate which customers were likely to leave and cancel their subscriptions.

predictor.label: CEFR B2
predictor.def: A person or tool that forecasts future outcomes based on data, patterns, or expertise.
predictor.ex: The economic predictor warned that interest rates would rise within the next quarter.

prefect.label: CEFR B2
prefect.def: A senior student given authority to maintain discipline and order in a school.
prefect.ex: The head prefect organised the charity fundraiser and ensured all students followed the new conduct guidelines.

prefer.label: CEFR A2
prefer.def: To like something or someone more than another thing or person.
prefer.ex: I prefer tea to coffee in the morning.

preferable.label: CEFR B2
preferable.def: Better or more suitable than something else; more desirable in comparison
preferable.ex: While both options are available, most customers find the digital version preferable to the printed version.

preferably.label: CEFR B2
preferably.def: In a way that is preferred or wanted more than other options.
preferably.ex: The conference should be held in a central location, preferably somewhere accessible by public transport.

preference.label: CEFR B1
preference.def: Something you like better than other things; what you choose when given options.
preference.ex: My preference is for coffee rather than tea in the morning.

prefix.label: CEFR B1
prefix.def: A group of letters added to the beginning of a word to change its meaning.
prefix.ex: The prefix 'un-' in 'unhappy' means the opposite of the root word.

pregnant.label: CEFR B1
pregnant.def: Expecting a baby; having a developing child in the womb.
pregnant.ex: She was pregnant with her first child and was due to give birth in three months.

prehistoric.label: CEFR B2
prehistoric.def: Belonging to the period of human history before written records existed.
prehistoric.ex: Archaeologists discovered prehistoric tools made from stone and bone at the ancient settlement.

prehuman.label: CEFR B2
prehuman.def: A human ancestor or early human-like creature that existed before modern humans evolved.
prehuman.ex: Scientists studying prehuman fossils discovered evidence of tool use dating back millions of years.

preindustrial.label: CEFR B2
preindustrial.def: Relating to the period before industrial manufacturing and factories became dominant in society.
preindustrial.ex: Many historians study preindustrial economies to understand how people lived before mechanised production transformed daily life.

prejudge.label: CEFR B2
prejudge.def: To form an opinion about something before having enough information or evidence
prejudge.ex: The judge warned the jury not to prejudge the defendant's guilt before hearing all the evidence presented in court.

prejudice.label: CEFR B1
prejudice.def: An unfair opinion about someone or something formed without knowing the facts.
prejudice.ex: She tried to overcome her prejudice against people from different countries by learning about their cultures.

preoccupy.label: CEFR B2
preoccupy.def: To occupy someone's thoughts or attention so they cannot focus on anything else.
preoccupy.ex: The upcoming exam preoccupied her mind for weeks, making it difficult to concentrate on other tasks.

preparation.label: CEFR B1
preparation.def: The process of getting ready for something by planning and organizing in advance.
preparation.ex: The team's careful preparation for the exam included weekly study sessions and practice tests.

prepare.label: CEFR A2
prepare.def: To get ready for something by doing necessary things beforehand.
prepare.ex: I need to prepare dinner before my family comes home from work.

prepared.label: CEFR B1
prepared.def: Ready and able to deal with something that may happen soon.
prepared.ex: She felt prepared for the job interview after practising her answers all week.

preposition.label: CEFR B1
preposition.def: A word that shows the relationship between a noun and other words, such as in, on, at, or with.
preposition.ex: In English grammar, you need to use the correct preposition before a noun to show time, place, or direction.

preschool.label: CEFR B1
preschool.def: Relating to education or activities for children aged three to five years old, before primary school.
preschool.ex: My daughter attends a preschool programme three times a week to prepare for starting primary school.

prescribe.label: CEFR B2
prescribe.def: To officially order or recommend a medicine or treatment for a patient.
prescribe.ex: The doctor prescribed antibiotics to treat the infection.

prescription.label: CEFR B1
prescription.def: An official written order from a doctor telling you which medicine to take and how to use it
prescription.ex: My doctor gave me a prescription for antibiotics to treat my infection.

presence.label: CEFR B1
presence.def: The fact of being in a particular place or with a particular person.
presence.ex: Your presence at the meeting was important because you could answer the technical questions.

present.label: CEFR B2
present.def: To give something to someone, or to show or introduce something to an audience.
present.ex: The researcher presented her findings at the international conference last month.

presentation.label: CEFR B1
presentation.def: A talk where someone shows information to an audience using slides or other materials.
presentation.ex: My presentation about climate change went well, and the audience asked many good questions.

presenter.label: CEFR B2
presenter.def: A person who introduces and discusses topics on television, radio, or at public events.
presenter.ex: The news presenter delivered the evening broadcast with professionalism and clarity.

preservation.label: CEFR B1
preservation.def: Keeping something in good condition so it stays safe and does not get damaged or lost.
preservation.ex: The preservation of old buildings helps us remember our history.

preserve.label: CEFR B1
preserve.def: To keep something in good condition so it lasts a long time.
preserve.ex: The museum uses special chemicals to preserve ancient paintings from damage.

presidency.label: CEFR B2
presidency.def: The position and period of time that a president holds office and leads a country or organization
presidency.ex: During his presidency, the country experienced significant economic growth and social reforms.

president.label: CEFR B1
president.def: The elected leader of a country or the head of an organisation or company.
president.ex: The president of the company announced a new strategy at the annual meeting.

presidential.label: CEFR B2
presidential.def: Relating to a president or the office and responsibilities of a president.
presidential.ex: The presidential election in November will determine the country's leadership for the next four years.

press.label: CEFR B1
press.def: To push something firmly with force or weight
press.ex: She pressed the button to start the washing machine.

pressure.label: CEFR A2
pressure.def: The force or weight that pushes down on something
pressure.ex: The pressure of the water pushed the door shut.

prestige.label: CEFR B2
prestige.def: High status and respect that someone or something gains from achievements or quality.
prestige.ex: Graduating from Oxford University carries significant prestige in the academic world.

prestigious.label: CEFR B2
prestigious.def: Highly respected and admired by many people because of excellence or high quality.
prestigious.ex: She was offered a job at a prestigious university in Oxford after completing her doctorate.

presumably.label: CEFR B2
presumably.def: Used to indicate that something is probably true based on available evidence or logical reasoning.
presumably.ex: The meeting was presumably cancelled due to the severe weather warnings issued yesterday.

pretend.label: CEFR A2
pretend.def: To act as if something is true when it is not; to imagine or make believe.
pretend.ex: The children pretended to be astronauts exploring a distant planet.

pretty.label: CEFR A2
pretty.def: Quite; fairly; somewhat (used to say something is more than a little)
pretty.ex: That film was pretty good, even though it was quite long.

prevail.label: CEFR B2
prevail.def: To be the most powerful or common; to win or succeed in a situation.
prevail.ex: Despite facing strong opposition, the new environmental policies prevailed and were implemented across the country.

prevent.label: CEFR A2
prevent.ex: Wearing a seatbelt can prevent serious injuries in a car accident.

prevention.label: CEFR B2
prevention.def: Actions taken to stop something bad from happening before it occurs.
prevention.ex: Regular exercise and a healthy diet are important for disease prevention.

preventive.label: CEFR B2
preventive.def: Designed to stop something harmful from happening rather than treating it after it occurs
preventive.ex: Preventive healthcare measures, such as regular exercise and vaccinations, help people stay healthy.

previously.label: CEFR B2
previously.def: At an earlier time in the past; before now or before something else happened
previously.ex: She had previously worked as a journalist before becoming a novelist.

price.label: CEFR B2
price.def: To set or decide the amount of money something costs.
price.ex: The supermarket priced the new organic products significantly higher than conventional alternatives.

priceless.label: CEFR B2
priceless.def: Extremely valuable and important, impossible to assign a monetary value to
priceless.ex: The antique painting was priceless, treasured by the museum for generations.

prick.label: CEFR B2
prick.def: A small hole or mark made by a sharp point; a sudden sharp pain or sensation.
prick.ex: She felt a prick of the needle as the doctor took a blood sample.

pride.label: CEFR A2
pride.def: A feeling of satisfaction and pleasure about something you have done well.
pride.ex: She felt great pride when she won the school competition.

priest.label: CEFR B1
priest.def: A person who leads religious services and ceremonies in a church or religious community.
priest.ex: The priest gave a sermon about kindness and forgiveness at Sunday's service.

primarily.label: CEFR B1
primarily.def: Mainly or mostly; more than anything else.
primarily.ex: The school focuses primarily on helping students develop strong reading skills.

primary school.label: CEFR B1
primary school.def: School for children aged approximately 5 to 11 years old, providing basic education before secondary school.
primary school.ex: My daughter started primary school last September and has already made many new friends in her class.

prime minister.label: CEFR B2
prime minister.def: The head of government in a parliamentary system, responsible for leading the country's policies and administration.
prime minister.ex: The Prime Minister announced new legislation to address the housing crisis during Parliament's session.

primitive.label: CEFR B1
primitive.def: Simple and basic, from an early stage of development or history.
primitive.ex: The primitive tools used by early humans were made from stone and bone.

prince.label: CEFR A1
prince.def: A male son of a king or queen, who may become a king.
prince.ex: The prince wore a crown at the royal ceremony.

princess.label: CEFR A1
princess.def: A female member of a royal family, usually the daughter of a king or queen.
princess.ex: The princess wore a beautiful golden crown at the royal celebration.

print.label: CEFR A2
print.def: To produce text or images on paper using a machine.
print.ex: I need to print this document before the meeting tomorrow.

printer.label: CEFR A2
printer.def: A machine that puts text and images from a computer onto paper.
printer.ex: I need to use the printer to make copies of my homework.

priority.label: CEFR B2
priority.def: Something that is more important than other things and deserves attention first.
priority.ex: The government has made public health its top priority during the pandemic.

prison.label: CEFR B1
prison.def: A place where people who have committed crimes are kept locked up as punishment.
prison.ex: The man was sentenced to five years in prison for theft.

prisoner.label: CEFR B1
prisoner.def: A person held in a prison because they have been accused or convicted of a crime.
prisoner.ex: The prisoner was released after serving a ten-year sentence for theft.

privacy.label: CEFR B1
privacy.def: The right to keep your personal life and information secret from other people.
privacy.ex: She values her privacy and doesn't share details about her family on social media.

private.label: CEFR A2
private.def: Belonging to one person or a small group, not for everyone to use or know
private.ex: She has a private bedroom where she can be alone.

privately.label: CEFR B2
privately.def: In a way that is not public or known by most people; away from others' view or knowledge.
privately.ex: The company's financial difficulties were discussed privately between the board members before any public announcement.

privilege.label: CEFR B2
privilege.def: A special right or advantage given to a person or group, not available to everyone.
privilege.ex: Attending an exclusive university was a privilege that shaped her entire career path.

prize.label: CEFR B1
prize.def: A reward given to the winner of a competition or lottery.
prize.ex: She won first prize in the photography competition and received a camera worth £500.

pro.label: CEFR A2
pro.def: A person who is skilled and paid to do something professionally.
pro.ex: She is a golf pro who teaches lessons at the club.

probability.label: CEFR B1
probability.def: The chance or likelihood that something will happen, expressed as a number between 0 and 1.
probability.ex: The probability of rain tomorrow is quite high, so you should bring an umbrella.

probable.label: CEFR B2
probable.def: Likely to happen or be true based on available evidence or reason.
probable.ex: It is probable that the company will announce redundancies before the end of the quarter.

probably.label: CEFR A2
probably.def: Very likely to happen or be true, but not certain.
probably.ex: It will probably rain tomorrow, so bring an umbrella.

probe.label: CEFR B2
probe.def: A careful investigation or examination to discover facts or information about something.
probe.ex: The government launched a probe into allegations of corruption within the department.

problem.label: CEFR A1
problem.def: Something difficult or bad that you need to fix or deal with
problem.ex: I have a problem with my phone—it won't turn on.

procedure.label: CEFR B2
procedure.def: An official or standard way of doing something, following set steps in order
procedure.ex: The hospital has a strict procedure for sterilising surgical instruments before any operation.

proclaim.label: CEFR B1
proclaim.def: To announce or declare something publicly and formally.
proclaim.ex: The president proclaimed a national day of celebration after the election victory.

procrastination.label: CEFR B1
procrastination.def: Delaying or postponing tasks even though you know you should do them now.
procrastination.ex: His procrastination meant he had to write the entire essay the night before the deadline.

produce.label: CEFR A2
produce.def: To make or grow something, especially in large quantities for sale.
produce.ex: The factory produces over 1,000 cars every month.

producer.label: CEFR B1
producer.def: A person or company that makes or creates goods, films, music, or other products for sale.
producer.ex: The film producer invested millions of pounds to make the action movie a success.

product.label: CEFR A2
product.def: Something that is made or grown to be sold or used by people.
product.ex: The supermarket sells many different products, including food, drinks, and cleaning supplies.

production.label: CEFR A2
production.def: The process of making or manufacturing goods or materials in a factory or business.
production.ex: The car production facility employs over two thousand workers.

productive.label: CEFR B1
productive.def: Producing or achieving a lot of useful work or results.
productive.ex: She had a productive day at work and completed three important projects.

profession.label: CEFR B1
profession.def: A job that requires special training or education, usually requiring a qualification.
profession.ex: She chose medicine as her profession because she wanted to help people.

professor.label: CEFR B1
professor.def: A teacher at a university or college who specializes in a particular subject.
professor.ex: Professor Ahmed has taught chemistry at the university for over fifteen years.

profile.label: CEFR B2
profile.def: A description of someone's characteristics, background, or online identity; the side view of a face.
profile.ex: The journalist created a detailed profile of the successful entrepreneur for the business magazine.

profit.label: CEFR B2
profit.def: Money that remains after all costs and expenses have been paid from revenue.
profit.ex: The company's profit increased by 15% last year despite rising production costs.

profitable.label: CEFR B2
profitable.def: Producing financial gain or surplus; generating more income than costs.
profitable.ex: The company's new digital strategy proved profitable, increasing annual revenue by thirty percent.

program/programme.label: CEFR B1
program/programme.def: To write instructions that tell a computer what to do.
program/programme.ex: She is learning to programme a simple game using Python.

programmer.label: CEFR B2
programmer.def: A professional who writes and tests computer code to develop software applications and systems.
programmer.ex: The programmer spent three weeks debugging the new feature before it was ready for release.

programming.label: CEFR B2
programming.def: Writing instructions for computers to perform specific tasks using code and technical languages.
programming.ex: She studied programming at university and now works as a software developer for a major technology company.

progress.label: CEFR B2
progress.def: To move forward or advance towards a goal, or to improve and develop over time.
progress.ex: The research has progressed significantly since we implemented the new methodology.

progressive.label: CEFR A2
progressive.def: Happening or developing slowly, step by step, over a period of time.
progressive.ex: She has a progressive illness that gets worse each year.

prohibit.label: CEFR B2
prohibit.def: To officially forbid something by law or authority; to make something illegal or not allowed.
prohibit.ex: Many countries prohibit the sale of certain pesticides that are harmful to the environment.

prohibition.label: CEFR B1
prohibition.def: An official rule or law that forbids something from being done or used
prohibition.ex: The government introduced a prohibition on smoking in public buildings to protect people's health.

projection.label: CEFR B2
projection.def: A calculation or estimate of a future amount based on current information and trends.
projection.ex: The government's projection suggests that inflation will decrease by next year.

prom.label: CEFR B2
prom.def: A formal dance event organised by a secondary school, typically for students nearing the end of their final year.
prom.ex: She spent weeks shopping for the perfect dress to wear to prom.

prominence.label: CEFR B2
prominence.def: The state of being famous, important, or easily noticed by many people.
prominence.ex: The young athlete gained prominence after winning three Olympic gold medals.

prominent.label: CEFR B1
prominent.def: Very noticeable or famous; standing out more than other things or people
prominent.ex: She is a prominent scientist who has won many international awards for her research.

promise.label: CEFR B1
promise.def: To tell someone you will definitely do something in the future.
promise.ex: She promised to help me with my homework after school.

promotion.label: CEFR B1
promotion.def: A move to a higher or more important job position, usually with better pay and more responsibility.
promotion.ex: After working hard for three years, Sarah received a promotion to team manager at her company.

prompt.label: CEFR B2
prompt.def: To cause someone to do or say something, or to remind someone of something they nearly forgot.
prompt.ex: The journalist's question prompted the minister to clarify the government's position on climate policy.

promptly.label: CEFR B2
promptly.def: Without delay; done quickly after something is requested or expected
promptly.ex: The company promptly responded to the customer's complaint within 24 hours.

pronoun.label: CEFR B1
pronoun.def: A word used instead of a noun or noun phrase, like he, she, it, or they.
pronoun.ex: Instead of saying 'John is a teacher,' you can use a pronoun and say 'He is a teacher.'

pronounce.label: CEFR A2
pronounce.def: To make the sounds of a word or language with your voice.
pronounce.ex: My teacher helped me pronounce the difficult French word correctly.

pronunciation.label: CEFR A2
pronunciation.def: The way a word is spoken or sounded out.
pronunciation.ex: My teacher helped me with the pronunciation of difficult English words.

proof.label: CEFR B1
