roam.ex: During his sabbatical, he embarked on a six-month roam through Southeast Asia, staying in each country only as long as it interested him.

roar.label: CEFR B2
roar.def: To make a loud, deep sound like a lion or angry crowd; to laugh very loudly.
roar.ex: The audience roared with laughter when the comedian delivered the punchline.

roast.label: CEFR A2
roast.def: To cook food with dry heat in an oven or over fire.
roast.ex: On Sunday, my family roasts a chicken with vegetables for dinner.

rob.label: CEFR A2
rob.def: To take money or things from a person or place illegally and by force.
rob.ex: The man robbed a bank and ran away with a lot of cash.

robbery.label: CEFR A2
robbery.def: Crime of taking money or things from a person or place using force or threats.
robbery.ex: The police arrested three men suspected of a bank robbery last week.

robin.label: CEFR A2
robin.def: A small bird with a red breast and brown back, common in Europe and North America.
robin.ex: A robin landed on the garden fence this morning.

robot.label: CEFR B1
robot.def: A machine programmed to perform tasks automatically, often mimicking human or animal movements.
robot.ex: The factory uses robots to assemble car parts quickly and safely.

rock.label: CEFR B2
rock.def: To move gently back and forth or side to side in a steady, soothing motion.
rock.ex: She rocked the cradle gently to help the baby fall asleep.

rocket.label: CEFR B2
rocket.def: A projectile or spacecraft propelled by engines that expel hot gases at high speed.
rocket.ex: The space agency launched a rocket carrying supplies to the International Space Station.

rocky.label: CEFR B2
rocky.def: Full of rocks or covered with large stones; unstable and difficult
rocky.ex: The mountain climbers had to navigate the rocky terrain carefully to avoid falling.

roll.label: CEFR B2
roll.def: To turn over and over, moving along a surface by rotating continuously.
roll.ex: The ball rolled across the garden and disappeared under the fence.

rolling.label: CEFR B1
rolling.def: Continuously moving forward in waves or undulating motions; also means continuing without interruption
rolling.ex: The rolling hills of the countryside stretched out for miles in every direction.

romance.label: CEFR A2
romance.def: A close, loving relationship between two people, often with physical attraction.
romance.ex: She fell in love with him, and their romance lasted for many years.

romantic.label: CEFR A2
romantic.def: Showing strong feelings of love or relating to love between two people.
romantic.ex: He gave his wife romantic flowers and chocolates for Valentine's Day.

roof.label: CEFR A2
roof.def: The structure that covers the top of a building and protects it from rain and snow.
roof.ex: We climbed onto the roof to fix the broken tiles.

room.label: CEFR B1
room.def: To have enough space for something or someone to fit inside
room.ex: The car doesn't room five people comfortably on long journeys.

roommate.label: CEFR B2
roommate.def: A person who shares an apartment or house with you, typically splitting rent and household expenses.
roommate.ex: My roommate and I take turns cleaning the kitchen and buying groceries for our flat.

root.label: CEFR A2
root.def: The part of a plant that grows under the ground and takes in water.
root.ex: The tree's roots go deep into the soil to find water.

rope.label: CEFR A2
rope.def: A thick, strong cord made by twisting fibres together, used for tying or pulling things.
rope.ex: The sailor tied the boat to the dock with a thick rope.

rose.label: CEFR A1
rose.def: A flower with a sweet smell, often red, pink, or white, with sharp points on the stem.
rose.ex: He gave his wife a red rose for her birthday.

rosy.label: CEFR B2
rosy.def: Optimistic or hopeful; or having a pink or reddish colour.
rosy.ex: Despite the economic challenges, the financial analyst painted a rosy picture of the company's future prospects.

rotten.label: CEFR B1
rotten.def: Decayed or spoiled, no longer fresh or good enough to use
rotten.ex: We threw away the rotten apples before they attracted insects to the fruit bowl.

rough.label: CEFR B1
rough.def: Having a surface that is not smooth; uneven or bumpy to touch
rough.ex: The rough texture of the sandpaper made it perfect for smoothing the wooden table.

roughly.label: CEFR A2
roughly.ex: There are roughly 30 students in my English class.

round.label: CEFR B2
round.def: To turn a corner or go around something in a curved path or motion.
round.ex: As the runner rounded the final bend of the track, she accelerated towards the finish line.

roundabout.label: CEFR A2
roundabout.def: A circular road where traffic moves around a central point before exiting
roundabout.ex: At the roundabout, turn left onto the main street.

routine.label: CEFR B1
routine.def: A set of normal activities you do regularly in the same order each day.
routine.ex: My morning routine includes having breakfast and checking my emails before work.

row.label: CEFR B2
row.def: To propel a boat through water using oars.
row.ex: We rowed across the lake in the early morning, enjoying the peaceful silence.

royal.label: CEFR A2
royal.def: Relating to a king or queen, or their family.
royal.ex: The royal family lives in a big palace in London.

royalty.label: CEFR B2
royalty.def: Payment made to a creator or rights holder for each sale or use of their work.
royalty.ex: The author receives royalties every time her book is sold in bookshops across the country.

rub.label: CEFR B2
rub.def: To apply pressure and move something back and forth across a surface repeatedly.
rub.ex: She rubbed her tired eyes after staring at the screen all afternoon.

rubber.label: CEFR B1
rubber.def: A stretchy material made from natural or synthetic substances, used to make tyres, erasers, and waterproof items.
rubber.ex: She used a rubber to remove the pencil marks from her notebook.

rubbish.label: CEFR B1
rubbish.def: Waste material or things you throw away because you no longer want them
rubbish.ex: I put the rubbish in the bin every Monday morning before the collection truck arrives.

rubble.label: CEFR B2
rubble.def: Broken pieces of stone, brick, or concrete from a destroyed building or structure.
rubble.ex: The earthquake left the city centre buried in rubble, with rescue teams searching for survivors for weeks.

rude.label: CEFR A1
rude.def: Not polite; speaking or behaving in a way that is not respectful or kind
rude.ex: It was rude of him to interrupt her while she was talking.

rudely.label: CEFR B1
rudely.def: In a way that is not polite or respectful to other people.
rudely.ex: He rudely interrupted her while she was speaking at the meeting.

rug.label: CEFR B2
rug.def: A thick piece of fabric covering part of a floor, typically smaller than a carpet
rug.ex: She placed a Persian rug in the centre of the living room to add warmth and colour to the space.

rugby.label: CEFR A2
rugby.def: A contact team sport played with an oval ball that players carry, throw, and kick.
rugby.ex: My brother plays rugby on weekends for his local club.

ruin.label: CEFR A2
ruin.def: To damage something so badly that it cannot be used or enjoyed anymore.
ruin.ex: The heavy rain ruined our picnic, so we went home early.

ruinous.label: CEFR B2
ruinous.def: Causing severe financial loss or complete destruction; extremely damaging or harmful.
ruinous.ex: The ruinous costs of the legal battle forced the company to declare bankruptcy.

rule.label: CEFR B2
rule.def: To make an official decision or judgement, especially in a legal or formal context.
rule.ex: The judge ruled that the contract was invalid due to the missing signatures.

ruler.label: CEFR A1
ruler.def: A long, flat tool used to draw straight lines or measure distances
ruler.ex: I used a ruler to draw a straight line on my paper.

rumor/rumour.label: CEFR A2
rumor/rumour.def: Information that people are talking about but may not be true.
rumor/rumour.ex: There is a rumour that the school will close next year, but nobody knows if it is real.

run.label: CEFR A1
run.def: To move very fast on your feet, faster than walking.
run.ex: She runs to school every morning.

runaway.label: CEFR B1
runaway.def: A person, usually young, who has left home without permission and does not intend to return.
runaway.ex: The police found the runaway sleeping rough under a bridge after three days of searching.

runner.label: CEFR A2
runner.def: A person who runs, especially as exercise or in a race or sport.
runner.ex: She is a fast runner and wins most of the competitions at school.

running.label: CEFR A2
running.def: Moving fast on foot as a sport or exercise.
running.ex: She goes running in the park every morning before work.

runway.label: CEFR B1
runway.def: A long, flat strip of ground where aircraft take off and land.
runway.ex: The plane accelerated down the runway before lifting into the sky.

rural.label: CEFR B2
rural.def: Relating to the countryside or farming areas rather than cities or towns.
rural.ex: Many rural communities have limited access to healthcare services compared to urban centres.

rush.label: CEFR B1
rush.def: To move or do something very quickly, often because you don't have much time.
rush.ex: I rushed to the station this morning because my train was leaving in five minutes.

rush hour.label: CEFR B2
rush hour.def: The busiest time of day when many people travel to or from work, typically causing traffic congestion.
rush hour.ex: Commuters face significant delays during rush hour on the motorway every weekday morning.

rusty.label: CEFR B2
rusty.def: Not used or practised for a long time, so skills or knowledge have decreased.
rusty.ex: After five years away from the sport, his tennis skills were quite rusty, though he quickly improved with practice.

sack.label: CEFR B2
sack.def: To dismiss someone from their job, usually suddenly or without notice.
sack.ex: The company decided to sack half of its workforce due to the economic downturn.

sacred.label: CEFR B1
sacred.def: Holy or deserving great respect because of religious or cultural importance.
sacred.ex: Many people consider their family traditions sacred and pass them down to their children.

sad.label: CEFR A1
sad.def: Feeling unhappy or upset about something.
sad.ex: She was sad when her friend moved to another city.

sadly.label: CEFR A2
sadly.def: In a way that shows you feel unhappy or disappointed about something.
sadly.ex: She sadly watched her friend move to another city.

sadness.label: CEFR B1
sadness.def: A feeling of unhappiness or sorrow about something.
sadness.ex: She felt a deep sadness after her grandmother passed away.

safari.label: CEFR B1
safari.def: A journey to watch wild animals in their natural habitat, usually in Africa.
safari.ex: We booked a safari in Kenya to see lions, elephants, and giraffes in the national park.

safe.label: CEFR A2
safe.def: Not in danger; protected from harm or risk
safe.ex: It is safe to cross the street when the traffic light is green.

safeguard.label: CEFR B2
safeguard.def: To protect something important from harm, loss, or damage.
safeguard.ex: The government introduced new regulations to safeguard consumers' personal data from cyber theft.

safely.label: CEFR B1
safely.def: In a way that prevents harm or danger from happening.
safely.ex: The driver safely crossed the busy intersection by waiting for the traffic lights.

safety.label: CEFR B1
safety.def: The condition of being protected from harm, danger, or risk.
safety.ex: The factory inspector checked the safety equipment to ensure workers were protected.

sag.label: CEFR B2
sag.def: A drooping or downward curve in something that should be straight or level.
sag.ex: The architect noticed a slight sag in the ceiling beam and recommended immediate repairs.

sage.label: CEFR B2
sage.def: A person respected for their wisdom and experience, often giving advice to others.
sage.ex: The village sage spent decades studying philosophy and was frequently consulted by younger residents seeking guidance.

sail.label: CEFR B2
sail.def: To travel across water in a boat using wind to move forward
sail.ex: The yacht will sail from Southampton to the Mediterranean next month.

sailing.label: CEFR A2
sailing.def: The sport or activity of moving a boat across water using wind and sails.
sailing.ex: My family goes sailing on the lake every summer weekend.

sailor.label: CEFR A1
sailor.def: A person who works on a ship or boat at sea.
sailor.ex: My grandfather was a sailor and travelled to many countries.

sake.label: CEFR A2
sake.def: The reason or purpose for doing something; benefit or interest of a person or thing
sake.ex: She studied hard for the sake of her future career.

salad.label: CEFR A1
salad.def: A dish of raw vegetables mixed together, sometimes with dressing or meat.
salad.ex: I eat a salad with tomatoes and lettuce for lunch.

salary.label: CEFR B2
salary.def: Fixed amount of money paid regularly to an employee, usually monthly or annually.
salary.ex: She negotiated a higher salary when she accepted the new position at the multinational company.

sale.label: CEFR A1
sale.def: A time when a shop sells things at lower prices than usual.
sale.ex: There is a big sale at the supermarket this week, so I bought new shoes for less money.

salesman.label: CEFR B1
salesman.def: A person whose job is to sell products or services to customers.
salesman.ex: The car salesman showed us several models and explained the features of each one.

salesmanship.label: CEFR B2
salesmanship.def: The skill of persuading people to buy products or services through effective communication and presentation techniques.
salesmanship.ex: Her excellent salesmanship helped the company exceed its quarterly targets by 40 percent.

salesperson.label: CEFR B2
salesperson.def: Someone employed to sell products or services to customers in shops, by phone, or online.
salesperson.ex: The car salesperson explained the vehicle's features and negotiated a competitive price with the buyer.

saleswoman.label: CEFR B2
saleswoman.def: A woman whose job is to sell products or services to customers
saleswoman.ex: The experienced saleswoman at the electronics store helped me choose the right laptop for my needs.

salmon.label: CEFR B1
salmon.def: A large fish with pink or orange flesh that lives in both ocean and fresh water.
salmon.ex: We grilled the salmon with lemon and herbs for dinner last night.

salon.label: CEFR B2
salon.def: A business providing personal care services such as hairdressing, beauty treatments, or nail care.
salon.ex: She decided to open a hair salon in the city centre after completing her training as a stylist.

salsa.label: CEFR B2
salsa.def: A spicy sauce made from tomatoes, onions, and chillies, or a lively Latin American dance style.
salsa.ex: The restaurant serves authentic Mexican salsa with crispy tortilla chips as a complimentary starter.

salt.label: CEFR A2
salt.def: A white mineral that makes food taste salty; used for cooking and seasoning.
salt.ex: I added a pinch of salt to the soup to make it taste better.

salty.label: CEFR B2
salty.def: Containing a lot of salt; also means bitter, resentful, or upset about something
salty.ex: After losing the match, he made some salty remarks about the referee's unfair decisions.

same.label: CEFR A1
same.def: Not different; exactly like something else
same.ex: I have the same bag as my friend.

sample.label: CEFR A2
sample.def: A small amount of something that shows what the rest is like.
sample.ex: The shop gave me a free sample of the new chocolate to try before buying.

sand.label: CEFR B1
sand.def: Fine, loose grains of rock found on beaches and in deserts.
sand.ex: We built a sandcastle on the beach and played in the sand all afternoon.

sandal.label: CEFR B1
sandal.def: A light shoe with straps across the foot, leaving most of it uncovered.
sandal.ex: I wore comfortable sandals to the beach because it was too hot for shoes.

sandstone.label: CEFR B2
sandstone.def: A type of sedimentary rock formed from sand grains compacted and cemented together over time.
sandstone.ex: The ancient sandstone cliffs along the coast have been shaped by centuries of wind and water erosion.

sandwich.label: CEFR A1
sandwich.def: Two pieces of bread with food between them.
sandwich.ex: I made a cheese sandwich for lunch.

sandy.label: CEFR A2
sandy.def: Containing sand or covered with sand.
sandy.ex: We walked along the sandy beach and built a sandcastle.

sanitary.label: CEFR B2
sanitary.def: Clean and safe from bacteria and disease; designed to protect health and prevent infection.
sanitary.ex: The restaurant failed its sanitary inspection because food was stored at improper temperatures.

sanitation.label: CEFR B2
sanitation.def: Systems and practices for keeping places clean and safe from harmful bacteria and disease.
sanitation.ex: The government invested in improved water sanitation to reduce the spread of cholera in rural areas.

satellite.label: CEFR B1
satellite.def: An object that orbits a planet or star, either natural like the Moon or artificial made by humans.
satellite.ex: The weather satellite provides important data about storms and climate patterns across the region.

satisfaction.label: CEFR B1
satisfaction.def: A feeling of pleasure or contentment because something meets your needs or expectations.
satisfaction.ex: The restaurant manager asked customers about their satisfaction with the meal and service.

satisfactory.label: CEFR B2
satisfactory.def: Acceptable and meeting requirements or expectations, though not particularly good or impressive.
satisfactory.ex: The student's exam results were satisfactory, earning him a pass but not high enough for a distinction.

satisfied.label: CEFR B1
satisfied.def: Happy and content because you got what you wanted or needed.
satisfied.ex: After eating a large dinner, I felt satisfied and didn't want any dessert.

satisfy.label: CEFR A2
satisfy.def: To make someone feel happy or pleased because they got what they wanted
satisfy.ex: The pizza satisfied my hunger, and I felt full and happy.

saturday.label: CEFR A1
saturday.def: The sixth day of the week, between Friday and Sunday.
saturday.ex: I play football with my friends on Saturday afternoon.

sauce.label: CEFR A2
sauce.def: A liquid or creamy topping added to food to give it flavour.
sauce.ex: She poured tomato sauce over her pasta.

saucepan.label: CEFR B1
saucepan.def: A round metal cooking pot with a long handle, used for heating food or liquid on a stove
saucepan.ex: She heated the milk in a saucepan before pouring it into the cup.

saucer.label: CEFR B1
saucer.def: A small flat plate, usually placed under a cup to catch spills and protect surfaces.
saucer.ex: She carefully placed the teacup on its saucer before serving it to her guest.

sausage.label: CEFR A2
sausage.def: A tube of skin filled with seasoned meat, cooked and eaten as food.
sausage.ex: My children like to eat sausages and chips for lunch.

save.label: CEFR A1
save.def: To keep money instead of spending it, or to rescue someone from danger
save.ex: I save money every month to buy a new bicycle.

saving/savings.label: CEFR B2
saving/savings.def: Money set aside from income or resources for future use rather than spent immediately.
saving/savings.ex: Her savings from years of careful budgeting allowed her to purchase a house without taking out a large mortgage.

saw.label: CEFR B2
saw.def: To perceive with your eyes; to witness or observe something happening.
saw.ex: I saw the documentary last week and was deeply moved by its message about climate change.

say.label: CEFR A1
say.def: To speak words; to tell someone something using your voice.
say.ex: She said hello to her friend at school.

scale.label: CEFR A2
scale.def: A series of numbers or marks used to measure or compare the size, amount, or level of something
scale.ex: On a scale of one to ten, how much do you like this film?

scan.label: CEFR B2
scan.def: To look over something quickly to find specific information or get a general idea.
scan.ex: She scanned the document for any mention of the deadline before the meeting.

scandal.label: CEFR B2
scandal.def: A situation causing public outrage due to immoral, illegal, or dishonourable behaviour by prominent people
scandal.ex: The financial scandal forced the CEO to resign and damaged the company's reputation.

scar.label: CEFR B2
scar.def: A permanent mark on skin left after a wound or injury heals.
scar.ex: The accident left a scar on her left arm that gradually faded over several years.

scarcely.label: CEFR B2
scarcely.def: Only just; almost not; in a way that is barely true or sufficient
scarcely.ex: We had scarcely finished breakfast when the phone rang.

scare.label: CEFR B1
scare.def: To make someone feel fear or sudden worry.
scare.ex: The loud noise outside scared me, so I turned on all the lights in my house.

scared.label: CEFR B1
scared.def: Feeling fear or anxiety about something that might happen or is happening
scared.ex: She felt scared when she heard strange noises outside her house late at night.

scarf.label: CEFR A2
scarf.def: A long piece of cloth you wear around your neck or shoulders to keep warm
scarf.ex: She wrapped her scarf around her neck before going outside in the cold.

scarlet.label: CEFR B2
scarlet.def: A bright red colour, often used in literary or formal contexts
scarlet.ex: The actress wore a scarlet dress to the premiere, which made her stand out against the photographers' backdrop.

scary.label: CEFR B1
scary.def: Causing fear or making you feel frightened or nervous.
scary.ex: The horror film was so scary that I couldn't watch it alone at night.

scatter.label: CEFR B1
scatter.def: To throw or spread things in different directions over a wide area
scatter.ex: The wind scattered the fallen leaves all across the garden path.

scene.label: CEFR A2
scene.def: A part of a book, film, or play where action happens in one place and time
scene.ex: In the first scene of the play, the two characters meet in a coffee shop.

scenery.label: CEFR A2
scenery.def: The natural landscape and views you see in a place, like mountains, forests, or fields.
scenery.ex: The scenery in the Swiss Alps was absolutely beautiful when we visited last summer.

scenic.label: CEFR B1
scenic.def: Having beautiful natural views or landscapes that are pleasant to look at.
scenic.ex: We drove along the scenic route through the mountains instead of taking the motorway.

scent.label: CEFR B2
scent.def: A distinctive smell, often pleasant, that is carried through the air from a source.
scent.ex: The scent of jasmine flowers drifted through the open window on the warm evening.

schedule.label: CEFR B2
schedule.def: To plan and arrange for something to happen at a particular time.
schedule.ex: The company scheduled the meeting for next Tuesday at 2 PM.

scheduled.label: CEFR B2
scheduled.def: Planned or arranged to happen at a particular time
scheduled.ex: The scheduled departure time for the flight is 14:30, but it may be delayed due to weather conditions.

scholar.label: CEFR B1
scholar.def: A person who studies a subject deeply and knows a lot about it.
scholar.ex: The medieval scholar spent years researching ancient manuscripts in the university library.

scholarship.label: CEFR B1
scholarship.def: Money given to a student to help pay for education at a school or university
scholarship.ex: She won a scholarship to study medicine at Oxford University because of her excellent exam results.

scholasticism.label: CEFR B2
scholasticism.def: A medieval philosophical approach emphasizing logical debate and reconciling faith with reason through systematic argument.
scholasticism.ex: Scholasticism dominated European universities during the Middle Ages, requiring students to master rigorous debate techniques and theological texts.

school.label: CEFR A1
school.def: A place where children go to learn and study.
school.ex: I go to school every day to learn English and maths.

schoolchild.label: CEFR A2
schoolchild.def: A young person who goes to school to learn.
schoolchild.ex: Every schoolchild in the class had to write an essay about their favourite book.

schoolgirl.label: CEFR B2
schoolgirl.def: A female student who attends school, typically from primary to secondary education level.
schoolgirl.ex: The schoolgirl waited nervously outside the headmaster's office for her exam results.

schoolmate.label: CEFR B1
schoolmate.def: A person who attends or attended the same school as you
schoolmate.ex: I bumped into my old schoolmate at the supermarket, and we hadn't seen each other in ten years.

schoolteacher.label: CEFR B1
schoolteacher.def: A person who teaches children or teenagers in a school.
schoolteacher.ex: My mother was a schoolteacher for thirty years before she retired.

schoolwork.label: CEFR A2
schoolwork.def: Tasks and exercises that students do for their classes at school or at home.
schoolwork.ex: My schoolwork takes about two hours every evening after classes finish.

science.label: CEFR A1
science.def: The study of nature and how things work using experiments and observation.
science.ex: I like science because we do fun experiments in the laboratory.

science fiction.label: CEFR B1
science fiction.def: Stories about imaginary future worlds, advanced technology, or space exploration that don't exist yet.
science fiction.ex: She loves reading science fiction novels about colonies on Mars and intelligent robots.

scientific.label: CEFR A2
scientific.def: Related to science and the study of nature through observation and experiments.
scientific.ex: Scientists use scientific methods to discover how plants grow.

scientifically.label: CEFR B2
scientifically.def: In a way based on careful observation, testing, and logical reasoning rather than opinion.
scientifically.ex: The researchers scientifically proved that the new treatment reduces symptoms by 40 percent.

scientist.label: CEFR A1
scientist.def: A person who studies nature and conducts experiments to learn new things.
scientist.ex: The scientist discovered a new type of medicine in the laboratory.

scissors.label: CEFR A2
scissors.def: A tool with two sharp blades used for cutting paper, cloth, or other materials.
scissors.ex: I used scissors to cut the ribbon for the present.

scold.label: CEFR B1
scold.def: To speak angrily to someone because they have done something wrong.
scold.ex: The teacher scolded the students for arriving late to class without an explanation.

scooter.label: CEFR B2
scooter.def: A small vehicle with a low platform, handlebars, and usually two wheels that you stand on and propel by pushing one foot against the ground.
scooter.ex: She commuted to work by scooter, weaving through traffic much faster than she could by bicycle.

score.label: CEFR B1
score.def: To gain points or goals in a game or competition
score.ex: She scored three goals in the football match yesterday.

scramble.label: CEFR B2
scramble.def: To move quickly and with difficulty, often using hands and feet; to rush urgently
scramble.ex: The hikers had to scramble up the rocky slope to reach the summit before sunset.

scratch.label: CEFR B2
scratch.def: To rub or scrape a surface with something sharp or rough, often causing marks or damage.
scratch.ex: She accidentally scratched the car door with her keys while opening it in the car park.

scream.label: CEFR B1
scream.def: To shout very loudly, usually because of fear, pain, or strong emotion
scream.ex: The audience screamed with excitement when their favourite band took the stage.

screen.label: CEFR B2
screen.def: To examine or test someone or something carefully to check for problems or disease.
screen.ex: All passengers are screened for security risks before boarding the aeroplane.

screw.label: CEFR B2
screw.def: To fasten or tighten something by twisting a screw into it with a tool.
screw.ex: She carefully screwed the bracket to the wall before hanging the shelf.

scribble.label: CEFR B2
scribble.def: Hasty, careless writing or drawing that is difficult to read or understand
scribble.ex: The artist's notebook was filled with scribbles and sketches exploring ideas for the exhibition.

script.label: CEFR A2
script.def: Written words for a play, film, or television show that actors speak
script.ex: The actor read the script carefully before the rehearsal began.

scrooge.label: CEFR B2
scrooge.def: A person who is extremely unwilling to spend money and keeps it for themselves.
scrooge.ex: His colleagues considered him a scrooge because he never contributed to office collections despite earning a high salary.

scuba.label: CEFR B2
scuba.def: Equipment and technique for breathing underwater while swimming, allowing exploration of submerged environments.
scuba.ex: She completed her scuba certification course last summer and has been diving at coral reefs ever since.

sculpture.label: CEFR B1
sculpture.def: A three-dimensional artwork made by carving or shaping materials like stone, wood, or metal.
sculpture.ex: The museum's garden displays beautiful marble sculptures from the Renaissance period.

scythe.label: CEFR B2
scythe.def: A long-handled tool with a curved blade, traditionally used for cutting grass or harvesting crops.
scythe.ex: The farmer sharpened his scythe before cutting the wheat in the early morning.

sea.label: CEFR A1
sea.def: A large area of salt water that covers much of Earth's surface.
sea.ex: Many people enjoy swimming in the sea during summer holidays.

seafood.label: CEFR A2
seafood.def: Fish and other animals that live in the sea and are eaten as food.
seafood.ex: My favourite seafood is salmon, but I also enjoy eating prawns and mussels.

seal.label: CEFR B2
seal.def: An official mark or design that confirms something is genuine, authorised, or closed securely.
seal.ex: The letter bore the government's official seal, indicating it was an authentic document.

search.label: CEFR B1
search.def: To try to find something by looking carefully in different places
search.ex: The police searched the house for evidence related to the crime.

seaside.label: CEFR A2
seaside.def: The area where the sea meets the land; the coast where people go for holidays.
seaside.ex: We spent our summer holiday at the seaside, swimming and building sandcastles.

season.label: CEFR B1
season.def: One of the four main periods of the year with different weather patterns and characteristics.
season.ex: Winter is my favourite season because I enjoy skiing in the mountains.

seasonal.label: CEFR B2
seasonal.def: Happening or available only during a particular time of year.
seasonal.ex: Many workers in the tourism industry rely on seasonal employment during summer months.

seat.label: CEFR A1
seat.def: A chair or similar object you sit on
seat.ex: Please take a seat at the table.

seawater.label: CEFR B1
seawater.def: Water from the ocean, containing salt and minerals.
seawater.ex: The ship's crew collected seawater samples to study ocean pollution.

seaweed.label: CEFR B1
seaweed.def: Plant that grows in the sea and is eaten as food in many cultures.
seaweed.ex: In Japanese cuisine, seaweed is commonly used to wrap sushi rolls.

second.label: CEFR A2
second.def: A very short period of time, one of sixty equal parts of a minute.
second.ex: Wait a second, I need to find my keys.

second person.label: CEFR B2
second person.def: The grammatical perspective referring to the person being addressed, using pronouns like you.
second person.ex: In the instruction manual, the writer uses second person to speak directly to the reader about how to operate the device.

secondary.label: CEFR B2
secondary.def: Less important or significant than something else; coming after the primary or main thing
secondary.ex: The report's secondary findings suggested that diet might also play a role in the patient's recovery.

secondary school.label: CEFR B1
secondary school.def: A school for students aged approximately 11 to 18, after primary school and before university.
secondary school.ex: My daughter starts secondary school next September, where she will study a wide range of subjects including maths, English, and science.

second-hand.label: CEFR B1
second-hand.def: Not new; previously owned or used by someone else before being sold again
second-hand.ex: We bought our car second-hand to save money, and it runs perfectly well.

secondly.label: CEFR B2
secondly.def: Used to introduce the second point or idea in a sequence of arguments.
secondly.ex: The proposal has several advantages: firstly, it reduces costs, and secondly, it improves efficiency.

secret.label: CEFR A2
secret.def: Something that you keep hidden and do not tell other people.
secret.ex: She told me a secret, and I promised not to tell anyone.

secretary.label: CEFR A2
secretary.def: A person who works in an office doing administrative tasks like writing, filing, and answering phones.
secretary.ex: The secretary at the hospital helps patients fill out forms and books appointments.

secretly.label: CEFR B1
secretly.def: In a way that is hidden or kept away from others' knowledge.
secretly.ex: She secretly saved money for months to buy her mother a gift.

security.label: CEFR B1
security.def: Protection from danger or harm; feeling safe and confident about something
security.ex: The airport has tight security to keep all passengers safe from threats.

see.label: CEFR A1
see.def: To perceive with your eyes; to look at something.
see.ex: I can see a bird in the tree.

seed.label: CEFR A2
seed.def: A small object produced by a plant that can grow into a new plant.
seed.ex: She planted a tomato seed in the garden and watered it every day.

seem.label: CEFR A2
seem.def: To appear to be something, based on what you observe or feel
seem.ex: She seems happy today, even though she had a difficult week.

seesaw.label: CEFR B2
seesaw.def: A plank balanced on a central pivot where two people sit at opposite ends and move up and down alternately.
seesaw.ex: The children took turns on the seesaw in the playground, laughing as they pushed off the ground to lift their friends into the air.

segment.label: CEFR B2
segment.def: A distinct part or section of something larger, often separated by clear boundaries.
segment.ex: The market research team analysed each customer segment to develop targeted marketing strategies.

seize.label: CEFR B1
seize.def: To take something quickly and forcefully, often without permission.
seize.ex: The police seized the stolen goods from the suspect's apartment.

seldom.label: CEFR B2
seldom.ex: She seldom visits her hometown, as she has lived abroad for the past decade.

selection.label: CEFR B1
selection.def: A group of things chosen from a larger group; the act of choosing.
selection.ex: The shop has a good selection of coffee beans from different countries.

self.label: CEFR A1
self.def: A person's own body, mind, or personality; who you are.
self.ex: I need to think about my own self and what makes me happy.

self-confidence.label: CEFR B2
self-confidence.def: Belief in your own abilities and judgment to succeed in what you do.
self-confidence.ex: Her self-confidence grew after she successfully completed the challenging project at work.

self-confident.label: CEFR B2
self-confident.def: Believing in your own abilities and feeling sure about yourself
self-confident.ex: She gave a self-confident presentation to the board, speaking clearly without any hesitation.

selfish.label: CEFR B1
selfish.def: Caring only about yourself and not thinking about what other people need or want
selfish.ex: It was selfish of him to take the last piece of cake without asking if anyone else wanted it.

selfless.label: CEFR B2
selfless.def: Caring more about others' needs and happiness than your own interests.
selfless.ex: Her selfless dedication to helping refugees throughout the war earned her international recognition.

self-service.label: CEFR B1
self-service.def: Describing a place or system where customers help themselves instead of being served by staff
self-service.ex: At the self-service restaurant, you pick up your food from the counter and pay at the till.

sell.label: CEFR A1
sell.def: To give something to someone in exchange for money.
sell.ex: My friend sells ice cream at the market every Sunday.

seller.label: CEFR B2
seller.def: A person or business that offers goods or services to customers for payment.
seller.ex: The online seller shipped the item within two days of receiving my order.

semester.label: CEFR A2
semester.def: One of two equal periods of time that a school or university year is divided into.
semester.ex: My first semester at university started in September and finished in December.

semicolon.label: CEFR B2
semicolon.def: A punctuation mark (;) used to join two independent clauses or separate items in a complex list.
semicolon.ex: The weather was unpredictable; however, they decided to proceed with the outdoor event anyway.

semi-final.label: CEFR B2
semi-final.def: A competition stage where the remaining competitors play to determine who advances to the final match.
semi-final.ex: The tennis semi-finals proved to be incredibly competitive, with both matches lasting over three hours.

seminar.label: CEFR B2
seminar.def: A small class where a teacher and students discuss a topic in depth together
seminar.ex: Professor Martinez led an intensive seminar on Victorian literature where students presented their own research.

senator.label: CEFR B2
senator.def: A member of a legislative body, typically an upper house of parliament elected or appointed to represent a state or region.
senator.ex: The senator delivered a passionate speech in parliament about healthcare reform.

send.label: CEFR A2
send.def: To make something go to another person or place, usually by mail or messenger.
send.ex: I sent my friend a birthday card yesterday.

senior.label: CEFR A2
senior.def: An older person or someone with more experience and higher rank at work or school.
senior.ex: My father is a senior at his company, so he helps train new employees.

sensation.label: CEFR B1
sensation.def: A physical feeling or experience you get through your body or senses.
sensation.ex: When I touched the ice, I felt a strange cold sensation on my fingers.

sense.label: CEFR B2
sense.def: To perceive or become aware of something, often without clear evidence or conscious thought
sense.ex: She could sense his anxiety the moment he walked into the room, even though he tried to appear calm.

sensible.label: CEFR B2
sensible.def: Showing good judgment; reasonable and practical rather than emotional or extreme.
sensible.ex: Investing in your education is a sensible decision that will benefit your future career.

sensitive.label: CEFR B2
sensitive.def: Easily hurt emotionally; quick to notice or react to small changes or criticism
sensitive.ex: She is sensitive about her appearance, so we avoid making comments about her weight.

sentence.label: CEFR B2
sentence.def: To impose an official punishment, typically imprisonment, on someone convicted of a crime
sentence.ex: The judge sentenced the defendant to five years in prison for the fraud conviction.

separate.label: CEFR B2
separate.def: To divide or move things apart so they are no longer together
separate.ex: The teacher separated the two students who were talking during the exam.

separately.label: CEFR B2
separately.def: In a way that keeps things distinct or apart from each other, not together as one group
separately.ex: The two companies operate separately, though they share the same building.

separation.label: CEFR B1
separation.def: The act of moving apart or the state of being apart from someone or something.
separation.ex: The separation between the two countries lasted for many years before they reunited.

september.label: CEFR A1
september.def: The ninth month of the year, between August and October.

serene.label: CEFR B2
serene.def: Calm, peaceful, and free from disturbance or worry.
serene.ex: The serene landscape of mountains reflected perfectly in the still lake inspired the painter to create her masterpiece.

serial.label: CEFR B2
serial.def: Happening or produced one after another in a series or sequence
serial.ex: The police arrested a serial killer who had committed crimes across multiple cities over several years.

serious.label: CEFR B1
serious.def: Requiring careful thought or attention; not joking or light-hearted.
serious.ex: The doctor had a serious conversation with him about his health.

seriously.label: CEFR A2
seriously.def: In a way that shows you think something is important and not a joke.
seriously.ex: She seriously wants to become a doctor, so she studies hard every day.

servant.label: CEFR B2
servant.def: A person employed to work in a household or for an employer, performing duties and following orders.
servant.ex: The Victorian household employed several servants to maintain the large estate and serve the family's daily needs.

serve.label: CEFR A2
serve.def: To give food or drink to someone at a meal or in a restaurant.
serve.ex: The waiter served us lunch at noon.

server.label: CEFR B1
server.def: A person who brings food and drinks to customers in a restaurant.
server.ex: Our server recommended the fish special and brought our drinks quickly.

service.label: CEFR B1
service.def: To repair or maintain something so it works properly
service.ex: The mechanic will service your car next week to check the engine and brakes.

session.label: CEFR B1
session.def: A period of time spent doing a particular activity or meeting with someone.
session.ex: I attended a training session on how to use the new software at work.

set.label: CEFR A1
set.def: To put something in a particular place or position
set.ex: Please set the glasses on the table.

setback.label: CEFR B2
setback.def: A delay or problem that makes progress slower or stops it temporarily.
setback.ex: The company faced a significant setback when their main supplier went out of business, forcing them to find alternative sources.

setting.label: CEFR B1
setting.def: The time and place where a story, film, or event happens
setting.ex: The novel's setting is a small village in nineteenth-century England.

settle.label: CEFR B1
settle.def: To move to a place and start living there permanently or for a long time.
settle.ex: After travelling for years, Maria decided to settle in a small village near the coast.

settlement.label: CEFR B1
settlement.def: A small community or village built in a new area where few people lived before
settlement.ex: The European settlement in North America changed the lives of indigenous people.

settler.label: CEFR B1
settler.def: A person who moves to a new place and builds a home and community there.
settler.ex: The early settlers arrived in North America and established farms and villages in the wilderness.

severe.label: CEFR B1
severe.def: Very serious or extreme in degree; causing great difficulty or suffering.
severe.ex: The severe weather conditions made it dangerous to drive on the motorway yesterday.

severely.label: CEFR B1
severely.def: In a serious, harsh, or extreme way; very badly.
severely.ex: The storm severely damaged many houses in the coastal town.

sew.label: CEFR A2
sew.def: To join pieces of cloth together using a needle and thread.
sew.ex: My grandmother taught me how to sew buttons onto my coat.

sex.label: CEFR B1
sex.def: The biological characteristic that makes someone male or female.
sex.ex: The form asked for my name, age, and sex.

sexism.label: CEFR B2
sexism.def: Prejudice or discrimination against people based on their sex or gender.
sexism.ex: The report revealed widespread sexism in the technology industry, where women were paid less than men for identical roles.

sexual.label: CEFR B2
sexual.def: Relating to the physical attraction between people or biological reproduction processes.
sexual.ex: The survey examined attitudes towards sexual health and education in secondary schools.

sexy.label: CEFR B2
sexy.def: Sexually attractive or arousing; having qualities that create strong physical desire
sexy.ex: The advertisement featured a sexy actress in a designer dress to appeal to luxury brand consumers.

shabby.label: CEFR B2
shabby.def: Worn, damaged, or in poor condition from age or heavy use; showing lack of care or quality.
shabby.ex: The old hotel had shabby carpets and peeling wallpaper, which disappointed the guests who expected better accommodation.

shade.label: CEFR A2
shade.def: An area that is dark because sunlight is blocked by something.
shade.ex: On a hot day, we sat in the shade of a big tree.

shadow.label: CEFR B1
shadow.def: To follow someone closely and secretly, usually to watch or monitor their movements.
shadow.ex: The detective shadowed the suspect for three days to gather evidence.

shadowy.label: CEFR B1
shadowy.def: Dark and not clearly visible; mysterious or unclear in origin or nature
shadowy.ex: The shadowy figure in the corner turned out to be just a coat on a chair.

shake.label: CEFR B1
shake.def: To move back and forth quickly and with force.
shake.ex: The explosion was so powerful that it shook the entire building.

shaken.label: CEFR B2
shaken.def: Emotionally upset or disturbed by something distressing or surprising
shaken.ex: She felt shaken after witnessing the accident, and it took her several hours to calm down.

shakespearean.label: CEFR B2
shakespearean.def: Relating to William Shakespeare, his plays, poetry, or the style and themes characteristic of his works.
shakespearean.ex: The director's interpretation of the classic love story relied heavily on Shakespearean conventions of tragedy and romance.

shaky.label: CEFR B2
shaky.def: Unstable or uncertain; lacking confidence or reliability.
shaky.ex: The government's shaky economic policy has caused concern among investors and analysts.

shall.label: CEFR A2
shall.def: Used to say what will happen in the future, or to make an offer or suggestion.
shall.ex: I shall go to the supermarket tomorrow to buy some milk.

shallow.label: CEFR B1
shallow.def: Not deep; having only a short distance from top to bottom.
shallow.ex: The children played in the shallow end of the swimming pool where the water reached only their waists.

shame.label: CEFR B1
shame.def: An unpleasant feeling you get when you have done something wrong or embarrassing.
shame.ex: He felt great shame when he realised he had lied to his best friend.

shameful.label: CEFR B1
shameful.def: Causing strong feelings of embarrassment or dishonour; something you feel ashamed about.
shameful.ex: It was shameful that the company ignored the safety problems for so many years.

shampoo.label: CEFR A2
shampoo.def: A liquid cleaning product you use to wash your hair.
shampoo.ex: I buy shampoo at the supermarket every month for washing my hair.

shape.label: CEFR B1
shape.def: To form or influence something to have a particular form or character.
shape.ex: Good teachers shape their students' futures by encouraging them to learn new skills.

share.label: CEFR A1
share.def: To give part of something to another person; to use something together with others
share.ex: I share my lunch with my friend at school.

sharp.label: CEFR B2
sharp.def: In a sudden or precisely timed manner; exactly at a specified time
sharp.ex: The meeting starts at 9 o'clock sharp, so please arrive a few minutes early.

sharply.label: CEFR B2
sharply.def: In a sudden, quick, or severe manner; with a noticeable change in direction or intensity.
sharply.ex: The company's profits fell sharply after the scandal was revealed in the media.

shatter.label: CEFR B2
shatter.def: To break into many small, sharp pieces suddenly and violently.
shatter.ex: The vase shattered into fragments when it fell from the shelf onto the marble floor.

shave.label: CEFR B1
shave.def: To cut hair very close to the skin using a razor or electric device.
shave.ex: He shaves every morning before going to work.

shed.label: CEFR B2
shed.def: A small, simple building used for storing tools, equipment, or other items.
shed.ex: He kept his gardening tools and lawnmower in the old wooden shed at the back of the garden.

sheep.label: CEFR A1
sheep.def: A farm animal with thick woolly coat, kept for wool and meat.
sheep.ex: The farmer has ten sheep in the field.

sheer.label: CEFR B1
sheer.def: Extremely thin and almost transparent; or complete and total with nothing mixed in.
sheer.ex: The sheer curtains in the bedroom allowed sunlight to filter through gently.

sheet.label: CEFR B1
sheet.def: A large piece of thin fabric used to cover a bed or wrap around something.
sheet.ex: She changed the bed sheets every Sunday morning.

shelf.label: CEFR A1
shelf.def: A flat piece of wood or metal attached to a wall to hold things
shelf.ex: I put my books on the shelf above my desk.

shell.label: CEFR B2
shell.def: To remove the hard outer covering from nuts, seeds, or seafood.
shell.ex: She carefully shelled the prawns before adding them to the pasta dish.

shelter.label: CEFR B1
shelter.def: A place that protects you from bad weather or danger
shelter.ex: During the heavy rain, we waited for the bus under a shelter in the town centre.

sheriff.label: CEFR B1
sheriff.def: A law enforcement officer responsible for keeping order and justice in a county or region.
sheriff.ex: The sheriff arrested the suspect after receiving reports of the robbery at the bank.

shield.label: CEFR B2
shield.def: A piece of protective equipment held to defend against weapons or harm during combat or conflict.
shield.ex: The riot police advanced with shields raised to protect themselves from the angry crowd.

shine.label: CEFR A2
shine.def: To produce bright light or reflect light brightly.
shine.ex: The sun shines brightly through the window every morning.

shiny.label: CEFR B1
shiny.def: Bright and reflecting light; having a smooth, polished surface
shiny.ex: She wore a shiny new watch that sparkled in the sunlight.

ship.label: CEFR B1
ship.def: To send goods to someone by truck, plane, boat, or other transport method.
ship.ex: The company will ship your order to your address within five business days.

shipping.label: CEFR B2
shipping.def: The business of transporting goods by sea, or the process of sending goods to customers.
shipping.ex: The company reduced shipping costs by consolidating orders and using more efficient logistics partners.

shipwreck.label: CEFR B2
shipwreck.def: The destruction or sinking of a ship, usually caused by storms, rocks, or collision at sea.
shipwreck.ex: The recently discovered shipwreck off the coast of Greece is believed to be from the 16th century.

shirt.label: CEFR A1
shirt.def: A piece of clothing worn on the top part of your body, with sleeves and buttons.
shirt.ex: I wear a blue shirt to work every day.

shiver.label: CEFR B1
shiver.def: To shake slightly because you are cold or frightened.
shiver.ex: I shivered when I stepped outside into the freezing winter air.

shock.label: CEFR B2
shock.def: To surprise and disturb someone deeply, usually causing them to feel upset or distressed.
shock.ex: The news of the accident shocked the entire community, leaving people struggling to process what had happened.

shocked.label: CEFR B1
shocked.def: Very surprised and upset by something unexpected or bad
shocked.ex: She was shocked to discover that her best friend had moved away without telling her.

shocking.label: CEFR B1
shocking.def: Very surprising and upsetting or offensive in a bad way.
shocking.ex: The shocking news that the factory would close left hundreds of workers without jobs.

shoe.label: CEFR A1
shoe.def: Something you wear on your foot to protect it and keep it warm.
shoe.ex: I put on my shoes before I go outside to play.

shoot.label: CEFR A2
shoot.def: To fire a gun or weapon; to take a photograph or film
shoot.ex: The hunter shot at the target with his rifle.

shooting.label: CEFR B2
shooting.def: The act of firing a gun or other weapon, or an incident where this occurs.
shooting.ex: The shooting at the concert left the community devastated and searching for answers.

shop.label: CEFR B1
shop.def: To go to stores and buy things you need or want
shop.ex: I usually shop for groceries on Saturday mornings at the supermarket.

shop assistant.label: CEFR A2
shop assistant.def: A person who works in a shop helping customers find and buy things.
shop assistant.ex: The shop assistant helped me find the right size of shoes.

shopkeeper.label: CEFR B2
shopkeeper.def: A person who owns or manages a small retail store selling goods directly to customers.
shopkeeper.ex: The shopkeeper greeted regular customers by name and knew exactly what products they preferred.

shoplift.label: CEFR B2
shoplift.def: To steal goods from a shop by hiding them and leaving without paying.
shoplift.ex: Security cameras caught a man shoplifting expensive electronics from the department store.

shoplifter.label: CEFR B2
shoplifter.def: A person who steals goods from a shop by hiding them without paying.
shoplifter.ex: The shoplifter was caught on CCTV concealing items in her bag before leaving the store without visiting the checkout.

shoplifting.label: CEFR B2
shoplifting.def: Stealing goods from a shop by hiding them and leaving without paying.
shoplifting.ex: The retailer installed new security cameras after shoplifting incidents increased by 15% during the summer months.

shopper.label: CEFR B1
shopper.def: A person who buys goods from shops or stores.
shopper.ex: Early morning shoppers filled the supermarket before it got crowded.

shopping.label: CEFR A1
shopping.def: The activity of buying things you need or want from shops.
shopping.ex: I go shopping every Saturday to buy food and clothes.

shore.label: CEFR A2
shore.def: The land beside the ocean or a large lake where water meets the land.
shore.ex: We walked along the shore and collected beautiful shells.

short.label: CEFR A1
short.def: For only a little time; not long
short.ex: I waited short time for the bus this morning.

short/shorts.label: CEFR A2
short/shorts.def: Trousers that end above the knee, worn in warm weather.
short/shorts.ex: I wore my shorts and a t-shirt to the beach on Saturday.

shortage.label: CEFR B1
shortage.def: A situation where there is not enough of something that people need or want.
shortage.ex: The country is facing a serious shortage of affordable housing in major cities.

shortly.label: CEFR B1
shortly.def: In a few moments; very soon or in the near future.
shortly.ex: The train will arrive shortly, so please get your bags ready.

shortness.label: CEFR B2
shortness.def: The quality of being small in length, duration, or supply; inadequacy or insufficiency.
shortness.ex: The shortness of their visit disappointed the family, as they had hoped for more time together.

short-term.label: CEFR B2
short-term.def: lasting for a limited period, not extending far into the future
short-term.ex: The company's short-term profits increased, but analysts worry about long-term sustainability.

shot.label: CEFR A2
shot.def: A quick attempt to do or achieve something.
shot.ex: Let me have a shot at solving this puzzle.

should.label: CEFR A1
should.def: used to say what is right, correct, or expected
should.ex: You should brush your teeth before bed.

shoulder.label: CEFR A1
shoulder.def: The part of your body between your neck and arm on each side.
shoulder.ex: She put her hand on his shoulder to get his attention.

shout.label: CEFR B1
shout.def: To speak or cry out very loudly, often with strong emotion or to be heard from far away
shout.ex: The football fans shouted with excitement when their team scored a goal.

show.label: CEFR A1
show.def: To let someone see something; to make something visible or clear.
show.ex: Can you show me your new toy?

showbiz.label: CEFR B2
showbiz.def: The entertainment industry, particularly film, television, and celebrity culture.
showbiz.ex: She's been in showbiz for twenty years and knows all the producers in Hollywood.

showcase.label: CEFR B2
showcase.def: A glass cabinet or display area in a shop where valuable or attractive items are presented to customers
showcase.ex: The jeweller arranged her finest diamond rings in the window showcase to attract potential buyers.

shower.label: CEFR B2
shower.def: To wash your body with water falling from above, typically in a bathroom enclosure.
shower.ex: She showers every morning before going to work.

shrimp.label: CEFR B1
shrimp.def: A small sea creature with a long tail, often pink, used as food.
shrimp.ex: The restaurant serves grilled shrimp with garlic and lemon sauce.

shrink.label: CEFR B2
shrink.def: To become smaller in size, amount, or volume, often due to heat, moisture, or pressure.
shrink.ex: The wool jumper shrank in the washing machine because I used hot water.

shrug.label: CEFR B2
shrug.def: To raise your shoulders briefly to show doubt, indifference, or lack of knowledge about something.
shrug.ex: When asked about his plans for the weekend, he simply shrugged and said he hadn't decided yet.

shut.label: CEFR A2
shut.def: To close something firmly, usually a door, window, or container.
shut.ex: Please shut the door when you leave the classroom.

shy.label: CEFR A1
shy.def: Not comfortable or confident talking to people you don't know.
shy.ex: She is shy, so she doesn't like to speak in class.

sick.label: CEFR A1
sick.def: Ill or unwell; having a disease or health problem.
sick.ex: I felt sick yesterday, so I stayed home from school.

sickness.label: CEFR B1
sickness.def: A feeling of nausea or the state of being ill, especially when travelling or at sea.
sickness.ex: She took medication for her sickness before boarding the ship to reduce the nausea.

side.label: CEFR A1
side.def: One of the flat surfaces of something, or the left or right part of your body.
side.ex: I have a pain on my left side.

sidewalk.label: CEFR B1
sidewalk.def: A paved path for pedestrians at the side of a street or road.
sidewalk.ex: She walked along the sidewalk to reach the café on the other side of town.

sigh.label: CEFR B2
sigh.def: To breathe out audibly, usually expressing sadness, relief, tiredness, or disappointment.
sigh.ex: After hearing the exam results, she sighed heavily and realised she would need to retake the test.

sight.label: CEFR A1
sight.def: The ability to see with your eyes.
sight.ex: She has very good sight and doesn't need glasses.

sightseeing.label: CEFR A2
sightseeing.def: Visiting interesting places and attractions as a tourist or visitor.
sightseeing.ex: We spent the afternoon sightseeing in the old city centre and visited three museums.

sign.label: CEFR B1
sign.def: To write your name on a document to show you agree with it or that it is official
sign.ex: You need to sign the contract before you can start your new job.

signal.label: CEFR B1
signal.def: A sign or message that gives information or tells someone to do something.
signal.ex: The traffic signal turned red, so all the cars stopped at the intersection.

signature.label: CEFR B1
signature.def: Your name written in your own special way, used to show you agree with something.
signature.ex: I need your signature on this form before we can process your application.

significance.label: CEFR B1
significance.def: The importance or meaning of something, especially how much it affects or matters to people.
significance.ex: The significance of learning English is that it opens doors to jobs and friendships around the world.

significantly.label: CEFR B2
significantly.def: In a way that is important or has a noticeable effect on something.
significantly.ex: The new marketing campaign significantly increased sales compared to the previous quarter.

signify.label: CEFR B2
signify.def: To be a sign or symbol of something; to have a particular meaning or importance.
signify.ex: The red traffic light signifies that drivers must stop immediately.

signpost.label: CEFR B1
signpost.def: A post with signs showing directions or information to help people find their way.
signpost.ex: We saw a signpost at the crossroads pointing towards the village centre.

silence.label: CEFR A2
silence.def: Complete absence of sound or noise.
silence.ex: The silence in the library made it easy to concentrate on studying.

silent.label: CEFR B1
silent.def: Making no sound or noise; very quiet
silent.ex: The library was completely silent except for the sound of pages turning.

silently.label: CEFR A2
silently.def: Without making any sound or noise.
silently.ex: She silently opened the door so she wouldn't wake the baby.

silk.label: CEFR B1
silk.def: Smooth, soft fabric made from threads produced by silkworms.
silk.ex: She wore a beautiful silk dress to the wedding that felt luxurious against her skin.

silly.label: CEFR A2
silly.def: Not serious or sensible; stupid or foolish.
silly.ex: He was being silly, making funny faces to make us laugh.

silver.label: CEFR A2
silver.def: A shiny, precious metal that is white and valuable, used for jewellery and coins.
silver.ex: My grandmother has a silver necklace that she wears every Sunday.

silverware.label: CEFR B2
silverware.def: Cutlery and table utensils made from silver or silver-coloured metal, used for eating meals.
silverware.ex: The formal dinner table was set with polished silverware and fine china plates.

similarity.label: CEFR B1
similarity.def: The quality of being alike or having features in common with something else.
similarity.ex: There is a strong similarity between the two paintings in terms of colour and style.

similarly.label: CEFR B1
similarly.def: In almost the same way or manner as something else.
similarly.ex: The weather in Spain is warm and sunny; similarly, Portugal has a pleasant climate in summer.

simple.label: CEFR A2
simple.def: Easy to understand or do; not complicated or difficult
simple.ex: The recipe is simple, so even children can make the cake.

simplification.label: CEFR B2
simplification.def: The process of making something less complicated or easier to understand.
simplification.ex: The simplification of the new tax system has made it much easier for small businesses to comply with regulations.

simplify.label: CEFR B1
simplify.def: To make something easier to understand or do by removing unnecessary parts.
simplify.ex: The teacher simplified the complex grammar rule by explaining it with everyday examples.

simply.label: CEFR A2
simply.def: In a way that is easy to understand; without anything added or complicated
simply.ex: The instructions are simply: mix the flour and eggs together.

simultaneous.label: CEFR B2
simultaneous.def: Happening or existing at exactly the same time as something else
simultaneous.ex: The two events were simultaneous, occurring on the same day across different cities.

simultaneously.label: CEFR B1
simultaneously.def: At the same time; happening together without a time difference.
simultaneously.ex: The two runners crossed the finish line simultaneously, so it was impossible to determine a winner.

since.label: CEFR B2
since.def: From a particular time in the past until now; used to show how long something has been true
since.ex: She has been working at the company since 2015 and has received two promotions.

sincere.label: CEFR B2
sincere.def: Genuinely felt or expressed without pretence or dishonesty.
sincere.ex: She offered sincere condolences to the family after their loss, speaking from the heart.

sincerity.label: CEFR B2
sincerity.def: The quality of being genuine and honest in what you say or do, without pretence.
sincerity.ex: The sincerity in her voice made it clear that she truly regretted her mistake.

sing.label: CEFR A1
sing.def: To make music with your voice.
sing.ex: I sing in the shower every morning.

singer.label: CEFR A1
singer.def: A person who produces musical sounds with their voice.
singer.ex: My favourite singer performs at the concert every Saturday.

singing.label: CEFR A2
singing.def: Making musical sounds with your voice.
singing.ex: She enjoys singing in the shower every morning.

single.label: CEFR B1
single.def: An unmarried person, especially one who is not in a romantic relationship.
single.ex: After her divorce, she was happy to be single and focus on her career.

singular.label: CEFR A2
singular.def: A single person or thing, not more than one.
singular.ex: In English, the singular of 'children' is 'child'.

sink.label: CEFR B1
sink.def: To go down slowly through water or another liquid until disappearing beneath the surface.
sink.ex: The ship began to sink after hitting the iceberg in the North Atlantic.

sir.label: CEFR A1
sir.def: A polite word used to speak to a man you don't know well
sir.ex: Excuse me, sir, can you help me find the train station?

sister.label: CEFR A1
sister.def: A female child who has the same parents as you.
sister.ex: My sister and I like to play together after school.

sister-in-law.label: CEFR B2
sister-in-law.def: The sister of your spouse, or the wife of your spouse's brother.
sister-in-law.ex: My sister-in-law invited us to dinner at her house last weekend.

sit.label: CEFR A1
sit.def: To be in a position where your bottom is supported by a chair or seat
sit.ex: I sit down at the table to eat lunch.

sitting room.label: CEFR A2
sitting room.def: A comfortable room in a house where people relax and spend time together.
sitting room.ex: We watched the film in the sitting room after dinner.

situation.label: CEFR A2
situation.def: A set of circumstances or condition at a particular time
situation.ex: The situation at the airport was chaotic because of the bad weather.

size.label: CEFR A1
size.def: How big or small something is, especially clothing that fits your body.
size.ex: I usually wear a size medium shirt.

skate.label: CEFR B1
skate.def: To move smoothly over ice or a hard surface wearing special boots with blades or wheels.
skate.ex: My sister loves to skate at the ice rink every weekend during winter.

skateboard.label: CEFR B2
skateboard.def: A board with wheels used for riding as a sport or form of transport, typically performed on concrete surfaces.
skateboard.ex: She's been practising skateboard tricks at the skate park every weekend since summer.

skateboarder.label: CEFR B2
skateboarder.def: A person who rides on a skateboard as a sport, hobby, or form of transportation.
skateboarder.ex: The professional skateboarder performed an impressive trick at the competition, landing a perfect kickflip.

skateboarding.label: CEFR A2
skateboarding.def: The sport of riding on a board with wheels and performing tricks.
skateboarding.ex: My brother loves skateboarding in the park after school.

skating.label: CEFR A2
skating.def: The activity of moving smoothly on ice or hard ground using special shoes with wheels or blades.
skating.ex: My sister loves ice skating at the rink every Saturday morning.

skeleton.label: CEFR B1
skeleton.def: All the bones in a body that form its structure and support
skeleton.ex: The doctor showed the patient an X-ray of her skeleton to explain the fracture.

sketch.label: CEFR B2
sketch.def: To draw something quickly and roughly without fine details.
sketch.ex: The architect sketched out her initial ideas for the building design before creating detailed plans.

ski.label: CEFR B1
ski.def: To glide down snowy mountains on long runners attached to your feet.
ski.ex: Every winter, my family goes to the Alps to ski for a week.

skiing.label: CEFR A2
skiing.def: A winter sport where you slide down snowy mountains wearing long boards on your feet.
skiing.ex: My family loves skiing in the Alps every winter holiday.

skill.label: CEFR A1
skill.def: The ability to do something well because you have practised it.
skill.ex: She has good cooking skills and makes delicious meals.

skilled.label: CEFR B2
skilled.def: Having or showing developed ability and knowledge gained through training and experience.
skilled.ex: The skilled surgeon completed the delicate operation with remarkable precision.

skillful/skilful.label: CEFR B2
skillful/skilful.def: Able to do something well through training and experience.
skillful/skilful.ex: The skilful surgeon completed the delicate operation with remarkable precision.

skillfully/skilfully.label: CEFR B2
skillfully/skilfully.def: In a way that shows high ability, training, and careful attention to detail.
skillfully/skilfully.ex: The surgeon skilfully removed the tumour without damaging surrounding tissue.

skim.label: CEFR B2
skim.def: To read quickly, looking for main ideas or specific information rather than reading every word.
skim.ex: She skimmed through the report to find the key findings before the meeting.

skin.label: CEFR B1
skin.def: The outer layer of your body that covers and protects your flesh.
skin.ex: She applied sunscreen to her skin before going to the beach.

skip.label: CEFR B2
skip.def: To omit or deliberately not do something you would normally do or be expected to do.
skip.ex: She decided to skip the meeting because she had too much work to finish on her project.

skirt.label: CEFR A1
skirt.def: A piece of clothing worn around the waist and legs, open at the bottom.
skirt.ex: She wore a blue skirt to school today.

sky.label: CEFR A1
sky.def: The space above the earth where clouds and the sun appear.
sky.ex: The sky is blue and the sun is shining today.

skylark.label: CEFR B2
skylark.def: A small brown songbird known for its beautiful song, often heard singing high in the sky.
skylark.ex: The skylark's melodious trilling filled the spring air as it climbed higher and higher above the moorland.

skyscraper.label: CEFR B1
skyscraper.def: A very tall building with many floors, usually in a city centre.
skyscraper.ex: The new skyscraper in the financial district has office space for thousands of workers.

skyward.label: CEFR B2
skyward.def: Directed or moving towards the sky or upwards into the air.
skyward.ex: The rocket shot skyward in a brilliant burst of flame, leaving a trail of smoke across the clear blue sky.

slam.label: CEFR B2
slam.def: To shut a door or window forcefully and noisily, or to push something down hard
slam.ex: She slammed the car door shut in frustration after the argument.

slang.label: CEFR B2
slang.def: Very informal language used by particular groups, often considered impolite in formal contexts.
slang.ex: The teacher explained that using slang like 'gonna' is inappropriate in academic essays.

slap.label: CEFR B2
slap.def: To hit someone or something quickly and forcefully with your open hand or palm
slap.ex: She slapped the mosquito on her arm before it could bite her.

slash.label: CEFR B2
slash.def: A strong cut made with a sharp weapon or tool; also a symbol (/) used in writing
slash.ex: The actor received a deep slash across his arm during the fight scene.

slaughter.label: CEFR B2
slaughter.def: The killing of large numbers of people or animals, often in violent or brutal circumstances.
slaughter.ex: The humanitarian organisation condemned the slaughter of innocent civilians during the conflict.

slave.label: CEFR A2
slave.def: A person who is owned by another and forced to work without payment or freedom.
slave.ex: Millions of people were taken from Africa and forced to work as slaves on plantations in America.

slavery.label: CEFR B1
slavery.def: The system where people are forced to work without pay and have no freedom or rights.
slavery.ex: Many historians study slavery in America during the 17th and 18th centuries to understand its lasting impact on society.

sleep.label: CEFR A1
sleep.def: To rest your body and mind with your eyes closed at night
sleep.ex: I sleep for eight hours every night.

sleepless.label: CEFR A2
sleepless.def: Unable to sleep or having no sleep during the night.
sleepless.ex: After too much coffee, she had a sleepless night and felt tired at work.

sleepy.label: CEFR A2
sleepy.def: Tired and ready to sleep; feeling the need to rest.
sleepy.ex: After playing football all afternoon, the children felt sleepy and went to bed early.

sleeve.label: CEFR B1
sleeve.def: The part of a piece of clothing that covers your arm.
sleeve.ex: She rolled up the sleeves of her shirt because it was too warm in the office.

slice.label: CEFR B2
slice.def: To cut something into thin, flat pieces using a sharp knife or blade.
slice.ex: She carefully sliced the bread into even portions for the sandwiches.

slide.label: CEFR B2
slide.def: To move smoothly along a surface with little friction, often without intending to stop.
slide.ex: The car slid dangerously on the icy road before the driver managed to regain control.

slight.label: CEFR B1
slight.def: A small amount or degree of something; a minor offense or disrespect shown to someone.
slight.ex: There was a slight of her work when her boss didn't mention her contribution in the meeting.

slightly.label: CEFR B1
slightly.def: A little bit; not very much or not very seriously.
slightly.ex: The temperature was slightly warmer today than yesterday, so I didn't need a heavy coat.

slim.label: CEFR A2
slim.ex: She has a slim figure and looks very healthy.

slip.label: CEFR B1
slip.def: To slide accidentally or lose your balance on a smooth or wet surface.
slip.ex: I slipped on the ice this morning and fell down the stairs.

sliver.label: CEFR B2
sliver.def: A very thin, narrow piece cut or broken off from something larger.
sliver.ex: A sliver of glass remained embedded in her fingertip after the accident.

slogan.label: CEFR B1
slogan.def: A short, memorable phrase or sentence used to promote an idea, product, or political message.
slogan.ex: The environmental campaign's slogan was 'Protect our planet for future generations.'

slope.label: CEFR B1
slope.def: A surface that is higher at one end than the other; a gentle hill or angle.
slope.ex: The shop ramp has a gentle slope so customers with pushchairs can easily enter the building.

sloppy.label: CEFR B2
sloppy.def: Careless and lacking attention to detail or precision in work or appearance.
sloppy.ex: The sloppy editing in the report resulted in numerous grammatical errors that embarrassed the company.

slot.label: CEFR B1
slot.def: A narrow opening or space where something fits or slides in
slot.ex: I put a coin in the slot of the vending machine to buy a drink.

slow.label: CEFR B1
slow.def: To move or happen at a reduced speed or pace
slow.ex: The traffic slowed down during rush hour, so my commute took much longer than usual.

slowly.label: CEFR A2
slowly.def: At a low speed; not quickly; taking a long time to move or happen.
slowly.ex: She walked slowly through the park, enjoying the flowers.

small.label: CEFR A1
small.def: Not large in size or amount
small.ex: I live in a small flat in the city centre.

smart.label: CEFR A1
smart.def: Intelligent and quick at learning; also means clean and neat in appearance
smart.ex: She is a smart student who always gets good grades.

smash.label: CEFR B2
smash.def: To break something forcefully into pieces, or to hit something with great force and violence.
smash.ex: The car smashed into the wall at high speed, causing significant damage to both vehicles.

smell.label: CEFR B1
smell.def: To perceive odours through your nose.
smell.ex: The kitchen smells wonderful when my mother is cooking traditional dishes.

smile.label: CEFR B1
smile.def: To turn up the corners of your mouth to show happiness or friendliness.
smile.ex: She smiled warmly when she saw her old friend at the airport.

smith.label: CEFR A1
smith.def: A person who makes or repairs things from metal using heat and tools.
smith.ex: The smith in the village repairs tools and makes horseshoes.

smoke.label: CEFR A2
smoke.def: To breathe in and out the burning gas from a cigarette, pipe, or cigar
smoke.ex: My father used to smoke twenty cigarettes every day, but he quit last year.

smoker.label: CEFR B1
smoker.def: A person who regularly inhales and exhales smoke from cigarettes, pipes, or cigars.
smoker.ex: My uncle is a heavy smoker, so he spends a lot of money on cigarettes every month.

smokestack.label: CEFR B2
smokestack.def: A tall pipe on a building or factory that releases smoke and gases into the air.
smokestack.ex: The old factory's smokestack billowed dark smoke across the industrial district every morning.

smoking.label: CEFR A1
smoking.def: Breathing in and out smoke from a cigarette or similar object
smoking.ex: My father enjoys smoking a pipe after dinner.

smooth.label: CEFR A2
smooth.def: Having a surface without bumps, rough spots, or unevenness.
smooth.ex: The baby's skin is very smooth and soft.

smoothly.label: CEFR A2
smoothly.def: Without problems or interruptions; in a calm and even way.
smoothly.ex: The new student settled into class smoothly and made friends quickly.

snack.label: CEFR A2
snack.def: A small amount of food eaten between main meals.
snack.ex: I had a snack of apple and cheese before lunch.

snake.label: CEFR A1
snake.def: A long animal with no legs that moves along the ground.
snake.ex: I saw a snake in the garden yesterday.

snap.label: CEFR B2
snap.def: To break suddenly with a sharp cracking sound, or to move with a quick sudden motion.
snap.ex: The dry branch snapped under the weight of the snow.

sneak.label: CEFR B2
sneak.def: To move quietly or secretly to avoid being noticed or discovered.
sneak.ex: She tried to sneak out of the meeting early without anyone seeing her leave.

sneer.label: CEFR B2
sneer.def: A contemptuous facial expression showing disrespect or scorn towards someone or something
sneer.ex: The critic's sneer at the avant-garde exhibition revealed his dismissal of modern art entirely.

sneeze.label: CEFR B2
sneeze.def: To suddenly blow air from your nose and mouth, usually when dust or a cold irritates your nose
sneeze.ex: During the presentation, he tried to stifle a sneeze but couldn't help disrupting the meeting.

sniff.label: CEFR B2
sniff.def: To breathe air in through the nose, often to detect or examine a smell.
sniff.ex: The dog sniffed around the garden, searching for interesting scents.

snore.label: CEFR B2
snore.def: To breathe noisily through the mouth and nose while sleeping.
snore.ex: His loud snoring kept everyone awake throughout the night.

snow.label: CEFR A2
snow.def: To fall from the sky in the form of soft white frozen water.
snow.ex: It snowed heavily last night, and now the whole town is white.

snowball.label: CEFR B2
snowball.def: A sphere of packed snow, often thrown in play or used to build snowmen during winter.
snowball.ex: The children had a snowball fight in the garden after the heavy snowfall.

snowboard.label: CEFR B1
snowboard.def: A board you stand on to slide down snow-covered mountains for sport or fun.
snowboard.ex: She bought a new snowboard and went to the mountains every weekend during winter.

snowboarding.label: CEFR A2
snowboarding.def: A winter sport where you stand on a board and slide down snowy mountains.
snowboarding.ex: My brother goes snowboarding every winter at the ski resort.

snowstorm.label: CEFR B1
snowstorm.def: A heavy fall of snow, often with strong wind and poor visibility.
snowstorm.ex: The weather forecast warned of a severe snowstorm approaching the city tomorrow.

snowy.label: CEFR A1
snowy.def: Covered with snow or having a lot of snow falling from the sky.
snowy.ex: We walked through the snowy forest and made footprints in the white ground.

so.label: CEFR A2
so.def: To a great degree; very much; or for this reason; therefore
so.ex: The weather was so hot that we stayed inside all day.

soak.label: CEFR B2
soak.def: To leave something in liquid for a period of time so it absorbs moisture.
soak.ex: The chef soaked the dried beans overnight before cooking them for the stew.

soaked.label: CEFR B2
soaked.def: Completely wet, having absorbed or been covered with large amounts of liquid.
