soaked.ex: After the heavy rain, his soaked clothes clung to his body as he rushed inside.

soaking.label: CEFR B2
soaking.def: Very wet; saturated with liquid or moisture throughout.
soaking.ex: After the heavy rain, the soaking grass made it impossible to play football in the garden.

soap.label: CEFR A2
soap.def: A cleaning product made from fat and chemicals, used with water to wash hands and body.
soap.ex: I wash my hands with soap and water before eating dinner.

soap opera.label: CEFR B1
soap opera.def: A television drama series with continuing stories about characters' relationships and personal problems.
soap opera.ex: My mother watches her favourite soap opera every weekday afternoon without missing an episode.

soar.label: CEFR B2
soar.def: To rise high and fast into the air or to increase suddenly and dramatically
soar.ex: Property prices soared by 25% after the new railway line opened in the area.

sob.label: CEFR B2
sob.def: To cry heavily with short, gasping breaths, usually from strong emotion.
sob.ex: She sobbed uncontrollably when she heard the news about her father's death.

so-called.label: CEFR B2
so-called.def: Used to indicate that a name or description is questionable or not entirely accurate.
so-called.ex: The so-called experts failed to predict the economic crisis that followed.

soccer.label: CEFR A1
soccer.def: A sport where two teams kick a ball to score goals.
soccer.ex: My brother plays soccer every Saturday morning in the park.

sociable.label: CEFR B2
sociable.def: Enjoying spending time with others and finding it easy to talk and interact with people.
sociable.ex: She has a naturally sociable personality and quickly makes friends at new jobs.

social.label: CEFR A1
social.def: Related to spending time with other people and making friends.
social.ex: My sister is very social; she likes to talk to many people at school.

social networking.label: CEFR B1
social networking.def: Using websites or apps to share information and stay connected with friends and family online.
social networking.ex: My sister spends hours on social networking sites like Instagram, posting photos of her daily life.

socialize/socialise.label: CEFR B2
socialize/socialise.def: To interact with others for pleasure or to help someone develop social skills and fit into society.
socialize/socialise.ex: After moving to a new city, she joined a book club to socialise and make new friends.

socially.label: CEFR B1
socially.def: In ways that involve being with other people or relating to society.
socially.ex: She is not very socially confident, so she prefers small gatherings to large parties.

society.label: CEFR A2
society.def: A group of people living together in an organised community with shared laws and customs
society.ex: Many different cultures live together in British society.

sock.label: CEFR A2
sock.def: A piece of clothing worn inside a shoe to cover your foot.
sock.ex: I put on a clean sock before putting on my shoe this morning.

soda.label: CEFR A2
soda.def: A sweet fizzy drink, often containing sugar and flavouring
soda.ex: My sister drinks a can of soda every afternoon.

sofa.label: CEFR A1
sofa.def: A long, comfortable seat with a back and arms for two or more people.
sofa.ex: We sat on the sofa and watched television together.

soft.label: CEFR A2
soft.def: Not hard or firm; smooth and pleasant to touch.
soft.ex: The baby's skin is very soft and delicate.

soft drink.label: CEFR A2
soft drink.def: A sweet drink with bubbles or flavour, usually without alcohol.
soft drink.ex: She ordered a soft drink instead of coffee at the café.

softly.label: CEFR A2
softly.def: In a quiet way, making little noise or sound
softly.ex: She spoke softly so the baby would not wake up.

softness.label: CEFR B2
softness.def: The quality of being smooth, gentle, or not hard to the touch.
softness.ex: The softness of the cashmere fabric made it perfect for sensitive skin.

software.label: CEFR A2
software.def: Computer programs and applications that you use to do tasks on a device.
software.ex: I use software on my computer to write letters and edit photos.

soil.label: CEFR B1
soil.def: To make something dirty or stained, especially with mud or a dark substance.
soil.ex: He soiled his new white shirt with chocolate while eating lunch.

solar.label: CEFR B2
solar.def: Relating to or powered by energy from the sun
solar.ex: The government has invested billions in solar technology to reduce carbon emissions.

soldier.label: CEFR A2
soldier.def: A person who fights in an army for their country.
soldier.ex: The soldiers marched in the parade to celebrate their return from overseas.

solid.label: CEFR B1
solid.def: Hard and firm, not liquid or gas; strong and well-made or reliable
solid.ex: The table is made of solid wood and will last for many years.

solidity.label: CEFR B2
solidity.def: The quality of being firm, stable, and unlikely to change or fail.
solidity.ex: The solidity of the company's financial position gave investors confidence despite the economic downturn.

soliloquy.label: CEFR B2
soliloquy.def: A speech in which a character speaks their thoughts aloud, alone or unaware others are listening.
soliloquy.ex: Hamlet's famous soliloquy, 'To be or not to be,' reveals his deep internal conflict about life and death.

solo.label: CEFR B2
solo.def: A performance or activity done by one person without others, or without accompaniment.
solo.ex: The guitarist played a beautiful solo during the concert that lasted nearly five minutes.

solution.label: CEFR A2
solution.def: An answer to a problem or a way to fix something
solution.ex: The best solution to the traffic problem is to build more roads.

solve.label: CEFR A1
solve.def: To find the answer or solution to a problem or question.
solve.ex: Can you help me solve this maths problem?

solvent.label: CEFR B2
solvent.def: A liquid that dissolves other substances, commonly used in cleaning or chemical processes.
solvent.ex: The technician used acetone as a solvent to remove the paint from the metal surface.

someday.label: CEFR A2
someday.def: At an unspecified time in the future.
someday.ex: Someday I want to travel around the world and see different countries.

somehow.label: CEFR B1
somehow.def: In a way that is not clear or specified; by some method you do not know or cannot explain
somehow.ex: I don't know how, but somehow we managed to finish the project on time despite all the problems.

someplace.label: CEFR B1
someplace.def: At or to some location that you are not naming or do not know exactly
someplace.ex: I left my keys someplace in the house, but I can't remember where.

sometime.label: CEFR B1
sometime.def: At an unspecified time in the past or future; at some point
sometime.ex: I'll visit Paris sometime next year, perhaps in the spring.

sometimes.label: CEFR B1
sometimes.def: At some times but not always; occasionally or at intervals
sometimes.ex: I sometimes go to the gym after work, but not every day.

somewhat.label: CEFR B2
somewhat.def: To a moderate degree; more than a little but less than very much.
somewhat.ex: The new policy has been somewhat controversial among the staff, though most people support it in principle.

somewhere.label: CEFR A2
somewhere.def: In or to a place that you do not know exactly or cannot remember
somewhere.ex: I left my keys somewhere in the house, but I can't find them now.

son.label: CEFR A1
son.def: A male child; a boy who has a mother and father.
son.ex: My son is five years old and he likes to play football.

song.label: CEFR A1
song.def: A piece of music with words that you sing.
song.ex: She likes to sing her favourite song in the shower.

son-in-law.label: CEFR B2
son-in-law.def: The husband of your daughter, or the husband of your spouse's daughter.
son-in-law.ex: My son-in-law recently took over the family business after marrying my youngest daughter.

sonnet.label: CEFR B2
sonnet.def: A 14-line poem with a specific rhyme scheme and rhythm pattern, traditionally used to express love or philosophical ideas.
sonnet.ex: Shakespeare wrote 154 sonnets exploring themes of beauty, time, and mortality throughout his literary career.

soon.label: CEFR A1
soon.def: In the near future; not far away in time.

soothe.label: CEFR B1
soothe.def: To make someone feel calmer or less worried or in pain.
soothe.ex: The nurse spoke in a gentle voice to soothe the anxious patient before the procedure.

sophisticated.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
sophisticated.def: Complex, refined, and showing advanced knowledge or understanding of something
sophisticated.ex: The company's sophisticated marketing strategy relied on detailed analysis of consumer behaviour across multiple platforms.

sophomore.label: CEFR B1
sophomore.def: A student in the second year of a four-year university or high school course.
sophomore.ex: As a sophomore, Maria had to choose her major subjects for the remaining two years of her degree.

sore.label: CEFR B1
sore.def: Painful or tender to touch; causing discomfort or ache
sore.ex: After the long hike, her legs were sore and she could barely walk the next day.

sorrow.label: CEFR B1
sorrow.def: Deep sadness or grief, usually caused by loss or disappointment.
sorrow.ex: She felt great sorrow when she heard about her grandmother's death.

sorry.label: CEFR A1
sorry.def: Feeling sadness or regret about something you did wrong.
sorry.ex: I'm sorry I broke your toy. I didn't mean to do it.

sort.label: CEFR B2
sort.def: To arrange or organize things into groups or categories based on specific characteristics.
sort.ex: The post office workers sort the mail by postcode before delivering it to different neighbourhoods.

soul.label: CEFR B1
soul.def: A person's spirit or the part of a person that is thought to contain their emotions and character.
soul.ex: She poured her soul into the painting, expressing all her deepest feelings through art.

sound.label: CEFR A2
sound.def: To make a noise or to seem to be something when heard
sound.ex: The alarm clock sounds very loud in the morning.

soundtrack.label: CEFR B2
soundtrack.def: All the music and songs recorded for a film, show, or other production.
soundtrack.ex: The soundtrack to the new Marvel film features some of the most popular artists in the world.

soup.label: CEFR A1
soup.def: A liquid food made by boiling vegetables, meat, or fish in water.
soup.ex: I had hot tomato soup for lunch.

sour.label: CEFR B1
sour.def: Having a sharp, acidic taste like lemon or vinegar; no longer fresh.
sour.ex: The milk had gone sour, so we had to throw it away.

south.label: CEFR A2
south.def: The direction opposite to north, where the sun is at midday.
south.ex: They travelled south for two weeks to reach the warmer cities.

southeast.label: CEFR B1
southeast.def: The direction between south and east, or the region in that part of a country or area.
southeast.ex: The southeast of England includes cities like Brighton and Dover on the coast.

southern.label: CEFR B1
southern.def: Located in or relating to the south part of a place or region.
southern.ex: The southern coast of Spain has beautiful beaches and warm weather.

southward.label: CEFR B2
southward.def: In the direction of the south; towards the southern part of a place or region.
southward.ex: The river flows southward through three countries before reaching the coast.

southwest.label: CEFR B1
southwest.def: The direction halfway between south and west; the area in this direction from a place.
southwest.ex: The southwest of the country is known for its warm climate and beautiful beaches.

souvenir.label: CEFR B1
souvenir.def: A small object you keep to remember a place or event you visited or experienced.
souvenir.ex: I bought a small Eiffel Tower souvenir from a shop in Paris to remind me of my holiday.

soviet.label: CEFR B2
soviet.def: A citizen of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics, the communist state that existed from 1922 to 1991.
soviet.ex: During the Cold War, many Soviets lived under strict government control and limited freedom of speech.

soybean.label: CEFR B2
soybean.def: A legume plant native to East Asia, grown for its protein-rich beans used in food and oil production.
soybean.ex: Soybean cultivation has expanded significantly across South America due to growing global demand for plant-based protein.

spa.label: CEFR B2
spa.def: A resort or facility offering health treatments like massages, thermal baths, and relaxation therapies.
spa.ex: After a stressful week at work, she booked a weekend at a luxury spa to unwind and rejuvenate.

space.label: CEFR A2
space.def: An empty area or gap between things, or a place where you can move freely
space.ex: There is not much space in our small apartment for a big table.

spaceship.label: CEFR A2
spaceship.def: A large vehicle designed to carry people or equipment into space and beyond Earth.
spaceship.ex: The spaceship launched from the space centre early this morning.

spaghetti.label: CEFR A2
spaghetti.def: Long, thin pasta made from flour and water, usually cooked in boiling water
spaghetti.ex: I cooked spaghetti for dinner and mixed it with tomato sauce.

span.label: CEFR B2
span.def: To extend across or cover the distance or period from one point to another.
span.ex: The Golden Gate Bridge spans the entrance to San Francisco Bay.

spare.label: CEFR B2
spare.def: To make something available to someone when it is not easy to do so.
spare.ex: She couldn't spare much time for the project because of her other commitments.

sparerib.label: CEFR B2
sparerib.def: A cut of meat from the rib section of a pig, with bones and little meat between them.
sparerib.ex: The restaurant's glazed spareribs were fall-off-the-bone tender and coated with a sticky barbecue sauce.

spark.label: CEFR B2
spark.def: A small particle of fire or electricity that flies off and may ignite something else.
spark.ex: The mechanic noticed sparks flying from the engine, which indicated an electrical fault in the vehicle.

sparkle.label: CEFR B1
sparkle.def: A small bright flash or glimmer of light that shines briefly.
sparkle.ex: The diamond caught the light and created a beautiful sparkle that made her smile.

sparkling.label: CEFR B2
sparkling.def: Shining brightly with flashes of light; lively and energetic in manner or performance.
sparkling.ex: The champagne was sparkling as she raised her glass to celebrate the company's sparkling financial results.

sparrow.label: CEFR B2
sparrow.def: A small brown bird commonly found in towns and countryside across Europe and Asia.
sparrow.ex: A sparrow landed on the garden fence and began to sing its cheerful song.

speak.label: CEFR A1
speak.def: To say words using your voice; to talk to someone

speaker.label: CEFR A2
speaker.def: A person who talks to a group of people, or a device that makes sound louder.
speaker.ex: The speaker at the conference talked about climate change for one hour.

special.label: CEFR A1
special.def: Different from others; important or unusual in a good way
special.ex: My birthday is a special day because my family celebrates with me.

specialist.label: CEFR B1
specialist.def: A person who has expert knowledge and skills in one particular area of work or study.
specialist.ex: She is a cardiac specialist who helps patients with heart problems.

specialize/specialise.label: CEFR B1
specialize/specialise.def: To focus your work or study on one particular area or subject rather than many different ones.
specialize/specialise.ex: After studying general medicine for two years, she decided to specialise in cardiology.

specially.label: CEFR B1
specially.def: In a way made or intended for a particular purpose or person.
specially.ex: This gluten-free bread was specially made for customers with allergies.

specialty.label: CEFR B2
specialty.def: A particular area of knowledge or activity in which someone has expert skill or deep knowledge.
specialty.ex: Her specialty in cardiac surgery made her one of the most sought-after doctors at the hospital.

species.label: CEFR B2
species.def: A group of living organisms that can breed together and produce fertile offspring.
species.ex: Scientists have identified over 400 species of sharks inhabiting the world's oceans.

specifically.label: CEFR B2
specifically.def: In a way that is exact, precise, or limited to one particular thing or person
specifically.ex: The report specifically mentions the need for improved safety measures in the workplace.

spectacle.label: CEFR B2
spectacle.def: A visually impressive or remarkable event, performance, or display that captures people's attention.
spectacle.ex: The opening ceremony of the Olympics was a spectacular spectacle, with thousands of performers and elaborate visual effects.

spectacular.label: CEFR B1
spectacular.def: Extremely impressive and exciting to see or watch.
spectacular.ex: The fireworks display over the city was spectacular, and thousands of people came to watch it.

spectator.label: CEFR B1
spectator.def: A person who watches a sporting event, performance, or other activity
spectator.ex: Thousands of spectators cheered loudly when their team scored the winning goal.

speech.label: CEFR A1
speech.def: Words spoken aloud to a group of people, usually to inform or persuade them.
speech.ex: The president gave a speech about climate change on television last night.

speed.label: CEFR B1
speed.def: To move or travel quickly, or to make something happen faster.
speed.ex: The car sped down the motorway at over 100 kilometres per hour.

speeder.label: CEFR B2
speeder.def: A person who drives a vehicle faster than the legal speed limit on a road.
speeder.ex: Police caught three speeders on the motorway yesterday, each travelling at over 100 mph.

spell.label: CEFR A2
spell.def: To say or write the letters of a word in order.
spell.ex: Can you spell your name for me, please?

spellbound.label: CEFR B2
spellbound.def: Completely fascinated and unable to look away or stop paying attention to something.
spellbound.ex: The audience sat spellbound throughout the magician's entire performance, barely breathing.

spelling.label: CEFR B1
spelling.def: The correct way to write the letters of a word in order.
spelling.ex: Her spelling improved significantly after she started using a dictionary to check her written work.

spend.label: CEFR A1
spend.def: To use money to buy something.
spend.ex: I spend ten pounds on a new shirt.

sphere.label: CEFR B1
sphere.def: A perfectly round ball shape, or an area of activity or knowledge
sphere.ex: The Earth is a sphere that rotates around the Sun.

spice.label: CEFR B1
spice.def: A flavouring powder or seed from plants, used to add taste to food
spice.ex: She added cinnamon spice to the apple cake to make it more flavourful.

spicy.label: CEFR B1
spicy.def: Food that has a strong, hot taste from chillies or spices.
spicy.ex: I really enjoy spicy curry, but my friend prefers milder flavours.

spider.label: CEFR B1
spider.def: Small creature with eight legs that spins webs to catch insects for food
spider.ex: A large spider had built its web across the corner of the window.

spill.label: CEFR A2
spill.def: To cause liquid to flow out of a container accidentally.
spill.ex: I spilled coffee on my shirt this morning.

spin.label: CEFR B2
spin.def: To rotate rapidly around a central point, or to present information in a particular way to influence opinion.
spin.ex: The politician tried to spin the economic data in a way that made the government look good.

spinach.label: CEFR B1
spinach.def: A dark green leafy vegetable that is very healthy and rich in iron
spinach.ex: Many people add fresh spinach to their salads because it is nutritious and full of vitamins.

spiral.label: CEFR B1
spiral.def: Moving in a circular pattern that gets gradually larger or smaller
spiral.ex: The spiral staircase in the old tower was difficult to climb because of its tight, twisting steps.

spirit.label: CEFR B1
spirit.def: A person's character, energy, or attitude about something; also, a supernatural being.
spirit.ex: Despite losing the match, the team showed great spirit and determination throughout the game.

spiritual.label: CEFR B1
spiritual.def: Connected to religion, beliefs, or the soul rather than physical things.
spiritual.ex: Many people find spiritual peace through meditation and prayer.

spite.label: CEFR B2
spite.def: A desire to hurt or humiliate someone because you are angry with them.
spite.ex: He quit his job out of spite after his boss refused to give him a promotion.

splash.label: CEFR A2
splash.def: To cause liquid to hit a surface and scatter in all directions.
splash.ex: The children splashed water at each other in the swimming pool.

splatter.label: CEFR B2
splatter.def: A scattered pattern of liquid or semi-liquid substance spread across a surface
splatter.ex: The painter's apron was covered in splatters of bright blue paint from the morning's work.

split.label: CEFR B2
split.def: To divide something into separate parts or groups, often with each part going to different people.
split.ex: After the project ended, the team split into smaller groups to work on different departments.

spoil.label: CEFR B1
spoil.def: To damage or ruin something so it is no longer good or useful.
spoil.ex: The hot weather will spoil the ice cream if you don't keep it in the freezer.

spokesman.label: CEFR B2
spokesman.def: A person who speaks officially for a group, organisation, or government on their behalf.
spokesman.ex: The government spokesman announced that the new policy would take effect at the beginning of next month.

spokeswoman.label: CEFR B2
spokeswoman.def: A woman who officially represents an organisation or government and speaks publicly on its behalf.
spokeswoman.ex: The hospital spokeswoman announced that the new wing would open in March following safety inspections.

sponge.label: CEFR B1
sponge.def: A soft, porous material that absorbs water, used for cleaning or bathing.
sponge.ex: She wiped down the kitchen counter with a wet sponge and some soap.

sponsor.label: CEFR B2
sponsor.def: To provide financial support or funding for an event, person, or organisation.
sponsor.ex: Several major companies sponsored the marathon to help raise money for the local children's hospital.

spoon.label: CEFR A2
spoon.def: A small tool with a handle and a round or oval bowl, used for eating or stirring.
spoon.ex: She used a spoon to stir the sugar into her tea.

sport.label: CEFR A1
sport.def: An activity you do for exercise or competition, like football or swimming.

sports.label: CEFR B1
sports.def: Related to physical games or competitions that involve exercise and skill.
sports.ex: My brother plays several sports activities at university, including football and tennis.

sports center/sports centre.label: CEFR A2
sports center/sports centre.def: A building with facilities and equipment for playing sports and physical activities.
sports center/sports centre.ex: My children go to the sports centre twice a week to play badminton and swim.

sportsmanship.label: CEFR B1
sportsmanship.def: Fair and respectful behaviour when playing sports, including accepting defeat gracefully.
sportsmanship.ex: The rugby player showed great sportsmanship by helping an injured opponent off the field.

spot.label: CEFR B2
spot.def: To notice or see something, often something that is difficult or not obvious.
spot.ex: The detective spotted a crucial clue that other investigators had missed.

spray.label: CEFR B2
spray.def: To scatter liquid in fine mist or droplets over a surface or area
spray.ex: The farmer sprayed pesticide on the crops to protect them from insects.

spread.label: CEFR B2
spread.def: To extend over a larger area or distribute something across a surface or among people.
spread.ex: The wildfire spread rapidly through the forest during the hot summer months.

spreadsheet.label: CEFR B2
spreadsheet.def: A computer program that displays data in rows and columns, allowing calculations and analysis
spreadsheet.ex: The accountant used a spreadsheet to track all the company's monthly expenses and income.

spring.label: CEFR A1
spring.def: The season between winter and summer when plants grow and flowers appear.
spring.ex: In spring, the flowers bloom and the weather becomes warmer.

sprint.label: CEFR B2
sprint.def: A short race run at maximum speed, or a sudden burst of fast running or movement.
sprint.ex: She won the 100-metre sprint with an impressive finishing kick.

spur.label: CEFR B2
spur.def: Something that encourages or motivates someone to take action or improve.
spur.ex: The criticism from her coach proved to be the spur she needed to train harder and eventually win the competition.

spy.label: CEFR A1
spy.def: A person who secretly watches others or gathers secret information for a government.
spy.ex: The spy hid behind the door and listened to their conversation.

squad.label: CEFR B2
squad.def: A small group of people organised for a specific purpose or task.
squad.ex: The detective squad investigated the crime scene thoroughly before drawing any conclusions.

square.label: CEFR A2
square.def: A flat shape with four equal sides and four right angles.
square.ex: The children played hopscotch on the squares drawn on the playground.

squash.label: CEFR B2
squash.def: To press or crush something into a flatter or smaller shape by applying force.
squash.ex: She squashed the empty cardboard box before putting it in the recycling bin.

squeeze.label: CEFR B2
squeeze.def: To press something firmly from opposite sides to reduce its size or extract liquid from it.
squeeze.ex: She squeezed the lemon over the fish to add flavour to the dish.

stab.label: CEFR B2
stab.def: To push a sharp pointed weapon quickly and forcefully into something or someone.
stab.ex: The investigator found that the victim had been stabbed multiple times with a kitchen knife.

stability.label: CEFR B2
stability.def: The quality of being steady, secure, and unlikely to change or fail suddenly.
stability.ex: The government implemented reforms to improve economic stability and restore investor confidence.

stack.label: CEFR B2
stack.def: A pile of objects arranged one on top of another, or a large quantity of something.
stack.ex: The librarian carefully arranged a stack of reference books on the desk for the research project.

stadium.label: CEFR A2
stadium.def: A large building with seating for watching sports or concerts.
stadium.ex: Thousands of people went to the stadium to watch the football match.

staff.label: CEFR A2
staff.def: A group of people who work together for an organisation or business.
staff.ex: The hospital staff work hard to help sick people every day.

stage.label: CEFR A1
stage.def: A raised platform where actors or performers stand in front of an audience
stage.ex: The singer walked onto the stage and began to sing.

stagger.label: CEFR B2
stagger.def: An irregular, unsteady walk or movement, often caused by drunkenness or physical weakness.
stagger.ex: The injured footballer's stagger across the pitch made it clear he needed medical attention immediately.

stain.label: CEFR B2
stain.def: To mark or discolour something with a substance that is difficult to remove.
stain.ex: He accidentally stained his white shirt with red wine at the dinner party.

stair.label: CEFR A2
stair.def: One step in a series that you climb up or down to move between different levels
stair.ex: She walked up the stairs to the second floor of the building.

stake.label: CEFR B2
stake.def: A financial investment or amount of money risked in a business venture or gamble.
stake.ex: The venture capitalists have a significant stake in the technology startup's success.

stall.label: CEFR B1
stall.def: A small shop or counter where goods are sold, usually in a market or street
stall.ex: She bought fresh vegetables from a stall at the farmer's market on Saturday morning.

stammer.label: CEFR B2
stammer.def: To speak with involuntary pauses or repetition of sounds, especially at the start of words.
stammer.ex: When he was nervous during the interview, he began to stammer and struggled to articulate his thoughts clearly.

stamp.label: CEFR B2
stamp.def: To press your foot down heavily on the ground, often to show anger or emphasis.
stamp.ex: She stamped her foot in frustration when she realised the shop had closed early.

stand.label: CEFR A2
stand.def: To be upright on your feet, not sitting or lying down.
stand.ex: I stood in the queue for twenty minutes waiting to buy my ticket.

standard.label: CEFR B1
standard.def: A level of quality or achievement that is expected or considered acceptable
standard.ex: The school maintains high academic standards to prepare students for university.

standby.label: CEFR B2
standby.def: Ready to be used or activated if needed; kept in reserve for emergency situations.
standby.ex: The hospital keeps standby generators in case of power failures during critical operations.

standstill.label: CEFR B2
standstill.def: A complete stop or absence of progress or movement in a situation or process.
standstill.ex: Traffic came to a standstill after the accident on the motorway, delaying thousands of commuters.

stanza.label: CEFR B2
stanza.def: A grouped section of lines in a poem, separated by blank space from other sections.
stanza.ex: The poet structured the sonnet in three quatrains and a final couplet, with each stanza exploring a different aspect of the theme.

star.label: CEFR B1
star.def: To be the main actor or performer in a film, play, or show.
star.ex: She starred in a popular television drama that won several awards.

stare.label: CEFR B1
stare.def: To look directly at someone or something for a long time without moving your eyes.
stare.ex: She stared at the painting in the museum, trying to understand what the artist meant.

start.label: CEFR A1
start.def: To begin doing something; to make something begin
start.ex: I start work at nine o'clock every morning.

startle.label: CEFR B2
startle.def: To surprise someone suddenly, causing them to react with shock or fear.
startle.ex: The loud noise startled me so much that I dropped my coffee cup.

starvation.label: CEFR B2
starvation.def: Extreme hunger and weakness caused by not having enough food for a long time.
starvation.ex: The humanitarian crisis has led to widespread starvation in the conflict-affected regions.

starve.label: CEFR B2
starve.def: To suffer or die from lack of food over an extended period.
starve.ex: During the siege, thousands of civilians began to starve as food supplies dwindled.

starving.label: CEFR B2
starving.def: Suffering from extreme hunger, or extremely wanting or lacking something essential.
starving.ex: The starving artists in the city struggled to afford rent while pursuing their creative careers.

state.label: CEFR B2
state.def: To express or communicate information clearly and formally in words.
state.ex: The report states that climate change has accelerated significantly over the past decade.

statement.label: CEFR A2
statement.def: Something said or written that gives information or expresses an opinion
statement.ex: The government made a statement about the new education policy on the news.

statesman.label: CEFR B2
statesman.def: An experienced politician who is respected for wisdom, skill, and dedication to serving their country.
statesman.ex: The former president was remembered as a skilled statesman who negotiated peace between two rival nations.

statewide.label: CEFR B2
statewide.def: Throughout or covering an entire state or all parts of a state
statewide.ex: The government introduced a statewide ban on single-use plastics to reduce environmental damage.

station.label: CEFR A1
station.def: A place where trains or buses stop to pick up and drop off people.
station.ex: I wait at the bus station every morning to go to school.

statistics.label: CEFR B2
statistics.def: Numerical data collected and analysed to show facts about a subject or population
statistics.ex: According to recent statistics, more people are working from home than ever before.

statue.label: CEFR A2
statue.def: A large carved or sculpted figure of a person or animal, usually made of stone or metal.
statue.ex: There is a famous statue of a king in the centre of the town square.

stay.label: CEFR A1
stay.def: To remain in a place for a period of time.
stay.ex: I stay at my grandmother's house every summer.

steadily.label: CEFR B1
steadily.def: In a continuous, stable way without stopping or changing speed.
steadily.ex: The company's profits have been increasing steadily over the past three years.

steady.label: CEFR B1
steady.def: Not changing, not moving, or staying the same over time
steady.ex: She has a steady job at the bank and earns a good salary.

steak.label: CEFR A2
steak.def: A thick slice of meat, usually beef, cooked by grilling or frying.
steak.ex: I ordered a steak and chips for dinner at the restaurant.

steal.label: CEFR A2
steal.def: To take something that belongs to another person without permission.
steal.ex: The boy stole money from his mother's purse.

steam.label: CEFR B1
steam.def: Hot gas or vapour that rises from boiling water
steam.ex: The steam from the kettle fogged up the kitchen window.

steel.label: CEFR B1
steel.def: A hard, strong metal made from iron mixed with carbon, used for tools and building.
steel.ex: The bridge was constructed using steel beams that can support heavy traffic.

steely.label: CEFR B2
steely.def: Hard, cold, and emotionally distant; showing no warmth or sympathy
steely.ex: She fixed him with a steely glare that made it clear she would not tolerate any excuses.

steep.label: CEFR B1
steep.def: Having a very sharp angle; rising or falling quickly with great slope.
steep.ex: The path up the mountain was so steep that we had to stop frequently to rest.

steer.label: CEFR B2
steer.def: To guide or direct something in a particular direction or towards a goal.
steer.ex: The captain steered the ship away from the rocks during the storm.

steering wheel.label: CEFR B2
steering wheel.def: The circular control in a vehicle that the driver turns to change direction.
steering wheel.ex: She gripped the steering wheel tightly as she navigated the narrow mountain road.

stem.label: CEFR B1
stem.def: To stop something from spreading or getting worse.
stem.ex: The government introduced new policies to stem the rise in unemployment.

step.label: CEFR A1
step.def: To lift your foot and put it down in a different place to move forward
step.ex: She stepped carefully across the wet floor.

stepfather.label: CEFR B2
stepfather.def: A man married to your mother who is not your biological father.
stepfather.ex: After her parents' divorce, Sarah developed a close relationship with her stepfather over the years.

stepmother.label: CEFR B2
stepmother.def: A woman who is married to one's father after the death or divorce of one's mother.
stepmother.ex: She had always been close to her stepmother, who treated her with genuine kindness and respect.

stereotype.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
stereotype.def: A fixed, oversimplified belief about a group of people that ignores individual differences.
stereotype.ex: The film challenged the stereotype that all successful businessmen are ruthless and dishonest.

stern.label: CEFR B2
stern.def: Serious, strict, and disapproving in manner or expression; showing no humour or gentleness
stern.ex: The teacher gave her students a stern warning about plagiarism before the assignment deadline.

stew.label: CEFR B2
stew.def: A dish of meat and vegetables cooked slowly together in liquid until tender.
stew.ex: She prepared a hearty beef stew for dinner, letting it simmer for hours until the flavours blended perfectly.

stick.label: CEFR B1
stick.def: To push a pointed object into or through something
stick.ex: She stuck the needle through the fabric to sew the button back on.

sticker.label: CEFR B1
sticker.def: A small piece of paper or plastic with a picture or design that you can stick onto a surface.
sticker.ex: My daughter collects animal stickers and puts them on her notebook at school.

sticky.label: CEFR B1
sticky.def: Covered with or having a substance that sticks to other things easily.
sticky.ex: The children's hands were sticky after eating sweets at the fair.

stiff.label: CEFR B2
stiff.def: Not flexible or bending easily; rigid and difficult to move or bend
stiff.ex: After sleeping in an uncomfortable position, she woke with a stiff neck and could barely turn her head.

still.label: CEFR A2
still.def: At this moment, or continuing from before; not moving or making noise
still.ex: It is late, but she is still working at her desk.

stimulant.label: CEFR B2
stimulant.def: A substance that increases alertness, energy, or activity in the body or mind temporarily.
stimulant.ex: Many travellers rely on coffee as a stimulant during long flights to stay awake.

stimulate.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
stimulate.def: To encourage or inspire someone to take action or become more active or interested.
stimulate.ex: The government's new policies aim to stimulate economic growth in rural areas.

stimulation.label: CEFR B2
stimulation.def: The act of encouraging interest, activity, or development by providing interesting or exciting experiences.
stimulation.ex: The research showed that mental stimulation in childhood improves cognitive development and academic performance later in life.

stingy.label: CEFR B2
stingy.def: Unwilling to spend money or give generously; miserly.
stingy.ex: The hotel's stingy portions of breakfast cereal frustrated many guests who expected more substantial meals.

stink.label: CEFR B2
stink.def: To produce a strong, unpleasant smell that is offensive or disgusting.
stink.ex: The fish market stinks of old seafood and rot, making it almost unbearable to walk through.

stir.label: CEFR B1
stir.def: To mix liquid or soft substance by moving a spoon or stick in circles.
stir.ex: She stirred the coffee with a spoon to help the sugar dissolve.

stitch.label: CEFR B2
stitch.def: A loop of thread or yarn passed through fabric with a needle, or one complete movement in sewing.
stitch.ex: The surgeon carefully placed each stitch to ensure the wound would heal properly.

stock.label: CEFR B2
stock.def: A supply of goods or materials that a business keeps available for sale or use.
stock.ex: The shop ran out of stock for the new iPhone model within two days of release.

stock market.label: CEFR B2
stock market.def: A system where shares of companies are bought and sold, allowing people to own parts of businesses.
stock market.ex: The stock market rose sharply after the government announced new economic policies.

stockpile.label: CEFR B2
stockpile.def: A large supply of goods or materials kept in reserve for future use or emergencies.
stockpile.ex: The government has been building a strategic stockpile of medical equipment in case of another pandemic.

stomach.label: CEFR A2
stomach.def: The organ inside your body that digests food after you eat
stomach.ex: After the long flight, my stomach felt empty and I was hungry.

stomachache.label: CEFR A2
stomachache.def: Pain or discomfort in your stomach or belly area.
stomachache.ex: I ate too much candy and now I have a stomachache.

stone.label: CEFR A1
stone.def: A hard rock material found on the ground or in buildings.
stone.ex: The children threw stones into the river.

stonework.label: CEFR B2
stonework.def: Decorative or structural elements built from stone, particularly in buildings and monuments.
stonework.ex: The cathedral's intricate stonework, carved over fifty years, showcases the craftsmanship of medieval artisans.

stool.label: CEFR B2
stool.def: A seat without a back or arms, typically with three or four legs.
stool.ex: She sat on a wooden stool at the kitchen counter whilst waiting for her coffee.

stop.label: CEFR A1
stop.def: To finish moving or doing something; to not continue.
stop.ex: Please stop talking during the lesson.

stopover.label: CEFR B2
stopover.def: A brief stop during a long journey, usually between transportation connections or to rest.
stopover.ex: During our flight to Australia, we had a six-hour stopover in Singapore where we explored the airport's shopping district.

stopwatch.label: CEFR B2
stopwatch.def: A device that measures time in seconds and fractions of seconds, used to time activities or events.
stopwatch.ex: The coach used a stopwatch to record the athlete's fastest sprint time during training.

storage.label: CEFR B1
storage.def: Space or container where things are kept for later use
storage.ex: The attic provides good storage for old clothes and furniture.

store.label: CEFR B2
store.def: To keep or save something in a place for future use.
store.ex: The company stores all its data on secure servers to prevent loss.

storm.label: CEFR A2
storm.def: A violent weather event with very strong wind, heavy rain, and often thunder.
storm.ex: The news report warned that a dangerous storm would hit the coast tomorrow.

stormy.label: CEFR B1
stormy.def: Characterized by strong winds, heavy rain, and rough weather conditions.
stormy.ex: The stormy weather forced the football match to be postponed until next weekend.

story.label: CEFR A1
story.def: A description of events or things that happened, real or imaginary.
story.ex: She read a bedtime story to her children every night.

storyteller.label: CEFR B1
storyteller.def: A person who tells stories to entertain or inform audiences, often as their profession.
storyteller.ex: The storyteller captivated the children with tales of adventure and magic during the library event.

stove.label: CEFR A2
stove.def: A piece of equipment used for cooking food with heat
stove.ex: She put the pot on the stove to make soup for dinner.

straight.label: CEFR A1
straight.def: In a direct line without turning or bending.
straight.ex: Go straight ahead and you will find the shop on your left.

straighten.label: CEFR B2
straighten.def: To make something straight or to correct a bent or curved condition.
straighten.ex: She straightened her desk before the meeting, arranging all her papers neatly.

strain.label: CEFR B2
strain.def: To injure a muscle or ligament by stretching it too much or working too hard.
strain.ex: She strained her back while lifting heavy boxes during the house move.

strait.label: CEFR B2
strait.def: A narrow passage of water connecting two larger bodies of water.
strait.ex: The Strait of Gibraltar separates Spain from Morocco and connects the Atlantic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea.

strand.label: CEFR B2
strand.def: A single thin length of something like hair, thread, or rope twisted together with others
strand.ex: A single strand of DNA contains millions of genetic instructions that determine our characteristics.

strange.label: CEFR A1
strange.def: Unusual or different from what you normally see or experience.
strange.ex: I heard a strange noise in the dark.

strangely.label: CEFR B1
strangely.def: In a way that seems unusual or surprising and hard to explain.
strangely.ex: Strangely, the café was completely empty on a busy Saturday morning.

stranger.label: CEFR A2
stranger.def: A person you do not know.
stranger.ex: A stranger asked me for directions to the train station.

strangle.label: CEFR B2
strangle.def: To kill someone by pressing their throat tightly so they cannot breathe.
strangle.ex: The detective discovered that the victim had been strangled with a silk scarf found at the crime scene.

strategic.label: CEFR B1
strategic.def: Carefully planned to achieve a specific goal or advantage
strategic.ex: The company made a strategic decision to invest in new technology rather than hire more staff.

straw.label: CEFR B1
straw.def: Thin tubes made from plastic or paper used for drinking liquids
straw.ex: She sipped her juice through a straw at the café.

strawberry.label: CEFR B1
strawberry.def: A small, soft, sweet red fruit with tiny seeds on the outside.
strawberry.ex: She bought fresh strawberries from the market to make jam.

stream.label: CEFR B1
stream.def: To flow continuously in one direction, or to broadcast audio or video online without downloading.
stream.ex: Water streams down the mountain after heavy rain, and millions of people stream their favourite shows every night.

street.label: CEFR A1
street.def: A road in a town or city where cars, buses, and people move
street.ex: I walk down the street to buy things at the shops.

strength.label: CEFR A2
strength.def: The power to lift heavy things or do hard physical work.
strength.ex: She used all her strength to push the heavy box across the room.

strengthen.label: CEFR B1
strengthen.def: To make something stronger or more powerful.
strengthen.ex: Regular exercise will strengthen your muscles and improve your health.

stressed.label: CEFR B1
stressed.def: Feeling worried, anxious, or unable to relax because of pressure or problems
stressed.ex: She felt stressed about her upcoming exam, so she studied every evening for two weeks.

stressful.label: CEFR B1
stressful.def: Causing worry, pressure, or anxiety that makes you feel tense
stressful.ex: Starting a new job can be stressful, but most people adapt quickly.

stretch.label: CEFR B1
stretch.def: To extend your body or a part of it to make your muscles longer and more relaxed.
stretch.ex: After sitting at my desk for hours, I stood up and stretched my arms above my head.

stricken.label: CEFR B1
stricken.def: Seriously affected or damaged by something bad or harmful
stricken.ex: The stricken ship had to be evacuated before it sank in the storm.

strict.label: CEFR A1
strict.def: Having and enforcing rules that must be followed closely; not allowing exceptions or relaxation.
strict.ex: My teacher is very strict about homework—we must finish it every night.

strictly.label: CEFR B2
strictly.def: In a way that allows no exceptions, deviations, or leniency; rigidly or precisely
strictly.ex: The headmaster warned that the uniform code would be enforced strictly, with no exceptions permitted.

stride.label: CEFR B2
stride.def: To walk with long, purposeful steps, covering distance quickly and confidently.
stride.ex: She strode across the boardroom and presented her findings with conviction.

strike.label: CEFR B1
strike.def: To hit something or someone with force.
strike.ex: The hammer struck the nail perfectly, driving it deep into the wood.

striking.label: CEFR B2
striking.def: Unusual or noticeable enough to attract immediate attention or cause strong reaction.
striking.ex: The striking difference between the two cities became apparent as soon as we crossed the border.

string.label: CEFR A2
string.def: A thin piece of cord or rope, usually made from twisted fibres or thread.
string.ex: I tied a string around the box to keep it closed.

strip.label: CEFR B2
strip.def: To remove all covering or clothing from something or someone completely.
strip.ex: The workers had to strip the old paint from the walls before redecorating the house.

stripe.label: CEFR B1
stripe.def: A long narrow band of colour that contrasts with the surface around it
stripe.ex: The zebra has black and white stripes running across its body.

strive.label: CEFR B2
strive.def: To make a great effort to achieve or reach something difficult or important.
strive.ex: The company continues to strive for excellence in customer service, even during challenging economic times.

stroke.label: CEFR B2
stroke.def: To move your hand gently and smoothly over something, usually repeatedly.
stroke.ex: She stroked the cat's fur gently whilst sitting on the sofa.

strong.label: CEFR A1
strong.def: Having great power or physical force; not weak.
strong.ex: He is strong and can carry heavy boxes easily.

strongly.label: CEFR A2
strongly.def: In a powerful or intense way; with great force or feeling.
strongly.ex: I strongly believe that exercise is good for your health.

struggle.label: CEFR B2
struggle.def: To make a determined effort against difficulty or opposition; to have great difficulty doing something.
struggle.ex: Many students struggle with advanced mathematics, but persistence and good tutoring can help them improve.

stubborn.label: CEFR B1
stubborn.def: Refusing to change your mind or opinion, even when others disagree with you.
stubborn.ex: My stubborn friend refused to admit he was wrong, even when we showed him the evidence.

stubbornly.label: CEFR B2
stubbornly.def: In a way that shows unwillingness to change one's mind or agree with others.
stubbornly.ex: She stubbornly refused to admit that her approach had failed, despite the clear evidence.

stuck.label: CEFR B1
stuck.def: Unable to move or change position; unable to make progress or find a solution.
stuck.ex: I've been stuck on this maths problem for an hour and can't figure it out.

student.label: CEFR A1
student.def: A person who learns at a school or university.
student.ex: My sister is a student at the local primary school.

studio.label: CEFR B1
studio.def: A room or building where an artist, photographer, or musician creates or performs their work.
studio.ex: She rents a small studio in the city centre where she paints and sells her artwork.

study.label: CEFR A1
study.def: To learn by reading books or doing exercises and thinking carefully about information.
study.ex: I study English every day at school.

stuff.label: CEFR B2
stuff.def: To fill something tightly with material or force something into a limited space
stuff.ex: She stuffed the suitcase with clothes before her trip to Paris.

stuffed.label: CEFR B1
stuffed.def: Completely full of something, unable to hold more.
stuffed.ex: After eating the large pizza, I felt too stuffed to have dessert.

stumble.label: CEFR B1
stumble.def: To trip or nearly fall when walking or running.
stumble.ex: I stumbled on the uneven pavement and caught myself against a wall.

stun.label: CEFR B1
stun.def: To shock or surprise someone so much they cannot react immediately.
stun.ex: The news that she had won the lottery absolutely stunned her for several minutes.

stunning.label: CEFR B1
stunning.def: Extremely impressive or beautiful; causing great surprise or admiration
stunning.ex: The sunset over the mountains was absolutely stunning, and everyone stopped to take photographs.

stupid.label: CEFR B1
stupid.def: Not intelligent or showing a lack of good judgment or understanding
stupid.ex: I made a stupid mistake by forgetting my passport at home before the flight.

style.label: CEFR A2
style.def: A particular way of doing, making, or presenting something that shows who you are.
style.ex: She has her own style of dressing that makes her look different from other people.

stylish.label: CEFR B1
stylish.def: Attractive and fashionable in appearance or design.
stylish.ex: She wore a stylish black coat that impressed everyone at the party.

stylist.label: CEFR B2
stylist.def: A professional who advises people on fashion, hair, or personal appearance to enhance their image.
stylist.ex: The celebrity hired a renowned stylist to prepare her outfit for the red carpet event.

subconscious.label: CEFR B1
subconscious.def: Existing in your mind without your active awareness or control.
subconscious.ex: She had a subconscious fear of heights that she didn't fully recognise until she tried to climb the ladder.

subcontinent.label: CEFR B2
subcontinent.def: A large landmass that forms a distinct geographical and cultural region within a continent.
subcontinent.ex: The Indian subcontinent comprises India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh, sharing common historical and cultural ties.

subculture.label: CEFR B2
subculture.def: A group within society with distinct values, beliefs, and practices that differ from mainstream culture.
subculture.ex: The hip-hop subculture emerged in New York during the 1970s and has profoundly influenced global music and fashion.

subdivision.label: CEFR B2
subdivision.def: A smaller section or part created by dividing a larger area, group, or system into pieces.
subdivision.ex: The property developer divided the land into a new subdivision of forty residential plots.

subject.label: CEFR A1
subject.def: A topic that you study or discuss, or the person who is being talked about.
subject.ex: My favourite subject at school is mathematics.

subjunctive.label: CEFR B2
subjunctive.def: A verb form expressing wishes, possibilities, or hypothetical situations rather than facts.
subjunctive.ex: In Spanish grammar lessons, students learn that the subjunctive is used after expressions of doubt or desire.

submarine.label: CEFR B1
submarine.def: A military ship that can travel underwater by filling special tanks with water.
submarine.ex: The navy deployed a nuclear submarine to patrol the coastal waters during the international crisis.

submit.label: CEFR B2
submit.def: To give something to a person in authority for their consideration or decision.
submit.ex: She submitted her research proposal to the university's ethics committee for approval.

subplot.label: CEFR B2
subplot.def: A secondary storyline in a narrative that develops alongside the main plot and often enriches the central theme.
subplot.ex: The novel's subplot involving the protagonist's estranged sister adds emotional depth to the main investigation story.

subside.label: CEFR B2
subside.def: To become less intense, violent, or noticeable; to gradually decrease or calm down.
subside.ex: After the storm subsided, the farmers were able to assess the damage to their crops.

substance.label: CEFR B2
substance.def: A particular kind of matter or material with specific physical and chemical properties.
substance.ex: Scientists discovered a new substance in the meteorite that had never been found on Earth before.

substantial.label: CEFR B1
substantial.def: Large in amount, size, or importance; significant and noticeable.
substantial.ex: The company made a substantial investment in new technology to improve its services.

subtitle.label: CEFR B2
subtitle.def: Translated text shown at the bottom of a film or video in a different language
subtitle.ex: The French film had English subtitles so the audience could understand the dialogue.

subtle.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
subtle.def: Not obvious or easily noticed; delicate and requiring careful attention to perceive
subtle.ex: There was a subtle difference in colour between the two fabrics that most people wouldn't notice.

subtract.label: CEFR B2
subtract.def: To take away a number or amount from another to find the difference.
subtract.ex: If you subtract 15 from 48, you get 33.

suburb.label: CEFR B2
suburb.def: A residential area on the edge of a city where most people live in houses rather than apartments.
suburb.ex: Many families choose to raise their children in the suburbs where property is more affordable and there is more green space.

subway.label: CEFR A1
subway.def: A train system that runs underground beneath a city.
subway.ex: I take the subway to school every morning.

succeed.label: CEFR A2
succeed.def: To achieve or accomplish something you want or try to do
succeed.ex: She studied hard and succeeded in passing her English exam.

success.label: CEFR A2
success.def: Achieving what you want or doing something well.
success.ex: She worked hard and had great success in her final exams.

successful.label: CEFR A1
successful.def: Achieving what you wanted or planned to do.
successful.ex: She had a successful test and got a good mark.

successfully.label: CEFR A2
successfully.def: In a way that achieves the desired result or goal.
successfully.ex: She successfully passed her driving test on the first try.

successive.label: CEFR B2
successive.def: Following one after another in order without interruption or gap
successive.ex: The team won three successive championships, cementing their dominance in the sport.

suck.label: CEFR B2
suck.def: To draw something into your mouth using air pressure; to be very bad or unpleasant
suck.ex: The vacuum cleaner sucked up all the dust from the carpet, though the new policy really sucked for employees.

sudden.label: CEFR A2
sudden.def: Happening quickly and without warning; unexpected
sudden.ex: There was a sudden loud noise that frightened everyone in the room.

suddenly.label: CEFR B1
suddenly.def: Happening or done quickly and without warning
suddenly.ex: The car suddenly stopped in front of us, and we almost crashed into it.

sue.label: CEFR B2
sue.def: To take legal action against someone in court to obtain money or justice.
sue.ex: The company decided to sue the manufacturer for producing defective equipment that caused injuries to their workers.

suffer.label: CEFR B1
suffer.def: To experience pain, illness, or difficulty that makes you unhappy or uncomfortable.
suffer.ex: Many people suffer from allergies during spring when pollen is in the air.

suffering.label: CEFR B2
suffering.def: Physical or emotional pain and distress experienced by a person or animal.
suffering.ex: The humanitarian organisation documented the suffering of refugees displaced by the conflict.

sufficiently.label: CEFR B2
sufficiently.def: To an adequate degree; enough to meet a need or requirement.
sufficiently.ex: The report didn't provide sufficiently detailed evidence to support the government's policy decision.

suffix.label: CEFR B1
suffix.def: Letters added to the end of a word to change its meaning or grammar form.
suffix.ex: Adding the suffix '-tion' to the verb 'educate' creates the noun 'education'.

sugar.label: CEFR A1
sugar.def: Sweet white or brown crystals from plants, used to make food taste sweet
sugar.ex: I put sugar in my tea every morning.

suggest.label: CEFR A2
suggest.def: To offer an idea or plan for someone to think about or try
suggest.ex: My teacher suggested that I should read more books to improve my English.

suggestion.label: CEFR A1
suggestion.def: An idea or plan that you offer to someone for them to think about.
suggestion.ex: My friend made a suggestion to go to the park on Saturday.

suicide.label: CEFR B1
suicide.def: The act of intentionally ending one's own life.
suicide.ex: Mental health charities are working to prevent suicide by offering support to people in crisis.

suit.label: CEFR A2
suit.def: To be acceptable or convenient for someone; to fit someone's needs well.
suit.ex: Does Tuesday suit you for the meeting, or would Wednesday be better?

suitable.label: CEFR A2
suitable.def: Right or appropriate for a particular person, situation, or purpose.
suitable.ex: This film is suitable for children because it has no violence.

suitably.label: CEFR B2
suitably.def: In a way that is right or appropriate for a particular purpose or situation.
suitably.ex: The candidate was suitably qualified for the senior management position, with over fifteen years of relevant experience in the field.

suitcase.label: CEFR A2
suitcase.def: A large bag with a handle used to carry clothes and belongings when travelling.
suitcase.ex: She packed her suitcase with clothes for her two-week holiday in Spain.

sulfur/sulphur.label: CEFR B2
sulfur/sulphur.def: A yellow chemical element that burns with a strong smell, used in industry and medicine.
sulfur/sulphur.ex: The volcanic region released clouds of sulphur dioxide, which created a distinctive acrid smell across the countryside.

summarize/summarise.label: CEFR B1
summarize/summarise.def: To give a short version of the main points of something longer.
summarize/summarise.ex: Can you summarise the main ideas from the article in three sentences?

summer.label: CEFR A1
summer.def: The warmest season of the year, between spring and autumn.
summer.ex: I love swimming in the sea during summer.

summit.label: CEFR B1
summit.def: The highest point of a mountain or the top of a hill
summit.ex: After climbing for six hours, the hikers finally reached the summit and could see for miles in every direction.

sun.label: CEFR A1
sun.def: The bright star in the sky that gives light and heat to Earth during the day.
sun.ex: We played in the sun at the park yesterday.

sunbathe.label: CEFR B1
sunbathe.def: To lie in the sun to make your skin brown and feel warm.
sunbathe.ex: On weekends, many people sunbathe on the beach to relax and get a tan.

sunbeam.label: CEFR B2
sunbeam.def: A ray of sunlight that shines through the air, often visible when dust particles catch the light.
sunbeam.ex: A golden sunbeam streamed through the library window, illuminating the dust dancing in its path.

sunday.label: CEFR A1
sunday.def: The first day of the week, after Saturday
sunday.ex: We go to church on Sunday morning.

sundial.label: CEFR B1
sundial.def: A device that shows the time of day using the shadow of the sun on a marked surface.
sundial.ex: The garden had an old sundial carved from stone that indicated the hours as the sun moved across the sky.

sunflower.label: CEFR A2
sunflower.def: A tall plant with a large yellow flower that turns to follow the sun.
sunflower.ex: She planted sunflowers in her garden last spring.

sunglasses.label: CEFR A2
sunglasses.def: Glasses with dark lenses that protect your eyes from bright sunlight.
sunglasses.ex: She wore sunglasses at the beach to protect her eyes from the sun.

sunlight.label: CEFR A2
sunlight.def: Light that comes from the sun.
sunlight.ex: Plants need sunlight to grow healthy and strong.

sunny.label: CEFR A1
sunny.def: When the sun shines brightly in the sky with few or no clouds.
sunny.ex: It is a sunny day, so we can play outside.

sunrise.label: CEFR B1
sunrise.def: The moment when the sun first appears above the horizon in the morning.
sunrise.ex: We woke up early to watch the beautiful sunrise over the mountains.

sunset.label: CEFR B1
sunset.def: The time of day when the sun disappears below the horizon in the evening.
sunset.ex: We watched the beautiful sunset over the ocean last night.

sunshine.label: CEFR A1
sunshine.def: Bright light and warmth from the sun
sunshine.ex: The children played in the sunshine all afternoon.

super.label: CEFR A1
super.def: Very good or excellent; much better than normal
super.ex: That was a super film! I really enjoyed it.

superb.label: CEFR B2
superb.def: Extremely good or of the highest quality; excellent
superb.ex: The museum's new exhibition received superb reviews from critics across the country.

superintendent.label: CEFR B2
superintendent.def: A senior official who manages and oversees an organisation, department, or facility and its staff.
superintendent.ex: The superintendent of schools announced new policies to improve student attendance across all district schools.

superior.label: CEFR B1
superior.def: Better in quality, skill, or status than something or someone else
superior.ex: The superior quality of their products makes them more expensive than competitors.

superlative.label: CEFR A2
superlative.def: The form of an adjective that shows something is the most or least of all things

supermarket.label: CEFR A1
supermarket.def: A large shop where you can buy food, drinks, and other everyday things.
supermarket.ex: I go to the supermarket every Saturday to buy groceries for the week.

supernatural.label: CEFR B2
supernatural.def: Beyond the laws of nature; involving ghosts, magic, or forces science cannot explain.
supernatural.ex: The film's supernatural elements, including ghostly apparitions and unexplained phenomena, kept audiences captivated throughout.

superstar.label: CEFR A2
superstar.def: A very famous and successful performer or athlete that many people know and love.
superstar.ex: The superstar singer performed in front of fifty thousand fans at the concert.

superstition.label: CEFR B1
superstition.def: A belief that certain things bring good or bad luck, not based on reason or evidence.
superstition.ex: Many people have superstitions about the number 13, believing it brings bad luck.

supervise.label: CEFR B2
supervise.def: To watch over and direct the work or behaviour of people to ensure quality and safety.
supervise.ex: The project manager will supervise the construction team to ensure all safety regulations are followed.

supervision.label: CEFR B2
supervision.def: The act of watching over and directing people or work to ensure quality and safety.
supervision.ex: The construction project required close supervision to meet safety standards and deadlines.

supervisor.label: CEFR B2
supervisor.def: A manager or senior employee responsible for overseeing and directing the work of other employees.
supervisor.ex: My supervisor reviewed my project proposal and gave me constructive feedback before I presented it to the board.

supper.label: CEFR A2
supper.def: A light meal you eat in the evening, usually before bed.
supper.ex: After supper, we watched television together.

supplement.label: CEFR B2
supplement.def: An extra substance or product taken in addition to food or medicine to improve health or fill nutritional gaps.
supplement.ex: Many people take a vitamin supplement daily to ensure they get enough nutrients from their diet.

supplier.label: CEFR B2
supplier.def: A person or company that provides goods or services to other businesses or consumers.
supplier.ex: The manufacturer works with several suppliers to obtain the raw materials needed for production.

supply.label: CEFR B2
supply.def: To provide or deliver something that is needed or wanted.
supply.ex: The warehouse supplies office equipment to businesses throughout the region.

support.label: CEFR B1
support.def: To help someone or something by giving money, encouragement, or assistance.
support.ex: My parents have always supported my dream of becoming a musician.

supporter.label: CEFR B1
supporter.def: A person who backs or helps a team, politician, or cause they believe in.
supporter.ex: Manchester United has millions of supporters around the world who follow the team closely.

supportive.label: CEFR B1
supportive.def: Helping and encouraging someone when they need it; showing care and sympathy.
supportive.ex: My supportive family helped me through a difficult time at university.

suppose.label: CEFR B1
suppose.def: To think or believe something is probably true, though you are not certain
suppose.ex: I suppose we should leave soon if we want to catch the train on time.

supposedly.label: CEFR B1
supposedly.def: According to what people say or believe, though it may not be true
supposedly.ex: He supposedly finished his homework, but I didn't see him do it.

suppress.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
suppress.def: To prevent something from being shown, known, or expressed; to stop something from happening
suppress.ex: The government was accused of trying to suppress information about the environmental damage caused by the factory.

sure.label: CEFR A2
sure.ex: I'm sure that Sarah will arrive on time tomorrow.

surely.label: CEFR B1
surely.ex: Surely you've finished your homework by now?

surf.label: CEFR A2
surf.def: To ride on waves in the ocean using a special board
surf.ex: My brother loves to surf at the beach every weekend.

surface.label: CEFR B1
surface.def: The outer or top layer of something, or the area you can see and touch on an object.
surface.ex: The surface of the table was smooth and polished.

surfing.label: CEFR A2
surfing.def: A water sport where you stand on a board and ride waves toward the shore.
surfing.ex: My brother loves surfing and goes to the beach every weekend to catch waves.

surge.label: CEFR B2
surge.def: A sudden, powerful increase or wave of something, such as emotion, activity, or physical force.
surge.ex: The hospital experienced a surge in patient numbers during the winter flu season.

surgeon.label: CEFR B1
surgeon.def: A doctor who performs medical operations on patients in a hospital.
surgeon.ex: After the accident, a skilled surgeon operated on his broken leg to repair it properly.

surgery.label: CEFR B1
surgery.def: A medical procedure where a doctor cuts open your body to repair or remove something.
surgery.ex: After her accident, she needed emergency surgery on her knee.

surname.label: CEFR A2
surname.def: Your family's last name, shared with parents and relatives.
surname.ex: My surname is Smith, the same as my father's.

surprise.label: CEFR B1
surprise.def: To cause someone to feel sudden wonder or shock by doing something unexpected.
surprise.ex: She surprised him with a birthday party that all his friends attended.

surprised.label: CEFR A2
surprised.def: Feeling surprised means something unexpected happened and you did not expect it.
surprised.ex: I was surprised when my friend gave me a birthday present.

surprising.label: CEFR A2
surprising.def: Causing someone to feel sudden shock or wonder; not expected
surprising.ex: It was surprising to see snow in April because the weather was usually warm.

surprisingly.label: CEFR B1
surprisingly.def: In a way that causes surprise or is unexpected.
surprisingly.ex: Surprisingly, the new employee finished the project two weeks early.

surrender.label: CEFR B2
surrender.def: To stop fighting or resisting; to give control or possession to someone else.
surrender.ex: After weeks of fighting, the general decided to surrender to the enemy forces rather than risk more casualties.

surround.label: CEFR B1
surround.def: To be on all sides of someone or something; to encircle completely.
surround.ex: The police surrounded the building to prevent anyone from escaping.

surrounding.label: CEFR B1
surrounding.def: All around or near something; in the area close to a particular place
surrounding.ex: The surrounding villages are connected by a small road that leads to the main town.

surroundings.label: CEFR B2
surroundings.def: The environment and things around a place or person, including buildings, natural features, and atmosphere.
surroundings.ex: The hotel offers beautiful surroundings with views of the mountains and forest.

surveillance.label: CEFR B2
surveillance.def: Close observation of people, places, or activities, often by authorities or cameras
surveillance.ex: The airport installed new surveillance cameras to improve security and monitor passenger movement.

survival.label: CEFR B1
survival.def: Remaining alive or continuing to exist in difficult situations.
survival.ex: The rescue team's quick action was essential for the survival of the hikers lost in the mountains.

survivor.label: CEFR B1
survivor.def: A person who remains alive after a dangerous or difficult event that kills others.
survivor.ex: The survivors of the plane crash were rescued by helicopter and taken to hospital.

suspect.label: CEFR B2
suspect.def: To think that someone probably did something wrong without having proof
suspect.ex: The police suspect that the theft occurred sometime between midnight and dawn.

suspicion.label: CEFR B1
suspicion.def: A feeling that someone has done something wrong or that something bad might happen, without proof.
suspicion.ex: Police have suspicion that the robbery was planned by someone working at the bank.

suspicious.label: CEFR B2
suspicious.def: Feeling doubt or distrust about someone's motives or the truth of something.
suspicious.ex: The bank became suspicious of the large withdrawal and asked the customer for identification.

suspiciously.label: CEFR B2
suspiciously.def: In a way that suggests something is wrong or dishonest; causing doubt or distrust.
suspiciously.ex: The company's sudden collapse looked suspiciously timed, occurring just before the fraud investigation was announced.

swallow.label: CEFR A2
swallow.def: To move food or drink down your throat into your stomach.
swallow.ex: She took a sip of water and swallowed it quickly.

swan.label: CEFR B2
swan.def: A large white or black water bird with a long curved neck, known for its grace and elegance.
swan.ex: The swan glided across the lake with its cygnets following closely behind.

swarm.label: CEFR B1
swarm.def: A large group of insects or small creatures moving together in the same direction.
swarm.ex: A swarm of bees attacked the garden when we accidentally disturbed their hive.

swear.label: CEFR B1
swear.def: To use rude or offensive language; to promise something solemnly and truthfully.
swear.ex: He swore he would never lie to her again.

sweat.label: CEFR A2
sweat.def: To produce liquid from your skin, usually when you are hot or nervous.
sweat.ex: I was sweating a lot after running in the park.

sweater.label: CEFR A2
sweater.def: A warm piece of clothing you wear on your upper body, usually made of wool or cotton.
sweater.ex: I wear my favourite blue sweater when it's cold outside.

sweatshirt.label: CEFR B1
sweatshirt.def: A warm, casual piece of clothing made of soft fabric, worn over the upper body.
sweatshirt.ex: I put on my grey sweatshirt when the weather got cold in the evening.

sweep.label: CEFR B2
sweep.def: To clean a floor or surface by pushing a brush or broom across it to remove dirt.
sweep.ex: She swept the kitchen floor before the dinner guests arrived.

sweet.label: CEFR A2
sweet.def: A small piece of candy or sugar-based food eaten as a treat.
sweet.ex: The child asked for a sweet after finishing her dinner.

sweetheart.label: CEFR A2
sweetheart.def: A person you love romantically; a boyfriend or girlfriend.
sweetheart.ex: My sweetheart gave me flowers on Valentine's Day.

swell.label: CEFR B1
swell.def: To become larger or grow in size, usually due to fluid or pressure building up.
swell.ex: The injured player's ankle began to swell after he twisted it during the match.

swift.label: CEFR B2
swift.def: Happening or moving quickly; rapid or fast
swift.ex: The company made a swift decision to invest in renewable energy after the government announced new policies.

swim.label: CEFR A1
swim.def: To move through water using your arms and legs.
swim.ex: My sister likes to swim in the pool every Saturday.

swimmer.label: CEFR B1
swimmer.def: A person who swims, either for sport, exercise, or enjoyment in water.
swimmer.ex: The swimmers at the local pool train every morning to prepare for the competition.

swimming.label: CEFR A1
swimming.def: Moving through water using your arms and legs for exercise or fun.
swimming.ex: I go swimming at the pool every Saturday morning.

swimming costume.label: CEFR A2
swimming costume.def: Clothing you wear to go swimming, covering your chest and legs or bottom.
swimming costume.ex: She bought a new swimming costume for her beach holiday.

swimming pool.label: CEFR A1
swimming pool.def: A large container filled with water for swimming and playing
swimming pool.ex: We go to the swimming pool every Saturday to swim and have fun.

swimsuit.label: CEFR A2
swimsuit.def: Clothing you wear for swimming, covering your chest and legs.
swimsuit.ex: She bought a new blue swimsuit for her beach holiday.

swing.label: CEFR B2
swing.def: To move back and forth in a smooth, continuous motion, or to turn sharply around a point.
swing.ex: The golfer swung her club with great force, sending the ball soaring down the fairway.

switch.label: CEFR B1
switch.def: To change from one thing to another, or to turn something on or off
switch.ex: I switched from drinking coffee to tea because it helps me sleep better.

swollen.label: CEFR B1
swollen.def: Enlarged or puffed up because of illness, injury, or fluid buildup.
swollen.ex: After the bee sting, her arm became swollen and painful.

sword.label: CEFR B1
sword.def: A long metal weapon with a sharp blade, used especially in fighting or historical combat.
sword.ex: The knight drew his sword and prepared to defend the castle from the invaders.

syllable.label: CEFR B2
syllable.def: A unit of sound in a word, typically containing one vowel sound.
syllable.ex: The word 'education' has four syllables: ed-u-ca-tion.

symbolic.label: CEFR B2
symbolic.def: Representing something else; having a meaning beyond its literal appearance or value.
symbolic.ex: The dove is symbolic of peace in many cultures around the world.

sympathetic.label: CEFR B2
sympathetic.def: Showing understanding and concern for someone's difficult feelings or situation
sympathetic.ex: The doctor was sympathetic to her concerns about the side effects of the medication.

sympathize/sympathise.label: CEFR B2
sympathize/sympathise.def: To understand and share someone's feelings or problems, showing emotional support and concern.
sympathize/sympathise.ex: I sympathise with your frustration about the delayed project, as I've experienced similar setbacks myself.

sympathy.label: CEFR B1
sympathy.def: Understanding and sharing someone's feelings or problems; feeling sorry for them.
sympathy.ex: I felt great sympathy for my friend when her dog died last month.

symphony.label: CEFR A2
symphony.def: A long piece of music for a large orchestra, usually with multiple parts.
symphony.ex: We listened to a beautiful symphony by Mozart at the concert hall last night.

symptom.label: CEFR B1
symptom.def: A physical or mental sign that shows you have an illness or health problem.
symptom.ex: If you have a cough and fever, these symptoms suggest you might have a cold.

syndrome.label: CEFR B1
syndrome.def: A group of symptoms or problems that happen together and show a medical condition.
syndrome.ex: After the long flight, he developed travel syndrome with back pain, headaches, and fatigue.

synonym.label: CEFR B2
synonym.def: A word or phrase with the same or nearly the same meaning as another word.
synonym.ex: The words 'happy' and 'joyful' are synonyms, so you can use them interchangeably in many contexts.

system.label: CEFR A2
system.def: A set of connected things or processes that work together to do something
system.ex: The public transport system in London includes buses, trains, and the Underground.

systematic.label: CEFR B1
systematic.def: Done carefully and in a planned, organized way, not random or careless
systematic.ex: The teacher took a systematic approach to teaching grammar, covering one rule each week.

table.label: CEFR A1
table.def: A piece of furniture with a flat top and legs, used for eating or working
table.ex: We eat breakfast at the table every morning.

table tennis.label: CEFR A2
table tennis.def: A sport where two or four players hit a small ball back and forth across a net on a table using small rackets.
table tennis.ex: My brother plays table tennis every weekend at the sports club.

tablecloth.label: CEFR B2
tablecloth.def: A piece of fabric spread over a table to protect or decorate its surface.
tablecloth.ex: She removed the white linen tablecloth before wiping down the dining table.

tablespoon.label: CEFR A2
tablespoon.def: A large spoon used for eating or measuring ingredients when cooking
tablespoon.ex: Add three tablespoons of sugar to the mixture.

tablet.label: CEFR B1
tablet.def: A small, flat computer that you use by touching the screen with your fingers
tablet.ex: She uses her tablet to read e-books and check emails while travelling.

tabloid.label: CEFR B2
tabloid.def: A newspaper with small pages, large headlines, and sensational stories about celebrities and scandals.
tabloid.ex: The tabloid printed exaggerated headlines about the actor's private life without verifying the facts.

tack.label: CEFR B2
tack.def: To change direction or approach, especially when the original method is not working well.
tack.ex: After the marketing campaign failed to reach younger audiences, the company decided to tack towards social media advertising instead.

tackle.label: CEFR B2
tackle.def: To try to deal with or solve a difficult problem or task in a determined way.
tackle.ex: The government is tackling climate change by investing in renewable energy sources.

tactile.label: CEFR B2
tactile.def: Related to the sense of touch; able to be felt or perceived by touching.
tactile.ex: The artist used various textured materials to create a tactile experience that engaged the viewer's sense of touch.

tag.label: CEFR B2
