tag.def: A label or marker attached to something to identify, classify, or provide information about it.
tag.ex: The price tag on the jacket showed it had been reduced from £50 to £35.

tail.label: CEFR B2
tail.def: The rear or end part of an animal's body, extending from the spine.
tail.ex: The cat swished its tail back and forth as it watched the birds outside the window.

take.label: CEFR A1
take.def: To get something and put it in your hand or carry it with you.
take.ex: Can you take this book to your room?

takeaway.label: CEFR B1
takeaway.def: A restaurant meal that you buy and eat somewhere else, not at the restaurant.
takeaway.ex: After work, we ordered a Chinese takeaway and ate it at home while watching a film.

takeoff/take-off.label: CEFR B1
takeoff/take-off.def: The moment when an aircraft leaves the ground and starts flying into the air.
takeoff/take-off.ex: The takeoff was delayed by two hours due to bad weather conditions.

tale.label: CEFR B1
tale.def: A story, often fictional or from the past, told to entertain or teach.
tale.ex: She read her daughter a bedtime tale about a magical forest filled with friendly creatures.

talent.label: CEFR A2
talent.def: A natural ability or skill that you are good at without much practice.
talent.ex: She has a real talent for playing the piano.

talented.label: CEFR B1
talented.def: Having a natural skill or ability to do something very well.
talented.ex: She is a talented musician who started playing piano when she was only five years old.

talk.label: CEFR A1
talk.def: To say words; to speak to someone.
talk.ex: I talk to my friends every day at school.

talkative.label: CEFR B1
talkative.def: Someone who talks a lot and enjoys speaking with other people.
talkative.ex: My talkative colleague always has interesting stories to share during lunch breaks.

tall.label: CEFR A1
tall.def: Having great height; measuring a long distance from bottom to top.
tall.ex: My father is very tall and plays basketball.

tame.label: CEFR B2
tame.def: Not wild; domesticated and accustomed to human contact or control.
tame.ex: The zoo's tame lions were comfortable being approached by visitors during the educational demonstration.

tan.label: CEFR B2
tan.def: Brown colour produced on skin after exposure to the sun's ultraviolet rays
tan.ex: After two weeks in Spain, she had developed a deep tan that made her look healthier.

tank.label: CEFR B1
tank.def: A large container that holds liquid or gas, often used for storage or transport.
tank.ex: The car's fuel tank was nearly empty, so we stopped at the petrol station.

tanned.label: CEFR B2
tanned.def: Having darker skin from exposure to the sun; having a brown colour from sunlight.
tanned.ex: After two weeks on the beach, she returned to work with beautifully tanned skin.

tap.label: CEFR B2
tap.def: To strike something lightly and quickly with your finger or a small object.
tap.ex: She tapped on the window to get his attention without disturbing the class.

tape.label: CEFR B1
tape.def: To record sound or video using a device, or to fasten something with adhesive tape.
tape.ex: I taped the conversation so I could listen to it again later.

taste.label: CEFR B1
taste.def: To perceive flavour by putting food or drink in your mouth
taste.ex: I tasted the soup and realised it needed more salt.

tasteless.label: CEFR B1
tasteless.def: Lacking flavour; having no interesting or pleasant taste.
tasteless.ex: The hospital food was so tasteless that I could barely finish my meal.

tasty.label: CEFR B1
tasty.def: Having a pleasant, delicious flavour that you enjoy eating or drinking.
tasty.ex: The restaurant serves tasty homemade pasta that customers love.

tatter.label: CEFR B2
tatter.def: A small piece of cloth that is torn and hanging loosely from a garment.
tatter.ex: The old flag hung in tatters after decades of harsh weather exposure.

tattoo.label: CEFR B2
tattoo.def: A permanent design or image made by injecting ink under the skin with a needle.
tattoo.ex: She decided to get a small tattoo of a bird on her shoulder to commemorate her travels.

taunt.label: CEFR B2
taunt.def: A remark intended to provoke anger or make someone feel ashamed or upset.
taunt.ex: The opposition's taunts about the team's poor performance only motivated them to play harder in the next match.

tax.label: CEFR B1
tax.def: Money that you must pay to the government from your income or purchases.
tax.ex: The price on the shelf doesn't include tax, so you'll pay a bit more at the checkout.

taxi.label: CEFR A1
taxi.def: A car with a driver that you pay to take you somewhere.
taxi.ex: I took a taxi to the airport this morning.

tea.label: CEFR A1
tea.def: A hot drink made by pouring hot water over dried leaves from a plant.
tea.ex: I drink tea every morning with breakfast.

teach.label: CEFR A1
teach.def: To give lessons or help someone learn something new.
teach.ex: My teacher teaches English at my school.

teacher.label: CEFR A1
teacher.def: A person who helps students learn subjects in a school or classroom.
teacher.ex: My teacher explains grammar rules every Monday morning.

teaching.label: CEFR B1
teaching.def: The job or activity of helping students learn new skills, knowledge, or subjects in a school or classroom.
teaching.ex: Her teaching experience of ten years has made her an excellent educator who understands how students learn.

team.label: CEFR A1
team.def: A group of people working together for a shared goal or purpose.
team.ex: Our football team won the match yesterday.

teammate.label: CEFR B1
teammate.def: A person who works or plays with you in the same team or group.
teammate.ex: My teammate scored the winning goal in yesterday's football match.

teamwork.label: CEFR B1
teamwork.def: Working together with other people to achieve a shared goal or task.
teamwork.ex: Good teamwork between the marketing and sales departments helped the company increase its profits.

tear.label: CEFR B1
tear.def: To pull or rip something apart, usually by force or accident.
tear.ex: I accidentally tore my shirt when I caught it on the fence.

tease.label: CEFR B2
tease.def: To make playful jokes about someone or annoy them in a lighthearted way.
tease.ex: My older brother likes to tease me about my terrible singing, but I know he means no harm.

teaspoon.label: CEFR B2
teaspoon.def: A small spoon used for stirring drinks or measuring ingredients in cooking and baking.
teaspoon.ex: Add one teaspoon of sugar to your morning coffee.

technically.label: CEFR B2
technically.def: According to the strict facts or rules, even if not what was meant or intended
technically.ex: Technically, she wasn't late because the meeting didn't start until 9 a.m., though she arrived just as it began.

technological.label: CEFR B1
technological.def: Related to machines, computers, or scientific methods and tools.
technological.ex: Technological advances have made smartphones more powerful and affordable for everyone.

teen.label: CEFR B1
teen.def: A person aged between 13 and 19 years old.
teen.ex: Most teens in the UK start secondary school at age 11 and finish their GCSEs at 16.

teenage.label: CEFR A2
teenage.def: Relating to young people aged 13 to 19 years old.
teenage.ex: My teenage son spends a lot of time playing video games with his friends.

teenager.label: CEFR A2
teenager.def: A young person between thirteen and nineteen years old.
teenager.ex: My teenager son enjoys playing football with his friends after school.

telecommunications.label: CEFR B1
telecommunications.def: Communication over long distances using technology like phones, internet, or television.
telecommunications.ex: The telecommunications company improved its internet service across rural areas.

telegram.label: CEFR B1
telegram.def: A short written message sent electronically over long distances, especially historically before modern communication
telegram.ex: She received a telegram from her brother announcing that he had arrived safely in Paris.

telephone.label: CEFR A2
telephone.def: To call someone using a phone to talk to them
telephone.ex: I telephoned my mother yesterday to ask how she was feeling.

telescope.label: CEFR B2
telescope.def: An optical instrument that magnifies distant objects, allowing observation of stars and planets.
telescope.ex: Astronomers used a powerful telescope to discover a new exoplanet orbiting a distant star.

television.label: CEFR A1
television.def: A machine that shows pictures and sound for entertainment and news.
television.ex: I watch television every evening after dinner.

tell.label: CEFR A1
tell.def: To say something to someone; to give information or facts to someone.
tell.ex: Can you tell me your name?

teller.label: CEFR B1
teller.def: A person who works in a bank and handles money for customers
teller.ex: The bank teller counted the cash and helped me deposit my paycheck.

temper.label: CEFR B1
temper.def: A person's tendency to become angry or lose emotional control in certain situations.
temper.ex: He has a quick temper and often shouts when things go wrong at work.

temperate.label: CEFR B2
temperate.def: Mild in climate; not extremely hot or cold; also, showing restraint or moderation in behaviour
temperate.ex: The temperate regions of Europe have moderate rainfall and pleasant summers that support diverse agriculture.

temperature.label: CEFR A2
temperature.def: How hot or cold something is, usually measured in degrees.
temperature.ex: The temperature outside is 25 degrees Celsius today.

temple.label: CEFR A1
temple.def: A building where people go to pray or worship in some religions.
temple.ex: We visited a beautiful temple with golden decorations.

temporarily.label: CEFR B2
temporarily.def: For a limited time; not permanently or for a long period
temporarily.ex: The museum closed temporarily for renovations and will reopen in three months.

tempt.label: CEFR B1
tempt.def: To try to make someone want to do something wrong or unwise.
tempt.ex: The smell of fresh cake was tempting me to eat a slice before dinner.

temptation.label: CEFR B2
temptation.def: A strong desire to do something you know you probably shouldn't do.
temptation.ex: Despite being on a diet, she found the chocolate cake an irresistible temptation at the party.

tend.label: CEFR B1
tend.def: To care for someone or something by doing what they need; to look after regularly.
tend.ex: She tends her garden every weekend, watering the plants and removing weeds.

tendency.label: CEFR B1
tendency.def: A natural inclination or likelihood to do or think something regularly.
tendency.ex: She has a tendency to arrive late for meetings, even though she tries hard to be punctual.

tender.label: CEFR B2
tender.def: Soft and easy to cut or chew; or showing gentle feelings and affection
tender.ex: The tender meat fell apart in my mouth after hours of slow cooking.

tenderly.label: CEFR B2
tenderly.def: In a gentle, caring, and delicate manner; with softness and affection.
tenderly.ex: She tenderly brushed the hair away from her daughter's forehead as she slept.

tenderness.label: CEFR B2
tenderness.def: A quality of being soft, gentle, and easily hurt or damaged; also, affectionate concern for someone.
tenderness.ex: The nurse treated the patient's wound with great tenderness and care.

tennis.label: CEFR A1
tennis.def: A sport where two or four players hit a ball with rackets over a net on a court.
tennis.ex: My friends and I play tennis on Saturdays at the park.

tense.label: CEFR B1
tense.def: A verb form that shows when an action happens: past, present, or future.
tense.ex: In English, the present tense describes actions that happen now or regularly.

tension.label: CEFR B1
tension.def: A feeling of worry, stress, or nervousness about something that might happen.
tension.ex: There was tension in the room when the exam results were announced.

tent.label: CEFR B1
tent.def: A portable shelter made of fabric stretched over a framework, used for camping.
tent.ex: We pitched our tent near the lake before exploring the hiking trails.

term.label: CEFR B1
term.def: A fixed period of time, especially one when school or court meets regularly.
term.ex: The autumn term at university starts in September and ends before Christmas.

terminal.label: CEFR B1
terminal.def: A building where buses, trains, or aeroplanes arrive and depart.
terminal.ex: We met at the bus terminal before taking our coach to the airport.

terrace.label: CEFR B2
terrace.def: A raised flat platform or paved area adjoining a building, or a row of houses built in a connected line.
terrace.ex: They sat on the terrace overlooking the garden, enjoying their morning coffee as the sun rose over the fields.

terrible.label: CEFR A1
terrible.def: Very bad or unpleasant; causing fear or worry.
terrible.ex: The weather was terrible, so we stayed inside and played games.

terribly.label: CEFR B1
terribly.def: Very much; in a way that is very bad or severe.
terribly.ex: The weather was terribly cold yesterday, so we stayed inside all day.

terrific.label: CEFR B1
terrific.def: Very good or excellent; impressive and enjoyable.
terrific.ex: We had a terrific time at the concert last night.

terrified.label: CEFR B1
terrified.def: Extremely frightened or afraid of something
terrified.ex: She was terrified when she saw the large spider in her bedroom.

terrify.label: CEFR A2
terrify.def: To make someone feel very afraid or frightened.
terrify.ex: The loud thunder terrified the small children during the storm.

terrifying.label: CEFR B2
terrifying.def: Extremely frightening or causing intense fear and anxiety.
terrifying.ex: The patient found the prospect of major surgery terrifying, though the surgeon assured her it was a routine procedure.

territory.label: CEFR B2
territory.def: An area of land under the control of a country, government, or organisation.
territory.ex: The disputed territory between the two nations remained heavily guarded for decades.

terror.label: CEFR B1
terror.def: Extreme fear or a violent act intended to frighten people into submission.
terror.ex: The sudden explosion filled the residents with terror, and many fled their homes immediately.

terrorism.label: CEFR B1
terrorism.def: Violence and fear used by groups to achieve political or religious goals.
terrorism.ex: The government has increased security measures to prevent terrorism in major cities.

terrorist.label: CEFR B1
terrorist.def: A person who uses violence and fear to achieve political or religious goals.
terrorist.ex: The police arrested a suspected terrorist who was planning an attack on the city.

test.label: CEFR B2
test.def: To examine or check if something works correctly or meets required standards.
test.ex: The engineers tested the new aircraft for safety before it was approved for commercial flights.

testify.label: CEFR B2
testify.def: To give evidence or make a formal statement, usually in court or before an official body.
testify.ex: The witness was asked to testify about what she had seen on the night of the incident.

testimony.label: CEFR B2
testimony.def: A formal statement or evidence given by a witness in court under oath.
testimony.ex: The victim's testimony was crucial in convincing the jury of the defendant's guilt.

text message.label: CEFR A2
text message.def: A short written message sent using a mobile phone
text message.ex: She sent me a text message to say she would be late for dinner.

textbook.label: CEFR A2
textbook.def: A book used in school that teaches about a subject like maths or history.
textbook.ex: I need to read chapter 3 in my English textbook for homework tomorrow.

thank.label: CEFR A1
thank.def: To tell someone you are grateful for what they did or gave you.
thank.ex: I thanked my mum for making dinner.

thankful.label: CEFR B1
thankful.def: Feeling or showing gratitude and appreciation for something good.
thankful.ex: I was thankful for my friend's help when I was going through a difficult time.

thanks.label: CEFR A1
thanks.def: Words you say to show gratitude or appreciation to someone.
thanks.ex: She gave him thanks for helping her carry the boxes.

that.label: CEFR B2
that.def: Used to indicate degree or extent, or to emphasise a statement or emotion.
that.ex: The weather was that bad we decided to cancel the outdoor event.

theater/theatre.label: CEFR A1
theater/theatre.def: A building where people go to watch plays, musicals, or films on a large screen.
theater/theatre.ex: We went to the theatre last night to see a wonderful play.

theft.label: CEFR B1
theft.def: The crime of taking something that belongs to someone else without permission.
theft.ex: Police arrested three suspects in connection with the theft of jewellery from the city centre store.

theme.label: CEFR B2
theme.def: The main subject or idea that a piece of writing, film, or art expresses or explores
theme.ex: The theme of isolation appears throughout the novel, with the protagonist struggling to connect with others.

then.label: CEFR A1
then.def: At that time in the past or future.
then.ex: First we ate breakfast, then we went to school.

theorist.label: CEFR B2
theorist.def: A person who develops or studies ideas and principles about how something works, rather than practical applications.
theorist.ex: The political theorist proposed a new framework for understanding how governments gain public trust.

therapist.label: CEFR B2
therapist.def: A trained professional who helps people manage mental, emotional, or physical health problems through counselling or treatment.
therapist.ex: After her divorce, Sarah started seeing a therapist to work through her feelings and develop coping strategies.

therapy.label: CEFR B2
therapy.def: Treatment using psychological methods or physical exercises to improve health and wellbeing.
therapy.ex: After her injury, she attended physiotherapy sessions twice weekly to regain strength in her leg.

there.label: CEFR A1
there.ex: My friend lives over there by the park.

thereafter.label: CEFR B2
thereafter.def: After that point in time; from then onwards
thereafter.ex: The company was founded in 1995, and thereafter expanded rapidly into European markets.

therefore.label: CEFR A2
therefore.def: For this reason; as a result of something that was just said
therefore.ex: She studied hard for the exam, therefore she got a good mark.

thermometer.label: CEFR B2
thermometer.def: An instrument that measures temperature by showing how hot or cold something is.
thermometer.ex: The doctor used a digital thermometer to check whether the patient had a fever.

thesis.label: CEFR B2
thesis.def: A long piece of research writing required for a university degree, presenting original findings or arguments.
thesis.ex: She spent two years researching and writing her thesis on climate change in developing countries.

thick.label: CEFR A1
thick.def: Having a large distance between two surfaces; not thin.
thick.ex: He wore a thick coat because it was very cold outside.

thickly.label: CEFR B2
thickly.def: In a dense or concentrated manner; with little space between parts or layers.
thickly.ex: The forest was thickly covered with snow, making it difficult to see the path ahead.

thickness.label: CEFR B2
thickness.def: The distance between two surfaces of an object; how thick something is
thickness.ex: The thickness of the concrete wall was measured at approximately 30 centimetres to ensure structural integrity.

thief.label: CEFR A2
thief.def: A person who takes things that do not belong to them without permission.
thief.ex: The police caught the thief who stole money from the shop yesterday.

thigh.label: CEFR B2
thigh.def: The upper part of your leg between your hip and knee.
thigh.ex: She pulled a muscle in her thigh while training for the marathon.

thin.label: CEFR A1
thin.def: Not thick; having little distance from one side to the other
thin.ex: She wore a thin scarf around her neck.

thing.label: CEFR A1
thing.def: An object; something you can see, touch, or use.
thing.ex: I put my things in my bag.

think.label: CEFR A1
think.def: To use your mind to consider or decide something
think.ex: I think English is difficult, but I like learning it.

thinker.label: CEFR B2
thinker.def: A person who spends time considering problems deeply and developing original or analytical ideas.
thinker.ex: She is a strategic thinker who often comes up with innovative solutions during team meetings.

third.label: CEFR A2
third.def: Coming after two others in order or position.
third.ex: My third cup of coffee this morning was too strong.

third person.label: CEFR B2
third person.def: A grammatical perspective where the speaker refers to someone or something other than themselves or their listener.
third person.ex: The author uses third person narration throughout the novel, allowing readers to see events through an external observer's viewpoint.

thirdly.label: CEFR B2
thirdly.def: Used to introduce the third point in a sequence of ideas or arguments.
thirdly.ex: The report highlighted three main issues: firstly, rising costs; secondly, staff shortages; and thirdly, outdated equipment.

thirst.label: CEFR B1
thirst.def: To have a strong desire or need for something, usually a drink or experience
thirst.ex: After years of studying, she thirsted for adventure and decided to travel abroad.

thirsty.label: CEFR A2
thirsty.def: Wanting or needing a drink of water or another liquid
thirsty.ex: After playing football in the sun, the children were very thirsty.

thorough.label: CEFR B1
thorough.def: Complete and careful, including all details and nothing missed.
thorough.ex: The doctor gave him a thorough examination before the surgery.

thoroughly.label: CEFR B2
thoroughly.def: In a complete and careful way, paying attention to every detail or aspect.
thoroughly.ex: The doctor examined the patient thoroughly before making a diagnosis.

though.label: CEFR B2
though.def: Despite the fact that something is true; however, or yet.
though.ex: The film received mixed reviews; though, most critics praised the lead actor's performance.

thought.label: CEFR A2
thought.def: An idea or opinion that comes into your mind
thought.ex: She had a sudden thought about what to cook for dinner.

thoughtful.label: CEFR B2
thoughtful.def: Showing careful consideration of other people's feelings and needs; mentally reflective.
thoughtful.ex: She gave a thoughtful response to the challenging question, pausing to consider multiple perspectives before speaking.

thoughtless.label: CEFR B2
thoughtless.def: Not showing care or consideration for other people's feelings or needs.
thoughtless.ex: His thoughtless comment about her appearance hurt her deeply, even though he didn't mean to be unkind.

thread.label: CEFR B2
thread.def: A thin strand of material like cotton or silk used for sewing or weaving.
thread.ex: She carefully chose a matching thread to repair the tear in her favourite dress.

threat.label: CEFR B1
threat.def: A statement saying you will hurt or harm someone if they do not do what you want
threat.ex: The government took the security threat seriously and increased airport security measures.

threaten.label: CEFR B2
threaten.def: To indicate you will cause harm or trouble unless someone does what you want.
threaten.ex: The company threatened to relocate overseas if the government did not reduce taxes.

threatening.label: CEFR B1
threatening.def: Making someone feel afraid or worried that something bad might happen
threatening.ex: The threatening storm clouds made us decide to cancel the outdoor picnic.

thrill.label: CEFR B2
thrill.def: A sudden feeling of excitement and pleasure, often mixed with slight fear or nervousness.
thrill.ex: She felt a thrill of anticipation as the roller coaster climbed to its highest point.

thrilled.label: CEFR B2
thrilled.def: Extremely happy and excited about something.
thrilled.ex: She was thrilled to receive the job offer after months of searching.

thriller.label: CEFR B1
thriller.def: A book, film, or play that is designed to excite and frighten the audience with suspense and danger
thriller.ex: She couldn't put down the thriller—the unexpected twist at the end completely surprised her.

thrilling.label: CEFR B2
thrilling.def: Extremely exciting and causing intense feelings of excitement or fear
thrilling.ex: The thrilling conclusion of the match left the spectators on the edge of their seats as the home team scored in the final seconds.

throat.label: CEFR B2
throat.def: The tube in your neck that carries food to your stomach and air to your lungs.
throat.ex: She had a sore throat after shouting at the football match.

through.label: CEFR B1
through.def: From one side or end of something to the other side or end
through.ex: We walked through the forest to reach the village on the other side.

throw.label: CEFR A2
throw.def: To move something through the air using your hand and arm with force
throw.ex: The boy threw the ball to his friend in the garden.

thumb.label: CEFR B1
thumb.def: The shortest, thickest finger on your hand, located on the side nearest your wrist.
thumb.ex: She held the paintbrush between her thumb and forefinger to paint the small details.

thump.label: CEFR B1
thump.def: To hit something hard with your fist or a heavy object, making a loud sound.
thump.ex: He thumped the table angrily when he heard the bad news.

thunder.label: CEFR B1
thunder.def: The loud sound that follows lightning during a storm.
thunder.ex: The thunder was so loud that it shook the windows of the house during the news broadcast about the severe weather.

thunderous.label: CEFR B1
thunderous.def: Very loud and making a deep, powerful sound like thunder
thunderous.ex: The crowd gave the winning team a thunderous round of applause that shook the stadium.

thunderstorm.label: CEFR A2
thunderstorm.def: A storm with heavy rain, thunder, lightning, and strong wind.
thunderstorm.ex: The thunderstorm last night was so loud that it woke up the whole neighbourhood.

thursday.label: CEFR A1
thursday.def: The fifth day of the week, between Wednesday and Friday
thursday.ex: I have football practice on Thursday afternoon.

thus.label: CEFR B1
thus.def: In this way or for this reason; as a result of what was just mentioned.
thus.ex: The company increased prices, and thus many customers decided to shop elsewhere.

tick.label: CEFR B1
tick.def: To make a small clicking sound repeatedly, or to mark something with a check mark.
tick.ex: The clock ticked loudly as we waited for the exam to finish.

ticket.label: CEFR A1
ticket.def: A piece of paper that shows you have paid to go somewhere or do something.
ticket.ex: I bought a ticket to watch the football match on Saturday.

tickle.label: CEFR B2
tickle.def: To touch someone lightly causing them to laugh or twitch involuntarily.
tickle.ex: She tickled her nephew under his arms until he couldn't stop giggling.

tide.label: CEFR B1
tide.def: A strong tendency or direction of opinion, feeling, or events that affects many people.
tide.ex: The newspaper reported that public opinion is shifting as a new tide of support emerges for environmental policies.

tidy.label: CEFR A2
tidy.def: To make a place clean and organized by putting things in order.
tidy.ex: I need to tidy my bedroom before my friends come over.

tie.label: CEFR B1
tie.def: To fasten or connect something with a knot or string
tie.ex: I always tie my shoelaces before I go for a run.

tiger.label: CEFR A1
tiger.def: A large wild cat with orange fur and black stripes that lives in Asia.
tiger.ex: The tiger is a very dangerous animal.

tight.label: CEFR B2
tight.def: In a way that is firmly fixed or held with little or no space to move.
tight.ex: She held the rope tight to prevent it from slipping through her hands.

tighten.label: CEFR B1
tighten.def: To make something firmer, stronger, or more firmly fastened or controlled.
tighten.ex: The government decided to tighten regulations on food safety after the scandal.

tightly.label: CEFR B1
tightly.def: In a way that is firmly fixed or held with little movement or space.
tightly.ex: She gripped the rope tightly as she climbed down the steep cliff.

tightrope.label: CEFR B2
tightrope.def: A rope stretched high above the ground on which acrobats balance and walk as entertainment.
tightrope.ex: The circus performer walked carefully across the tightrope without using a safety net, demonstrating years of training and skill.

tights.label: CEFR A2
tights.def: Close-fitting covering for legs and feet, usually worn by women and children.
tights.ex: She wore black tights with her dress on the cold winter day.

tile.label: CEFR B1
tile.def: A thin, flat square or rectangular piece of material used to cover floors, walls, or roofs.
tile.ex: The bathroom floor was covered with blue ceramic tiles.

time.label: CEFR B2
time.def: To measure how long something takes or to choose when something happens
time.ex: The coach timed the runners to see who completed the race fastest.

timeless.label: CEFR B2
timeless.def: Not affected by the passage of time; remaining relevant or attractive regardless of when it was created
timeless.ex: The timeless design of that building makes it beautiful even after a hundred years.

timeliness.label: CEFR B2
timeliness.def: The quality of doing or happening at exactly the right moment when needed.
timeliness.ex: The timeliness of the government's response to the crisis was praised by international observers.

timely.label: CEFR B1
timely.def: Happening at the right time; arriving or done when needed or expected
timely.ex: The manager appreciated the timely report, which helped the team make important decisions before the deadline.

timetable.label: CEFR A2
timetable.def: A schedule showing when events or classes happen on specific days and times.
timetable.ex: My school timetable shows that I have English at 9am on Monday and Friday.

timing.label: CEFR B2
timing.def: The choice of when something happens or should happen for best effect.
timing.ex: The timing of the government's announcement surprised many economists and affected market reactions.

tin.label: CEFR B1
tin.def: A soft, silvery metal used to make cans and coat other metals to prevent rust.
tin.ex: My grandmother stored biscuits in an old tin box on the kitchen shelf.

tiny.label: CEFR B1
tiny.ex: She lives in a tiny flat in the city centre with barely enough room for her bed and desk.

tip.label: CEFR B2
tip.def: To lean or cause something to move into a tilted or overturned position.
tip.ex: The waiter tipped the bottle carefully to pour the wine without spilling.

tiptoe.label: CEFR B2
tiptoe.def: To walk quietly on the tips of your toes to avoid making noise or being noticed.
tiptoe.ex: She tiptoed across the creaky floorboards so she wouldn't wake her sleeping roommate.

tire.label: CEFR B1
tire.def: To become very tired or to make someone very tired.
tire.ex: After working ten hours, I started to tire and needed a break.

tired.label: CEFR A1
tired.def: Needing sleep or rest because you are very physically or mentally exhausted.
tired.ex: After playing football, I was very tired and went to bed early.

tiredness.label: CEFR B2
tiredness.def: The physical or mental state of lacking energy and needing rest
tiredness.ex: After working twelve-hour shifts all week, she couldn't shake the overwhelming tiredness that made even simple tasks feel exhausting.

tiresome.label: CEFR B2
tiresome.def: Causing annoyance or boredom; tedious or repetitively dull.
tiresome.ex: After listening to the same tiresome complaints for weeks, she decided to stop answering his calls.

tiring.label: CEFR B1
tiring.def: Making you feel physically or mentally exhausted and needing rest
tiring.ex: After a long day of meetings and phone calls, Sarah found the work incredibly tiring.

tissue.label: CEFR B1
tissue.def: Thin, soft paper used for cleaning your nose or face
tissue.ex: She grabbed a tissue from the box when she started sneezing.

title.label: CEFR A2
title.def: The name of a book, film, song, or other work
title.ex: The title of my favourite book is 'Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone'.

toast.label: CEFR A2
toast.def: Bread that has been heated until brown and crispy.
toast.ex: I like to eat toast with butter and jam for breakfast.

tobacco.label: CEFR B1
tobacco.def: A plant whose dried leaves are smoked in cigarettes, cigars, or pipes.
tobacco.ex: Many countries have increased taxes on tobacco products to discourage smoking.

today.label: CEFR A1
today.def: This day; the present day that is happening right now.
today.ex: I am happy today because I play with my friends.

toe.label: CEFR A2
toe.def: One of five small body parts at the end of your foot.
toe.ex: I stubbed my toe on the chair leg this morning.

toenail.label: CEFR B2
toenail.def: The hard, protective covering on the upper surface of the end of your toe.
toenail.ex: She noticed her toenail had turned slightly yellow and decided to visit a podiatrist.

together.label: CEFR A1
together.def: with another person or people at the same time in one place
together.ex: My friends and I went to the park together.

toil.label: CEFR B2
toil.def: To work extremely hard for a long time, especially at a difficult or tiring task.
toil.ex: The farmers toiled in the fields from dawn until dusk during the harvest season.

toilet.label: CEFR A1
toilet.def: A porcelain bowl connected to pipes for flushing away waste from your body
toilet.ex: I need to use the toilet before we go to school.

tolerant.label: CEFR B2
tolerant.def: Able to accept and respect beliefs or practices different from your own
tolerant.ex: A tolerant society allows people of different religions and cultures to live together peacefully.

tolerate.label: CEFR B2
tolerate.def: To accept or allow something you dislike or disagree with to exist or continue.
tolerate.ex: Many employers tolerate flexible working arrangements to keep talented staff satisfied.

toll.label: CEFR B2
toll.def: A fee paid to use a road, bridge, or motorway, typically collected at a gate or booth.
toll.ex: Drivers on the motorway must pay a toll to cross the bridge during rush hour.

tomato.label: CEFR A1
tomato.def: A round red vegetable used in cooking and salads.
tomato.ex: I like to eat tomatoes in my salad for lunch.

tomb.label: CEFR B2
tomb.def: A stone or underground structure built to hold the body of a dead person, often of historical or archaeological importance.
tomb.ex: Archaeologists discovered ancient artefacts inside the pharaoh's tomb buried beneath the desert sands.

tomorrow.label: CEFR A1
tomorrow.ex: I will see you tomorrow at school.

ton.label: CEFR B2
ton.def: A unit of weight equal to 1,000 kilograms, or 2,240 pounds in British measurement.
ton.ex: The cargo ship was carrying over 500 tons of cargo when it departed from the port.

tone.label: CEFR A2
tone.def: The quality of sound, especially whether it is high or low, loud or soft.
tone.ex: She spoke in a gentle tone of voice when she told him the sad news.

tongue.label: CEFR B1
tongue.def: The flexible muscle in your mouth used for tasting, swallowing, and speaking.
tongue.ex: She stuck her tongue out playfully at her younger brother.

tonight.label: CEFR A2
tonight.def: This evening and the night that follows; the period of time from now until morning.
tonight.ex: I'm going to the cinema tonight with my friends.

too.label: CEFR A1
too.def: More than enough or more than you want; also or as well.
too.ex: This coffee is too hot to drink right now.

tool.label: CEFR A1
tool.def: An object you hold and use to do a job or task.
tool.ex: A hammer is a useful tool for putting nails in the wall.

tooth.label: CEFR A1
tooth.def: One of the hard white parts in your mouth that you use to bite and chew food.
tooth.ex: I brush my teeth every morning and before I go to bed.

toothache.label: CEFR A2
toothache.def: Pain in or around a tooth.
toothache.ex: I have a terrible toothache and need to see the dentist tomorrow.

toothbrush.label: CEFR A2
toothbrush.def: A small brush with a handle used to clean your teeth
toothbrush.ex: I brush my teeth with my toothbrush every morning and before bed.

toothpaste.label: CEFR B1
toothpaste.def: A thick substance you put on your toothbrush to clean your teeth.
toothpaste.ex: I brush my teeth with toothpaste every morning and before bed.

top.label: CEFR A1
top.def: The highest point or surface of something.
top.ex: I can see the mountain top from my window.

torch.label: CEFR B2
torch.def: A portable light source powered by batteries or burning fuel, typically held by hand.
torch.ex: The hikers carried torches to navigate through the dark forest path.

tornado.label: CEFR B1
tornado.def: A violent spinning column of air that moves across land and destroys buildings.
tornado.ex: The tornado destroyed several homes in the town yesterday evening.

tortoiseshell.label: CEFR B2
tortoiseshell.def: The hard, mottled shell of a sea turtle, historically used to make jewellery and decorative items.
tortoiseshell.ex: The antique comb was made from tortoiseshell and had intricate patterns carved into its surface.

torture.label: CEFR B2
torture.def: To inflict severe pain on someone as punishment or to extract information.
torture.ex: Human rights organisations condemned the government for torturing political prisoners during the conflict.

toss.label: CEFR B1
toss.def: To throw something lightly or carelessly through the air.
toss.ex: She tossed the ball to her friend across the garden.

total.label: CEFR B1
total.def: The complete amount when you add everything together.
total.ex: The total of all our expenses this month was £2,500.

totally.label: CEFR B1
totally.def: Completely; to the greatest possible degree.
totally.ex: I totally agree with your idea about the school project.

touch.label: CEFR B1
touch.def: To put your hand or finger on something to feel it or connect with it.
touch.ex: She touched his shoulder gently to get his attention in the crowded room.

tough.label: CEFR B2
tough.def: Difficult to deal with or endure; requiring considerable strength or determination
tough.ex: The interview panel asked some tough questions that really tested the candidates' knowledge and composure.

tour.label: CEFR B1
tour.def: To visit a place and see the important or interesting things there, usually with a guide.
tour.ex: We toured the ancient ruins with a local historian who explained the history of each building.

tour guide.label: CEFR A2
tour guide.def: A person who shows visitors around interesting places and explains what they see
tour guide.ex: Our tour guide told us interesting stories about the ancient castle as we walked through it.

tourism.label: CEFR B1
tourism.def: The business of providing travel, accommodation, and activities for visitors to a place.
tourism.ex: The tourism industry in Spain employs thousands of people and generates significant income for the country.

tourist.label: CEFR A2
tourist.def: A person who visits a place for holiday or leisure, usually for a short time.
tourist.ex: Many tourists visit the Big Ben when they come to London.

tournament.label: CEFR B1
tournament.def: A competition where many players or teams compete against each other to find a winner.
tournament.ex: She won the chess tournament after defeating ten different opponents.

towel.label: CEFR A1
towel.def: A piece of cloth used to dry your body or hands after water.
towel.ex: I used a towel to dry myself after my shower.

tower.label: CEFR A1
tower.def: A very tall building or structure that is much higher than it is wide.
tower.ex: The clock tower in the town centre is over 200 years old.

town.label: CEFR A1
town.def: A place with many buildings, shops, and streets where many people live and work
town.ex: My town has a big market and a railway station.

toxic.label: CEFR B2
toxic.def: Poisonous or harmful to health; capable of causing serious damage or illness.
toxic.ex: The factory's toxic emissions were polluting the river and affecting wildlife downstream.

toy.label: CEFR A1
toy.def: A thing children play with for enjoyment and fun
toy.ex: My little sister loves playing with her toy cars.

trace.label: CEFR B2
trace.def: To find or discover something by following evidence or a sequence of clues.
trace.ex: The detective traced the stolen painting through several auctions before recovering it from a private collector.

track.label: CEFR B2
track.def: To follow or monitor the movement or progress of someone or something over time.
track.ex: The scientists tracked the migration patterns of Arctic terns across multiple continents using GPS technology.

tracksuit.label: CEFR B1
tracksuit.def: A loose, comfortable set of matching clothes worn for exercise or casual wear.
tracksuit.ex: She put on her blue tracksuit before going to the gym for her morning workout.

trade.label: CEFR B2
trade.def: To exchange goods or services with another person or country for something you want.
trade.ex: The company decided to trade its old equipment for newer machinery to improve efficiency.

trader.label: CEFR B2
trader.def: A person who buys and sells goods, securities, or currencies to make a profit.
trader.ex: The commodity trader spent the morning monitoring oil prices before deciding to sell her positions.

traditional.label: CEFR A2
traditional.def: Done or made in the way that has been done for a long time in a culture or family
traditional.ex: In my country, we eat traditional food at Christmas time.

traditionally.label: CEFR B1
traditionally.def: In the way that has been done for a long time in a culture or family.
traditionally.ex: Traditionally, British families eat roast turkey on Christmas Day.

traffic.label: CEFR A2
traffic.def: Cars, buses, and other vehicles moving on roads.
traffic.ex: There is a lot of traffic on the main street during rush hour.

traffic jam.label: CEFR B1
traffic jam.def: A long line of vehicles moving very slowly or not moving at all on a road.
traffic jam.ex: We were stuck in a terrible traffic jam on the motorway for over an hour this morning.

traffic light.label: CEFR A2
traffic light.def: A signal with red, yellow, and green lights that tells drivers and pedestrians when to stop or go.
traffic light.ex: We waited at the traffic light until it turned green so we could cross the street.

tragedy.label: CEFR B1
tragedy.def: A serious play or story in which the main character dies or suffers a terrible fate.
tragedy.ex: Shakespeare's Hamlet is one of the most famous tragedies in English literature.

tragic.label: CEFR B1
tragic.def: Very sad and causing suffering or death; deeply unfortunate.
tragic.ex: The tragic accident killed three people and shocked the whole community.

trail.label: CEFR B2
trail.def: To follow someone or something by tracking their movements or path.
trail.ex: The detective trailed the suspect through the city streets for several hours.

trailer.label: CEFR B2
trailer.def: A vehicle without its own engine, towed behind a truck or car to carry goods or equipment.
trailer.ex: The film studio released a two-minute trailer for the upcoming blockbuster on social media.

train.label: CEFR A2
train.def: To teach someone a skill or how to do something through practice and instruction.
train.ex: She trained as a nurse for three years before getting her first job.

trainee.label: CEFR B2
trainee.def: A person who is learning a job or skill through practical experience under supervision.
trainee.ex: After completing her degree, Sarah joined the company as a management trainee to gain real-world experience.

trainer.label: CEFR B2
trainer.def: A professional who teaches skills or provides instruction to individuals or groups in specific areas.
trainer.ex: The company hired a new trainer to develop the staff's digital marketing capabilities.

training.label: CEFR A2
training.def: Learning and practice to develop skills for a job or activity.
training.ex: She completed her training as a nurse and started working at the hospital.

trait.label: CEFR B2
trait.def: A characteristic or quality that a person or thing has, especially one that is noticeable or important.
trait.ex: Patience is a valuable trait for teachers who work with young children.

traitor.label: CEFR B2
traitor.def: A person who betrays their country, leader, or group by giving secrets or support to an enemy.
traitor.ex: The government condemned the diplomat as a traitor for leaking classified information to foreign intelligence agencies.

tram.label: CEFR A2
tram.def: A large electric vehicle that runs on metal tracks in streets and carries many passengers.
tram.ex: I took the tram to the city centre this morning instead of driving my car.

tramp.label: CEFR B2
tramp.def: A person without a permanent home or job who travels from place to place.
tramp.ex: The old tramp had been sleeping rough in the city park for several months before the shelter offered him a bed.

trample.label: CEFR B2
trample.def: The action of treading heavily and repeatedly on something, often causing damage or harm.
trample.ex: The crowd's trample of the flower beds during the festival left the garden completely destroyed.

transcript.label: CEFR B2
transcript.def: A written or printed record of words spoken in a meeting, court case, or broadcast.
transcript.ex: The journalist obtained a transcript of the politician's confidential meeting to verify the controversial statements.

transform.label: CEFR B1
transform.def: To change something completely in appearance, form, or character.
transform.ex: The new technology transformed the way people communicate with each other.

transformation.label: CEFR B1
transformation.def: A complete change in the appearance, nature, or character of something or someone.
transformation.ex: The old building underwent a remarkable transformation after the renovation project was completed.

transfusion.label: CEFR B2
transfusion.def: Medical procedure where blood from one person is transferred into another person's body.
transfusion.ex: The accident victim required an emergency blood transfusion to replace the blood she had lost.

transient.label: CEFR B2
transient.def: Lasting only a short time; not permanent or stable.
transient.ex: The transient nature of social media trends means companies must constantly adapt their marketing strategies.

transistor.label: CEFR B2
transistor.def: A tiny electronic device that controls or amplifies electrical signals in computers and other electronics.
transistor.ex: Modern smartphones contain billions of transistors that process information at incredible speeds.

transition.label: CEFR B2
transition.def: A change from one state, condition, or activity to another, often requiring adjustment.
transition.ex: The transition from secondary school to university can be challenging for many students.

transitional.label: CEFR B1
transitional.def: Existing or happening during a period of change from one state or situation to another.
transitional.ex: The government set up a transitional government to prepare the country for democratic elections.

transitive.label: CEFR B2
transitive.def: Describing a verb that requires a direct object to complete its meaning.
transitive.ex: In the sentence 'She wrote a letter', 'wrote' is a transitive verb because it needs the object 'letter' to make sense.

translate.label: CEFR B1
translate.def: To change words from one language into another language while keeping the meaning the same.
translate.ex: She translated the French novel into English so more people could read it.

translation.label: CEFR B2
translation.def: A written or spoken version of text converted from one language into another.
translation.ex: The translation of the Japanese novel into English introduced thousands of new readers to the author's work.

translator.label: CEFR B2
translator.def: A person who converts written or spoken text from one language into another, maintaining the original meaning.
translator.ex: The translator worked between English and Mandarin, helping international companies communicate with their clients.

transmission.label: CEFR B2
transmission.def: The passing of something from one person, place, or thing to another, especially disease or information.
transmission.ex: The transmission of data across the internet happens almost instantaneously through fibre optic cables.

transmit.label: CEFR B2
transmit.def: To send or pass something, such as information, signals, or disease, from one place or person to another.
transmit.ex: The radio station transmits news broadcasts every hour throughout the day.

transparent.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
transparent.def: Clear enough to see through; also means open and honest about information or intentions.
transparent.ex: The government's transparent approach to reporting the data helped build public trust in their findings.

transport.label: CEFR B2
transport.def: To move people or goods from one place to another using vehicles or other means
transport.ex: The company transports fresh produce to markets across the country every morning.

transportation.label: CEFR B1
transportation.def: The system or means of moving people or goods from one place to another
transportation.ex: Good transportation links make it easier for workers to commute to the office every day.

trap.label: CEFR A2
trap.def: A device or hidden danger designed to catch someone or something unexpectedly
trap.ex: The mouse fell into the trap and couldn't escape.

trash.label: CEFR B1
trash.def: Waste material or things that are no longer wanted or useful.
trash.ex: We put the trash in the bin every Friday morning before collection day.

travel.label: CEFR A1
travel.def: To go from one place to another, often far away.
travel.ex: We travel to the beach every summer.

travel agent.label: CEFR B1
travel agent.def: A person who helps customers plan and book holidays, flights, and hotel accommodation.
travel agent.ex: We visited the travel agent to book our flights and accommodation for our summer holiday.

traveler/traveller.label: CEFR A2
traveler/traveller.def: A person who goes from one place to another, especially on a journey or holiday.
traveler/traveller.ex: The traveller boarded the plane with her suitcase and passport.

traverse.label: CEFR B2
traverse.def: A path or route across something, especially a difficult or dangerous area of land.
traverse.ex: The mountaineers planned their traverse of the rocky terrain carefully to avoid the steepest sections.

tray.label: CEFR B2
tray.def: A flat container with raised edges, used for carrying or serving items, especially food and drinks.
tray.ex: The waiter brought a tray of champagne glasses to the guests at the reception.

tread.label: CEFR B2
tread.def: To walk on or step on something; to press down with your foot.
tread.ex: Visitors are asked not to tread on the grass in the botanical garden.

treasure.label: CEFR A2
treasure.def: Something valuable and important that you keep and protect carefully.
treasure.ex: My grandmother's old letters are a treasure to me.

treat.label: CEFR B2
treat.def: To behave towards someone in a particular way, or to provide medical care for an illness or injury.
treat.ex: The doctor is treating her patients with a new medication that has shown promising results in clinical trials.

treatment.label: CEFR B1
treatment.def: Medical care or therapy given to help cure or manage an illness or injury.
treatment.ex: After her accident, she received physiotherapy treatment for six weeks to help her leg heal.

treaty.label: CEFR B2
treaty.def: A formal written agreement between two or more countries or governments.
treaty.ex: The two nations signed a trade treaty to reduce tariffs and increase economic cooperation between them.

tree.label: CEFR A1
tree.def: A tall plant with a thick brown stem and green leaves or branches.
tree.ex: There is a big tree in the park where children play.

trek.label: CEFR B2
trek.def: A long, difficult journey on foot, typically through remote or challenging terrain.
trek.ex: The mountaineers completed a gruelling trek across the Himalayas, covering over 100 kilometres in three weeks.

tremble.label: CEFR B1
tremble.def: To shake slightly, usually because of cold, fear, or emotion.
tremble.ex: Her voice began to tremble as she read the letter from her sick mother.

tremendous.label: CEFR B1
tremendous.def: Extremely large in size, amount, or degree; very impressive or remarkable.
tremendous.ex: The new shopping centre has had a tremendous impact on the local economy since it opened last year.

tremendously.label: CEFR B2
tremendously.def: To a very great extent; extremely or significantly.
tremendously.ex: The new technology has improved our productivity tremendously, allowing us to complete projects weeks ahead of schedule.

tremor.label: CEFR B2
tremor.def: A slight, continuous shaking movement of something, often caused by fear, cold, or earthquake activity.
tremor.ex: The earthquake sent a tremor through the building, causing some pictures to fall from the walls.

trial.label: CEFR B2
trial.def: A test of something to see if it works well or is suitable before full use.
trial.ex: The new drug underwent clinical trials involving thousands of patients before regulatory approval.

triangle.label: CEFR B2
triangle.def: A flat shape with three straight sides and three angles.
triangle.ex: The architect used a triangle as the basic structure for the roof design.

tribe.label: CEFR B2
tribe.def: A social group sharing a common culture, language, territory, and often a shared ancestor.
tribe.ex: The Amazon tribe has maintained its traditional hunting practices for hundreds of years despite outside pressures.

trick.label: CEFR B2
trick.def: To deceive someone into doing or believing something by being clever or dishonest.
trick.ex: The magician tricked the audience into thinking the card had disappeared completely.

tricky.label: CEFR B2
tricky.def: Difficult to handle or understand; requiring careful thought or skill to manage successfully.
tricky.ex: The contract contained some tricky clauses that the lawyer had to explain carefully.

trim.label: CEFR B1
trim.def: The cutting or removal of unwanted parts from something to make it neat or smaller.
trim.ex: The garden looks much better after a trim of the hedges and bushes.

trio.label: CEFR B2
trio.def: A group of three people or things, especially musicians or performers working together.
trio.ex: The jazz trio performed a brilliant set at the concert, with piano, bass, and drums in perfect harmony.

trip.label: CEFR B2
trip.def: To stumble or fall forward because your foot catches on something.
trip.ex: She tripped over the loose carpet and caught herself on the bannister.

triumph.label: CEFR B1
triumph.def: A great success or victory, especially after facing difficulty or opposition.
triumph.ex: Winning the championship was a triumph for the team after years of hard work.

trivial.label: CEFR B2
trivial.def: Too small or unimportant to deserve serious consideration or attention
trivial.ex: The committee dismissed the complaint as trivial and focused on more pressing issues instead.

trolley.label: CEFR B2
trolley.def: A wheeled cart or basket used for carrying goods in shops or at airports.
trolley.ex: She pushed her trolley through the supermarket, filling it with fresh vegetables and dairy products.

troop.label: CEFR B2
troop.def: A group of soldiers, especially a unit of cavalry or an organised military group.
troop.ex: The military deployed an entire troop to the border region to maintain security.

trophy.label: CEFR B2
trophy.def: A prize or award given to the winner of a competition or sporting event.
trophy.ex: The football team lifted the trophy after winning the championship match.

tropic.label: CEFR B2
tropic.def: Relating to the warm regions of Earth near the equator with high temperatures and rainfall.
tropic.ex: The tropic climate of Costa Rica supports an incredible diversity of plant and animal species.

tropical.label: CEFR B1
tropical.def: Relating to warm regions near the equator with high rainfall and dense vegetation.
tropical.ex: Many tropical countries in Southeast Asia are popular tourist destinations because of their beautiful beaches and warm climate.

trouble.label: CEFR B1
trouble.def: To cause someone worry, difficulty, or inconvenience.
trouble.ex: I didn't want to trouble you, but could you help me move this box?

troublesome.label: CEFR B2
troublesome.def: Causing difficulty, annoyance, or problems that require attention and effort to resolve.
troublesome.ex: The new software proved troublesome for the IT department, requiring extensive updates before it could be deployed company-wide.

trousers.label: CEFR A1
trousers.def: A piece of clothing that covers your legs, with separate parts for each leg.
trousers.ex: He wore blue trousers and a white shirt to work.

truck.label: CEFR A1
truck.def: A large vehicle used to carry heavy goods and materials.
truck.ex: The delivery truck brought new clothes to the shop this morning.

true.label: CEFR A1
true.def: Correct or accurate; based on facts, not false.
true.ex: It is true that the Earth goes around the Sun.

truly.label: CEFR A2
truly.def: In a way that is honest and real; really or actually.
truly.ex: I truly love spending time with my family.

trumpet.label: CEFR B1
trumpet.def: A brass musical instrument that you play by blowing air through it and pressing valves with your fingers.
trumpet.ex: The trumpet player in the jazz band played a beautiful solo during the performance.

trunk.label: CEFR B2
trunk.def: The main stem of a tree, or a large box used for storing or travelling with belongings.
trunk.ex: The elephant wrapped its trunk around the branch to pull down the leaves.

trust.label: CEFR A2
trust.def: To believe that someone is honest and will not harm you or let you down.
trust.ex: I trust my best friend because she always tells me the truth.

trustworthy.label: CEFR B1
trustworthy.def: Reliable and honest; someone you can depend on to do what they promise
trustworthy.ex: My friend is very trustworthy, so I felt comfortable lending her my laptop while I was away.

trusty.label: CEFR B2
trusty.def: Reliable and dependable, having proven worthy of confidence through consistent performance.
trusty.ex: After fifteen years of loyal service, his trusty old bicycle finally needed replacement.

truth.label: CEFR A2
truth.def: Something that is real and correct, not false or made up.
truth.ex: She told the truth about what happened at school.

truthful.label: CEFR B2
truthful.def: Honest and accurate; giving information that is true and sincere.
truthful.ex: The witness gave a truthful account of the accident, despite pressure from lawyers to exaggerate the facts.

truthfulness.label: CEFR B2
truthfulness.def: The quality of being honest and telling the truth in what you say or do.
truthfulness.ex: The journalist was praised for her truthfulness in reporting the investigation, even when the facts were uncomfortable for powerful people.

try.label: CEFR A2
try.def: To attempt to do something; to make an effort to accomplish a goal.
try.ex: I tried to call you yesterday, but your phone was off.

t-shirt/tee-shirt.label: CEFR A1
t-shirt/tee-shirt.def: A casual piece of clothing with short sleeves, usually made of cotton
t-shirt/tee-shirt.ex: I wear a blue T-shirt when I go to the gym.

tub.label: CEFR B2
tub.def: A large, open container filled with water for bathing or washing.
tub.ex: She soaked in the hot tub for an hour to relieve her muscle tension.

tube.label: CEFR A1
tube.def: A long, hollow pipe made of plastic, metal, or rubber.
tube.ex: She squeezed toothpaste out of the tube.

tuberculosis.label: CEFR B2
tuberculosis.def: A serious infectious disease that mainly affects the lungs, spread through the air by coughing or sneezing.
tuberculosis.ex: Tuberculosis remains a significant health concern in many developing countries, though modern treatments have reduced mortality rates considerably.

tuesday.label: CEFR A1
tuesday.def: The second day of the week, after Monday and before Wednesday.
tuesday.ex: I have English class on Tuesday morning.

tug.label: CEFR B2
tug.def: A strong, sudden pull or jerk movement in a particular direction.
tug.ex: She felt a tug on her sleeve and turned to see her younger brother trying to get her attention.

tuition.label: CEFR B2
tuition.def: Fees paid for instruction and teaching, especially at schools or universities.
tuition.ex: International students often pay higher tuition fees than domestic students at British universities.

tulip.label: CEFR B2
tulip.def: A spring-flowering plant with cup-shaped flowers in various bright colours, grown from bulbs.
tulip.ex: The Dutch are famous for their tulip gardens, which attract millions of visitors each spring.

tumble.label: CEFR B1
tumble.def: To fall or roll downwards, often in an uncontrolled way
tumble.ex: The children tumbled down the grassy hill, laughing as they went.

tumor/tumour.label: CEFR B2
tumor/tumour.def: An abnormal growth of cells in the body that can be harmful or harmless.
tumor/tumour.ex: The patient was relieved to learn that the tumour discovered during screening was benign and did not require immediate surgery.

tuna.label: CEFR B1
tuna.def: A large ocean fish with firm flesh, commonly eaten as food.
tuna.ex: I ordered a tuna sandwich for lunch because it's healthy and filling.

tune.label: CEFR A2
tune.def: A series of musical notes that form a melody; a piece of music.
tune.ex: My little sister loves to sing the tune of 'Happy Birthday' at parties.

tunnel.label: CEFR B2
tunnel.def: An underground passage built beneath a road, river, or mountain to allow transport or passage through.
tunnel.ex: The new railway tunnel under the city centre will reduce traffic congestion significantly when it opens next year.

turbulence.label: CEFR B1
turbulence.def: Sudden, irregular air movements that make aircraft shake and move around unexpectedly
turbulence.ex: The plane experienced severe turbulence during the storm, causing passengers to grip their armrests tightly.

turbulent.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
turbulent.def: Characterized by conflict, disorder, or unpredictable change; emotionally or physically unstable.
turbulent.ex: The airline advised passengers that the flight might experience turbulent weather over the Atlantic.

turkey.label: CEFR A1
turkey.def: A large bird that people eat, especially at Christmas and Thanksgiving
turkey.ex: We cooked a turkey for our family dinner on Christmas Day.

turn.label: CEFR A1
turn.def: To rotate or move something in a circular direction
turn.ex: Please turn the handle to open the door.

turning.label: CEFR B1
turning.def: The act of rotating or changing direction, or a point where something changes course.
turning.ex: At the turning near the supermarket, take the road on your left.

turtle.label: CEFR B1
turtle.def: A reptile with a hard shell on its back that it can pull its body into for protection.
turtle.ex: The old turtle moved slowly across the garden towards the pond.

tutor.label: CEFR B2
tutor.def: A teacher who gives private instruction to one student or a small group, often specialising in specific subjects.
tutor.ex: She hired a tutor to help her prepare for the chemistry examination.

tv.label: CEFR A1
tv.def: A box-shaped machine that shows moving pictures and sounds for entertainment.
tv.ex: I watch TV every evening after dinner.

twice.label: CEFR A2
twice.def: Two times; one time and then one more time.
twice.ex: I go to the gym twice a week, on Monday and Friday.

twin.label: CEFR B1
twin.def: One of two children born to the same mother at the same time.
twin.ex: My twin brother and I look very similar, but we have completely different personalities.

twinkle.label: CEFR B2
twinkle.def: A soft, flickering light that appears and disappears quickly and regularly
twinkle.ex: The twinkle of distant stars filled the night sky as we gazed upward from the mountain.

twist.label: CEFR B1
twist.def: To turn or rotate something using your hands in opposite directions
twist.ex: She twisted the jar lid until it opened.

twisted.label: CEFR B2
twisted.def: Bent or turned out of its original shape; also meaning strange or morally wrong
twisted.ex: The accident left the metal railings twisted and barely recognisable.

type.label: CEFR B1
type.def: To write words using a keyboard or typewriter.
type.ex: I need to type my essay on the computer before tomorrow's deadline.

typhoid.label: CEFR B2
typhoid.def: A serious infectious disease spread through contaminated food or water, causing high fever and illness.
typhoid.ex: During the 19th century, improved sanitation systems helped reduce typhoid outbreaks in major cities.

typical.label: CEFR B1
typical.def: Normal or usual; what you usually expect to see or happen
typical.ex: A typical Monday morning for me starts with a cup of coffee and checking my emails.

typically.label: CEFR B1
typically.def: In the way that usually happens or is most common.
typically.ex: British people typically drink tea in the afternoon.

tyranny.label: CEFR B2
tyranny.def: Cruel and oppressive rule by a leader who has absolute power and no accountability.
tyranny.ex: The international community condemned the regime's tyranny against political opponents and journalists.

tyrant.label: CEFR B2
tyrant.def: A cruel leader who rules with absolute power and treats people unfairly.
tyrant.ex: The international community condemned the tyrant for suppressing freedom of speech and imprisoning political opponents.

ugly.label: CEFR A1
ugly.def: Not pleasant to look at; not beautiful.
ugly.ex: The old building was very ugly, so the city decided to paint it.

ultimately.label: CEFR B2
ultimately.def: In the end; finally, after everything else has been considered or happened.
ultimately.ex: The company faced many challenges during the merger, but ultimately it proved to be a successful decision that strengthened their market position.

umbrella.label: CEFR A1
umbrella.def: A thing you hold above your head to stay dry when it rains.
umbrella.ex: I took my umbrella because it was raining outside.

unable.label: CEFR B1
unable.def: Not having the power, skill, or possibility to do something
unable.ex: She was unable to attend the meeting because she had a doctor's appointment.

unacceptable.label: CEFR B2
unacceptable.def: Not good enough to be approved, tolerated, or allowed in a particular situation.
unacceptable.ex: The quality of the report was unacceptable, so the manager asked the team to rewrite it completely.

unafraid.label: CEFR B1
unafraid.def: Not feeling fear or worry about something dangerous or difficult.
unafraid.ex: She was unafraid to speak up in the meeting, even though her boss was there.

unashamedly.label: CEFR B2
unashamedly.def: Without feeling embarrassed or apologetic about something, openly and honestly.
unashamedly.ex: She unashamedly admitted that she preferred romantic comedies to serious films.

unattractive.label: CEFR B2
unattractive.def: Not pleasing to look at; lacking beauty or appeal in appearance
unattractive.ex: The government's proposed tax increase proved unattractive to most voters across the political spectrum.

unauthorized/unauthorised.label: CEFR B2
unauthorized/unauthorised.def: Done or acting without official permission or approval from the proper authority.
unauthorized/unauthorised.ex: The company discovered unauthorised access to its customer database during a security audit.

unavailable.label: CEFR B2
unavailable.def: Not able to be obtained, accessed, or used at the present time.
unavailable.ex: The manager was unavailable for comment when journalists contacted the company about the merger.

unaware.label: CEFR B2
unaware.def: Not knowing or realising that something exists or is happening
unaware.ex: She was unaware that her colleague had already solved the problem, so she spent hours working on it independently.

unbearable.label: CEFR B2
unbearable.def: So difficult or unpleasant that you cannot accept or tolerate it any longer.
unbearable.ex: The heat in the office was unbearable, so the manager finally decided to repair the air conditioning system.

unbeatable.label: CEFR B2
unbeatable.def: So strong or good that no one can defeat or surpass it
unbeatable.ex: The team's unbeatable defence prevented their opponents from scoring a single goal throughout the entire season.

unbelievable.label: CEFR B1
unbelievable.def: So surprising or extreme that it is hard to believe it is real or true.
unbelievable.ex: The player's performance in the final match was unbelievable—he scored five goals in twenty minutes.

uncertain.label: CEFR A2
uncertain.def: Not sure about something; not knowing what will happen
uncertain.ex: I am uncertain whether I should go to the party or stay home.

uncertainty.label: CEFR B1
uncertainty.def: The state of not being sure or confident about something.
uncertainty.ex: There is uncertainty about when the project will be finished because of unexpected problems.

unchanged.label: CEFR B2
unchanged.def: Remaining the same; not modified or altered in any way
unchanged.ex: The government's position on climate policy remained unchanged despite public pressure.

uncharacteristic.label: CEFR B2
uncharacteristic.def: Not typical of someone's usual behaviour or nature; unexpected.
uncharacteristic.ex: It was uncharacteristic of Sarah to arrive late, as she is normally very punctual.

uncle.label: CEFR A1
uncle.def: Your parent's brother, or your aunt's husband.
uncle.ex: My uncle took me to the football match last Saturday.

unclear.label: CEFR B1
unclear.def: Not clear or easy to understand; confusing or difficult to see
unclear.ex: The instructions were unclear, so several students asked the teacher for help.

unclearly.label: CEFR B2
unclearly.def: In a way that is difficult to understand or not clearly expressed or visible.
unclearly.ex: The instructions were written unclearly, leaving many customers confused about how to assemble the furniture.

uncomfortable.label: CEFR A2
uncomfortable.def: Feeling slightly worried, embarrassed, or physically uneasy in a situation.
uncomfortable.ex: I felt uncomfortable when everyone looked at me during the presentation.

uncomfortably.label: CEFR B2
uncomfortably.def: In a way that causes physical discomfort or emotional unease.
uncomfortably.ex: She sat uncomfortably in the tight dress, aware that everyone was staring at her.

uncommon.label: CEFR B2
uncommon.def: Not frequently seen, experienced, or found in most places or situations.
uncommon.ex: Severe allergic reactions to peanuts are uncommon in young children but can be life-threatening.

unconcerned.label: CEFR B2
unconcerned.def: Not worried or interested in something that might affect you
unconcerned.ex: Despite the growing criticism, the company remained unconcerned about its environmental impact.

unconscious.label: CEFR B2
unconscious.def: Not aware of what is happening around you; in a state of sleep or loss of consciousness
unconscious.ex: After the accident, he remained unconscious for three days before waking in the hospital.

uncontrollable.label: CEFR B1
uncontrollable.def: Unable to be stopped or managed; difficult or impossible to control
uncontrollable.ex: The child's uncontrollable laughter made everyone in the cinema turn around.

uncover.label: CEFR B1
uncover.def: To reveal something hidden or to remove the cover from something
uncover.ex: The investigation uncovered evidence that proved the company had broken the law.

underage.label: CEFR B1
underage.def: Below the legal age required for something, such as voting or buying alcohol.
underage.ex: Many countries have strict laws to prevent underage drinking in bars and clubs.

underestimate.label: CEFR B2
underestimate.def: To think something is less important, difficult, or valuable than it actually is.
underestimate.ex: Many analysts underestimated the impact that social media would have on traditional journalism.

undergraduate.label: CEFR B2
undergraduate.def: A student studying for their first degree at a university or college.
undergraduate.ex: Most undergraduates complete their studies over three or four years before applying for postgraduate programmes.

underground.label: CEFR B2
underground.def: Beneath the surface of the ground or hidden from public view and authority
underground.ex: The resistance movement operated underground, communicating through secret networks to avoid government detection.

underline.label: CEFR B1
underline.def: To draw a line under a word or words to make them stand out or show they are important.
underline.ex: The teacher asked us to underline all the key vocabulary words in the text.

underneath.label: CEFR B2
underneath.def: Below or under something; on the lower side of something.
underneath.ex: The address was printed underneath the company logo on the letterhead.

underpants.label: CEFR B1
underpants.def: Short underwear worn on the lower part of the body under other clothes
underpants.ex: He put on clean underpants before getting dressed for work.

undersea.label: CEFR B2
undersea.def: Existing or occurring beneath the surface of the ocean.
undersea.ex: Researchers discovered a previously unknown undersea volcano during their deep-ocean exploration mission.

understand.label: CEFR A2
understand.def: To know the meaning of something or to comprehend what someone says or does
understand.ex: I don't understand this question. Can you explain it again?

understandable.label: CEFR B2
understandable.def: Able to be comprehended or grasped by the mind without excessive difficulty.
understandable.ex: Her frustration was completely understandable given the circumstances of the project's failure.

understanding.label: CEFR B2
understanding.def: Knowledge or comprehension of what something means or how it works
understanding.ex: Her understanding of climate science was based on years of research and observation.

underwater.label: CEFR B2
underwater.def: beneath the surface of water; in or into the water below the top level
underwater.ex: The documentary showed coral reefs and the creatures that live underwater in the deep ocean.

underwear.label: CEFR B2
underwear.def: Clothing worn underneath outer clothes, next to the skin for comfort and hygiene.
underwear.ex: She packed several pairs of underwear and socks for her week-long business trip.

undo.label: CEFR B1
undo.def: To reverse or cancel something you have done, returning it to how it was before.
undo.ex: I made a mistake in my email, so I clicked undo to restore the text I had deleted.

undoubtedly.label: CEFR B2
undoubtedly.def: Without doubt; certainly true or definitely happening in a way that cannot be questioned.
undoubtedly.ex: The new technology has undoubtedly changed the way we communicate with each other.

undreamed.label: CEFR B2
undreamed.def: Greater in extent or quality than could have been imagined or expected possible.
undreamed.ex: The young entrepreneur achieved undreamed wealth within five years of launching her startup.

undress.label: CEFR B1
undress.def: To remove clothes from your body or another person's body.
undress.ex: She undressed quickly and got into bed after a long day at work.

undressed.label: CEFR B2
undressed.def: Wearing no clothes or only partially clothed; having removed one's outer garments.
undressed.ex: The undressed mannequins in the shop window were waiting for the new season's clothing to arrive.

unease.label: CEFR B2
unease.def: A feeling of worry or nervousness without knowing exactly why.
unease.ex: There was a sense of unease among investors following the unexpected announcement about the company's financial results.

uneasily.label: CEFR B2
uneasily.def: In a way that shows worry, nervousness, or lack of comfort about something.
uneasily.ex: She glanced uneasily at the clock, wondering if the interview panel would notice her late arrival.

uneasy.label: CEFR A2
uneasy.def: Feeling worried or uncomfortable about something; not calm or relaxed.
uneasy.ex: She felt uneasy about the dark forest and stayed close to her friend.

unemployed.label: CEFR B1
unemployed.def: Without a job; not currently working for a company or organisation.
unemployed.ex: After the factory closed, many unemployed workers struggled to find new jobs in the town.

unemployment.label: CEFR B1
unemployment.def: The state of not having a job, or the number of people without work in a country.
unemployment.ex: The government is trying to reduce unemployment by creating more job opportunities for young people.

unenthusiastic.label: CEFR B2
unenthusiastic.def: Not showing much interest, excitement, or eagerness about something.
unenthusiastic.ex: The committee gave an unenthusiastic response to the proposed budget cuts, raising concerns about their feasibility.

uneven.label: CEFR B2
uneven.def: Not level, smooth, or equal; having differences in height, quality, or distribution.
uneven.ex: The uneven distribution of wealth across regions remains a significant challenge for policymakers.

unexpected.label: CEFR B1
unexpected.def: Surprising because you did not think it would happen.
unexpected.ex: The unexpected rain forced us to cancel the outdoor picnic at the last minute.

unexpectedly.label: CEFR B1
unexpectedly.def: In a way that surprises you because you did not think it would happen
unexpectedly.ex: The weather changed unexpectedly, so we had to cancel the outdoor picnic.

unfair.label: CEFR A2
unfair.def: Not treating people equally or justly; not done in an honest way
unfair.ex: It was unfair that my brother got a bigger piece of cake than me.

unfairly.label: CEFR B1
unfairly.def: In a way that is not just or equal; treating some people better than others without good reason
unfairly.ex: She complained that the teacher had marked her exam unfairly compared to her classmates.

unfamiliar.label: CEFR B2
unfamiliar.def: Not known or recognised before; strange or new to someone's experience
unfamiliar.ex: When she moved to Japan, many of the customs seemed unfamiliar at first, but she gradually adapted to her new surroundings.

unfashionable.label: CEFR B2
unfashionable.def: No longer considered stylish or popular; out of date with current trends.
unfashionable.ex: Wearing unfashionable clothes didn't bother him, as he preferred comfort over trends.

unfit.label: CEFR B1
unfit.def: Not healthy or strong enough for a particular activity or purpose.
unfit.ex: After months without exercise, he felt unfit to run a marathon.

unfold.label: CEFR B1
unfold.def: To open something that was folded, or to gradually develop and become clear.
unfold.ex: As the story unfolds, we discover the main character's secret past.

unforeseen.label: CEFR B2
unforeseen.def: Unexpected; not anticipated or predicted beforehand despite reasonable planning
unforeseen.ex: The project was delayed due to unforeseen circumstances beyond the team's control.

unforgettable.label: CEFR A2
unforgettable.def: So good or special that you remember it for a very long time.
unforgettable.ex: That holiday was unforgettable because we saw amazing animals in Africa.

unfortunate.label: CEFR B1
unfortunate.def: Unlucky or sad; happening by bad chance rather than planning
unfortunate.ex: It was unfortunate that she missed the interview due to a train delay.

unfortunately.label: CEFR A2
unfortunately.def: sadly; it is sad that something happened
unfortunately.ex: Unfortunately, it rained on the day of the school trip.

unfriendly.label: CEFR B1
unfriendly.def: Not warm or kind; making people feel unwelcome or uncomfortable
unfriendly.ex: The receptionist at the hotel was unfriendly, so we decided not to stay there.

ungodly.label: CEFR B2
ungodly.def: Shocking or extreme in a way that seems morally wrong or unreasonable
