ungodly.ex: She complained about the ungodly hour at which the flight departed, making it impossible to say goodbye to her family.

unhappiness.label: CEFR B2
unhappiness.def: A feeling of sadness, dissatisfaction, or lack of contentment and joy.
unhappiness.ex: The unhappiness in their relationship became evident after months of constant arguments.

unhappy.label: CEFR A2
unhappy.def: Sad or not content; not feeling pleased or satisfied.
unhappy.ex: She was unhappy about her test results because she studied hard but still failed.

unhealthy.label: CEFR A2
unhealthy.def: Not good for your body or mind; likely to cause illness or harm
unhealthy.ex: Eating too much fast food is unhealthy and can make you tired.

unheard.label: CEFR B1
unheard.def: Not heard or listened to; when someone's voice or opinions are ignored or not given attention
unheard.ex: Many workers felt unheard when management made decisions without asking for their views.

unhelpful.label: CEFR B2
unhelpful.def: Not providing useful assistance or support; making a situation more difficult or unproductive
unhelpful.ex: His unhelpful comments during the meeting only distracted the team from finding a solution to the problem.

unify.label: CEFR B1
unify.def: To bring separate things or people together as one unit or group.
unify.ex: The new government policy aims to unify the different regions under a single leadership.

unimaginable.label: CEFR B2
unimaginable.def: So shocking or extreme that it is very difficult to believe or understand
unimaginable.ex: The scale of the disaster was unimaginable, affecting millions of people across the region.

unimaginably.label: CEFR B2
unimaginably.def: In a way that is so extreme or shocking that it is difficult to comprehend or visualise
unimaginably.ex: The scale of the climate crisis is unimaginably vast, affecting billions of people across the globe.

unimportant.label: CEFR A2
unimportant.def: Not important; not needing much attention or care
unimportant.ex: Don't worry about that small mistake—it's unimportant.

uninterested.label: CEFR B1
uninterested.def: Not interested or not wanting to know more about something.
uninterested.ex: My brother seemed completely uninterested in the football match, so he went to his room instead.

uninteresting.label: CEFR B1
uninteresting.def: Not able to hold your attention or make you want to know more
uninteresting.ex: The lecture was so uninteresting that most students fell asleep.

union.label: CEFR B1
union.def: An organization formed when people or groups join together for a common purpose or goal.
union.ex: The workers formed a union to negotiate better wages and working conditions with their employer.

unit.label: CEFR A2
unit.def: A single thing, or a fixed amount used for measuring or counting.
unit.ex: Each apartment in the building is a separate unit with its own kitchen and bathroom.

unite.label: CEFR B1
unite.def: To join together with others to achieve a shared goal or purpose.
unite.ex: The two companies decided to unite their efforts to create a more successful business.

united.label: CEFR B2
united.def: Joined together for a common purpose or goal; acting as one group.
united.ex: The united nations worked together to address the global humanitarian crisis.

universal.label: CEFR B2
universal.def: Existing or accepted everywhere in the world or among all people.
universal.ex: Despite their cultural differences, the desire for family security seems to be a universal human value.

universe.label: CEFR B1
universe.def: All of space, stars, planets, and everything that exists in it.
universe.ex: Scientists study the universe to understand how stars and galaxies are formed.

university.label: CEFR A2
university.def: A school where students study advanced subjects and earn degrees after secondary school.
university.ex: My sister studies medicine at the university in London.

unjustly.label: CEFR B2
unjustly.def: In a way that is unfair or not based on proper legal principles or evidence.
unjustly.ex: The court ruled that the defendant had been unjustly convicted without sufficient evidence.

unkind.label: CEFR B2
unkind.def: Not considerate of other people's feelings; lacking in compassion or gentleness
unkind.ex: His unkind remarks about her appearance upset her for days.

unknown.label: CEFR A2
unknown.def: Not known or identified; not familiar to you
unknown.ex: She received a letter from an unknown sender.

unlike.label: CEFR A2
unlike.def: Different from something else; not the same
unlike.ex: Unlike her sister, Maria prefers to study alone rather than in groups.

unlikely.label: CEFR B1
unlikely.def: Not probable; having little chance of happening or being true.
unlikely.ex: It's unlikely that it will rain tomorrow because the forecast shows sunny weather all day.

unlimited.label: CEFR B2
unlimited.def: Without restriction or limit; as much as you want or need
unlimited.ex: The smartphone plan offers unlimited data, so users can browse the internet without worrying about extra charges.

unload.label: CEFR B2
unload.def: To remove goods or cargo from a vehicle, ship, or container.
unload.ex: The workers unloaded the containers from the cargo ship at the port.

unlock.label: CEFR B2
unlock.def: To open something that is locked by using a key or code.
unlock.ex: She unlocked the door and stepped into the empty office building.

unlucky.label: CEFR B1
unlucky.def: Having bad luck; experiencing unfortunate events that you cannot control
unlucky.ex: He was unlucky to miss the penalty kick in the final seconds of the match.

unmistakable.label: CEFR B2
unmistakable.def: So clear or obvious that there is no doubt about it; impossible to confuse with something else
unmistakable.ex: The unmistakable smell of fresh coffee drifted through the office every morning.

unmistakably.label: CEFR B2
unmistakably.def: In a way that is completely clear and cannot be doubted or misunderstood.
unmistakably.ex: The accent was unmistakably American, despite her claiming to be from Canada.

unnatural.label: CEFR B2
unnatural.def: Not existing or occurring in nature; strange or not normal.
unnatural.ex: The bright green colour of the water looked unnatural, suggesting chemical pollution.

unnecessary.label: CEFR A2
unnecessary.def: Not needed or required; you can do something without it
unnecessary.ex: You don't need to bring unnecessary items on the trip; just pack what you really need.

unofficial.label: CEFR B2
unofficial.def: Not approved or authorised by the people in charge; informal or unauthorised.
unofficial.ex: According to unofficial sources, the company plans to announce the merger next month.

unpack.label: CEFR B1
unpack.def: To open a bag or container and take out the things inside.
unpack.ex: After arriving at the hotel, she unpacked her suitcase and hung up her clothes in the wardrobe.

unpleasant.label: CEFR A2
unpleasant.def: Not nice; making you feel bad or unhappy
unpleasant.ex: The smell in the kitchen was unpleasant, so I opened the window.

unpopular.label: CEFR B2
unpopular.def: Not liked or supported by many people; not fashionable or acceptable to most.
unpopular.ex: The government's unpopular decision to raise taxes triggered widespread protests across the country.

unpredictable.label: CEFR B1
unpredictable.def: Impossible to know or guess what will happen; changing in ways that are difficult to expect
unpredictable.ex: The weather in spring is unpredictable, so you should always carry an umbrella.

unrealistic.label: CEFR B2
unrealistic.def: Not based on facts or practical possibilities; impossible or unlikely to happen.
unrealistic.ex: Setting a goal to learn Japanese fluently in two weeks is unrealistic without years of study.

unreasonable.label: CEFR B2
unreasonable.def: Not fair, sensible, or acceptable; demanding too much without good justification.
unreasonable.ex: The landlord's request for a deposit equal to six months' rent seemed unreasonable to the tenant.

unrelated.label: CEFR B1
unrelated.def: Not connected or having no link between each other.
unrelated.ex: The two crimes seemed unrelated, but the detective eventually found a connection between them.

unreliable.label: CEFR B2
unreliable.def: Not able to be trusted or depended on; frequently failing or inconsistent
unreliable.ex: The bus service in that area is notoriously unreliable, so she always leaves extra time for her commute.

unrest.label: CEFR B2
unrest.def: A state of anxiety, dissatisfaction, or tension in society, often marked by protest or disorder.
unrest.ex: Growing economic unrest led to demonstrations in several major cities across the country.

unsafe.label: CEFR B2
unsafe.def: Presenting a risk of harm or injury; not protected from danger.
unsafe.ex: The government declared the old factory unsafe for workers and ordered it to be closed.

unsatisfactory.label: CEFR B2
unsatisfactory.def: Not good enough to meet required standards or expectations; disappointing in quality or performance.
unsatisfactory.ex: The student's unsatisfactory grades meant she would have to retake the course before advancing to the next level.

unselfish.label: CEFR B2
unselfish.def: Concerned with others' needs and happiness rather than your own benefit or advantage
unselfish.ex: Her unselfish decision to donate her savings to help the homeless community earned widespread admiration.

unstoppable.label: CEFR B2
unstoppable.def: Impossible to stop or prevent from succeeding; having such momentum or power that nothing can block it
unstoppable.ex: The team's unstoppable momentum in the second half led to a convincing victory over their rivals.

unsuccessful.label: CEFR B2
unsuccessful.def: Not achieving the desired result or outcome; failing to accomplish a goal.
unsuccessful.ex: Despite several unsuccessful attempts to fix the engine, the mechanic finally identified the real problem.

unsuitable.label: CEFR B2
unsuitable.def: Not appropriate or fitting for a particular purpose, person, or situation.
unsuitable.ex: The film was deemed unsuitable for children due to its violent content.

unsure.label: CEFR B2
unsure.def: Not confident or certain about something; having doubt or hesitation.
unsure.ex: She remained unsure about whether to accept the job offer until she had spoken with her family.

untalented.label: CEFR B2
untalented.def: Lacking natural ability or skill in a particular area despite practice or effort.
untalented.ex: Despite years of lessons, he remained an untalented musician and eventually gave up performing.

unthinkably.label: CEFR B2
unthinkably.def: In a way that is impossible to imagine or almost too terrible to believe.
unthinkably.ex: The cost of the disaster was unthinkably high, affecting millions of people across the region.

untidy.label: CEFR B1
untidy.def: Not neat or organized; having things scattered or in disorder
untidy.ex: Her bedroom was so untidy that she couldn't find her homework on the desk.

untie.label: CEFR B2
untie.def: To unfasten or undo something that has been tied or knotted together.
untie.ex: She untied her shoelaces before removing her boots after the long hike.

untreated.label: CEFR B2
untreated.def: Not given medical care or therapy; left in its original state without processing or handling
untreated.ex: The untreated water from the river contained harmful bacteria that made people ill.

untrimmed.label: CEFR B2
untrimmed.def: Not cut, shortened, or made neat; left in its original or unfinished state.
untrimmed.ex: The director released an untrimmed version of the film that included several scenes cut from the theatrical release.

unusual.label: CEFR A2
unusual.def: Not common or normal; different from what you usually see or experience
unusual.ex: It is unusual to see snow in summer in most countries.

unusually.label: CEFR B1
unusually.def: In a way that is not normal or typical; differently from what usually happens.
unusually.ex: The weather this winter has been unusually warm for this region.

unusualness.label: CEFR B2
unusualness.def: The quality of being rare, strange, or different from what is normally expected or seen.
unusualness.ex: The unusualness of the weather pattern this winter has prompted climate scientists to conduct further investigations.

unwanted.label: CEFR B1
unwanted.def: Not desired or welcome; something you do not want to have or experience.
unwanted.ex: She received many unwanted phone calls from companies trying to sell her products.

unwell.label: CEFR B1
unwell.def: Feeling sick or not in good health; not feeling normal or fine
unwell.ex: She stayed home from work because she was feeling unwell.

unwilling.label: CEFR B2
unwilling.def: Not wanting to do something; reluctant or resistant to an action or idea.
unwilling.ex: The company was unwilling to negotiate with the striking workers, which prolonged the industrial dispute.

unwise.label: CEFR B2
unwise.def: Not showing good judgment or prudent decision-making; likely to have negative consequences.
unwise.ex: It would be unwise to invest all your savings in a single company without researching its financial stability first.

unworldly.label: CEFR B2
unworldly.def: Seeming strange or unusual because not connected to ordinary everyday life or reality.
unworldly.ex: The orchestra's unworldly sound created an atmosphere that felt almost ethereal and transcendent.

up.label: CEFR A2
up.def: towards a higher position or place
up.ex: Please stand up when the teacher enters the classroom.

upbringing.label: CEFR B2
upbringing.def: The treatment, education, and experiences a child receives from birth until adulthood.
upbringing.ex: Her strict upbringing in a rural village shaped her values and approach to life.

update.label: CEFR B1
update.def: To give someone the most recent information about something.
update.ex: The manager updated the team about the project progress during the meeting.

upgrade.label: CEFR B2
upgrade.def: To replace something with a newer or better version; to improve the quality or standard of something.
upgrade.ex: The company decided to upgrade its computer systems to improve productivity and reduce security risks.

uplifted.label: CEFR B1
uplifted.def: Feeling happy, inspired, or more positive than before.
uplifted.ex: After the motivational speech, the team felt uplifted and ready to tackle the project.

upload.label: CEFR B1
upload.def: To transfer a file or information from your computer to the internet or a server.
upload.ex: I need to upload my essay to the learning platform before midnight.

upper.label: CEFR B1
upper.def: Located in a higher position or at a higher level than something else.
upper.ex: The upper shelves of the bookcase are difficult for children to reach.

upright.label: CEFR B2
upright.def: In a vertical position; standing straight up without leaning or bending over.
upright.ex: She sat upright in her chair during the entire presentation, maintaining an attentive posture.

upset.label: CEFR B2
upset.def: To make someone feel worried, unhappy, or emotionally disturbed about something.
upset.ex: The criticism from her manager upset her more than she expected, though she tried not to show it.

upside down.label: CEFR B2
upside down.def: In a position where the top is facing downward and the bottom is facing upward.
upside down.ex: The acrobat hung upside down from the bar, her long hair almost touching the ground.

upstairs.label: CEFR B1
upstairs.def: The upper floor or floors of a building, especially a house or apartment.
upstairs.ex: My bedroom is upstairs, so I have to climb the stairs to get there.

up-to-date.label: CEFR B1
up-to-date.def: Containing the newest information or latest changes; current and modern.
up-to-date.ex: I try to keep my CV up-to-date so I'm ready if a good job opportunity comes along.

upward.label: CEFR B1
upward.def: Moving or pointing towards a higher position or level
upward.ex: The company has seen an upward trend in sales over the past year.

urban.label: CEFR B2
urban.def: Relating to cities and towns, including their buildings, people, and way of life.
urban.ex: Urban planners are designing new public spaces to make city centres more accessible and liveable for residents.

urge.label: CEFR B2
urge.def: To strongly encourage or persuade someone to do something.
urge.ex: The government urged citizens to reduce their carbon footprint to combat climate change.

urgent.label: CEFR B1
urgent.def: Needing immediate attention or action; very important and requiring quick response
urgent.ex: The hospital received an urgent call about a patient having a heart attack.

urgently.label: CEFR B2
urgently.def: In a way that requires immediate action or attention because something is very important or pressing.
urgently.ex: The hospital urgently needed more staff after several nurses fell ill during the pandemic.

usage.label: CEFR B1
usage.def: The way something is used or employed in practice.
usage.ex: The usage of social media has changed how teenagers communicate with their friends.

use.label: CEFR A1
use.def: To do something with a tool or object to accomplish a task.
use.ex: I use a pen to write my name.

used.label: CEFR A2
used.def: Already owned or worked with by someone else before
used.ex: I bought a used car from my uncle last month.

used to.label: CEFR B1
used to.def: Did something regularly or as a habit in the past, but no longer does it now.
used to.ex: I used to go running every morning, but I stopped when I injured my knee last year.

useful.label: CEFR A2
useful.def: Helpful and able to do something good for you.
useful.ex: A dictionary is useful when you are learning English.

useless.label: CEFR B1
useless.def: Not helpful or not able to do what is needed; having no purpose or value
useless.ex: He tried calling the number, but it was useless because nobody answered.

user.label: CEFR B2
user.def: A person who operates or accesses a system, application, or service regularly.
user.ex: The software company surveyed thousands of users to understand their preferences and improve the application.

usher.label: CEFR B2
usher.def: To guide or direct someone to their seat or to a place, especially as an official duty.
usher.ex: The security guard ushered the VIP guests through the exclusive entrance to the theatre.

usual.label: CEFR A2
usual.def: Normal and happening most of the time, not different or special.
usual.ex: I wake up at 7 o'clock as usual, then have breakfast before work.

usually.label: CEFR A1
usually.def: Most of the time; in most cases; normally.
usually.ex: I usually eat breakfast at 7 o'clock in the morning.

utility.label: CEFR B2
utility.def: The usefulness or practical value that something provides to people.
utility.ex: The utility of public transport systems lies in their ability to reduce traffic congestion and lower carbon emissions.

utilize/utilise.label: CEFR B2
utilize/utilise.def: To use something in a practical or effective way for a specific purpose.
utilize/utilise.ex: The company decided to utilise the new software to streamline their workflow and reduce operational costs.

utter.label: CEFR B2
utter.def: To speak or say something, often expressing thoughts or feelings aloud.
utter.ex: She didn't utter a single word during the entire meeting, which made everyone uncomfortable.

utterly.label: CEFR B2
utterly.def: Completely or absolutely; to the greatest possible degree.
utterly.ex: The new policy was utterly rejected by the opposition parties, who called it fundamentally flawed.

vacancy.label: CEFR B1
vacancy.def: An empty job position available for someone to fill in a company or organization
vacancy.ex: The advertising agency posted a vacancy for a junior graphic designer on their website last week.

vacant.label: CEFR B2
vacant.def: Empty and available for use; not occupied or filled
vacant.ex: The landlord advertised the vacant apartment in the newspaper, hoping to find a suitable tenant quickly.

vacation.label: CEFR A1
vacation.def: A period of time away from work or school for rest and enjoyment
vacation.ex: We took a vacation to the beach last summer and had a wonderful time.

vaccine.label: CEFR B2
vaccine.def: A medical preparation that helps your body fight specific diseases by building immunity against them.
vaccine.ex: Many travellers get vaccinated against yellow fever before visiting tropical countries.

vague.label: CEFR B1
vague.def: Not clear or not giving enough detail; uncertain or unclear in meaning
vague.ex: The instructions were too vague, so I wasn't sure exactly what I needed to do.

vain.label: CEFR B1
vain.def: Excessively proud of your appearance or abilities; unwilling to accept failure or criticism.
vain.ex: He was too vain to wear glasses, so he struggled to read the board at work.

valentine.label: CEFR B2
valentine.def: A greeting card or romantic gift sent to someone you love, traditionally on February 14th.
valentine.ex: She received dozens of valentines from admirers on Valentine's Day, though she only kept the one from her boyfriend.

valley.label: CEFR A2
valley.def: A low area of land between hills or mountains.
valley.ex: The small village sits in a green valley surrounded by tall mountains.

valuable.label: CEFR B1
valuable.def: Worth a lot of money, or important and useful.
valuable.ex: The painting in the museum is very valuable, so it is protected carefully.

value.label: CEFR B2
value.def: To consider something important or estimate its worth or usefulness
value.ex: The company values innovation and encourages employees to develop creative solutions.

van.label: CEFR B1
van.def: A large vehicle with an enclosed cargo area, often used to transport goods or groups of people.
van.ex: The delivery van arrived this morning with my online shopping order.

vanish.label: CEFR B2
vanish.def: To disappear suddenly or completely, often in a mysterious or unexpected way.
vanish.ex: The magician made the rabbit vanish in front of the astonished audience.

vanity.label: CEFR B2
vanity.def: Excessive pride in one's appearance, abilities, or achievements.
vanity.ex: Her vanity about her looks meant she spent hours preparing her appearance before leaving the house.

variation.label: CEFR B2
variation.def: A difference or change in something, especially in amount, quality, or form across different instances.
variation.ex: There is considerable variation in house prices depending on the neighbourhood and size of the property.

varied.label: CEFR B2
varied.def: Consisting of many different types or kinds; not all the same.
varied.ex: The university offers a varied curriculum that allows students to study subjects ranging from engineering to philosophy.

variety.label: CEFR B1
variety.def: A lot of different types or kinds of something.
variety.ex: The supermarket offers a wide variety of fresh vegetables from around the world.

various.label: CEFR B1
various.def: Many different types or kinds of things; not all the same.
various.ex: The café serves various types of coffee, including espresso, cappuccino, and latte.

vase.label: CEFR A1
vase.def: A container made of glass or ceramic, used for holding cut flowers with water
vase.ex: She put the roses in a blue vase on the table.

vast.label: CEFR B1
vast.def: Very large in size or area; covering a huge space or amount
vast.ex: The vast desert stretched for hundreds of kilometres in every direction.

vegetable.label: CEFR A1
vegetable.def: A plant or part of a plant that people eat, such as carrots or lettuce.
vegetable.ex: I eat vegetables every day because they are good for my health.

vegetarian.label: CEFR B1
vegetarian.def: A person who does not eat meat, fish, or poultry, but eats plants and dairy products.
vegetarian.ex: My sister became a vegetarian five years ago and now she cooks delicious meals with beans and vegetables.

vehicle.label: CEFR B1
vehicle.def: A machine with wheels or engine used to transport people or goods from one place to another.
vehicle.ex: She drives a large vehicle to transport her family and camping equipment.

vein.label: CEFR B1
vein.def: A blood vessel that carries blood back to the heart from other parts of the body.
vein.ex: The nurse found a good vein in my arm to take a blood sample.

venture.label: CEFR B2
venture.def: A risky or daring business project or undertaking with uncertain outcomes.
venture.ex: The startup's venture into the renewable energy market proved more successful than analysts had predicted.

venue.label: CEFR B2
venue.def: A place where an event, performance, or meeting is held.
venue.ex: The concert was held at a small intimate venue in central London rather than a large stadium.

verb.label: CEFR A2
verb.def: An action word that describes what someone does or what happens
verb.ex: In the sentence 'She runs fast', the verb is 'runs'.

verbal.label: CEFR B2
verbal.def: Relating to words, speech, or communication using spoken language rather than written or physical means.
verbal.ex: The job interview included both written tests and verbal communication exercises to assess candidates' speaking skills.

verbally.label: CEFR B2
verbally.def: In spoken words rather than written or shown in other ways.
verbally.ex: The teacher verbally praised the students for their excellent work during the presentation.

verse.label: CEFR B2
verse.def: To compose or express something in poetic or metrical form.
verse.ex: The songwriter versed the melody into a powerful ballad that moved the audience to tears.

version.label: CEFR B2
version.def: A particular form or variant of something, differing from other forms in some way.
version.ex: The latest version of the software includes several improvements and bug fixes.

very.label: CEFR A1
very.def: To a great degree; much more than usual or normal.
very.ex: The coffee is very hot, so be careful.

vessel.label: CEFR B1
vessel.def: A container, usually made of clay, metal, or glass, for holding or carrying liquids or other things.
vessel.ex: She poured the wine into a ceramic vessel before serving it to her guests.

vet.label: CEFR B1
vet.def: A person with special training who treats sick or injured animals.
vet.ex: My cat had an infection, so I took her to the vet for antibiotics.

veteran.label: CEFR B2
veteran.def: A person with long experience in a particular job, activity, or military service.
veteran.ex: The veteran journalist had covered major conflicts across three continents during her forty-year career.

vibrate.label: CEFR B2
vibrate.def: To move back and forth rapidly with small movements.
vibrate.ex: The loud music caused the windows to vibrate throughout the concert.

vibration.label: CEFR B2
vibration.def: A rapid, continuous shaking movement back and forth or side to side.
vibration.ex: The vibration from the construction work next door made the windows rattle all morning.

vice.label: CEFR B1
vice.def: Next in rank or authority to the main person in charge.
vice.ex: She was promoted to vice president of the company last year.

vice president.label: CEFR B1
vice president.def: A high-ranking official who assists the president and takes over if the president cannot work
vice president.ex: The vice president attended the international conference when the president was ill.

victim.label: CEFR B1
victim.def: A person who is harmed, injured, or killed by someone or something bad.
victim.ex: The police are interviewing victims of the accident to understand what happened.

victory.label: CEFR B1
victory.def: A successful outcome in a battle, competition, or struggle against someone.
victory.ex: The football team celebrated their victory after winning the championship match.

video.label: CEFR A1
video.def: A recording of moving pictures and sound that you can watch
video.ex: I watched a video of cats playing on my computer.

video clip.label: CEFR B1
video clip.def: A short recorded piece of film or movie, usually a few minutes long.
video clip.ex: She shared a funny video clip of her dog dancing on social media.

video game.label: CEFR A2
video game.def: An electronic game played on a computer, phone, or special gaming device with a screen
video game.ex: My brother plays video games on his console every evening after school.

view.label: CEFR B1
view.def: To look at or watch something carefully, or to have an opinion about something.
view.ex: I viewed the exhibition at the museum last weekend and thought the paintings were impressive.

viewer.label: CEFR B2
viewer.def: A person who watches television, films, or other visual content.
viewer.ex: The documentary attracted millions of viewers when it aired on the BBC last month.

viewpoint.label: CEFR B1
viewpoint.def: A particular way of thinking about or understanding something; a person's opinion or perspective.
viewpoint.ex: From the teacher's viewpoint, the students needed more practice with grammar exercises.

vigor/vigour.label: CEFR B2
vigor/vigour.def: Physical strength and good health, or enthusiastic energy in doing something
vigor/vigour.ex: The young athletes trained with great vigour, determined to win the championship.

vigorous.label: Oxford 5000 · C1
vigorous.def: Done with great energy, strength, and enthusiasm; showing powerful physical or mental effort.
vigorous.ex: The vigorous exercise routine left her exhausted but satisfied after just one week.

vigorously.label: CEFR B2
vigorously.def: With energy, force, and determination; in a strong, active way.
vigorously.ex: The protesters vigorously opposed the new government policy at yesterday's demonstration.

villa.label: CEFR B2
villa.def: A large, comfortable house, typically in the countryside or by the sea, often rented for holidays.
villa.ex: We rented a villa in Tuscany for two weeks and enjoyed views of the vineyards from the terrace.

village.label: CEFR A2
village.def: A small town in the countryside with a few houses, shops, and local buildings.
village.ex: My grandmother lives in a quiet village near the mountains where everyone knows each other.

villager.label: CEFR B2
villager.def: A person who lives in a village, typically a rural community smaller than a town.
villager.ex: The villagers gathered in the square to discuss plans for improving the local school.

vinegar.label: CEFR B2
vinegar.def: A sour liquid made by fermenting alcohol, used as a condiment or cleaning agent.
vinegar.ex: The chef drizzled balsamic vinegar over the salad to add a sharp, tangy flavour.

violate.label: CEFR B2
violate.def: To break or fail to obey a rule, law, or agreement
violate.ex: The company was fined for violating environmental protection regulations.

violence.label: CEFR B1
violence.def: Aggressive physical force that causes harm or injury to people.
violence.ex: The news report covered the violence that broke out during the protest yesterday.

violent.label: CEFR A2
violent.def: Using force to hurt people or things; very rough and forceful
violent.ex: The violent storm destroyed many houses in the village.

violently.label: CEFR B1
violently.def: In a sudden, forceful, and intense way that causes harm or damage.
violently.ex: The earthquake shook the building violently, causing cracks in the walls.

violin.label: CEFR A2
violin.def: A musical instrument with four strings that you play with a bow to make music.
violin.ex: She has been learning to play the violin for two years.

virtual reality.label: CEFR B2
virtual reality.def: A computer-created environment that simulates a real or imaginary world, experienced through special headsets and equipment.
virtual reality.ex: Architects are using virtual reality to show clients how buildings will look before construction begins.

virtually.label: CEFR B2
virtually.def: Almost or nearly completely, in effect though not formally or officially
virtually.ex: The new policy is virtually certain to pass parliament, though the final vote hasn't been scheduled yet.

virtue.label: CEFR B2
virtue.def: A morally good quality or behaviour that is considered praiseworthy and worth developing.
virtue.ex: Honesty is a virtue that is highly valued in professional and personal relationships.

virus.label: CEFR B1
virus.def: A tiny living thing that causes illness and spreads from person to person.
virus.ex: She caught a virus on her flight and had to cancel the rest of her holiday.

visa.label: CEFR B1
visa.def: Official permission from a country's government to enter, stay, or work there
visa.ex: She applied for a student visa to study at a university in Canada for two years.

visible.label: CEFR B1
visible.def: Able to be seen; not hidden or concealed from view.
visible.ex: The mountains are visible from our house on clear days.

visibly.label: CEFR B2
visibly.def: In a way that can be clearly seen or noticed by others.
visibly.ex: The minister was visibly uncomfortable during the press conference, shifting in her seat and avoiding eye contact.

visit.label: CEFR A1
visit.def: To go to see a person or place for a short time.
visit.ex: I visit my grandmother every Sunday.

visitor.label: CEFR A2
visitor.def: A person who comes to see or spend time with someone or at a place.
visitor.ex: We had visitors from France stay at our house last weekend.

visual.label: CEFR B1
visual.def: Related to seeing or things you can see with your eyes.
visual.ex: The film uses stunning visual effects to create an underwater world.

visually.label: CEFR B1
visually.def: In a way that relates to sight or what you can see.
visually.ex: The two paintings are visually similar, even though they were created by different artists.

vital.label: CEFR B2
vital.def: Extremely important or necessary for success or survival
vital.ex: Regular exercise is vital for maintaining good physical and mental health throughout your life.

vitamin.label: CEFR B2
vitamin.def: An organic substance found in food that your body needs in small amounts to stay healthy and function properly.
vitamin.ex: Studies show that vitamin D deficiency can weaken bones and increase the risk of fractures in older adults.

vivid.label: CEFR B1
vivid.def: Bright, clear, and easy to see or remember; very colourful or detailed
vivid.ex: The sunset painted the sky in vivid oranges and purples that I'll never forget.

vividly.label: CEFR B1
vividly.def: In a way that is bright, clear, and easy to picture in your mind.
vividly.ex: She described the accident so vividly that everyone could imagine exactly what happened.

vocabulary.label: CEFR A2
vocabulary.def: All the words a person knows and uses in a language.
vocabulary.ex: Learning new vocabulary every day will help you speak English better.

vocalist.label: CEFR B2
vocalist.def: A person who sings professionally, especially as the main singer in a band or musical group.
vocalist.ex: The lead vocalist of the band captivated the audience with her powerful performance at the concert.

voice.label: CEFR A2
voice.def: The sound that comes from your mouth when you speak or sing.
voice.ex: She has a beautiful voice and loves to sing in the choir.

volcano.label: CEFR B1
volcano.def: A mountain with an opening where hot liquid rock and gases erupt from beneath Earth's surface.
volcano.ex: The volcano in Iceland erupted last year and caused flights to be cancelled across Europe.

volleyball.label: CEFR A1
volleyball.def: A sport where two teams hit a ball over a net with their hands.
volleyball.ex: We played volleyball at the beach last weekend.

volunteer.label: CEFR B1
volunteer.def: To offer to do work or help without payment because you want to.
volunteer.ex: She volunteers at the local animal shelter every Saturday morning.

vomit.label: CEFR B2
vomit.def: Partially digested food and liquid expelled from the stomach through the mouth due to illness or nausea
vomit.ex: The passenger cleaned up some vomit in the aircraft lavatory before the flight attendant could assist.

vote.label: CEFR B1
vote.def: To make an official choice in an election or decision by marking a ballot or raising your hand
vote.ex: I voted in the local election last week to choose our new mayor.

voter.label: CEFR B2
voter.def: A person who has the legal right to choose political leaders or decide on policies in elections.
voter.ex: Exit polls showed that younger voters were more concerned about climate change than the economy.

vow.label: CEFR B2
vow.def: A solemn promise or commitment, especially one made formally or in an important ceremony.
vow.ex: After years of searching, she made a vow to never give up on finding her missing brother.

vowel.label: CEFR B1
vowel.def: A letter that represents an open sound made with your mouth, such as a, e, i, o, or u.
vowel.ex: The word 'beautiful' contains four vowels and five consonants.

voyage.label: CEFR B1
voyage.def: A long journey, especially by ship or spacecraft across water or space.
voyage.ex: The sailors completed their voyage across the Atlantic Ocean after three weeks at sea.

wage.label: CEFR B2
wage.def: Regular payment received from an employer for work, usually calculated hourly or weekly.
wage.ex: She negotiated a higher wage before accepting the position at the manufacturing plant.

wagon.label: CEFR B1
wagon.def: A vehicle with four wheels pulled by a horse or other animal, used to carry goods.
wagon.ex: The farmer loaded hay onto the wagon to transport it to the barn.

waist.label: CEFR B2
waist.def: The part of your body between your ribs and hips, usually the narrowest part.
waist.ex: She wore a belt around her waist to keep her loose trousers in place.

waistcoat.label: CEFR B2
waistcoat.def: A sleeveless jacket worn over a shirt, typically under a jacket as part of formal dress.
waistcoat.ex: He wore a burgundy waistcoat with a matching tie to the wedding ceremony.

wait.label: CEFR A1
wait.def: To stay in a place until someone arrives or something happens.
wait.ex: I waited for my friend at the train station for ten minutes.

waiter.label: CEFR A1
waiter.def: A person who serves food and drinks to customers in a restaurant.
waiter.ex: The waiter brought our drinks to the table.

waitress.label: CEFR A1
waitress.def: A female worker who serves food and drinks to customers in a restaurant or café
waitress.ex: The waitress brought our coffee and smiled at us.

wake.label: CEFR B1
wake.def: To stop sleeping or to make someone stop sleeping.
wake.ex: I wake up at 7 o'clock every morning to get ready for work.

walk.label: CEFR A1
walk.def: To move forward on your feet one step at a time.
walk.ex: I walk to school every morning.

walkabout.label: CEFR B2
walkabout.def: A long journey or trip, often taken to explore or escape from routine; originally an Aboriginal Australian practice.
walkabout.ex: After finishing university, she decided to take a walkabout through Southeast Asia before starting her career.

walker.label: CEFR B2
walker.def: A person who moves on foot, especially someone who regularly walks for exercise or transportation.
walker.ex: As an experienced walker, she completed the 20-mile trail without any difficulty.

walking.label: CEFR A2
walking.def: Moving forward slowly on foot, typically for exercise or to get somewhere.
walking.ex: I enjoy walking in the park every morning before work.

wall.label: CEFR A1
wall.def: A vertical structure made of brick or stone that forms the side of a room or building.
wall.ex: I hung a picture on the bedroom wall.

wallboard.label: CEFR B2
wallboard.def: Large sheets of gypsum or wood-based material used to cover walls and ceilings in building construction
wallboard.ex: The builders installed wallboard throughout the new office building before painting the walls.

wallet.label: CEFR A2
wallet.def: A small flat case for carrying money and cards in your pocket or bag.
wallet.ex: I put my credit card and cash in my wallet before going to the shop.

walnut.label: CEFR B2
walnut.def: A hard-shelled nut with a wrinkled kernel, commonly eaten as food or used in cooking.
walnut.ex: The chef added crushed walnuts to the salad for a crunchy texture and rich flavour.

wander.label: CEFR A2
wander.def: To walk slowly without a fixed direction or destination.
wander.ex: We decided to wander through the old town and explore the shops.

want.label: CEFR A1
want.def: To desire or wish to have or do something
want.ex: I want to drink some water.

war.label: CEFR A1
war.def: Armed conflict between countries or groups using weapons and soldiers.
war.ex: Many people died in the war between the two countries.

ward.label: CEFR B1
ward.def: A division or district of a city or town, especially for local government or hospital purposes.
ward.ex: The nurse works in the children's ward at the hospital and takes care of patients every day.

wardrobe.label: CEFR B1
wardrobe.def: All the clothes that a person owns or keeps for different occasions.
wardrobe.ex: She spent the summer building a new wardrobe with clothes suitable for hot weather.

warm.label: CEFR B1
warm.def: To make something increase in temperature or become hot
warm.ex: I warmed the milk in a pan before pouring it into my coffee.

warmly.label: CEFR B1
warmly.def: In a friendly and sincere manner; showing genuine care or affection
warmly.ex: She warmly welcomed her guests at the door with a big smile and hug.

warmth.label: CEFR B1
warmth.def: The pleasant feeling of being warm; a friendly and kind quality in someone's manner.
warmth.ex: The warmth of the sun felt wonderful on her face after the cold winter.

warn.label: CEFR B1
warn.def: To tell someone about a possible danger or problem before it happens
warn.ex: The weather forecast warned us that a storm was coming, so we decided to stay home.

warning.label: CEFR B1
warning.def: A message telling someone about a danger or problem that might happen soon.
warning.ex: The weather forecast issued a warning about heavy snow expected this weekend.

warranty.label: CEFR B1
warranty.def: A written promise that a seller will fix or replace a product if it breaks within a set time.
warranty.ex: The laptop comes with a one-year warranty that covers manufacturing defects.

warrior.label: CEFR B1
warrior.def: A person who fights in battles, especially in former times or in armed conflicts.
warrior.ex: The ancient warrior trained for years to master his combat skills.

warship.label: CEFR B2
warship.def: A military ship equipped with weapons and designed for combat at sea.
warship.ex: The naval commander deployed several warships to the disputed waters to maintain regional security.

wartime.label: CEFR B2
wartime.def: A period when a country is fighting a war against another country or enemy.
wartime.ex: During wartime, many civilians had to leave their homes and move to safer areas.

wash.label: CEFR A1
wash.def: To clean something with water and usually soap.
wash.ex: I wash my hands before eating lunch.

washbowl.label: CEFR B1
washbowl.def: A large bowl or sink used for washing your hands and face, typically found in a bedroom or bathroom.
washbowl.ex: She filled the washbowl with warm water and soap to clean up before dinner.

washing machine.label: CEFR A2
washing machine.def: An electric machine that cleans clothes using water and soap automatically.
washing machine.ex: I put my dirty clothes in the washing machine every Sunday.

washing-up.label: CEFR A2
washing-up.def: Cleaning dishes, plates, and other items used during cooking and eating.
washing-up.ex: After dinner, I did the washing-up while my mum dried the dishes.

wasp.label: CEFR B2
wasp.def: A stinging insect with a narrow waist, usually yellow and black, that builds paper nests.
wasp.ex: A wasp buzzed around the outdoor café, attracted by the sweet desserts on nearby tables.

waste.label: CEFR B1
waste.def: To use something badly or carelessly without getting good value from it
waste.ex: Don't waste water by leaving the tap running while you brush your teeth.

wasteful.label: CEFR B1
wasteful.def: Using more of something than necessary; not being careful about saving money or resources
wasteful.ex: It is wasteful to leave the lights on in empty rooms all day.

watch.label: CEFR A1
watch.def: To look at something carefully for a period of time.

water.label: CEFR B2
water.def: To pour liquid onto plants or soil to help them grow and survive.
water.ex: She waters her garden every morning before work to keep the flowers healthy.

waterfall.label: CEFR B1
waterfall.def: A flow of water falling from a high place down a cliff or rock to a lower level
waterfall.ex: We hiked through the forest and discovered a beautiful waterfall hidden among the trees.

waterproof.label: CEFR B2
waterproof.def: Not allowing water to pass through; protected from water damage or absorption.
waterproof.ex: The mountaineer wore a waterproof jacket during the heavy rainfall in the Alps.

wave.label: CEFR B1
wave.def: To move your hand or arm back and forth to greet someone or get their attention
wave.ex: She waved at me from across the street, so I waved back.

wax.label: CEFR B1
wax.def: To cover or polish something with a smooth, shiny coating made from wax.
wax.ex: She decided to wax her car before the summer road trip to protect the paint.

way.label: CEFR A1
way.def: A path or direction you move along to get somewhere
way.ex: The way to the park is down this street.

weak.label: CEFR A2
weak.def: Not having much physical strength or power
weak.ex: After being ill for two weeks, she felt weak and tired.

weaken.label: CEFR B2
weaken.def: To become less strong or powerful; to make something less effective or intense.
weaken.ex: The government's credibility weakened after several ministers resigned over the scandal.

weakly.label: CEFR B2
weakly.def: In a way that lacks strength, force, or conviction; without much intensity or effectiveness.
weakly.ex: The candidate weakly defended his position during the debate, failing to convince the audience of his argument.

weakness.label: CEFR B1
weakness.def: Lack of physical strength or a feeling of being tired and unable to do something
weakness.ex: After recovering from illness, she felt a general weakness in her legs for several weeks.

wealth.label: CEFR A2
wealth.def: A large amount of money or valuable possessions that someone owns.
wealth.ex: She inherited great wealth from her grandmother, including several houses and investments.

wealthy.label: CEFR B2
wealthy.def: Having a large amount of money or valuable possessions; rich.
wealthy.ex: The wealthy businessman donated millions to fund a new research centre at the university.

weapon.label: CEFR B1
weapon.def: An object or tool designed to hurt or kill someone during a fight or war.
weapon.ex: The police found an illegal weapon hidden in the suspect's car during the arrest.

wear.label: CEFR A1
wear.def: To have clothes or shoes on your body.
wear.ex: I wear a blue shirt to school every day.

wearisome.label: CEFR B2
wearisome.def: Causing someone to feel tired, bored, or annoyed through being repetitive or tedious
wearisome.ex: The wearisome bureaucratic process took months to complete, frustrating everyone involved.

weary.label: CEFR B2
weary.def: Very tired, usually from effort, stress, or something continuing too long
weary.ex: After months of dealing with the same problems, she felt weary and decided to take a break.

weather.label: CEFR A1
weather.def: The condition of the atmosphere, like rain, sun, wind, or snow.
weather.ex: The weather is sunny today, so we can play outside.

weather forecast.label: CEFR B1
weather forecast.def: A prediction of what weather conditions will be like for the next few days.
weather forecast.ex: I checked the weather forecast before planning our camping trip.

weave.label: CEFR B2
weave.def: A pattern created by interlacing threads or fibres in fabric production.
weave.ex: The designer chose a tight weave for the jacket to make it more durable and water-resistant.

web.label: CEFR A2
web.def: A network of connected pages and information on the internet that you can view on a computer.
web.ex: I found that recipe on the web in just two minutes.

web page.label: CEFR A2
web page.def: A document you view on the internet using a browser, containing text, images, and links.
web page.ex: I found a web page with information about learning English online.

webcam.label: CEFR B1
webcam.def: A small camera connected to a computer that records or broadcasts video over the internet.
webcam.ex: During the pandemic, many teachers used webcams to teach their classes online.

website.label: CEFR A2
website.def: A place on the internet with information, pictures, or services that you can look at using a computer.
website.ex: I visited the school's website to find information about classes.

wed.label: CEFR B2
wed.def: To marry someone in a formal ceremony, or to unite two things closely together.
wed.ex: They decided to wed in a small ceremony by the coast rather than a large formal affair.

wedding.label: CEFR A2
wedding.def: A ceremony where two people get married in front of family and friends.
wedding.ex: My sister's wedding is next Saturday at the church.

wednesday.label: CEFR A1
wednesday.def: The fourth day of the week, between Tuesday and Thursday.
wednesday.ex: I have English class on Wednesday afternoon.

weed.label: CEFR B2
weed.def: A wild plant that grows where it is not wanted and competes with cultivated plants for resources.
weed.ex: The gardener spent the morning pulling weeds from between the vegetable rows.

week.label: CEFR A1
week.def: A period of seven days, usually starting on Monday and ending on Sunday.
week.ex: I have school five days a week from Monday to Friday.

weekday.label: CEFR A2
weekday.def: Any day of the week except Saturday and Sunday.
weekday.ex: I go to school on weekdays and stay home on weekends.

weekend.label: CEFR A1
weekend.def: Saturday and Sunday, the two days when most people do not work
weekend.ex: I like to play football with my friends on the weekend.

weekly.label: CEFR A2
weekly.def: Happening or done once every seven days
weekly.ex: I go to the gym weekly on Monday evenings.

weep.label: CEFR A2
weep.def: Tears that fall from your eyes when you cry
weep.ex: She wiped away the weep from her cheeks after hearing the sad news.

weigh.label: CEFR A2
weigh.def: To measure how heavy something or someone is using a scale.
weigh.ex: I weigh myself every morning to check my health.

weight.label: CEFR A2
weight.def: How heavy something is, measured in kilograms or pounds.
weight.ex: The weight of the box was too much for me to carry alone.

weird.label: CEFR B1
weird.def: Strange or unusual in a way that surprises or confuses you
weird.ex: The film had a weird ending that nobody expected.

welcome.label: CEFR A1
welcome.def: To greet someone in a friendly and happy way when they arrive.
welcome.ex: My mother welcomed me at the door with a big hug.

well.label: CEFR A1
well.def: In a good or successful way; satisfactorily.

well-balanced.label: CEFR B2
well-balanced.def: Having all parts or elements in correct proportion and harmony; fair and reasonable.
well-balanced.ex: The chef created a well-balanced menu that offered diverse cuisines alongside traditional local dishes.

well-built.label: CEFR B2
well-built.def: Strong and solidly constructed with good materials and careful workmanship.
well-built.ex: The well-built Victorian house has maintained its structural integrity for over a century.

well-dressed.label: CEFR B1
well-dressed.def: Wearing expensive or fashionable clothes that look neat and attractive
well-dressed.ex: She walked into the meeting looking well-dressed in her tailored suit and polished shoes.

well-known.label: CEFR A2
well-known.def: Known by many people; famous or recognised by lots of people.
well-known.ex: That well-known actor was in several popular films last year.

well-organized/well-organised.label: CEFR B2
well-organized/well-organised.def: Arranged neatly and efficiently with clear structure and planning.
well-organized/well-organised.ex: The conference was well-organised, with detailed schedules and clearly marked registration areas.

well-paid.label: CEFR B2
well-paid.def: Earning a high salary or income for the work you do.
well-paid.ex: She moved to a well-paid position in finance after completing her MBA.

west.label: CEFR A2
west.def: The direction where the sun goes down in the evening.
west.ex: The west coast of the country has beautiful beaches.

western.label: CEFR B2
western.def: Relating to or located in the west; characteristic of countries in western Europe and North America
western.ex: Western nations have historically dominated global trade and politics since the 19th century.

westward.label: CEFR B1
westward.def: Moving or directed towards the west
westward.ex: The pioneers travelled westward across the mountains in search of new land.

wet.label: CEFR A2
wet.def: Covered or soaked with water or other liquid.
wet.ex: My hair is wet after I washed it this morning.

wetland.label: CEFR B1
wetland.def: Low-lying land that is covered by water or very wet soil for much of the year.
wetland.ex: The nature reserve protects an important wetland where many bird species come to breed.

whale.label: CEFR B1
whale.def: The largest animal in the world that lives in the ocean and breathes air through a hole on top of its head.
whale.ex: We went on a boat trip and saw a whale swimming near the shore.

wheat.label: CEFR B2
wheat.def: A cereal crop with grain used to make flour, bread, and other food products.
wheat.ex: Global wheat production has increased significantly due to improved farming techniques and climate adaptation.

wheel.label: CEFR A1
wheel.def: A round object that turns and helps cars, bikes, and other things move.

wheelchair.label: CEFR A2
wheelchair.def: A chair with wheels that people who cannot walk use to move around.
wheelchair.ex: My grandfather uses a wheelchair to go shopping in town.

when.label: CEFR A1
when.def: At what time or during the time that something happens

whenever.label: CEFR B1
whenever.def: At any time that something happens; every time something occurs
whenever.ex: Whenever you have a problem, you can ask your teacher for help.

where.label: CEFR A1
where.def: At or in what place or location

while.label: CEFR B1
while.def: A period of time, especially one that continues for some length
while.ex: We waited for a while before the train finally arrived.

whip.label: CEFR B2
whip.def: To move or strike something with a quick, sharp motion using a flexible object.
whip.ex: The wind began to whip the branches back and forth against the window.

whiskey/whisky.label: CEFR B2
whiskey/whisky.def: A strong alcoholic spirit made from grain or other ingredients, typically aged in wooden barrels.
whiskey/whisky.ex: The Scottish whisky had been matured for twelve years before being released to the market.

whisper.label: CEFR B2
whisper.def: To speak very softly so that only people nearby can hear you.
whisper.ex: She whispered his name so quietly that he almost missed it in the crowded café.

whistle.label: CEFR B2
whistle.def: To produce a high musical sound by forcing air through your lips or a small device.
whistle.ex: The referee whistled to signal the end of the match.

white.label: CEFR A1
white.def: The lightest colour, like snow or milk.
white.ex: She painted her bedroom walls white.

whole.label: CEFR B1
whole.def: Something complete, with all its parts together and nothing removed or missing.
whole.ex: She ate the whole apple, including the skin.

why.label: CEFR A1
why.def: Used to ask the reason or cause for something.

wide.label: CEFR A2
wide.def: Across a large distance from one side to the other.
wide.ex: The river is very wide, and we need a boat to cross it.

widely.label: CEFR A2
widely.def: In many places or by many people; happening or used across a large area
widely.ex: English is widely spoken around the world.

widen.label: CEFR B2
widen.def: To make something broader or to become broader in extent or scope.
widen.ex: The government plans to widen the motorway to reduce traffic congestion in the region.

widespread.label: CEFR B1
widespread.def: existing or happening in many places or affecting many people
widespread.ex: The widespread use of smartphones has changed how people communicate around the world.

widow.label: CEFR B2
widow.def: A woman whose husband has died and who has not remarried.
widow.ex: After her husband passed away, she became a widow and eventually found support through a local grief counselling group.

width.label: CEFR B2
width.def: The measurement of how far something extends from one side to the other.
width.ex: The river's width varies from 50 metres in some places to over 200 metres where it widens near the coast.

wife.label: CEFR A1
wife.def: A woman who is married to a man.
wife.ex: My wife and I have been together for ten years.

wild.label: CEFR A2
wild.def: Living or growing in nature, not controlled or trained by people
wild.ex: We saw wild horses running free across the grasslands.

wilderness.label: CEFR B2
wilderness.def: A large natural area of land that is not developed or cultivated, often remote and wild.
wilderness.ex: The national park protects thousands of square kilometres of pristine wilderness where few people ever venture.

wildlife.label: CEFR B1
wildlife.def: Animals and plants that live naturally in their environment, not kept by people.
wildlife.ex: The national park protects endangered wildlife such as tigers and rare birds.

wildly.label: CEFR B1
wildly.def: In a way that is very energetic, emotional, or uncontrolled.
wildly.ex: The crowd cheered wildly when their team scored the winning goal.

will.label: CEFR B2
will.def: A legal document stating how someone's property and possessions should be distributed after their death.
will.ex: She wrote a will to ensure her children would inherit the family home and her savings.

willing.label: CEFR B2
willing.def: Ready and prepared to do something without being forced or reluctant.
willing.ex: She was willing to compromise on the budget in order to reach an agreement with her colleagues.

willingly.label: CEFR B2
willingly.def: In a way that shows you want to do something, without being forced or reluctant
willingly.ex: She willingly accepted the challenging role, seeing it as an opportunity to develop her skills.

willingness.label: CEFR B1
willingness.def: A feeling of being ready or wanting to do something.
willingness.ex: Her willingness to help made her a popular team member at work.

win.label: CEFR A1
win.def: To be successful in a competition or game; to get first place.
win.ex: My team won the football match yesterday.

wind.label: CEFR B2
wind.def: To turn or coil something around itself or another object in loops or spirals.
wind.ex: She wound the rope tightly around the pole to secure the boat.

winding.label: CEFR B2
winding.def: Following a twisting or curving path with many turns rather than going straight
winding.ex: The winding mountain road offered spectacular views but made the journey much longer than expected.

window.label: CEFR A1
window.def: An opening in a wall or roof that lets light and air come into a room or building.
window.ex: I opened the window to let fresh air into the bedroom.

windscreen.label: CEFR B1
windscreen.def: The front window of a car or vehicle that protects passengers from wind and weather.
windscreen.ex: The rain was so heavy that the driver turned on the windscreen wipers to see the road clearly.

windshield.label: CEFR B2
windshield.def: The transparent glass panel at the front of a vehicle that protects occupants from wind and weather
windshield.ex: The heavy rain made it difficult to see through the windshield, so the driver turned on the wipers.

windsurfing.label: CEFR B1
windsurfing.def: A water sport where you stand on a board and use a sail to move across water.
windsurfing.ex: My brother tried windsurfing for the first time last summer at the beach and loved it.

windy.label: CEFR A2
windy.def: With a lot of wind blowing; air is moving quickly and strongly.
windy.ex: It was a windy day, so we couldn't fly our kite in the park.

wine.label: CEFR A2
wine.def: An alcoholic drink made from fermented grapes.
wine.ex: We drank red wine with dinner last night.

wing.label: CEFR B1
wing.def: One of the two flat body parts that birds and insects use to fly through the air.
wing.ex: The eagle spread its wings wide and soared above the mountains.

wink.label: CEFR B2
wink.def: A quick closing and opening of one eye, often to communicate something secret or playful.
wink.ex: She gave him a wink to suggest she was joking about what she'd just said.

winner.label: CEFR A2
winner.def: A person or team that wins a game, contest, or competition.
winner.ex: She was the winner of the spelling competition at school.

winter.label: CEFR A1
winter.def: The coldest season of the year, when it is cold and often snows.
winter.ex: In winter, we wear warm coats and hats.

wipe.label: CEFR B2
wipe.def: To clean or dry a surface by rubbing it with cloth or hand
wipe.ex: She wiped the sweat from her forehead after finishing her workout.

wire.label: CEFR B2
wire.def: To connect or install electrical cables or systems to make something functional.
wire.ex: The electrician wired the new kitchen appliances directly to the main circuit board.

wisdom.label: CEFR A2
wisdom.def: Knowledge and good judgment from experience and learning.
wisdom.ex: The teacher shared her wisdom about how to study effectively for exams.

wise.label: CEFR A2
wise.def: Having good judgment and understanding; making sensible decisions based on experience
wise.ex: It was a wise decision to study hard before the exam.

wish.label: CEFR A2
wish.def: To want something to happen or to want something very much
wish.ex: I wish I could travel to Japan next summer.

wit.label: CEFR B2
wit.def: The ability to use words cleverly and humorously to make intelligent observations or jokes.
wit.ex: The professor's wit and sarcasm made her lectures entertaining despite the difficult material.

withdraw.label: CEFR B2
withdraw.def: To remove money from a bank account or to take something out from a place.
withdraw.ex: She decided to withdraw her savings before the interest rates changed.

withdrawal.label: CEFR B2
withdrawal.def: The action of taking money out of a bank account or removing something that was present.
withdrawal.ex: The bank charged a fee for each cash withdrawal from the ATM.

wither.label: CEFR B1
wither.def: To become dry and weak; to lose freshness and strength gradually.
wither.ex: The flowers began to wither after we forgot to water them for two weeks.

witness.label: CEFR B1
witness.def: To see something happen in person, especially an event or crime
witness.ex: Several people witnessed the accident on the motorway yesterday and spoke to police.

witty.label: CEFR B2
witty.def: Clever and funny, showing quick and intelligent humour
witty.ex: Her witty remarks during the presentation had the entire audience laughing and impressed.

wizard.label: CEFR B1
wizard.def: Extremely skilled or impressive at doing something; having exceptional talent or ability.
wizard.ex: She's a wizard at solving complex mathematical problems that confuse most students.

wolf.label: CEFR B2
wolf.def: A large wild dog that hunts in packs and lives in forests or mountains.
wolf.ex: The conservation team tracked the wolf population across the national park to understand their hunting patterns.

woman.label: CEFR A1
woman.ex: The woman at the shop helped me find my size.

wonder.label: CEFR A2
wonder.def: To think about something and want to know the answer.
wonder.ex: I wonder what time the train arrives tomorrow.

wonderful.label: CEFR A1
wonderful.def: Very good; causing you to feel happy and excited.
wonderful.ex: We had a wonderful time at the beach yesterday.

wondrous.label: CEFR B2
wondrous.def: Inspiring awe or amazement through being extremely beautiful, impressive, or extraordinary.
wondrous.ex: The wondrous architecture of the cathedral left visitors speechless as they gazed at its intricate details.

wood.label: CEFR A2
wood.def: Hard material from trees, used to make furniture, buildings, and many other things.
wood.ex: We burned wood in the fireplace to keep warm during winter.

woodcarving.label: CEFR B2
woodcarving.def: The art of shaping wood using tools to create decorative or sculptural objects.
woodcarving.ex: She developed a passion for woodcarving after attending a workshop at the local arts centre, and now creates intricate figurines that she sells at craft fairs.

wooden.label: CEFR A2
wooden.def: Made from wood; hard material from trees
wooden.ex: We sat on a wooden bench in the park.

wool.label: CEFR A2
wool.def: Thick, warm material made from sheep's hair, used to make clothes and blankets.
wool.ex: I wear a wool sweater in winter to stay warm.

word.label: CEFR A1
word.def: A single unit of language that has meaning and stands alone when written or spoken

wordlessly.label: CEFR B2
wordlessly.def: Without speaking; using only actions, expressions, or gestures to communicate.
wordlessly.ex: She wordlessly handed him the letter, her expression conveying everything she could not say aloud.

work.label: CEFR A1
work.def: To do a job or activity to earn money or achieve something.
work.ex: I work in an office five days a week.

workaholic.label: CEFR B2
workaholic.def: Compulsively devoted to working, often at the expense of rest, relationships, or personal wellbeing.
workaholic.ex: His workaholic tendencies meant he rarely took holidays and often worked late into the evenings.

workbench.label: CEFR B2
workbench.def: A sturdy table or surface where craftspeople or engineers perform detailed work and repairs
workbench.ex: The carpenter arranged his tools neatly on the workbench before starting to build the cabinet.

worker.label: CEFR A1
worker.def: A person who does a job and receives money for it.
worker.ex: My mother is a nurse worker at the hospital.

working.label: CEFR B1
working.def: In operation or functioning; doing a job or activity
working.ex: She spent the afternoon on a working farm, learning how to care for the animals.

workman.label: CEFR B2
workman.def: A man employed to do physical or manual labour, often skilled in a particular trade or craft
workman.ex: The experienced workman repaired the roof with remarkable precision and efficiency.

workout.label: CEFR B1
workout.def: A session of physical exercise to improve fitness or stay healthy.
workout.ex: She goes to the gym for a workout three times a week to build strength.

workplace.label: CEFR B1
workplace.def: A place where people go to do their job or work, such as an office or factory.
workplace.ex: My workplace is a modern office building in the city centre where I work with twenty other people.

workshop.label: CEFR B1
workshop.def: A place where people make or repair things using tools and equipment.
workshop.ex: He spent Saturday afternoon in his woodworking workshop building a bookshelf.

worktable.label: CEFR B2
worktable.def: A sturdy table designed for doing practical work, crafts, or manual tasks with tools and materials.
worktable.ex: The carpenter organised his tools neatly on the worktable before starting the furniture repair.

world.label: CEFR A1
world.def: The planet Earth and all the people, countries, and things on it.
world.ex: There are many different countries in the world.

worldly.label: CEFR B2
worldly.def: Experienced in many different places and cultures; not concerned with religious or spiritual matters.
worldly.ex: Her worldly knowledge came from years of travelling across Europe and Asia.

worldview.label: CEFR B2
worldview.def: A person's set of beliefs and assumptions about life, reality, and how the world works.
worldview.ex: Her worldview was shaped by growing up in different countries and experiencing diverse cultures.

worldwide.label: CEFR A2
worldwide.def: In or to every part of the world
worldwide.ex: That music star is famous worldwide and has fans in many countries.

worm.label: CEFR B2
worm.def: A small, long, thin creature with no legs that lives in soil or inside other animals.
worm.ex: The gardener dug up the soil and found several worms, which are important for keeping the earth healthy.

worn.label: CEFR B1
worn.def: Looking old and damaged from use or age; showing signs of being used a lot.
worn.ex: His worn leather jacket had been with him for over twenty years.

wornout/worn out/worn-out.label: CEFR B2
wornout/worn out/worn-out.def: Exhausted from hard work or use; no longer in good condition from being used too much
wornout/worn out/worn-out.ex: After months of caring for her elderly mother, Sarah felt completely worn out and needed a proper holiday.

worried.label: CEFR A2
worried.def: Feeling nervous or anxious about something that might happen or go wrong.
worried.ex: She was worried about her test tomorrow, so she studied all evening.

worry.label: CEFR A2
worry.def: To feel anxious or nervous about something that might happen or go wrong.
worry.ex: I worry about my exam next week because I haven't studied enough.

worrying.label: CEFR B2
worrying.def: Causing concern or anxiety; making someone feel uneasy or troubled.
worrying.ex: The worrying trend of rising unemployment rates has prompted the government to announce new economic measures.

worse.label: CEFR B2
worse.def: A more serious or difficult state or condition; something more negative or unpleasant than before
worse.ex: The situation had deteriorated to a point where things could only get worse without immediate intervention.

worsen.label: CEFR B2
worsen.def: To become worse or make something worse in quality or condition.
worsen.ex: The patient's condition worsened after they stopped taking their medication.

worship.label: CEFR B2
worship.def: Feeling and showing great love or respect for someone or something, often through religious or spiritual practice
worship.ex: The newspaper reported that thousands of people gathered at the temple for worship during the religious festival.

worst.label: CEFR B1
worst.def: The most bad or least good person or thing in a group or situation.
worst.ex: Even on my worst day, I try to stay positive and do my best work.

worth.label: CEFR B1
worth.def: Having value or importance equal to a certain amount of money or effort
worth.ex: That antique painting is worth thousands of pounds, so we keep it in a safe place.

worthless.label: CEFR B2
worthless.def: Having no value, use, or importance; not worth keeping or considering.
worthless.ex: The company's worthless shares became virtually impossible to sell after the scandal.

worthwhile.label: CEFR B1
worthwhile.def: Important or useful enough to spend time, money, or effort on.
worthwhile.ex: Learning a new language is worthwhile because it opens doors to better job opportunities.

worthy.label: CEFR B1
worthy.def: Having good qualities that deserve respect, attention, or support
worthy.ex: That charity does worthy work helping homeless people in our community.

would.label: CEFR A1
would.def: Used to show what someone wanted to do or what happened regularly in the past.
would.ex: When I was a child, I would play football every day after school.

wound.label: CEFR B2
wound.def: To injure someone by cutting or piercing their skin, typically with a weapon or sharp object.
wound.ex: The soldier was wounded in the chest during the battle and required immediate medical attention.

wrap.label: CEFR B1
wrap.def: To cover or enclose something completely with paper, cloth, or other material.
wrap.ex: She wrapped the present in colourful paper before the party.

wrapping.label: CEFR B1
wrapping.def: Material used to cover or protect something, such as paper or plastic around a package.
wrapping.ex: The present arrived in beautiful gold wrapping paper that was decorated with red ribbons.

wreck.label: CEFR B2
wreck.def: The remains of a ship, vehicle, or building that has been severely damaged or destroyed.
wreck.ex: Divers discovered the wreck of the cargo ship on the ocean floor, thirty years after it sank.

wrist.label: CEFR B1
wrist.def: The joint between your hand and forearm that allows your hand to bend and rotate.
wrist.ex: She wore a silver bracelet around her wrist.

wristwatch.label: CEFR B1
wristwatch.def: A small clock that you wear on your wrist to tell the time.
wristwatch.ex: I checked my wristwatch and realised I was already ten minutes late for the meeting.

write.label: CEFR A1
write.def: To mark letters, words, or symbols on paper or a surface with a pen or pencil
write.ex: I write my name on the paper every day.

writer.label: CEFR A1
writer.def: A person who creates books, stories, articles, or other written works.
writer.ex: My mother is a writer who works for a newspaper.

writing.label: CEFR A2
writing.def: The activity of putting words and letters on paper or a computer using a pen or keyboard.
writing.ex: She practises her writing every day by doing homework and keeping a journal.

written.label: CEFR B1
written.def: Recorded or expressed using letters and words on paper or screen
written.ex: I need to submit a written report by Friday for my work.

wrong.label: CEFR A2
wrong.def: not correct or accurate; not the right way
wrong.ex: I spelled the word wrong on my test yesterday.

wrongly.label: CEFR B2
wrongly.def: in a way that is not correct or not based on accurate information or reasoning
wrongly.ex: She was wrongly accused of stealing the money, but evidence later proved her innocence.

x-ray.label: CEFR B2
x-ray.def: A form of electromagnetic radiation that can pass through solid objects, used in medical imaging to see inside the body.
x-ray.ex: The doctor ordered an X-ray to check for fractures after the accident.

yacht.label: CEFR B2
yacht.def: A large, luxurious sailing or motor-powered boat used for recreation or racing.
yacht.ex: The billionaire's private yacht was anchored off the coast of Monaco during the summer season.

yard.label: CEFR A1
yard.def: An outdoor area of ground next to a house, usually with grass or plants.
yard.ex: The children are playing in the yard behind their house.

yawn.label: CEFR B2
yawn.def: To open your mouth wide and breathe in deeply, usually when tired or bored.
yawn.ex: During the lengthy lecture, several students began to yawn and shift uncomfortably in their seats.

yeah.label: CEFR A2
yeah.def: Informal way to say yes or agree with someone.
yeah.ex: Yeah, I'd love to come to your party tomorrow!

year.label: CEFR A1
year.def: A period of 365 or 366 days; the time it takes Earth to go around the sun once
year.ex: I have lived in London for one year.

yearn.label: CEFR B2
yearn.def: To have a strong, persistent desire for something you do not currently have.
yearn.ex: After years abroad, she yearned to return to her childhood home in the countryside.

yell.label: CEFR B2
yell.def: To shout loudly, often in anger, fear, or excitement.
yell.ex: The coach yelled at the players during the timeout to remind them of the game plan.

yellow.label: CEFR A1
yellow.def: A bright colour like the sun or ripe bananas
yellow.ex: My favourite colour is yellow, and I love wearing yellow shirts.

yes.label: CEFR A1
yes.def: Used to show agreement or say that something is true or allowed.
yes.ex: Do you like ice cream? Yes, I do!

yesterday.label: CEFR A1
yesterday.ex: I went to the park yesterday and played football with my friends.

yet.label: CEFR A1
yet.def: Up to now; used to ask if something has happened or finished
yet.ex: Have you finished your homework yet?

yield.label: CEFR B2
yield.def: To produce or give something as a result, profit, or harvest.
yield.ex: The company's new product line is expected to yield higher profits within the first year.

yoga.label: CEFR B1
yoga.def: An ancient practice combining physical exercises, breathing techniques, and meditation for fitness and mental wellbeing.
yoga.ex: She practises yoga three times a week to reduce stress and improve her flexibility.

yogurt/yoghurt.label: CEFR A1
yogurt/yoghurt.def: A thick, creamy food made from milk, eaten cold, often with fruit or honey.
yogurt/yoghurt.ex: I eat yoghurt with berries for breakfast every morning.

young.label: CEFR A1
young.def: In the early years of life, not old.
young.ex: The young children played in the park.

youth.label: CEFR A2
youth.def: The period of life when you are young, usually from around age 13 to 25.
youth.ex: In her youth, she travelled around Europe and had many adventures.

youthful.label: CEFR B2
youthful.def: Having the energy, appearance, or qualities typical of young people; seeming younger than one's actual age.
youthful.ex: Despite being in her sixties, her youthful enthusiasm and energetic approach to life inspired everyone around her.

yummy.label: CEFR B2
yummy.def: Delicious and very pleasant to eat or taste.
yummy.ex: The chocolate cake was absolutely yummy, and everyone asked for seconds.

zebra.label: CEFR B2
zebra.def: A large African mammal with black and white striped fur, related to horses and donkeys.
zebra.ex: The zebra herd migrated across the savanna in search of fresh water and grazing land.

zip.label: CEFR B2
zip.def: A fastener with two rows of interlocking teeth that opens and closes a garment or bag.
zip.ex: The zip on her jacket was stuck, so she had to pull it down carefully to avoid damaging the fabric.

zone.label: CEFR A2
zone.def: An area or region with particular characteristics or purpose.
zone.ex: The northern zone of the country has colder winters than the south.

zoo.label: CEFR A1
zoo.def: A place where wild animals are kept in cages or enclosures for people to see.
zoo.ex: We went to the zoo on Saturday and saw lions, elephants, and monkeys.

zoom.label: CEFR B2
zoom.def: A rapid increase in size or magnification, or the sound made by something moving very fast.
zoom.ex: The camera's zoom feature allowed her to capture distant details of the landscape without moving closer.

analyse.label: Academic · Sublist 1
analyse.def: To examine something carefully in order to understand it or explain it.
analyse.ex: The researchers analysed data from over 2,000 participants.

approach.label: CEFR B2
approach.def: A way of dealing with a situation or problem; to begin to deal with something.
approach.ex: The study takes a comparative approach to examine policy differences.

area.label: CEFR A2
area.def: A subject or range of activity; a particular topic or field.
area.ex: This paper focuses on the area of environmental regulation.

assess.label: CEFR B2
assess.def: To judge or decide the quality, importance, or value of something.
assess.ex: It is difficult to assess the full impact of these policy changes.

assume.label: CEFR B1
assume.def: To accept that something is true without having proof of it.
assume.ex: The model assumes that all variables remain constant.

authority.label: CEFR B1
authority.def: The power or right to give orders and make decisions; an expert source.
authority.ex: Local authorities are responsible for implementing the new regulations.

available.label: CEFR B1
available.def: Able to be used, obtained, or accessed.
available.ex: The available evidence suggests a strong correlation between the two factors.

benefit.label: CEFR B2
benefit.def: An advantage or positive effect; to receive an advantage from something.
benefit.ex: The benefits of early intervention have been well documented.

concept.label: CEFR B1
concept.def: An abstract idea or general principle.
concept.ex: The concept of sustainability is central to modern urban planning.

consist.label: CEFR A2
consist.def: To be made up of particular things or people.
consist.ex: The sample consisted of 150 undergraduate students.

constitute.label: CEFR B1
constitute.def: To form or make up a whole; to be considered as something.
constitute.ex: These findings constitute a significant contribution to the field.

context.label: CEFR A2
